Absalom.title: Absalom
Absalom.extract: For more than 4,000 years, Absalom (pronounced AB-sah-lahm) has been the City at the Center of the World, a metropolis-sized showcase of the greatest treasures in all Golarion. The importance and influence of Absalom upon the Inner Sea and the whole of Golarion can not be overstated. The city not only holds a key strategic position for both commercial and military endeavors in the region, but encompasses the site of the ascension of four deities and claims to have been founded by none other than the Last Azlanti, the god Aroden. It is not without reason that the passage of time throughout the Inner Sea region is counted in Absalom Reckoning.

Copperwood.title: Copperwood
Copperwood.extract:
For other meanings of "Copperheads", please see Copperheads (disambiguation).

Copperwood is a small town located northeast of Absalom just outside the city's walls that is the home of many of Absalom's laborers, tradesmen, and domestic servants. Originally a tent city of unskilled laborers who could not afford to live in the city proper, conditions steadily improved over the centuries until Copperwood became a town in its own right. The locals are often known as "Copperheads".

Dawnfoot.title: Dawnfoot
Dawnfoot.extract: The small town of Dawnfoot lies just southeast of the city of Absalom, between the city walls surrounding the Precipice Quarter and Starwatch Keep. It began as a camp for members of the Starwatch (who must live outside the city walls due to an ancient agreement with the First Guard), but has since grown into a somewhat insular community of military families, many of whom can look back on ten generations who have served in the Starwatch. Those families who do not have members in the Starwatch generally work in some other support capacity in Starwatch Keep. Its city hall is housed in a converted siege engine.

Shoreline.title: Shoreline
Shoreline.extract: Shoreline is a small town located just outside the never-breached walls of Absalom, directly west of the district called the Puddles and the Sally Port.

Westerhold.title: Westerhold
Westerhold.extract: Westerhold is the richest of Absalom's outlying towns. Members of Absalom's wealthiest families own small estates in Westerhold. The town is also known for its large number of dwarven inhabitants and trustworthy stables.

Wellspring Tower.title: Wellspring Tower
Wellspring Tower.extract:
The Wellspring Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located on the north side of the Isle of Kortos, near the Verdant Passage and the small town of Matten Cleave. It is known for having a sweet water spring at its base that provides water and greenery to the surrounding area.

Castinlee.title: Castinlee
Castinlee.extract: Castinlee is an agricultural settlement located in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is known for the many orchards of productive fruit and nut trees in the area.

Matten Cleave.title: Matten Cleave
Matten Cleave.extract: Matten Cleave is a town in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos used primarily by local farmers for transporting crops to the port of Kerrick and buying agricultural supplies. It is located near the northwestern shore of the Isle of Kortos on the Inner Sea, northeast of Kerrick.

Black Whale.title: Black Whale
Black Whale.extract:
Established in 4693 AR, the Black Whale is a prison on the western part of the Flotsam Graveyard intended for the most notorious or politically sensitive prisoners too dangerous to be kept in the Brine in Puddles.

The prison consists of six beached ships, bolted to it with long chains and each lashed together. The guards and other staff stay on the northernmost of the ships, which holds their quarters, all the rowboats, the armory, and the storerooms. An adjacent ship is home to the galley and processing rooms where prisoners are fed and readied for the other ships. A series of pulleys connects ropes and anchors to all six ships, and during rough seas, the prisoners push a pulley axle on each ship to tow supply ships in.

Due to the nature of these criminals, only the Grand Council holds a complete list of who resides within the prison, and few survive long in the squalid conditions aboard (although death typically occurs only due to natural causes). The Lord Warden of the Black Whale is the Fourth Lord of Laws, Guirden of House Gixx. Due to the importance of these prisoners, guards are ever watchful of prisoners breaking out, or outsiders breaking in.

Acting Primarch Wynsal Starborn has proposed overhauling the Black Whale and replacing its leadership due to reports of corruption within the prison; corruption he witnessed firsthand when he was wrongly imprisoned there.

Crimson Coin.title: Crimson Coin
Crimson Coin.extract: Crimson Coin is a bustling two-story tavern and gambling house located in the Foreign Quarter in the city of Absalom. It was one of the bars at which the Pathfinder Society was founded on 23 Desnus, 4307 AR.

Absalom Lighthouse.title: Absalom Lighthouse
Absalom Lighthouse.extract: Absalom Lighthouse is located on Pilot Island at the center of Absalom's Flotsam Graveyard. It is a famed piece of architecture built under the command of the god Aroden. At 655 feet in height, the light from its tower can be seen from as far as 50 miles away on a clear night and at least 25 miles on stormy evenings or bright afternoons. All Inner Sea navigators know its light, which has helped make the City at the Center of the World a successful trade port. Along with the Blue Tower, the Spire of Nex, and the peaks of the Kortos Mounts, it is among the most recognizable parts of Absalom's skyline.

Blue Tower.title: Blue Tower
Blue Tower.extract: The Blue Tower is the tallest building within the city walls of Absalom, and the third-tallest building in the whole city. Residents across the city use both the Blue Tower, the northwestern Spire of Nex, and the peaks of the Kortos Mounts as navigational landmarks.

Absalom Mint.title: Absalom Mint
Absalom Mint.extract:
The Absalom Mint, located near Azlanti Keep in Absalom's Wise Quarter that produces much of the city's coinage—a function that, due to the City at the Center of the World's outsized influence on trade and commerce, literally and figuratively shapes money across the Inner Sea region and beyond. Coins minted in Absalom are either accepted in locations as distant as Tian Xia and Vudra, or is the standard by which nations measure and mint their own currency.

The Mint re-mints currencies from other nations, often increasing the resulting coins' purity in the process. Its building also houses the city's Office of Taxation.

Beast.title: Beast
Beast.extract: A beast is part of a broad classification of creatures similar to animals but with intelligence near, equaling, or surpassing sentient creatures. Many can understand and communicate using language, and are capable of reason.

Otari.title: Otari
Otari.extract: Otari is a small port town near the Immenwood on the south coast of the Isle of Kortos, about halfway between Absalom and Diobel. It is located at the head of a bay of the Inner Sea. It is a modern but relatively quiet settlement, and is popular among retired Pathfinders, many of whom operate businesses there. Important to the economy of Absalom as a whole is Otari's lumber trade: timber cut from the Immenwood arrives by road into Otari and, from there, is traded across the Inner Sea region.

Ferny.title: Ferny
Ferny.extract: The village of Ferny is the largest settlement of shoonies in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos.

Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall.title: Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall
Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall.extract: Morilla Palace is a beautiful residence with magnificent gardens, incredible paintings and sculptures, and traps everywhere, capable of killing in ways one can't even imagine. It's one of the places most avoided by thieves, as the members of House Morilla are adept at hundreds of methods of killing, and the Lotus Guard patrols the area severely, more to ensure the thieves' safety than out of fear of robberies. Trapmaster Tok is responsible for maintaining these traps, and such is the palace's reputation that thieves come to steal just for the reputation boost if they manage to escape alive. The Tally Wall is a memorial that began as graffiti with the names of thieves who perished in their robbery attempts, and a priest of Pharasma is hired to maintain the shrine. Payment for his services is a collaboration between the House and the Bloody Barbers, a cooperation that neither group emphasizes.

Blackhill's.title: Blackhill's
Blackhill's.extract: Blackhill's is a tavern in Eastgate that at first glance looks like a thatched roof a foot off the ground, leading to a constantly ventilated cellar where Dege Blackhill creates his famous spicy drinks, popular with those from Numeria. Members of the Post Guard can also be seen there, spending their free time looking for missions to take on in the outskirts of town, and Dege is a good source of rumors, as he's quite the gossip. One of the rumors he'd like to see disappear concerns his past, specifically how he had to leave home after some kind of scandal, and the hints of nervousness he shows whenever someone brings up the subject suggest there's some truth to them.

Spire of Nex.title: Spire of Nex
Spire of Nex.extract: The Spire of Nex, also called the Tower of Nex, is a tall siege castle created by the archmage Nex as part of his unsuccessful siege of Absalom in 166 AR. Nex constructed it using a shard from his personal demiplane known as the Crux of Nex.

Frothripper.title: Frothripper
Frothripper.extract:
Frothripper is a waterfall about 200 feet tall that is fed from the Kortos Mounts north of Absalom.

This page contains spoilers for the following products: major plot events and an encounter in The Mantis's Prey.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The waterfall hides an abandoned bardic college once known as Allegro College.

Meravon.title: Meravon
Meravon.extract: Meravon, sometimes spelled Merovan, is a logging settlement located within the Immenwood on the Isle of Kortos. It is considered the center of Absalom's lumber industry, and the wood cut in the forest is transported either by the Deluge River to Diobel or overland to Otari.

Old Forest Tower.title: Old Forest Tower
Old Forest Tower.extract:
The Old Forest Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located in the Swardlands on the Isle of Kortos in the Immenwood.

Unlike the other Aeon Towers, this tower contains a shrine to Aroden inside.

Turpin Rowe.title: Turpin Rowe
Turpin Rowe.extract: Turpin Rowe is a small logging town on the Isle of Kortos managed by the Yalbrough Consortium of Absalom. Its economy is driven by the lumber trade but derives its name from the nearby distillation of turp spirits.

Arbo.title: Arbo
Arbo.extract: Arbo is a mostly abandoned limestone quarry village in the western Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos, located north of the Irr River on the western edge of the Inland Hills on a farm track that connects the northern Husna Crossroads to Stump south of Arbo.

Brume.title: Brume
Brume.extract: Brume is a gypsum mining town village in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos named for its misty mornings.

Cawshax.title: Cawshax
Cawshax.extract: Cawshax, originally Cow Shacks, is an agricultural village in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is located south of Potter's Lake at the crossroads of the Merovan Road and the roads connecting to Castinlee and Turpin Rowe, with a locally known but obscure cow path leading directly to the road to Kerrick. Both farmers and herders use the lands around Cawshax, with barley and millet as its crops and cattle as its livestock.

Stump.title: Stump
Stump.extract: Stump, originally called Egert, is a fishing village and former logging town in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos. It is located northeast of Potter's Lake on the route between Brume and the rest of the Swardlands.

Arazlant Mox.title: Arazlant Mox
Arazlant Mox.extract: Arazlant Mox is the highest peak in the Kortos Mounts, the mountain range that dominates the center of the Isle of Kortos. It is over 26,400 feet in height. The peak was the first home of the Starstone when Aroden raised the island from the sea, and is sacred to all four Ascended gods.

Hazrak.title: Hazrak
Hazrak.extract: Hazrak is a minotaur settlement located in the Riven Hills of the Isle of Kortos in Absalom. Its inhabitants can trace their town's origins all the way back to the First Siege of Absalom in 23 AR. During this war, the minotaur warlord Voradni Voon brought thousands of his kind through the Brazen Arch to lay siege to Absalom. After their defeat by the god Aroden himself, the surviving minotaurs fled to the central highlands of the island, where they (or their descendants) founded Hazrak, and continue to harass the other races of the island to this day.

Famished Mind.title: Famished Mind
Famished Mind.extract: The Famished Mind is an entity that resides within a citrine sphere of unknown origins, hanging from an overpass in the Kortos Mounts. It was discovered decades ago by a clutch of xulgaths who found a cache of orange crystal shards humming with hunger in their home, and followed the trail of increasingly big crystals, believing this to be a sign from their patron Zevgavizeb. When the xulgaths approached, they were consumed, mind and body, by the Famished Mind, which gained the ability to affect and bind basalt in the process. Now the Famished Mind builds orange and grey xulgath simulacra and sends them ranging across the Kortos Mounts. These simulacra seek to bring prey to the Famished Mind to consume, allowing it to grow in knowledge and subtlety and spread its enigmatic agenda.

Eagle Garrison Fort.title: Eagle Garrison Fort
Eagle Garrison Fort.extract: The Eagle Garrison Fort is a fortress of the Eagle Garrison located in the northern Kortos Mounts foothills, about 100 miles' travel from Diobel on land by way of Meravon.

Saddlebark Ranch.title: Saddlebark Ranch
Saddlebark Ranch.extract:
Saddlebark Ranch is a breeder and trainer of faerie mounts and Taldan corgis located in the foothills of the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. Its proprietor is Timblethorn Baublebrand, who launched the ranch after recognizing that riding dogs were more surefooted than axebeaks or camels in the Mounts.

Once an innovator, Saddlebark was as of 4720 AR merely one of a dozen such ranches in the region, and the Aspis Consortium had been bullying ranchers and acquiring property for a cut of the business.

Weeping Grandfather.title: Weeping Grandfather
Weeping Grandfather.extract: The Weeping Grandfather is a volcanic peak in the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. It is best known as the location for the Cave of Zero Incantation—a now abandoned site sacred to the Muted God.

Willowside.title: Willowside
Willowside.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Siege of the Dinosaurs.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Willowside is an old, reclusive town on the northwestern coast of the Isle of Kortos, which has suffered greatly from a strange phenomenon known as the Welt in recent years.

Bosco.title: Bosco
Bosco.extract: Bosco is a town on the eastern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom, just southeast of the Tyrant's Grasp.

Flesk.title: Flesk
Flesk.extract: Flesk is a settlement on the central eastern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom.

Mount Ganog.title: Mount Ganog
Mount Ganog.extract: Mount Ganog is a twin-peaked mountain in the northern Kortos Mounts, located on the Isle of Kortos within the domain of the city-state of Absalom. At 21,000 feet tall, it is not the tallest peak in the range—a title held by Arazlant Mox. Unlike the rest of the craggy mountains of that range, Ganog is known for its gentle slopes and small mining communities, but that does not mean that there are no songs and tales told about it.

Marsh Tower.title: Marsh Tower
Marsh Tower.extract:
The Marsh Tower, also known by locals as the Marsh Stone Tower or the "Tower of Empty Stones", is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located on the northern side of the Isle of Kortos, near the small town of Willowside. Its aeon orb was stolen by the Circle of Stones in 4710 AR, and the tower was subsequently inhabited by a splinter group of the Circle's nature priests known as the Empty Stones. The loss of the aeon orb accelerated the advance of the Welt—a blight that as of 4720 AR threatened to overtake the tower despite the efforts of the Empty Stones.

Aerie.title: Aerie
Aerie.extract:
The Aerie is a fortress of Andoren Eagle Knights located in the northern reaches of the Kortos Mounts on the Isle of Kortos. Knights assigned to the post deal with thin air due to its elevation, poor food, and local hostilities in order to monitor and prevent any potential threats to Andoran that might originate from the Isle.

As of 4720 AR, several scouts of the Aerie had recently been found decapitated. Despite a lack of evidence suggesting a suspect, most of the fort's troops believed the nearby Gorebreather tribe of minotaurs were responsible.

Grandfather's Arms.title: Grandfather's Arms
Grandfather's Arms.extract: Grandfather's Arms refers to the settled area between two volcanic ridges north and northwest of the Weeping Grandfather volcano on the Isle of Kortos. The Earthsong tribe of minotaurs have lived in Grandfather's Arms for generations, farming the rich soil and crafting items for trade out of bone and stone.

Almas.title: Almas
Almas.extract: Almas (pronounced AHL-mehs) is the capital of the free nation of Andoran, and one of the largest and most influential cities on the Inner Sea. In many ways, the city's egalitarianism and multi-culturalism typify the assumptions and prejudices that are held about Andoran society in general.

Souston.title: Souston
Souston.extract: Souston is a small Andoren fishing village on the Inner Sea, approximately 240 miles due east of the capital of Almas. The town consists of small, brightly colored shacks that cling to the gently sloping coast.

Riverford.title: Riverford
Riverford.extract: Riverford is a small Andoren agricultural community situated up the Andoshen River from the capital of Almas.

Cyremium.title: Cyremium
Cyremium.extract: Cyremium is a small, isolated fishing town on the Inner Sea coast, just east of the southern tip of the Cape of Hope on the Missa Shelf. Now located within the Arthfell region of Andoran, the town once was the site of a Taldan fortress that was abandoned soon after the end of the Even-Tongued Conquest of 4081 AR. Local settlers began dismantling the four watchtowers to build their homes as soon as the soldiers left, although the outer, sun-bleached walls still remain.

Lavieton.title: Lavieton
Lavieton.extract: Lavieton is a small Andoran fishing village on the Inner Sea coast, approximately 250 miles southeast of the city of Augustana. The finest fish in all of the Inner Sea are said to be caught here, and the fishmonger at the Sign of the Blue Sail is known throughout the Inner Sea region. The best fishing is from midsummer through early autumn, after which Waldry Quence and the other workers at the Sign of the Blue Sail smoke the fish and prepare them for export. The houses of Lavieton are generally small and of wattle and daub construction, and sit on sandy dunes on the top of a seaside bluff.

Claes.title: Claes
Claes.extract:
The Andoren fishing village of Claes is located on the Inner Sea coast, just over 50 miles south of the Andoshen River delta. A humble town, the inhabitants make their living from the ample harvest of the sea, supplementing it with some subsistence farming. When pirates or slavers attack, the people of Claes retreat to an ancient, underground Azlanti fortress known as the Claes Redoubt; the ruin is known to be quite deep.

General Reginald Cormoth, commander of Andoran's Eagle Knights, was born in Claes.

Kozan.title: Kozan
Kozan.extract: Kozan is a large port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located about halfway between the capital of Oppara and Taldor's southern border with Qadira. Its nearest neighbors are Mut (to the north), and Merciful Bay (to the south).

Abberton.title: Abberton
Abberton.extract: Abberton is a small, declining town in the eastern part of the Isle of Erran. It is debated whether the town's name is shortened from Abadartown, or if it was named after the founding Abber family.

Galizhur.title: Galizhur
Galizhur.extract: Galizhur is a largely dwarven mining settlement on the southern coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom.

Erran Tower.title: Erran Tower
Erran Tower.extract:
The Erran Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat top.

The tower is located on the east side of the Isle of Erran, and is responsible for magically giving life to the area.

Diobel.title: Diobel
Diobel.extract: Diobel is one of the larger towns under the rule of the city-state of Absalom. While not as large as Escadar off the Isle of Kortos' northern coast, Diobel plays an important role in the commercial and political affairs of the City at the Center of the World.

Oppara.title: Oppara
Oppara.extract: Oppara, the City of Empire, (pronounced oh-PAH-rah) is the capital of Taldor, and by far the largest city in the realm. It is an ancient and thriving city which earned the title the Gilded City because of the many roofs, spires, and domes that once glittered with gold plating in the high afternoon sun. Even though the metal was long ago stripped away, Oppara remains the example of what the empire once was and what it could be again, though, today, it is plagued with mismanagement, selfish aristocrats, poverty, and crime.

Ridonport.title: Ridonport
Ridonport.extract: Ridonport is a small coastal city located in the empire of Taldor, south of the city of Cassomir. The settlement is best known as the home of General Arnisant, the Taldan hero responsible for imprisoning Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant.

Mut.title: Mut
Mut.extract:
Mut is a small city located on the peninsula at the southern side of the mouth of the Porthmos River on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, about 40 miles downriver from the capital city of Oppara.

The city is famous for its grand lighthouse, known as Glory's Flame.

Old Sehir.title: Old Sehir
Old Sehir.extract: Old Sehir is a small town in western Taldor, approximately 90 miles northwest of the capital city of Oppara.

Stepped Tower of Djedefar.title: Stepped Tower of Djedefar
Stepped Tower of Djedefar.extract: The Stepped Tower of Djedefar is a site in northern Osirion. It is located on the north eastern tip of the Alamein Peninsula. The tower is home to a community of Irori-worshiping monks.

Hor-Aha.title: Hor-Aha
Hor-Aha.extract: The great lighthouse of Hor-Aha is located on a small island north of the Coast of Graves in Osirion. During the height of ancient Osirion, the lighthouse was a key navigation aid. Its magical light not only marked the location but also gave information about the upcoming weather. Hor-Aha has remained dark for thousands of years, but recently lurid red and purple light strobes from its lantern room, the sign of violent gales. The few vessels that have since made landfall on the small isle were never heard from again.

Pastorling.title: Pastorling
Pastorling.extract:
Pastorling is a small Taldan hamlet that stands in both the literal and figurative shadow of the nearby fortress of Canalsguard. Pastorling is located at the intersection of three of the Lingian Prefecture's canals (alongside Canalsguard), and the reek of their stagnant waters has kept the hamlet free from significant aristocratic interference.

The Lingian Canals are notoriously dangerous, and at least some of this is due to one of Pastorling's residents: Lesah Voriovixis. To the town she is but a humble baker, but in reality she is the heart of a network of organised crime known as the Canal Syndicate. She cunningly encrypts her communications using a hidden cypher based on the braids and fillings of the pies she sends to certain underlings.

Eagle's Head.title: Eagle's Head
Eagle's Head.extract:
Eagle's Head is both an amphitheatre and the settlement that assembles around it every summer while the theatre is in season. Located in the Kazuhn Prefecture of Taldor, Eagle's Head has existed for many millennia, having been built shortly after the god Aroden ascended to full godhood at the beginning of the Age of Enthronement. Ever since then, Eagle's Head has been a centre for the performing arts which attracts viewers and performers from across the Inner Sea region. Practically, every major work of Taldan drama has been performed at Eagle's Head. Every performer from across Taldor dreams of performing here and that their performance captures the imagination of the people and elevates them as a new cultural phenomena, as has happened to countless artists before them.

Every summer, the land for a mile around Eagle's Head becomes a makeshift city of tents, performers, and theatre-goers that is in constant flux as artists and theatre aficionados come and go as the season progresses. This chaotic atmosphere also proves a heaven for those with less-refined interests. Scheming nobles, spies, revolutionaries, and just plain old criminals all use the cover of the Eagle's Head season to conduct their business, its underhanded side an open secret across the nation. The Brotherhood of Silence, one of the Inner Sea's largest thieves' guilds, has a large presence at Eagle's Head. Many members of the Brotherhood of Silence use Eagle's Head as a place to lie low after committing particularly attention-grabbing crimes in nearby Oppara. Eagle's Head propmaster Rouriss Barthel is secretly a member of the Brotherhood and is the closest thing it has to a leader in the theatrical city.

Because of the amount of espionage, intrigue, and general skulduggery that goes on at Eagle's Head, the Lion Blades, Taldor's secret spy organisation, has a strong presence here. They are led by Myro Gravilla, a long-time regular of Eagle's Head, and between them they attempt to keep a secret catalogue of all the season's intrigues. Unbeknownst to most, almost every season sees the Lion Blades foil some major plot to disrupt, poison, invade, or destroy Eagle's Head. The Lions Blades keep this undercover, this way next season's villain will assume the festive settlement to be an easy target.

For the last five years, the elven virtuoso Tirilee Ambrasa has been the talk of Eagle's Head. This is a near-unprecedented hot streak, most performers are only able to claim the spotlight for, at most, a couple of seasons. Despite being a powerful bard, Tirilee's popularity seems to have nothing to do with magic. Her sway over the attendees gives Tirilee incredible access to the Taldan nobility and prestigious guests from other nations meaning the Lion Blades keep a close eye on her. Tirilee maintains an air of mystery by returning to her home nation of Kyonin at the end of every season.

Escadar.title: Escadar
Escadar.extract: Escadar is a city on the Isle of Erran off the north coast of the Isle of Kortos. It houses a city-sized military outpost, acting as one of Absalom's primary bases of naval operations.

Pier's End.title: Pier's End
Pier's End.extract: Pier's End is a small, squalid fishing village on the north coast of the Isle of Kortos within the domain of the city-state of Absalom. It is surrounded by a hard-to-cross bog known as the Dunmire.

Temple of the Eternal Obelisk.title: Temple of the Eternal Obelisk
Temple of the Eternal Obelisk.extract: The Temple of the Eternal Obelisk is an ancient, buried temple found below the salt flats of the northern Ketz Desert of Qadira. One of the Pathfinder Chronicles holds rumors that the Eternal Obelisk within contains the secret of eternal youth.

Hawah.title: Hawah
Hawah.extract: Hawah is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.

Kerrick.title: Kerrick
Kerrick.extract: Kerrick is the largest town in the Swardlands, located west of the mouth of the Husna River on the western coast of the Isle of Kortos.

Elyon.title: Elyon
Elyon.extract: Elyon is a settlement on the western coast of the Isle of Kortos in the nation of Absalom. It is located in the western Swardlands just west of the town of Kerrick.

Ehur.title: Ehur
Ehur.extract: Ehur is a city in Qadira. Lying in the center of the Ketz Desert, this genie city is where denizens of the various Elemental Planes have their embassies and serves as a trading hub for these people within Qadira.

Liferoot Stone.title: Liferoot Stone
Liferoot Stone.extract:
The Liferoot Stone is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower was a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers, though it is now partially collapsed.

The tower is located in the Swardlands on the Isle of Kortos, and is the southernmost of the towers, located near the town of Castinlee.

Rovagug's Hall.title: Rovagug's Hall
Rovagug's Hall.extract: Rovagug's Hall is a ruin that is most known for being raided by a group that took their name after their exploits here, the Raiders of Rovagug's Hall, and is located in the southwest section of the Scrape region of the Isle of Kortos.

Avilan.title: Avilan
Avilan.extract: Avilan is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located along the northern banks of the Pashman River halfway between Demirah and the capital, Katheer.

Golsifar.title: Golsifar
Golsifar.extract:
Golsifar is a small port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located approximately 90 miles north of the mouth of the Jalrune River in the prefecture of Moda. It serves as the prefectural capital.

As the southern staging point for the Taldan Imperial Navy, the settlement unsurprisingly has strong military leanings.

Merciful Bay.title: Merciful Bay
Merciful Bay.extract: Merciful Bay is a large port town on Taldor's Inner Sea coast, located between the towns of Kozan to the north, and Golsifar to the south.

Almas University.title: Almas University
Almas University.extract: Almas University is Andoran's premier educational institution, and is located in the North End district of Almas, just north of the Field of Concord. Even though it is technically open to any Andoren citizen, it accepts only a limited number of new students, which means that competition is fierce, despite the significant cost to attend. Felandriel Morgethai is the school's provost, and has held the position for over 100 years.

Avenue of the Gods.title: Avenue of the Gods
Avenue of the Gods.extract: The Avenue of the Gods is a wide road studded with over a dozen temples and shrines that runs through Almas' East Hill neighborhood. Its spectacular architecture attracts the pious and curious alike who come to visit and admire the former Cathedral of Aroden (now a Pathfinder Society lodge), the Golden Cathedral, the temples of Abadar, Cayden Cailean, Erastil, Pharasma, Shelyn, and Torag, along with smaller chapels to various empyreal lords and other demigods.✝︎

Godsgate.title: Godsgate
Godsgate.extract: The Godsgate is the largest gate in the outer walls of the Andoren capital of Almas. It is also the easternmost gate, and from here visitors can easily access the East Hill, Portside, and Liberty District. Ever since the People's Revolt of 4669 AR brought democracy to the nation, the doors of the Godsgate have never been closed, a source of great pride for the people of Almas.

Railford.title: Railford
Railford.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Decline of Glory.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Railford is a small settlement located on a small wooded hill a few miles inland from the larger settlement of Golsifar, on the Inner Sea coast of Taldor. The settlement is in the prefecture of Moda.

Tower of Winds.title: Tower of Winds
Tower of Winds.extract: The Tower of Winds is a strange structure located on a peninsula on the Plains of Paresh in northwestern Qadira near the Inner Sea's Saray Coast.

High Harbor.title: High Harbor
High Harbor.extract: High Harbor is a once-grand settlement reduced to ruins located on the northeast coast of the Scrape region of the Isle of Kortos.

Delenah.title: Delenah
Delenah.extract: Delenah is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located along the northernmost limits of the nation along its border with Taldor on the southern banks of the Jalrune River.

Demirah.title: Demirah
Demirah.extract: Demirah is one of the numerous massive trading cities that dot the Qadiran region of the Plains of Paresh, but is often overshadowed by the capital of Katheer. It is located at the south-western limits of the Plains of Paresh along the banks of the Pashman River estuary as it reaches the Inner Sea.

Senara.title: Senara
Senara.extract: Senara is a Chelaxian town located in the devil-spawn-infested Whisperwood. It is often used as an example of Cheliax's infernal experiment gone too far. More than half of the town's inhabitants are descended from devils, and nearly a quarter are cambions.

Golden Cathedral.title: Golden Cathedral
Golden Cathedral.extract: The Golden Cathedral is a former temple of Aroden located in the Liberty District of Andoran's capital city of Almas. Situated at the east end of the Field of Concord, this structure has been re-purposed as a library and repository of work dedicated to the concept of Common Rule.

Golden Aerie.title: Golden Aerie
Golden Aerie.extract: The Golden Aerie is the headquarters of the Eagle Knights of Andoran, and is located at the center of the Field of Concord in the Liberty District of the capital city of Almas.

Citadel of the Supreme Elect.title: Citadel of the Supreme Elect
Citadel of the Supreme Elect.extract: The Citadel of the Supreme Elect is a walled complex that houses the offices and residence of the Supreme Elect, who is the chief executive of the democratic nation of Andoran, and who also serves as the mayor of its capital city, Almas. The small keep stands near the western end of the Field of Concord in the Liberty District of Almas. The citadel is topped by a large copper dome, and guarded by a battalion of Steel Falcons. The House of the Demarchy Assembly, which holds the offices of the ministers who advise the Supreme Elect, is also located within its walls, as are buildings that contain the offices of consuls, ministers, and other state officials.

Benedict's Hope.title: Benedict's Hope
Benedict's Hope.extract: Benedict's Hope is a small, rocky island in the Andoshen River just outside the harbor of the Andoren capital of Almas. A small lighthouse, whose ever-burning flame guides ships into port, stands on the island. At the lighthouse's feet sits a small, brass statue of Alysande Benedict, shown hunched over a desk and scribbling with a feather stylus.

Grand Exchange.title: Grand Exchange
Grand Exchange.extract: The Grand Exchange is the largest money exchange in the Andoren capital of Almas. Located in the East Hill neighborhood of the mercantile Portside district, it stands close to Godsgate, and is home to agents and currency exchanges from nearly every nation of the Inner Sea region. These factors deal not only in their own currency, but also in information, alliances, and veiled threats. Many adventurers come to the Grand Exchange looking for sponsorship to explore Golarion's hidden corners.

Balmore's Canal.title: Balmore's Canal
Balmore's Canal.extract: Balmore's Canal is an artificial waterway that bisects the Tamuth District in the southwest corner of the Andoren capital of Almas. Originally built to transport merchant barges, the canal has become a repulsive dumping ground for industrial waste from Smithtown, and more organic waste from Mugget. The canal's appearance and stench have become an embarrassment for the city, but no one has come up with a satisfying solution for the problem.

Iron and Hoof Gate.title: Iron and Hoof Gate
Iron and Hoof Gate.extract: The Iron and Hoof Gate is the southwestern fortified entrance to the Andoren capital of Almas. It leads directly into the industrial and working class Tamuth District, and is wide enough to accommodate both draft animals and large wagons.

Doommark.title: Doommark
Doommark.extract: Doommark is the seat of Molthune's Umbral Basin province and home to the only significant fetchling population in the nation, many of whom work for the territorial governor, General Lord Francyne Magga.

Well of Gorum.title: Well of Gorum
Well of Gorum.extract: The Well of Gorum was formed when a piece of his armor struck Almas, thankfully only destroying a couple of empty warehouses, and the metal was recovered by the city guard. After the storms, the crater has filled with water, and a patrol anxiously watches the area.

Augustana.title: Augustana
Augustana.extract: Augustana is an Inner Sea port city in the nation of Andoran located on Aspo Bay. It is the second-largest city in the nation, and boasts one of the largest shipyards of the Inner Sea region.

Ostenso.title: Ostenso
Ostenso.extract: Ostenso is a major port city on the southern coast of Cheliax, just east of Cape Erebus, and is the easternmost city in the country. Ostenso is currently the home port of the Chelaxian Navy with grand, spacious docks. The naval works here are the largest on the continent, making up a quarter of the city that sprawls over the water. Another quarter of the city stands upon a line of stones known as Custodisce Break, which stretches half a mile into the sea and forms the natural harbour.

Alvis.title: Alvis
Alvis.extract:
Located in the Arthfell region of southeastern Andoran near Aspo Bay, the town of Alvis is best known for a series of kidnappings perpetrated by a local cult in 4708 AR. Alvis is also home to the Andoran Alchemical Society.

In 4708 AR the Sheriff of Alvis was an idealistic young man named Eraltis.

In 4725 AR many people came to the town to join the guild to investigate the effects of the Godsrain, creating a need for new housing.

Triela.title: Triela
Triela.extract: Triela is a large town on Star Bay of the Inner Sea near the mouth of the Sellen River in south-eastern Andoran, close to its border with Taldor. It is the final stop for hundreds of lumberjacks every year who float their logs down from the Verduran Forest and flood the settlement with their hard-earned pay, spending it on company and drink. This has given Triela a reputation for debauchery, one which the wealthier citizens who reside on the bluff above the taverns and brothels have tried hard to overcome.

Brastlewark.title: Brastlewark
Brastlewark.extract: Brastlewark is an almost exclusively gnomish city in eastern Cheliax, located on the western edge of the Aspodell Mountains, at the head of the Brastle River. It is believed to be the largest gnome settlement in the Inner Sea region, perhaps even in the entire world.

Laekastel.title: Laekastel
Laekastel.extract: The Chelaxian city of Laekastel is located on northern shores of the Inner Sea, where the Brastle River flows into the Bay of Deng.

Cettigne.title: Cettigne
Cettigne.extract:
Formerly an independent city-state, Molthune annexed Cettigne in 4708 AR when the city was beset by plague and famine. The nation installed Irina Klim, Molthune's youngest General Lord, as Cettigne's new ruler, but the locals continue to chafe at their new-found servitude. The traditional Free City Citizen Militia continues to protect the city independently of the General Lords. Friction between the locals and the national forces may soon lead to violence.

Cettigne is built around four castles representing the various periods of its history; they are reflected on the city's flag, four white castles on a green field. A fifth castle is under construction, representing Molthuni rule.

Cettigne has a small temple of Abadar.

Chimera Cove.title: Chimera Cove
Chimera Cove.extract: Chimera Cove is a small, nominally Andoren fishing village located on Aspo Bay on the northern shores of the Inner Sea. The coast here is rough, battered by waves and wind, and usually cloaked in fog. The village sprang up during the difficult years following Andoran's split with Cheliax and is named Chimera Cove because of the three large islands just offshore: the Lion, the Dragon, and the Goat. Due to its precarious location, the town has failed to expand and thrive, although its residents have no trouble feeding themselves via subsistence farming and fishing.

Halidon.title: Halidon
Halidon.extract: Halidon is a small logging community on the edge of the northern Backar Forest in Molthune. Lying roughly halfway between the capital of Canorate and Braganza, the town is a frequent stop for travelers and has, therefore, a large number of inns with respect to its resident population.

Tripolne.title: Tripolne
Tripolne.extract: Tripolne is the seat of the Backar territory of Molthune.

Oregent.title: Oregent
Oregent.extract: Oregent (pronounced OHR-eh-gehnt) is a city in central Andoran located south of Darkmoon Vale. It is home to the Lumber Consortium and acts as a gateway for traffic both to and from the Vale.

Falcon's Hollow.title: Falcon's Hollow
Falcon's Hollow.extract:
Falcon's Hollow is a town in Darkmoon Vale, in the nation of Andoran. The settlement sits only a short distance from Darkmoon Wood and is little more than a logging village, comprised almost exclusively of lumberers in the employ of the Lumber Consortium and their families. With few exceptions, the buildings and their inhabitants belong to the Consortium and totally depend on the overpriced goods delivered by the organization.

It is said that in times long past dwarves settled in the surroundings of Falcon's Hollow; rumors persist that forgotten treasures of their fallen civilization yet remain to be found. Yet today it is a place for the desperate with no other place to go, for outcasts and religious zealots seeking to avoid hindrances brought on by their surroundings.

Piren's Bluff.title: Piren's Bluff
Piren's Bluff.extract: Piren's Bluff is a small border hamlet in Andoran that sits atop a flat rock overlooking a narrow canyon leading deep into the Aspodell Mountains. It is surrounded by a tall wooden palisade with six watchtowers. An ancient keep watches over the hamlet from the highest elevation and protects Andoran's western border. The keep is surrounded by massive stone walls and guard towers, and has always served as home to the leaders of Piren's Bluff.

Olfden.title: Olfden
Olfden.extract: Olfden is a large Andoren town located in the nation's Darkmoon Vale region, halfway between Falcon's Hollow and Oregent, and only a few miles away from Adamas, headquarters of the Diamond Regiment.✝︎

Carpenden.title: Carpenden
Carpenden.extract:
This article is about the Andoren city. For the sparkling wine commonly called "Carpenden" after Carpenden Hill, see bubble wine.

Carpenden (sometimes spelled Carpendren) is the most influential city✝︎ in the Carpenden region of Andoran. Located on the north-central Carpenden Plains, it is surrounded by rolling hills that lend themselves to the creation of numerous plantations and orchards.

Steyr.title: Steyr
Steyr.extract: Steyr is an inland town in far-eastern Andoran on the Carpenden Plains, approximately 150 miles west of the Sellen River. It houses a large community of expatriates from the tumultuous nation of Galt, many of them artists and philosophers.

Fusil.title: Fusil
Fusil.extract: Fusil is a town in far-eastern Andoran surrounded on all sides by the Verduran Forest. Ages ago, the inhabitants of the hamlet made a pact with the local druids and fey-folk. In exchange for the location of a rich gem mine, the citizens of Fusil pledged that no one would chop down a single tree of the forest. The terms of the agreement have not been breached, and the inhabitants make a decent living from the mine, use only stone in the construction of their homes, and rely on peat fires for warmth.

Misarias.title: Misarias
Misarias.extract: The city of Misarias in northeastern Cheliax stands on the banks of the River Keld on the Isgeri border, and serves as a waypoint for travelers of all sorts—thieves to diplomats, and brigands to scouts. With a suspicious eye on Andoran and Molthune, and with memories of the Goblinblood Wars ever in mind, Her Infernal Majestrix's generals keep most of the nation's massive army stationed in the region, where they could hold back an invasion ere reinforcements could come across the Fields of Chelam or the Sirmium Plains to aid them. Despite the large military presence, rumors hold that agents of the Molthune Armsrunners, a mercenary group operating out of Logas in Isger, keep rooms in Misarias where the organization is recruiting guards, customers, and scouts within Cheliax's borders.

Haugin's Ear.title: Haugin's Ear
Haugin's Ear.extract: The Isgeri town of Haugin's Ear is situated on the Keld River south of the Chitterwood, right on the border with Cheliax.

Occarin.title: Occarin
Occarin.extract: Occarin is a village in the Carpenden Plains region of Andoran whose inhabitants are unknowingly and secretly almost all changeling children of three green hags.✝︎

Sauerton.title: Sauerton
Sauerton.extract: Sauerton is a settlement in northeastern Andoran in the Carpenden Plains region, located between the banks of the Andoshen River and the Verduran Forest.

Logas.title: Logas
Logas.extract: Logas is the second city of the nation of Isger. It was once the capital until that honor passed to Elidir, and its nobility dream of restoring Logas to its rightful status as the country's first city.

Umok.title: Umok
Umok.extract: The gnome settlement of Umok can be found in central Isger on the northeastern edge of the Chitterwood. It has suffered an existence rife with conflict with local goblin tribes, and only the intervention of a visiting gnome paladin of Shelyn, Challo Fignoble, protected Umok through the final battles of the Goblinblood Wars.

Gillamoor.title: Gillamoor
Gillamoor.extract:
Gillamoor is a village in Isger on the edge of the Chitterwood. It managed to survive the destruction of the Goblinblood Wars but has now fallen to what is being called the Gillamoor Plague.

According to the usually fearless caravan guards of the Mercenary League, many of the villagers have been transformed into ravenous disease-carrying zombies. The village is under quarantine by order of the Isgeri army, and its leaders secretly hope that the steward's 5,000 gold piece reward for eliminating the undead plague will convince outsiders to cleanse the town for them. A few of the village's inhabitants are still believed to be alive, trying to last out the siege in heavily barricaded homes.

Taggoret.title: Taggoret
Taggoret.extract:
For another meaning of "Taggoret", please see Taggoret (city).

The Everlasting Kingdom of Taggoret was one of the five original dwarven kingdoms in Five Kings Mountains. It was established by Taggrick I, also known as Taggun I, in 1562 AR. Taggoret fell in 2526 AR during the invasion of orcs into the heart of the Five Kings Mountains, which began in 2492 AR.

Jernashall.title: Jernashall
Jernashall.extract: Jernashall was the preeminent city of the dwarven kingdom of Tar Khadurrm, and was located in the southern reaches of the Five Kings Range.

Davarn.title: Davarn
Davarn.extract: The town of Davarn in the southwestern Five Kings Mountains is one of the centers of dwarven culture in the region.

Kar-Azakh.title: Kar-Azakh
Kar-Azakh.extract:
Kar-Azakh is a town located in a cavern of the Five Kings Mountains that surrounds a thunderous 200-foot-tall waterfall called the Cascade. It is located in the southern Five Kings Range halfway between Taggoret and Kykar.✝︎

The falling water's roar and cavern's echoes drown out most attempts at verbal communication, and most residents wear hearing protection outside of their homes and use sign language to communicate. The waterfall also turns dozens of waterwheels that power its many mills.

Elara's Halfway House.title: Elara's Halfway House
Elara's Halfway House.extract: Elara's Halfway House was an orphanage in Darkmoon Vale located on a hill at the edge of Darkmoon Wood eight miles from Falcon's Hollow. It burned down under suspicious circumstances around 4707 AR✝︎ with everyone within already dead, leaving it a fire-blackened ruin.

Saringallow.title: Saringallow
Saringallow.extract: The Isgeri town of Saringallow is located on the Conerica River, south of the capital of Elidir.

Gildside.title: Gildside
Gildside.extract: Gildside is a vertical mining town in the southeastern Druman region of Kahlehlin consisting of six levels built into the side of the Rancor Canyon.

New Jelheg.title: New Jelheg
New Jelheg.extract: New Jelheg is a very small settlement in southern Druma.

Rhuvasi.title: Rhuvasi
Rhuvasi.extract: Rhuvasi was a dwarven settlement whose inhabitants were forced out by the dwarves of Taggoret after the Battle of Scattered Ash.

Kykar.title: Kykar
Kykar.extract: Kykar is a city in the Five Kings Mountains located beneath Mount Gustus in the southeastern Five Kings Range. It is the largest city lacking a seat on the Gathering Council, and its Lord Mayor Hansin Struckiron backs King Borogrim of Highhelm in the hopes that a centralized government extends more influence to cities like his.

Grimsburrow.title: Grimsburrow
Grimsburrow.extract: As of 4712 AR, the Regulate of Grimsburrow was a newly constructed independent dwarven settlement in western Druma, north of the end of the Five Kings Range. Its residents were master crafters from across dwarven kingdoms who had traveled to follow a so-called renaissance in engineering, architecture, and art led by Brond, the commune's leader.

Swiftrun.title: Swiftrun
Swiftrun.extract: Swiftrun is a village of superstitious people in Isger, located on the Conerica River about 50 miles✝︎ from Saringallow.

Macridi.title: Macridi
Macridi.extract:
The town of Macridi (pronounced ma-KRID-ee) is located in central Druma in the middle of the Palakar Forest, at the confluence of the Great Goldpan River and the Profit's Flow. It is primarily a logging town.

Although just as affluent as the other population centers of the Kalistrocracy, Macridi's residents do not feel the need to display their wealth as openly. This gives the wooded town a somewhat less opulent feel.

Elidir.title: Elidir
Elidir.extract: Elidir (pronounced EL-eh-deer) is both the capital of Isger and the nation's largest city. Far from the Chitterwood, Elidir escaped the worst of the Goblinblood Wars, a fact that still engenders bitterness from those whose homes were overrun. It is a commonly held belief throughout the rest of the country that if the capital had been in greater danger during the war, the fighting would have been over much faster.

Rolgrimmdur.title: Rolgrimmdur
Rolgrimmdur.extract: Rolgrimmdur is built among the ruins of Grakodan by Khadon's engineers and is one of the most fortified dwarven cities in Five Kings Mountains. It is located about 50 miles southwest of the capital city of Highhelm.

Caldamin.title: Caldamin
Caldamin.extract: Caldamin is a small, rural town in northernmost Andoran, nestled between the Five Kings Mountains to the west, the Verduran Forest to the east, and the Dragonfly River to the south. This otherwise unremarkable settlement has become somewhat of a tourist attraction of late, due to the presence of two extraordinary creatures who make their home nearby: a red reaver named Auger and a gray render called Shepherd Graygulp. These two foes occasionally battle each other, and their fights are of such ferocity that people come from far afield to witness the clashes.

Dustpawn.title: Dustpawn
Dustpawn.extract: Dustpawn is a small town in eastern Isger, about seven miles from the Conerica River and close to the border with Druma, and was founded in 4324 AR.

Finder's Gulch.title: Finder's Gulch
Finder's Gulch.extract: Finder's Gulch is a ruined and abandoned village in the Menador Mountains of western Isger that is home to a powerful priestess of Urgathoa named Illcayna Alonnor. She was transformed by her goddess into a monstrosity known as a daughter of Urgathoa and raids the countryside around Finder's Gulch—including neighboring Cheliax and Molthune—for victims to expand her growing army of wights.

Alabastrine.title: Alabastrine
Alabastrine.extract:
For another meaning of "Alabastrine", please see Alabastrine Peaks.

Alabastrine is a city in Druma built in 4607 AR to accommodate Arodenite refugees after the deity's death. However, the migration never happened in the numbers its investors expected, and its hasty construction ceased before it was completed. High Prophet Kelldor purchased the partially finished city in 4711 AR, and since then the Druman government has auctioned control of the city's districts in five-year spans to the highest bidders. The most recent auction on record occurred in 4716 AR.

Petitioner's Port.title: Petitioner's Port
Petitioner's Port.extract: Petitioner's Port is a town in southwestern Druma, in the region of Kahlehlin, near the border with Isger. It is well-connected to the rest of the country, serves as the southern gateway to Druma from Inner Sea nations, and has a reputation for initiating newcomers into the Prophecies of Kalistrade.

Court of Seasons.title: Court of Seasons
Court of Seasons.extract: The Court of Seasons is a court of nymphs that actively resists anyone coming into their domain without permission.

Tuskhead Stoneworking.title: Tuskhead Stoneworking
Tuskhead Stoneworking.extract: Tuskhead Stoneworking is a relatively new business located in the heart of the Isgeri town of Breachill on the banks of Breach Creek that specializes in the construction of roads and public structures made of stone. Its owner, Rorsk Axebane, enjoys good relations with the town council but has recently been poaching clients from his competitors at Posandi Bros., suspecting that they are not entirely honorable.

Highhelm.title: Highhelm
Highhelm.extract:
This article covers the dwarven city. For the Pathfinder Lost Omens sourcebook, see Highhelm (sourcebook).

Highhelm is among the most influential of the ancient and expansive dwarven settlements known as Sky Citadels. Constructed and founded in -4880 AR during the Age of Darkness✝︎ within and beneath lofty Emperor's Peak in the Five Kings Range, it has remained the center of dwarven culture on Avistan for millennia.

Bellis.title: Bellis
Bellis.extract: Bellis is a remote settlement in far northeastern Andoran along the banks of the Sellen River. Established as a lumber town, it now produces additional agricultural goods in addition to cut timber. Bellisians like to refer to themselves as "foresters" and regard "lumberjacks" as undisciplined amateurs.

Canorate.title: Canorate
Canorate.extract:
For another meaning of "Canorate", please see Canorate (province).

Canorate is the capital of the nation of Molthune. It is an impressive city which manages to combine architectural splendor with defensive fortifications. It is said that even the poor of Canorate live in luxury, and to a certain extent this is true—citizens allow a select number of necessary laborers to live in fine accommodation (but naturally kept separate from the rest of the population) in order to encourage them to settle in the city.

The city's most exclusive district is known as the Sweet Orchard. Non-residents are not allowed to enter this exclusive section without a valid work or travel permit.

The Imperial Castle dominates the center of the city. The ruler of Molthune, Imperial Governor Markwin Teldas, maintains an impressive war room within the castle. Pride of place is given to a large scale model of the Inner Sea region, on which the generals plot their next campaigns.

Kovlar.title: Kovlar
Kovlar.extract: Kovlar✝︎ is a small dwarven city in Five Kings Mountains built in the tunnels and caverns at the southern outskirts of the fallen great city of Saggorak.

Gulshire.title: Gulshire
Gulshire.extract: Gulshire is a small, exclusive hamlet in Rhuzam region of Druma that specializes in breeding, grooming, and handling prestigious animals.

Cedar Court.title: Cedar Court
Cedar Court.extract: Cedar Court is less of a settlement in Druma, and more a small group of the oldest dryads' trees in the region. This fey court is one of the most reclusive, and hides somewhere deep in the Palakar region.

Court of Endless Revelry.title: Court of Endless Revelry
Court of Endless Revelry.extract: The Court of Endless Revelry is the largest congregation of satyrs in the Palakar region, and is the closest thing to a satyr court.

Lyperro.title: Lyperro
Lyperro.extract: Lyperro is a small village in the Rhuzam region of Druma.

Ulm's Delve.title: Ulm's Delve
Ulm's Delve.extract: As of 4712 AR, Ulm's Delve was a small gold-mining settlement in Druma.

Ringsmith Ranch.title: Ringsmith Ranch
Ringsmith Ranch.extract: Ringsmith Ranch is one of the Five Kings Mountains towns near the Andoran border that benefits from the region's caravan trade. Primarily dedicated to raising pack mules for transportation, Ringsmith Ranch is experiencing increasingly frequent bandit attacks.

Cassomir.title: Cassomir
Cassomir.extract: Cassomir is located at the mouth of the Sellen River, and is the second largest settlement in the empire of Taldor. It acts as a major trade city, and through its ports enter goods from the Inner Sea, as well as from nations to the north that utilize the Sellen as a trade route. Cassomir is also home to the greatest of Taldor's imperial shipyards, even though the majority of the fleet itself is stationed in Oppara.

Prophet's Home.title: Prophet's Home
Prophet's Home.extract: The city of Prophet's Home is located in the northeast of the country of Druma. It is best known for housing the training facilities of the Mercenary League, the military protection and enforcement arm of the Prophets of Kalistrade.

Larrad.title: Larrad
Larrad.extract:
Larrad is a large dwarven city in the region of Five Kings Mountains. The city is built up around a cluster of temple-caverns carved out by the dwarves in ancient times during the first period after the completion of their Quest for Sky. The temple-caverns served as a place of worship for their gods and as a necropolis for their honored dead. Over the centuries, the temples grew into great complexes hosting many dwarven clerics and acolytes. At the same time, the support settlements around the temples expanded and overlapped.

Larrad's necropolis is used by only the renowned members of dwarven aristocracy. The Cathedral of Magrim still maintains the tombs of the Five Kings' oldest residents, and employs necromantic magic to commune with the spirits of the ancestors. The Cathedral also supports expeditions into the ruins of Saggorak which is under threat from undead and creatures from the Darklands.

After the death of Gorum in 4724 AR, the dwarven deity Grundinnar and an array of dwarven heroes battled the primordial being Otolmens over the fate of a warshard that crashed at the gates of Larrad. According to the Molthuni novel When Gods Make War, Otolmens claimed the warshard was a threat to the physics of reality and demanded that Larrad and all beings exposed to the warshard must be deleted from existence, which Grundinnar refused to allow. As Grundinnar battled Otolmens, dwarven heroes rushed to Grundinnar's aid, were slain, and were raised to serve Otolmens as celestials. However, both divine entities and nearly all of the dwarven combatants were suddenly removed from reality in a loud flash, as if neither had ever existed.

Hope's Hollow.title: Hope's Hollow
Hope's Hollow.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Tears at Bitter Manor.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The quiet agricultural town of Hope's Hollow is an hour's ride along the eastern road from the city of Cassomir in Taldor, through the Blackwood Swamp.

Matriculum's Charge.title: Matriculum's Charge
Matriculum's Charge.extract: Matriculum's Charge, formally known as Hillsprung, gets its name from the dragon that guards it, Matriculum.

Bolgrad.title: Bolgrad
Bolgrad.extract: Bolgrad is an underground mining city in the northeastern Five Kings Range that specializes in gemstones; the cavern city's gem-rich ceiling earned it the nickname "Star-Studded Bolgrad". Most of its majority dwarven population works either in the mining and processing of gems or as jewelers.

Varook's Deep.title: Varook's Deep
Varook's Deep.extract:
Varook's Deep is both the lowest dwarf-inhabited point of the Five Kings Mountains at 17,621 feet below sea level, a defensive city that keeps watch over the Darklands, and a trading post that hosts caligni, drathnelars, and underground fey. Its garrison is led by General Flinda Strikespark.

Due to the city's depth, few surface-dwelling dwarves visit Varook's Deep.

Hillhome.title: Hillhome
Hillhome.extract: Hillhome is the surface name of an independent and reclusive underground settlement of kovintus located beneath the northeastern foothills of the Five Kings Mountains and northwestern Verduran Forest. Its true name is known only to its kovintus.

Calladels.title: Calladels
Calladels.extract: Calladels is located near the Sellen River on the border with both Andoran and Galt. The wet, watery environment makes the village seem like a place primarily dedicated to fishing and rural life, but it serves as a base of operations for the Brazen Bravos, who use the river to conduct their smuggling operations throughout the region.

Gottacker.title: Gottacker
Gottacker.extract: Gottacker is a small community located on one of the tributaries of the Profit's Flow, inhabited by exiles from the Five Kings Mountains. The settlement is therefore a pleasant destination for those seeking to rewrite their past after making mistakes from which there is no return.

Dunholme.title: Dunholme
Dunholme.extract: Dunholme is a ruined settlement in Taldor's Verduran Forest. The site fell victim to a mysterious event known as "the Dunholme Doom" in which an as-yet-unknown threat emerged from a well, first claiming the lives of townsfolk Verniya Klamer, Maix Vivermer, and Jasmique Tunhold. A trade road still runs through Dunholme toward Belhaim and Wispil further north.

Nazilli.title: Nazilli
Nazilli.extract: The ruins of the town of Nazilli are located on the southern edges of the Verduran Forest in Taldor. The settlement was destroyed by the black dragon Aeteperax during a period of Taldan history known as the Dragon Plague.

Lotheedar.title: Lotheedar
Lotheedar.extract: The small town of Lotheedar is the capital of Meratt County in the Tandak Prefecture of Taldor. The settlement is officially administered by Count Bartelby Lotheed, who holds the position of tribune, although he has delegated most responsibilies to his seneschal, Sir Gul Gusairne.

Cascina.title: Cascina
Cascina.extract: The tiny hamlet of Cascina is a sleepy little settlement located in Meratt County in the Tandak Prefecture south of the Verduran Forest and east of Cassomir. Cascina consists of only a few extended families who once worked for the Voinum barony most of whom now live as simple farmers of goats, sheep, or wheat. The hamlet's most notable resident is Yarelle Towsine, a minor wizard who offers her spells to help the local community. As part of Meratt County, Cascina is subjected to the harsh rule of House Lotheed.

Pensaris.title: Pensaris
Pensaris.extract: Pensaris is a small village nestled on the bank of Lake Pensaris and one of the most prosperous settlements in Meratt county of Taldor, behind only the larger trade town of Lotheedar in terms of wealth. This prosperity is due to the wise stewardship of House Okerra under the leadership of Baron Nicolaus Okerra, who rules from the neighbouring Okerra Estate. Baron Okerra assists this prosperity by sometimes giving no-interest loans to the most enterprising people of Pensaris, but recently this practice has ceased due to the increased taxes levied by Count Lotheed, much to the annoyance of Baron Okerra.

Moost.title: Moost
Moost.extract:
The hamlet of Moost grew up around the nearby Crabbe Estate and attends to the family's cranberry bogs but has also flourished into a small, almost self-sufficient little community (Moost still somewhat relies on the financial support of House Crabbe). Moost is also famous for hosting the largest and nicest coaching house along the North Cassomir Highway.

Dame Parthena Crabbe used to run a very successful conservatory tutoring young musicians and dancers at the nearby Crabbe Estate that bolstered Moost's economy but the number of students has dwindled since Parthena's husband passed away. Moost is awash with gossip, the most common being around possible suitors for the Crabbe heiress, Sepsinia Crabbe.

Solscrene.title: Solscrene
Solscrene.extract: Solscrene is located on the stretch of the Sellen River within the Verduran Forest, and it faces Andoran on the river's opposite bank. As one travels north from Cassomir to the Isle of Arenway, Solscrene is considered to be the last village before one reaches the true deep forest. Solscrene sits on a small craggy island and its inhabitants are rather insular, constantly circulating foul stories about distant Galtans, neighbouring Andorens, the mysterious druids of Arenway and even gnomes.

Hangman's Harbor.title: Hangman's Harbor
Hangman's Harbor.extract: Hangman's Harbor is a village along the Sellen River in Taldor, north of Cassomir. The village is located between the Sellen and a large granite cliff, and it covers not only the riverfront but the top of the cliff, and the cliff itself. Some of its buildings jut out over the water, some are wedged onto narrow ledges, and some are carved directly into the cliff face. Because of the lack of flat land, the village is very built up, with individual buildings often being shared by multiple businesses. The mayor's house boasts an ominous noose that prominently hangs over the river. The town is home to a tavern called The Hanging Bottle.

New Towne.title: New Towne
New Towne.extract:
New Towne is the most rundown settlement in the Meratt County region of Taldor. It is made up of the former inhabitants of Jambis, who fled the now-ruined settlement and live in squalid conditions, with many residents inhabiting little more than tents. The residents are still technically under the protection of House Telus, but the last baron, Araig Telus, has been absent for at least a decade.

After fleeing Jambis, many of the residents settled in an area that looked promising for farming. The soil was poor, however, and New Towne has struggled to support itself since being founded about a decade prior to 4718 AR.

As of that year, the settlement was led by Tribune Caphridius Vort, who is considered to be a good man but more interested in opera and finery than rulership and far out of his depth. An aiuvarin upstart named Felsha has been advocating on behalf of New Towne to anyone who will listen.

Whiterush.title: Whiterush
Whiterush.extract: Whiterush is a small, isolated village located in the Verduran Forest region of Andoran, along a patch of rapids on the Sellen River. Its inhabitants are known for their friendly, chatty nature, and the town itself boasts cheery-looking houses covered in thatched roofing. The inhabitants of Whiterush are renowned for their distrust of the residents of nearby Rippleden, claiming that Whiterush and its residents are obviously the superior of the two rival villages.

Wispil.title: Wispil
Wispil.extract: Located in the depths of the Verduran Forest, Wispil is the gnomish city responsible for most of Taldor's supply of timber and much of its wood craft. Wispil provides the timber that fuels the bustling Imperial Naval Shipyards of Cassomir, which in turn grant the Taldan Imperial Navy its power.

Tregan.title: Tregan
Tregan.extract:
Tregan is a small town on the border of Taldor and Galt,✝︎ and is renowned as a refuge for Galtan refugees of the Red Revolution, who make up a fair percentage of the population. The Reconciliation Council is working to reunite expatriates with their loved ones now that the revolution as finally ended and the town is experiencing a boost in population that is finding hard to accommodate.

Among the town's businesses is the Hornet's Nest tavern, owned by the bard Vallyn.

Adrast.title: Adrast
Adrast.extract: The Barony of Adrast is a small barony and settlement in the north-west of Taldor, located north of the Verduran Forest and south of the westernmost mountains of the Fog Peaks in the Northern Tandak Prefecture. The Baron of Adrast is Baron Stelan who is married to Baroness Lenelle. The headsman of Adrast settlement, a small village, is Odric, and the captain of the guard is the dromaar Captain Drelm. Following the baron's professed faith, the predominant religion is that of Abadar, and the barony has a cleric and temple to that god.

Belhaim.title: Belhaim
Belhaim.extract: Belhaim is a small town located on a tributary of the Verduran Fork in the nation of Taldor's Verduran Forest.

Torcova.title: Torcova
Torcova.extract: Torcova is a small town in northwestern Taldor in the southwestern foothills of the Fog Peaks in Northern Tandak Prefecture. Outside of the town, lies the manor home of a local noble, Lord Sauton Beane, who hides a terrible secret. He is secretly an ogrekin, albeit a handsome one, who has gone through seven wives in the hopes of begetting a human offspring. He has not succeeded so far, and has locked his monstrous progeny in a cave beneath his home after murdering their mothers. He only releases his ogrekin children to punish his enemies or kidnap his next wife.

Blackwood Moot.title: Blackwood Moot
Blackwood Moot.extract:
Blackwood Moot is an isolated stand of Blackwood trees deep in the Verduran Forest of Taldor that serves as a meeting place for wild residents dissatisfied with the Treaty of the Wildwood. Those who meet at Blackwood Moot view the members of the Wildwood Lodge as traitors who have enabled Taldor's destruction of the Verduran Forest.

The Blackwood Moot is led by the arboreal regent Oakadence, a powerful sorcerer who has been organising secretive raids on Wispil and the Isle of Arenway using the wild beasts of the forest as disposable foot soldiers.

Clear Waters.title: Clear Waters
Clear Waters.extract: The city of Clear Waters floats atop Lake Clear Waters in the centre of the Forest of the Sacred Eye and draws visitors from across Xopatl and beyond. Clear Waters is renowned for its beauty and anyone wishing to travel through the city must use one of the city's small boats, most of which are decorated with a special flower that blooms every new moon. Clear Waters draws in the most visitors during the full moon, however, as during the height of the full moon Lake Clear Waters begins to glow with moonlight.

Grand Pass.title: Grand Pass
Grand Pass.extract: The small city of Grand Pass is located on the cusp of the Mildanesi Mountains, next to the pass that links Xopatl with the neighbouring nation of Innazpa. During the height of the Razatlan Empire, Grand Pass was little more than a watch post but, after the empire's fall, the soldiers who once manned the border-post established the city of Grand Pass. Nowadays, Grand Pass is mostly known for being home to the most renowned fighting school in Xopatl. This school holds a biannual tournament that concludes with a spectacular battle atop the nearby Panischo Mountain, the tournament is so well known that it draws combatants from across the whole of Arcadia.

Yan Tlomíl.title: Yan Tlomíl
Yan Tlomíl.extract: Yan Tlomíl is the largest agricultural centre in Xopatl since the fall of Chídanza. The land surrounding it yields an incredible bumper crop each year, but no one knows which crop will benefit, so each farmer plants multiple crops each year and waits to see which will flourish. When one of the plants starts thriving beyond all the others, it sparks a mad rush of farmers scrabbling to secure more seeds and land to grow more of this bumper crop. Due to this element of chance and the chaotic competition to capitalise on the flourishing crop, Yan Tlomíl's agricultural influence has yet to equal Chídanza's.

Zilpatzi.title: Zilpatzi
Zilpatzi.extract: Zilpatzi is a city in Xopatl that has suffered greatly since the nearby Manzinago Forest became too dangerous to log. Before the Deepweave came to the Manzinago Forest, much of its wealth came from boat caravans that would float down the rivers of Xopatl accompanying the floating cut wood. Since they can no longer safely make lumber, the focus of these floating caravans has now pivoted to focus entirely on trade and shipping goods up and down the length of the rivers. These voyages can sometimes venture further afield across all of Arcadia and can last months at a time. Within the city, local legend claims that some kind of smoke dragon lives beneath the local church of Cihua Coatl.

Detmer.title: Detmer
Detmer.extract: The main shipyard that powers the naval arm of Druma's mercantile empire, the city of Detmer is located on a rocky outcrop that is thrust out into the Gulf of Accord on Lake Encarthan. It is from here that the barges and caravels that cross the lake and ensure Druma's wealth are launched. The city is famous for its Sapphire Harbor which, thanks to Druma's incredible wealth, is a more literal description than most think. Very little trade (at least by Drumish standards) takes place in Detmer, as it is primarily focused on shipbuilding and maintenance. The Drumish Navy is headquartered here as well, providing security in the Gulf of Accord and wherever else Drumish merchant ships travel in the pursuit of trade.

Eranmas.title: Eranmas
Eranmas.extract: The walled city of Eranmas is on the shores of Lake Encarthan. It is the second largest city in Molthune, and home of the country's navy. This navy is the largest fleet on the lake, and its main role is to blockade Tamran, capital of the "rebel" nation of Nirmathas. The city is governed by Lord Resket.

Kraggodan.title: Kraggodan
Kraggodan.extract:
This article covers the dwarven settlement in the Mindspin Mountains. For the dwarven leader of the same name, see Kraggodan (dwarf).

The dwarven city-state of Kraggodan can be found in the southern Mindspin Mountains within the borders of the nation of Nirmathas on its western border with the nation of Oprak, just north of the ruined village of Graybanks

Korholm.title: Korholm
Korholm.extract: Korholm is the gateway to Molthune, the seat of the so-called First General Lord, and the territorial seat of the Shrikewood province. Located at the mouth of the Nosam River, Korholm is also Molthune's largest port on Lake Encarthan. Soldiers and officers bound for the war against Nirmathas drill here. The city's entrance by land is the great Green Gate, which leads to a wide avenue that runs to the Hall of Governance at the city's center. Korholm is home to Molthune's most elite soldiers and its western third, the Arsenal District, houses those soldiers.

Gujaraldi.title: Gujaraldi
Gujaraldi.extract: Gujaraldi, the capital of Xopatl, is located in the central part of the country.

Jolizpan.title: Jolizpan
Jolizpan.extract: Jolizpan is a city located in the extreme northwest of Xopatl, near Tumbaja Mountain, the nexus of Xopatl's Veins of Creation.

Garizés City.title: Garizés City
Garizés City.extract: Sitting in the north-western reaches of the Arcadian nation of Xopatl, Garizés City is renowned for the prominent musical style that is named after it. This musical style is a lively one typified by an inclusion of common instruments, like flutes, guitars and trumpets, alongside the more unusual garizan, a musical instrument made of entwined plant matter that is squeezed and stretched to make different sounds. Garizés City is the primary place that garizans are manufactured and those that are not made in Garizés City are normally viewed as being of inherently inferior quality.

Talípas.title: Talípas
Talípas.extract: Talípas is a city in the Arcadian nation of Xopatl that is renowned for brewing and distillation. Once Talípas was little more than just another sleepy fishing village sat along Xopatl's western coastline but, in the last few centuries, it has instead embraced distillation and grown in size enormously. Various lake plants found on Lake Tapipallati, which surrounds the island Talípas is built on, can be crushed into a paste that is then fermented for several weeks. The drink created from this fermentation, also called "talípas", is highly intoxicating. The exact process of creating the drink talípas is such a closely guarded secret that the brewers and distillers of the city are willing to kill to ensure it remains secret.

Yanti Iñulde.title: Yanti Iñulde
Yanti Iñulde.extract: Yanti Iñulde is a city within Xopatl that is renowned as the nation's religious centre. Yanti Iñulde was the home town of the last hero-god to be born in Xopatl back in the nation's ancient history. From this Yanti Iñulde rose as a centre for religious worship of the hero-gods and other deities beyond that, becoming a city riddled with temples and shrines, some so old that the deities they were built to honour have been completely forgotten. The city holds a week-long festival to celebrate all the deities worshipped within the city, including those whose memory has been all but lost to time.

Barbarous Arena.title: Barbarous Arena
Barbarous Arena.extract: The Barbarous Arena is located in the underground of the Pilgrimage District, hosting fights every week between recruits from the city and creatures brought in from abroad. Although no one knows the owner's identity, the consistency of the fights and their ability to operate outside the Mercenary League's watchful eye suggest they are someone powerful indeed. One reason why little is known about the arena is that most of its workers suffer from induced amnesia and have no memory of working that day.

Skelt.title: Skelt
Skelt.extract: A place that is nigh impregnable to its enemies, the city of Skelt is of strategic importance to the whole of Nirmathas, being situated along the Shining River, which flows into the Tourondel River in northern Nirmathas.

Tamran.title: Tamran
Tamran.extract: The city of Tamran, capital of Nirmathas, is a ramshackle city, especially for a nation's capital. It is built on piers and pilings over the marshlands at the mouth of the Marideth River,✝︎ where it meets the banks of Lake Encarthan. The city is connected by a web of bridges, ramps, and causeways, and a flotilla of barges and skiffs, and all of the buildings are made from wood.
