The town is perhaps the most scenic and affluent regions in the county, largely due to the miles of vineyards and flower farms surrounding the settlement. Each year, chefs, vintners, perfumers, jewelers, and artists of all walks compete in the annual "Last Breath" festival for the highly coveted mantle "Worth Dying For". The competition is mired in regular tragedies and scandals including the use of human ingredients, assassinations, and mysterious deaths. Modern participants are unperturbed by this disasters, giving the competition a dangerous reputation and a morbid theme which has served to heighten its infamy.

Vauntil was the location where troops of the Shining Crusade established a beachhead in their war against Tar-Baphon in 3801 AR.

Hallein Town.title: Hallein Town
Hallein Town.extract: Hallein Town is a small Lastwall settlement located on the Path River downstream of Vigil. Situated at the foot of a high tarn rising from the shores of Lake Hallensee, access to it by land is almost impossible. Hallein Town is the home of the workers for the salt mines in the Virlych hills of nearby Ustalav, and provides vital trade goods for the rest of the nation.

Grayce.title: Grayce
Grayce.extract:
Grayce is an Ustalavic settlement in the south-west of the county of Caliphas with the border of Lastwall, the Path River, a mere stone's throw away. This proximity means the town is a bridge to Lastwall with the Chapel Bridge, a drawbridge that can span the Path, literally fulfilling this role. From the southern end of the bridge, roads lead to Vigil, north-west of Grayce, and to Vellumis to the south.

The townsfolk are predominantly farmers, shepherds, and weavers.

Saad.title: Saad
Saad.extract:
Saad is a small town in the River Kingdoms region controlled by Daggermark, and home to Daggermark's cavalry.

The hamelt's two dozen large timber buildings are protected by a motte-and-bailey wall, with several tall timber towers that overlook the surrounding plains. Outside of the wall are many well-guarded horse farms. All of these war-horses are the interbred stock of Yenchabur stallions brought to the area in 4402 AR.

Saad is also the main trading center for the merchant traffic coming from Tymon, and is often the target of bandits who plague all trade routes across the region.

Lockridge.title: Lockridge
Lockridge.extract:
Lockridge is one of the three large towns in the River Kingdom of Lambreth. It was founded by Angelidis family in 4394 AR. Lockridge overlooks the rapids formed where the Sellen River emerges from deep canyons cut through the Juviler Hills. The rapids were a serious obstacle for merchants travelling this part of the river, requiring skilled pilots and small crafts. The Angelidis family constructed a number of canal locks that bypassed the cataract and demolished the shanty town of Troxell, replacing it with stone wharves and warehouses along the canals. While the family carefully respects the freedom of the river itself, many merchants pay to take advantage of the locks, especially those hauling quarried Juviler granite and polished agate.

Morissa Angelidis is the elected mayor of Lockridge, travelling to Maashinelle only upon matters of importance.

Goldenflame.title: Goldenflame
Goldenflame.extract: Goldenflame is an abandoned college town in southeastern Lastwall, immediately north of the Iomedaean Goldenflame College.

Scrawny Crossing.title: Scrawny Crossing
Scrawny Crossing.extract:
The abandoned hamlet of Scrawny Crossing is in the western✝︎ River Kingdoms. It served as a ferry crossing and caravan stop until 4706 AR, when its inhabitants disappeared in mysterious circumstances.

Unbeknownst to the folk of the region, Scrawny Crossing now serves as a base for a band of aquatic slavers—scrag/ulat-kini cross-breeds. These monsters capture humanoids for use in strange experiments. Their leaders are a naga ("Lord Slissk"), a scrag sorcerer ("Thult-Lorth") and a sea hag ("Mother Slug"), although they in turn take their orders from a telepathic creature that might be an alghollthu master.

Stranger still, next to the ruins of Scrawny Crossing's old smith sits an oddity known as the Boiling Well, a well whose water always came out at body temperature. Unbeknownst to anyone living there, this was because the well harboured a secret soulgate that linked to the Infernal Duke Eaqueo's domain of the Finishing Knot on Hell's first layer, Avernus.

Hyannis.title: Hyannis
Hyannis.extract: Hyannis is an Ustalavic coal-mining settlement in the county of Versex; the forlorn shacks of the villagers are set on the edge of the Hungry Mountains near to the source of the Danver River. The village and the mines are still haunted by the greenish spectre of the witch called Black Aggie, whom the villagers rose up against nearly forty years ago; they ripped the monster to pieces then burnt her before casting her remains into an abandoned mine. Worse than the hauntings, though, are the miners who become possessed by what is considered Black Aggie's suffocating breath.

Redleaf.title: Redleaf
Redleaf.extract: The settlement of Redleaf is located in the western portion of the Ustalavic county of Varno, circling the small Lake Redleaf.

Thrushmoor.title: Thrushmoor
Thrushmoor.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: plot elements of the Strange Aeons Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The settlement of Thrushmoor can be found in the Ustalavic county of Versex. A fishing community on Lake Encarthan's Avalon Bay, the town is known to harbor a significant number of followers of the unsavory Old Cults, and the equally unsavoury Night Heralds.

Rozenport.title: Rozenport
Rozenport.extract: Rozenport is a bustling town located in the south-west of the county of Versex in Ustalav perched high on the steep cliffs above the Danver River. The town is most famous as the seat of the Sincomakti School of Sciences founded in 4570 AR. Many of the buildings of the town mirror the archaic architectural style of the world-renowned school, for instance, pointed steeples and manor houses topped with flat roofs. The town hall boasts a dome shaped like a bubble. All this is set within a veritable maze of roads and thoroughfares.

Chateau Douleurs.title: Chateau Douleurs
Chateau Douleurs.extract:
Chateau Douleurs is home to the Caliphvaso family and is located in the north of the Ustalavic county of Caliphas.

Located on the eastern shore of Lake Raiteso, Chateau Douleurs boasts a paradise of labyrinthine gardens, statue-guarded pavilions, strange menageries, and luxurious retreats. The palace also contains the accumulated art, treasures, and decadences of the Caliphvaso clan. Yet few ever look upon the estate grounds or are permitted into the palace's interior, as a legion of deadly family guards defend the grounds with lethal efficiency. Such extreme measures to ensure privacy has spawned countless rumors of orgies, murderous hunts and various sordid family secrets.

The halls of the chateau are haunted are haunted by the ghost of Ralagen Caliphvaso.

Corvischior.title: Corvischior
Corvischior.extract: Corvischior, sometimes spelt Corvischoir, is located in the south of the Ustalavic county of Varno. The town is now a shadow of its former self, the derelict county capital, and sits across the water from the equally forlorn Castle Corvischior, once the residence of the noble Tiriac family. Though still technically subject to the absentee Lord Ristomaur Tiriac, it has sat fallow for decades, though rumours speak of strange lights and unnerving shadows up at the castle. The most pessimistic residents whisper that Castle Corvischior, once known as Korsinoria Palace, is cursed, and whatever fell hex hangs over the castle has now settled over Corvischior as well.

Eran's Rest.title: Eran's Rest
Eran's Rest.extract:
Eran's Rest is a remote but picturesque settlement within the Hungry Mountains, perched on the eastern side of the Ghorcha Pass among the Hundred Haunted Vales and overlooking the Adghain Valley. It is located in the Ustalavic county of Amaans, and a steep goat path provides access to the village.

The village is dominated by herders and weavers but is also associated with fairy tales and other stories of badly-behaved fey. The villagers portray these tales in their art and architecture as well as their stories. Unfortunately, there may be more to the tales than mere story as the village suffers regular disappearances and other strange events.

Sezgin.title: Sezgin
Sezgin.extract: Sezgin is a small town in the River Kingdom of Lambreth.

Satravah.title: Satravah
Satravah.extract: Satravah, an Ustalavic village deep in the heart of Ulcazar, is better known as the Vale of Honorless Graves—site of countless secret burials in the Shining Crusade against Tar-Baphon.

Castle Urion.title: Castle Urion
Castle Urion.extract: Castle Urion is the first town most crusaders heading to Mendev up the West Sellen River see when the enter Numeria, but it is far from the typical barbarian settlements that are strewn across the country. This settlement is technically under the control of the Black Sovereign, but it is in reality controlled by an order of knights dedicated to Iomedae. These knights built Castle Urion as a way to protect and guide those crusaders sailing up the River Sellen (or "River Road" as it is often called) from Cassomir in Taldor to Chesed. This order of Iomedaean knights is particularly special, as they ride trained griffons, which help them patrol vast stretches of the river, certainly more than they could if they patrolled only on horseback. The current commander of Castle Urion is Caroliss Minerran.

Mosswater.title: Mosswater
Mosswater.extract:
The River Kingdoms settlement of Mosswater was established as a quiet fishing retreat by an exiled Ustalavic merchant named Artoor Geera. Geera's apothecary discovered a valuable process for making luminous dyes from the unusual glowing plant life of the nearby Glow Water Lake.

In 4664 AR a tribe of merrow moved into the area and slaughtered the inhabitants of the village. The aquatic ogres have driven off all subsequent attempts to re-occupy the site and take advantage of the lucrative dye-making process.

Cesca.title: Cesca
Cesca.extract: Cesca is a settlement in eastern Ustalav, located in the county of Varno.

Marian Leigh.title: Marian Leigh
Marian Leigh.extract: Marian Leigh is a settlement in the southern portion of Ustalav's Canterwall county, close to Virlych's dangerous Hungry Mountains and on the northern banks of the Orphield River. Despite the proximity to such inherent evil, the town is one of the most pleasant in Ustalav and boasts a female to male ratio of ten to one. The town is centred on the garden estate of Lady Urora Demerrval and incorporates many greenhouses and gardens. The only suspicion overhanging the town is the disturbing and unexplained disappearances of some of the male visitors to the community.

Sturnidae.title: Sturnidae
Sturnidae.extract: The town of Sturnidae is located in the south of the Ustalavic county of Barstoi, very close to the blasted wasteland known as the Furrows. Its proximity to the Furrows is never forgotten as, when the wind blows aright, a pall of grey ash from the wasteland is blown over the town; it lands on the fields tainting the crops and the food with decay. Furthermore, locals report seeing visions of the war in the clouds of dust, such as marching peasant soldiers and charging knights.

Ruwido.title: Ruwido
Ruwido.extract: Ruwido is a hovel-village secreted away in the notorious Ustalavic county of Virlych where misbegotten, cast-out, mad, and deformed souls can find a home, hide their faces from the world, and find a modicum of peace and acceptance. The village is located in the western hills of the Ghorcha Pass close to the waters of the Barrow Run. The settlement is shunned by practically everyone, including patrols from Lastwall and Amaans, while the villagers themselves are suspicious of any strangers approaching. One of the few regular and trusted visitors to Ruwido is Azra, a mute cleric of Desna.

Feldgrau.title: Feldgrau
Feldgrau.extract: Feldgrau was a village in the Furcina area of the county of Ardeal in Ustalav located close to the source of the Millrun River. As of 4711 AR, it was a silent ruin in the middle of the blasted wasteland called The Furrows. During the War Without Rivals, the people of Feldgrau were slaughtered and their bodies dumped into a mass grave. However, the memories of the dead still linger in and haunt the place.

Wait's Span.title: Wait's Span
Wait's Span.extract:
Wait's Span is the name of a tiny hamlet next to a wooden bridge of the same name that sits near the southeastern shore of Lake Kavapesta in the Ustalavic province of Ulcazar. It is located only six miles east of the Monastery of the Veil, and was the site of a massive fire that destroyed most of the village. Although Bishop Senir of the Monastery claims this was a natural event, most locals believe otherwise.

The Wait's Span has since also been referred to as the Senir Bridge and it is noted as being quite a large bridge. Here ancient stones mark the border between the mountain county of Ulcazar and the vales of Amaans. Gargoyles, their faces worn by time, decorate the sides of the high arches of the bridge. It has not been well maintained and it has large crumbling gaps along its length at various places.

Fort Riverwatch.title: Fort Riverwatch
Fort Riverwatch.extract:
Fort Riverwatch is a small town located ten miles northwest of the village of Thornkeep, on the banks of the West Sellen River. It is the last settlement of the River Kingdoms before one crosses into Numeria or Ustalav.

The town serves the barge traffic along the West Sellen. After Mosswater's destruction, Fort Riverwatch is the only port along the river between Lockridge, more than 100 miles to the south, and the muddy fishing town of Dravod Knock, 250 miles to the north. The knights of Iomedae established a garrison at Riverwatch in 4623 AR, and assumed the task of protecting the area. The knights govern the settlement and keep the river and roads safe for travel although they don't have the strength to clean out the lawless village of Thornkeep.

Thornkeep.title: Thornkeep
Thornkeep.extract:
This article covers the River Kingdoms town of Thornkeep. For other uses of the term, see Thornkeep (disambiguation).

The small town of Thornkeep is located within the Echo Wood of the northwestern River Kingdoms, about 10 miles east of the West Sellen River and 100 miles northwest of Daggermark at the junction of the Mosswater, Dagger, and Forest roads. Its reputation as a rowdy and dangerous settlement draws unscrupulous adventurers, bandits, and entrepreneurs for many types of illicit trade. The large number of nearby ruins and monsters attracts many adventurers looking to start making a name for themselves. This includes those interested in the nearby Emerald Spire.

Thornkeep has a wizard's guild called the Goldenfire Order.

Brunderton.title: Brunderton
Brunderton.extract: The mining town of Brunderton in eastern Rostland in Brevoy is home to a relatively large number of dwarves for the region. Most dwarves throughout southern Brevoy and the northern River Kingdoms have relatives from here, and gem and ore traders from Brunderton can often be found peddling their wares along the Sellen's many tributaries.

Silverhall.title: Silverhall
Silverhall.extract: Silverhall is a city that is located on the Awzera River in Brevoy, just before the river empties into Lake Reykal. This town is the family seat of House Lebeda, and it was here that the Lebeda family kept the Horn of Aroden for many years.

Torch.title: Torch
Torch.extract:
For another meaning of "Torch", please see Grandmaster Torch.

Torch is an unusual town in central Numeria that sits in a crescent around a bizarre, black hillock of shiny black stone known as Torch Hill. Atop the hill is the town's namesake, a brilliant purple bonfire that occasionally explodes into a devastating miles-high pillar of flame.

Citadel Altaerein.title: Citadel Altaerein
Citadel Altaerein.extract:
This article covers the former Hellknight citadel. For the Age of Ashes Adventure Path issue, see Hellknight Hill (adventure).

Citadel Altaerein is the abandoned former headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Nail, located in eastern Isger, high in the northwestern Five Kings Mountains, close to the border with Druma. It overlooks the town of Breachill, whose residents call it Hellknight Hill and largely ignore it.

Mormouth.title: Mormouth
Mormouth.extract: Mormouth is a small village on the Sellen River in the River Kingdom of Pitax.

Breachill.title: Breachill
Breachill.extract: Breachill is a small town in eastern Isger, in the foothills of the Five Kings Mountains.

Morta's Mortuary.title: Morta's Mortuary
Morta's Mortuary.extract: Morta's Mortuary is the main funeral home in the Isgeri town of Breachill. The building is painted black and black curtains are draped in every window. Numerous paintings and decorations of psychopomps fill the building, as its owner, Morta Valaskin, has an affinity for such outsiders.

Pickled Ear.title: Pickled Ear
Pickled Ear.extract:
The Pickled Ear is a bar in the north of the Isgeri town of Breachill frequented by both locals and adventurers, which often leads to fights between the two groups after a few beers. The bartender, Roxie Denn, dislikes these fights, as they drive away new customers from the bar, so she looks for new ways to promote her tavern.

A trapdoor in the bar's basement connects to Citadel Altaerein through a tunnel magically created many years ago, and few in the bar know of its existence.

Reliant Book Company.title: Reliant Book Company
Reliant Book Company.extract: Reliant Book Company was the name of a bookstore in Breachill that, as of 4719 AR, sold antique magic tomes and adventurers' journals from decades past. The owner was Voz Lirayne, and her apprentice, Calmont Trenault, performed more ordinary tasks. The bookstore was quite small, and its specialized collection meant it received few customers.

Wizard's Grace.title: Wizard's Grace
Wizard's Grace.extract: Wizard's Grace is an inn and tavern near Breachill Town Hall, in the south-east of the Isgeri town, making it a popular stop for both adventurers and locals. Its friendly atmosphere is largely due to the efforts of its innkeeper, Trinil Uskwold, the granddaughter of a local adventurer. Being so close to the town center, the clientele is generally well-mannered, and adventurers and council members often chat over meals. It is also a common rendezvous spot for those seeking employment through the council's monthly Call for Heroes, when Trinil serves her traditional boar-and-lentil stew.

Highdelve.title: Highdelve
Highdelve.extract: Highdelve is a settlement built by House Garess in the Golushkin Mountains in Western Brevoy.

Stoneclimb.title: Stoneclimb
Stoneclimb.extract: Stoneclimb is a fortress along the eastern side of the Icerime Peaks in Brevoy. It is south of Skywatch and it is in the north of lands controlled by House Medvyed and from where that House lives and rules.

Eagle's Watch.title: Eagle's Watch
Eagle's Watch.extract: The town of Eagle's Watch is located near the summit of Mount Veshka in Brevoy. It is the seat of House Orlovsky, one of the noble families of Brevoy and an ally of the vanished House Rogarvia.

Golushkin.title: Golushkin
Golushkin.extract: Golushkin is a dwarven settlement located in the Golushkin Mountains. The Golka clan leads the settlement. These dwarves had a trade relationship with House Garess of Brevoy, but all contact has been lost from roughly the same time as the Vanishing of Brevoy's House Rogarvia.

Mishkar.title: Mishkar
Mishkar.extract:
Unusually for a human community in Iobaria, Mishkar is not built from the scavenged remains of cyclopean ruins. Originally established in 809 AR as a guard post against threats from the Grenalf Plateau, it was abandoned by Iobaria around 4607 AR, only to be re-established by Varisian and Taldan settlers around 4659 AR.

The town has since become a largely self-sufficient city-state with a large agricultural and trade base. The people of Mishkar greatly value their independence and have remained unaffiliated with Iobaria's various factions, though they have forged an occasional alliance with centaur tribes against goblin hordes from the highlands.

In 4719 AR, the town was damaged by a massive forest fire in the nearby Finadar Forest, but had largely recovered by 4720 AR.

Kirya.title: Kirya
Kirya.extract:
The temple city of Kirya is located in the peaks above the Syrzemyan Highlands of Iobaria, and is accessible only by flight or via the cyclopean Koloran road that runs across the Grenalthrost and the Grenalf Plateau. An ancient cyclops temple city surrounding a clear mountain lake called Orost-Kar, the modern city of Kirya was founded by the Ulfen explorer Grenalf in 846 AR who named the city after his wife. Priests of various Iobarian faiths soon reconsecrated the temples to their own gods, and the city became a centre for pilgrimages from across Iobaria.

Kirya eventually became one of Iobaria's largest cities, with new building materials brought in from the Orostgard forest, until the logging awoke something, leading to the depopulation of Kirya by 1574 AR. Since then, various powers have taken control of Kirya, including a group of cyclopes and hill giants who built the Temples of Blood and Bone out of human bodies. The cyclopes and giants were cleared out by one of Iobaria's many plagues, and succeeded by hobgoblins, who ruled until the rise of New Iobaria in 3212 AR when humans reclaimed Kirya.

Under the leadership of grand druid Fedor Vasylk, the temple city is maintaining its independence in the conflicts between Orlov, Kridorn, and Mirnbay. Its inhabitants embrace 36 local nature gods who are seen as Erastil's children.

Dragons who were once allies of Iobaria's noble houses are known to inhabit the ruins beneath Kirya.

Graymoor.title: Graymoor
Graymoor.extract: Graymoor is a relatively peaceful town in Sovereign's Reach, a region of Numeria known more for its history of war and technological horrors. Graymoor is one of the few places on Golarion where androids are welcomed and can live alongside the locals peaceably.

Chitterhome.title: Chitterhome
Chitterhome.extract:
Chitterhome is the largest ratfolk settlement in Numeria and serves as a de facto capital to the ysoki ethnicity of Numerian Tinkerers. It is located in the Sellen Hills region, about 50 miles east of Scrapwall. The settlement itself is a square mile in size and consists of natural caverns that have been further excavated.

Chitterhome contains three, and possibly more, carefully guarded entrances to Nar-Voth.

Outsiders are not allowed in Chitterhome, but its residents trade throughout eastern Numeria, particularly Chesed, Hajoth Hakados, and along the Sellen River.

Sunder Horn.title: Sunder Horn
Sunder Horn.extract: Sunder Horn is a small town in the Sellen Hills of eastern Numeria that is home to the Kellid Sunder Horns tribe.

Zmeyka.title: Zmeyka
Zmeyka.extract: Zmeyka is a town located in western Brevoy, southeast of the Golushkin Mountains. In 4717 AR, it became a flashpoint in the ongoing conflict between House Orlovsky and House Surtova over rulership of Brevoy.

Heibarr.title: Heibarr
Heibarr.extract: The ruins of Heibarr are located on a branch of the Sellen River in the River Kingdoms, downstream of Mormouth in Pitax and upstream of Outsea.

Fort Drelev.title: Fort Drelev
Fort Drelev.extract: Fort Drelev is located on the north-western banks of Lake Hooktongue in the Stolen Lands region of the River Kingdoms and is ruled by Baron Hannis Drelev, originally under a charter from Brevoy.

Myrnorosk.title: Myrnorosk
Myrnorosk.extract: One of the youngest towns in Iobaria, Myrnorosk was founded in 4661 AR by dwarven and halfling settlers around a series of artesian wells on the eastern edges of the Coreth Woods. It is an open secret in Myrnorosk that kodlak and kodlok beings are held in high regard, and its people revere Erastil alongside other lesser deities with a touch of animal to them. Even lycanthropes are accepted so long as they can control their changes, while those who openly hunt lycanthropes will find few friends and allies in Myrnorosk.

Orost.title: Orost
Orost.extract:
Orost is a trade town of increasing importance in Iobaria. Founded in 780 AR, Orost is the only safe stop on the roads between Mirnbay and Orlov, and thus a site of great importance to the various factions vying for control of Iobaria. Envoys and diplomats from Mirnbay, Orlov, Kridorn, and Veka frequent the town alongside those seeking goods, and local law enforcement is forced to protect local citizens from the intrigue and battles these visitors bring.

At the centre of Orost stands a massive wood and stone building that houses the current komar. The temple is a human construct built over a massive, ancient stone temple. The mosaics on the floor of this temple feature stars, and while the town and its fortifications have taken on a rough star shape over the years, it is from these mosaics that Orost gets its name.

Evaren.title: Evaren
Evaren.extract: The town of Evaren is a centre for trade between Iobaria's human and non-human populations. Located on the edge of the Syrzemyan Highlands, the town is still recovering from losing much of its population over the course of the past two plagues, though it does attract those tired of living solitary lives in the wilderness. The town is ruled by the Council of Three, the most notable being Councilor Pavla Trynar, regarded as the most powerful magic user in all of Iobaria. Trynar's many apprentices help to protect the town and keep the peace.

Voluse.title: Voluse
Voluse.extract: Voluse is a small town in the River Kingdom of Touvette, located on the confluence of the Yhalt and Pitax Rivers. It is the site of one of Touvette's three charity buildings, along with the capital of Avendale and the town of Seredain.

Seredain.title: Seredain
Seredain.extract: Seredain is a large town in the River Kingdom of Touvette. It is the site of one of Touvette's three charity buildings, along with the capital of Avendale and the town of Voluse.

Sarain.title: Sarain
Sarain.extract: Sarain is a small village in the River Kingdom of Pitax, close to the border with Mivon. Sarain is famous for its vineyards and, before the arrival of Castruccio Irovetti, the Liacenza family had overall control over most of the wine-making business of the area.

Varnhold.title: Varnhold
Varnhold.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Varnhold Vanishing.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Varnhold is a seemingly abandoned settlement located southwest of Restov, near the northern Tors of Levenies in the Nomen Heights region of the Stolen Lands. It is notable for being inexplicably and suddenly vacated save for a single word scrawled on one of its buildings.

Ker Sharic.title: Ker Sharic
Ker Sharic.extract:
Ker Sharic is a secluded settlement located on the shores of the Lake of Mists and Veils in Iobaria. Despite the outward appearance of simplicity and plainness among its residents, Ker Sharic is known for its exceptional craftsmanship and artistry, which can rival even the most masterful artisans in grand cities like New Stetven and Oppara.

The village's artisans produce exquisite goods, including flawless window glass, delicate gossamer damask tapestries, and finely glazed stoneware. These exceptional works of art have garnered local fame and a high demand among collectors.

However, the residents of Ker Sharic are a very insular people. The villagers lead a meager and modest lifestyle and tend to remain within the confines of their community. They seldom venture beyond their village's fenced borders.

One of the key mysteries surrounding Ker Sharic is the village's enigmatic trade partners. Every month, cloaked visitors bearing a mysterious white-and-blue banner arrive in the village. These visitors purchase the village's exquisite crafts, offering unmarked gold coins in return. The income from these transactions serves as Ker Sharic's lifeblood, but the people remain in the dark about the origin and intentions of these visitors. The people are always anxious that one day these mysterious patrons might cease their monthly visits, which could disrupt the village's fragile yet steady source of income.

Sevenarches.title: Sevenarches
Sevenarches.extract:
Sevenarches is a town in the southwestern River Kingdoms built around seven stone arches scattered around the settlement, and a loosely organized nation of the surrounding wildlands. It is an ancient site sacred to elves, but has not been in elven hands since they left Golarion for the safety of Sovyrian millennia ago.

Since then, Sevenarches has been under the rule of the Oakstewards, a group of secretive druids. Indeed, Sevenarches is the only surviving kingdom from the initial wave of Kellid migration into the River Kingdoms region after the exit of the elves.

Greengold.title: Greengold
Greengold.extract: The port city of Greengold is the second-largest settlement in Kyonin and the first in the elven kingdom to admit non-elves. Situated on Lake Encarthan, Greengold is a popular port of call for traders and diplomats. Greengold generally resembles any other human dockside settlement, except for the trees that grow in unexpected parts of the city and its distinct yellow roofs.

Nivakta's Crossing.title: Nivakta's Crossing
Nivakta's Crossing.extract: Nivakta's Crossing is a village at the very southern edge of Rostland situated on the northern bank of the Shrike River and overlooking the Stolen Lands of the River Kingdoms to its south.

Three Pines Ford.title: Three Pines Ford
Three Pines Ford.extract: Three Pines Ford is a sizable town nestled on the Tourondel River of Lastwall, east of the forest of Fangwood, and directly on its border with Nirmathas. It serves as a meeting-place for Lastwall officials and fresh recruits from Nirmathas. Heroes of Nirmathas use Three Pines Ford as a hiding place from agents of Molthune, due to the town's close proximity to the Nirmathi border.

Crystalhurst.title: Crystalhurst
Crystalhurst.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Ironfang Invastion Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Crystalhurst is a secretive town in the Southern Fangwood ruled by a circle of druids who make sure the homes and other structures of the town blend in with the natural landscape. The druids willingly help the Nirmathi repel Molthuni invaders, now that their previous central concern, the fight against the Darkblight, was ended with the fall of the debased fey ruler Arlantia in 4717 AR.

Littletown.title: Littletown
Littletown.extract: As of 4710 AR, Littletown was a modest community located in Pitax in the River Kingdoms.

Omesta.title: Omesta
Omesta.extract: Omesta is a town in the northwestern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, inhabited primarily by treehouse-dwelling gnomes, who have been living there since before the elves' return to Golarion.

Pilgrimage.title: Pilgrimage
Pilgrimage.extract: Pilgrimage is a small but growing port town in the nation of Razmiran, on the northeastern shores of Lake Encarthan.

Crowstump.title: Crowstump
Crowstump.extract: Crowstump is a port town at the mouth of the Tourondel River where it enters Lake Encarthan. It serves as a waypoint for Mendevian Crusaders due to its proximity to southern Lastwall and lively selection of taverns, but is also known as a regional center of academia, trade market, and relatively peaceful settlement in a chaotic region.

Blackpipe.title: Blackpipe
Blackpipe.extract: Blackpipe is a small farming town in Numeria, named after the large adamantine pipe that sticks out of the ground in the farming area's fertile soil.

Artume.title: Artume
Artume.extract: Artume is a small kingdom amid hills and fertile plains within the River Kingdoms. Artume is located on a western branch of the Sellen River, not far from the cities of Daggermark, Gralton, and Sevenarches. The region boasts high-quality livestock, especially horses, as well as leather goods and stone. The kingdom welcomes adventurers, who are frequently requested to deal with bandits and nagas. The nation has recently experienced a rebellion, resulting in the overthrow of Regent Waike in favour of Edryd Artume and the restoration of the monarchy.

Deadbridge.title: Deadbridge
Deadbridge.extract: Deadbridge is the capital of the Protectorate of the Black Marquis in the River Kingdoms.

Valahuv.title: Valahuv
Valahuv.extract: As of 4713 AR, Valahuv was a tiny town in Sarkoris that had resisted the invading demonic hordes of the Worldwound for over a century. Unfortunately, it drew the strength for its independence from its daemonic overlord Ommors, the Bringer of Twilight, who was worshiped as a goddess.

Iadenveigh.title: Iadenveigh
Iadenveigh.extract: Iadenveigh is a reclusive and isolated town of woodsmen and hunters in southern Numeria on the upper reaches of the Dagger River. Located on the northern outskirts of the Echo Wood, the citizens of Iadenveigh reject any association with the Technic League that controls the Numerian capital of Starfall.

Lackthroat.title: Lackthroat
Lackthroat.extract: The walled town of Lackthroat is located near the source of the Egelsee River in northern Numeria, hard against the Estrovian Forest of the crusader nation of Mendev. Known as a haven for ne'er-do-wells and mercenaries, the citizens of Lackthroat take pride in indulging their own vices and those of anyone passing through. Despite, or perhaps because, of this, Lackthroat has served as the staging ground for many legends among mercenaries and other adventurers from Numeria and beyond.

Solanas.title: Solanas
Solanas.extract:
Solanas is a frontier town in the region of the River Kingdoms controlled by Daggermark.

As of 4710 AR it was ruled by the Solanas Concern criminal gang, and while the town's sheriff does what he can to keep the peace, the Concern interferes to protect its interests. Solanas' people are accustomed to daily killings and assassinations that happen every day, and the mayor had sent an emissary to Daggermark to beg lord Martro Livondar for help.

Solanas is home to an important Pathfinder lodge, complete with a main hall, a dormitory, and a stable full of fresh horses. The lodge is guarded by a group of mercenaries.

Wilkesmont.title: Wilkesmont
Wilkesmont.extract:
Wilkesmont is a small town located inside the Shroudwood in the River Kingdom of Daggermark. The town was the home of the bandit lord Miklos Jakab, self-proclaimed ruler of the Shroudwood.

Jakab, the last serious bandit threat to Daggermark, was killed in 4566 AR; his followers swore fealty to Daggermark. Due to its location in the wood, the town is relatively cut off from the rest of the kingdom and an easy target whenever the Daggermark military fails to protect it. Wilkesmont has been burned to the ground three times, and each time has been rebuilt bigger than before. The town lacks any fortifications, relying on the heavy wood to keep most raiders away.

Recently, a bandit lord from Sevenarches boasted he will claim the Shroudwood, so the residents of Wilkesmont plan to use assassins from Daggermark to stop him.

Whispertruth.title: Whispertruth
Whispertruth.extract: Whispertruth is a small settlement of runaways located in the Vergan Forest of Razmiran. Paranoid of being discovered, they do everything they can to keep the location of their town a secret from the government.

Marstol.title: Marstol
Marstol.extract: Marstol is one of the larger farming settlements in the Numerian Plains of Numeria.

Szamrak's Haven.title: Szamrak's Haven
Szamrak's Haven.extract: Szamrak's Haven is a hidden enclave of androids, and the largest settlement of androids, in Numeria.

Drezen.title: Drezen
Drezen.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Wrath of the Righteous Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Founded as a city-fortress during the hubris of the First Mendevian Crusade, Drezen was overtaken by the demonic hordes of the Worldwound. For years, it stood as a demonic monument to the foolish naivety of those early crusaders, until it was freed with the rest of the Worldwound by members of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade in 4718 AR.

Riverton.title: Riverton
Riverton.extract: Riverton is a large village located on the banks of Kallas Lake in the southern River Kingdoms.

Bacul Gruii.title: Bacul Gruii
Bacul Gruii.extract: The village of Bacul Gruii was taken over by Petchki, a foreign merchant, who enslaved the locals to harvest wood and livestock for his band of mercenaries. Petchki's men slew Otok, the village chief, and mounted his head in the town square to keep the villagers afraid. Petchki pays the villagers next to nothing and only does so to avoid violating the fifth River Freedom prohibiting slavery. To legitimize his rule over Bacul Gruii, the mercenary captain plans to marry one of Otok's daughters and declare himself the new village chief.

Mimere.title: Mimere
Mimere.extract:
Mimere is a small town on the eastern bank of the Sellen River in the forest river kingdom of Hymbria. It serves as a trading outpost and boasts a sturdy dock, many small shops, a clean and comfortable inn, and a lively alehouse. River pirates seldom attack the peaceful village, because they have learned that at the smallest threat to the settlement, countless elven warriors descend from the trees, leaving only a few survivors to tell the story. The village has shrines to Calistria, Desna, Gozreh, and Nethys.

Hymbrian elves get along well with their visitors, as long as they stay in the trading outposts. Only elves are permitted beneath the trees of the Northern Hymbrian Forest; others are slain on sight.

Halvon.title: Halvon
Halvon.extract: Halvon is a town in northern Galt, just north of the Boarwood. On the Night of the Gray Death, nearly 2,000 of its residents were killed by a gray death released from the town's final blade, laying waste to Halvon. As of 4725 AR, only 263 residents remain, and the town continues to decline.

Stavintower.title: Stavintower
Stavintower.extract: Stavintower is a town in eastern Galt, just south of the Boarwood who is quite isolated from the rest of the nation.

Rosehaven.title: Rosehaven
Rosehaven.extract: Rosehaven is a small Galtan village located on the East Sellen River south of the River Kingdoms city of Liberthane. They celebrate an annual three-day autumn festival known as Mercylight, commemorating the community's defeat of a small coven of Gyronna-worshipers in 4684 AR.

Thom.title: Thom
Thom.extract:
For other meanings of "Thom", please see Thom (disambiguation).

Thom is a small gnomish town in the central River Kingdoms.

Farnvale.title: Farnvale
Farnvale.extract: Farnvale is an isolated town with a dark secret, located deep in Galt's Boarwood, where the infamous Gray Gardeners never visited.

Bergemonte.title: Bergemonte
Bergemonte.extract: Bergemonte is a Galtan lumberjack settlement bordering the Boarwood close to Isarn. The town takes its name from the Beast of Bergemonte, which is said to have killed a large number of travelers in the area between the years of 4696 AR and 4699 AR.

Uringen.title: Uringen
Uringen.extract:
An enigma among the settlements of the River Kingdoms, Uringen lies in the eastern regions of the country near the Embeth Forest. The settlement seems to exist in two realms, part of it "static" in reality, the other "unstuck"—disappearing and reappearing with the mists its inhabitants speaking Skald, a language not spoken in the River Kingdoms for ages.

Most outside the town theorize that the town is under some sort of foul magic or evil spell, but the true source of its strange behavior is a malfunctioning time-altering magical clocktower at the town's center that warped a third of the town to a pocket dimension between the Universe and the First World. The frequency of this phasing seems to be increasing, leading many in town to worry that it is moving closer to the First World.

Iz.title: Iz
Iz.extract: The city of Iz (pronounced IZ) was the first to fall to the demonic forces which poured out of the Worldwound in 4606 AR.

Fort Liberthane.title: Fort Liberthane
Fort Liberthane.extract: Fort Liberthane is the only settlement in the tiny River Kingdom of Liberthane founded by the revolutionary "Young Knight" Achille Parsall in 4689 AR. The small fortress was the base of operations of a notorious bandit before Parsall and his followers seized control of it. The fort is home to approximately 50 soldiers. A number of farming families have settled around the fort.

Dubrov.title: Dubrov
Dubrov.extract: Dubrov is a small village on the western edge of the Estrovian Forest in Mendev. During the Mendevian Crusades, its main industry was supplying the holy warriors with arrows, bows, and bowstrings. Legends claim that Dubrov was founded by a pair of half-brothers who shared a common human mother: one was a dromaar, while the other was an aiuvarin.

Krega.title: Krega
Krega.extract: Krega is a small town on the western edge of the Estrovian Forest in the nation of Mendev that acts as a stop on the crusade route between the port city of Egede to the east and the front of the Worldwound to the west. Because the Fourth Mendevian Crusade attracts warriors from all over the Inner Sea region, Krega has a diverse population, most of whom work to supply the crusaders with anything they might need, from weapons and armor to food, shelter, and more earthly pleasures.

Katrivish.title: Katrivish
Katrivish.extract: Katrivish is a small town in Iobaria's Norinor forest inhabited by seemingly mad druids who worship the cyclops god Prathos, and hunt those who trespass in the woods around their home.

Jovvox.title: Jovvox
Jovvox.extract: Jovvox is a largely gnomish town east of the city of Mivon. Originally built around a remote monastery by the enterprising gnome merchant Jov, Jovvox's weaponsmiths specialize in producing large quantities of cheap weapons, fast, thereby undercutting competition. Their raw materials come from a small nearby iron mine and from halfling and human scouts who also make Jovvox home and scavenge battlefields throughout the region for cast-offs that could be recycled. Kellywells, the current leader of the town, is known for crafting exotic, novel weapons.

Shimmerford.title: Shimmerford
Shimmerford.extract: Shimmerford is a town in the River Kingdoms known for legends of the cryptid Mosquito Witch, in whose name the town stages an annual festival.

Zharech.title: Zharech
Zharech.extract: Zharech is a town in western Mendev known for its gladiatorial combat sports.

Vugorak.title: Vugorak
Vugorak.extract: Vugorak is a community of werecreatures hidden deep within the Hymbrian Forest on the Galtan bank of the East Sellen River. The community welcomes those who are werecreatures and gives them the opportunity to start a new life in the village.

Orlov.title: Orlov
Orlov.extract:
Orlov, the former coastal capital and oldest city of Iobaria, is today but a ruin of its former glory. Located on the Lake of Mists and Veils, Orlov was founded in 753 AR by explorers from Okormirr who discovered the ancient Koloran Roads. Built on top of the ruins of a cyclopean city, Orlov grew quickly, becoming larger than Orkomirr in 764 AR, and eventually the first proper city in the nation of Njalgard.

In 905 AR, Iobar, heir of Orlov's ruler Kjell, usurped the nine koffars of Njalgard and established the nation of Iobaria, with Orlov as its capital. However, after the fall of Old Iobaria in 3150 AR, Orlov was conquered by foreign powers and their pawns. In 3212 AR, Orlov was reclaimed by three warlords, marking the start of the century-long War of Restoration. During the period of New Iobaria that followed, Antoll served as capital instead of Orlov.

During 4000 AR the city was attacked by, and repelled, an invasion by frost giants from the Ice Steppes who had pillaged their way across the nation. In 4659 AR, the "plague of boils and bone-aches" hit Franax, and that town's great shipworks moved to Orlov.

Due to the town's numerous ups and downs, its population now numbers at less than half of its former numbers, and much of the city has crumbled into ruins, with the stonemasons walling off complexes deemed too ruined to inhabit. Its scheming prince, Tzakiv Korya, dreams of re-establishing the Empire, but he lacks enough troops to fully man the city's ancient fortifications. Still, the Prince has managed to build some alliances with adventure-seekers and investors in Cheliax and the River Kingdoms.

The city contains many towering structures, built by cyclopes.

Domora.title: Domora
Domora.extract:
See also: Scion of Domora

The ruins of the city of Domora are located on the Sarkorian Steppe of Sarkoris, at the edge of a dried lakebed that once was the famed Hume's Pool.

Valas's Gift.title: Valas's Gift
Valas's Gift.extract: Valas's Gift was a small town in the north of the nation of Mendev, located on the road north from Kenabres. Although it was populated as of 4711 AR, the town was nothing more than overgrown ruins by 4725 AR, and all that remained were bubbling springs of blood-red water said to be the fertilizing blessing of Valas, a holy man.

Orlendas.title: Orlendas
Orlendas.extract:
Orlendas has been destroyed and rebuilt over a dozen times in its history. Plagues, fires, wars, and centaur hordes have all been the cause of Orlendas' fall at least once, but the town's importance to Orlov and Franax as a source of timber for their shipbuilding industries has kept settlers returning. The demand for lumber has waned over the years, but, along with small pelts, it still remains one of the town's primary imports.

The town's current komar, Galtan ranger Kasrel Unilich, has brought investment to the town, and Orlendas is now better defended and more prosperous than at any point in its history.

Aerduin's Folly.title: Aerduin's Folly
Aerduin's Folly.extract: Aerduin was the capital of the Arch-Duchy of Melcat until the coming of the Living God, Razmir. When Duke Melcat refused to pledge his fealty to Razmir, the wizard destroyed the town and all of his inhabitants. Now known as Aerduin's Folly, the ruins have lain abandoned for 50 years.

Xin-Grafar.title: Xin-Grafar
Xin-Grafar.extract: Xin-Grafar, the City of Golden Death, is the lost underground treasure city of Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant, located on the Isle of Terror.

Leydis.title: Leydis
Leydis.extract: Leydis is a moss-covered ruined city located in the wood north of Rookwarden in the River Kingdoms region known as Loric Fells. It was founded by a Taldan prospector who struck pay dirt nearby, but was abandoned soon after it was founded. Its marble buildings and water-stained mosaics, not to mention the wealth in the abandoned nearby mine, still attract creatures and adventurers, but no one has been able to claim it for more than a year. Those who enter the ruins often find the skeletons of their predecessors hanging from its walls. Leydis is currently held by the medusa Chryphaena and her blind zombies.

Shatterglass.title: Shatterglass
Shatterglass.extract:
Shatterglass is a small and picturesque village located in the north-easternmost corner of the nation of Mendev, on the shore of the Lake of Mists and Veils. The village's name is derived from the shimmering lake glass that blankets its shores.

The settlement is primarily inhabited by retired pirates, and the entire village is constructed from lumber salvaged from ships. The carpenters of Shatterglass are meticulous in choosing the source of their materials and prefer using the hulls of famous pirate ships for construction, even though many of these buildings have a reputation for being haunted.

Zurakan.title: Zurakan
Zurakan.extract: Zurakan is the walled capital of the nation of Hukaris that sits at the foot of Mount Jabel in the centre of the country.

Nesya.title: Nesya
Nesya.extract: Nesya is the capital of the nation of Thieron located on the northern Kardaji Bay coast of the country.

Niswan.title: Niswan
Niswan.extract: The port city of Niswan is the capital of the Impossible Kingdom of Jalmeray. Niswan is a many-tiered city of pagodas renowned for its beauty. Every building supports colourful silk banners that flutter in the cold ocean winds, and red stone streets crisscross the hillsides. Cosmopolitan Niswan is a meeting place for travellers and scholars from all over Golarion, and offers sights seen nowhere else in the region. Here, gold-draped eunuchs walk the streets, and Vudran princes offering strange tribute to statues of gods utterly unknown in this region. Niswan is renowned as a centre of religious learning: its High-Holy district is home to scholars and gurus renowned as some of the wisest people on the face of Golarion, and the city is dotted with numerous shrines, statues, and temples dedicated to the hundreds of deities of the Vudran pantheon.

Padiskar.title: Padiskar
Padiskar.extract:
Padiskar was once known as the Shimmering Jewel of Jalmeray, when it was a city famous both for pleasure and for arcane learning. The Arclords of Nex found a nameless horror in the vaults beneath Old Padiskar, and today the city is reduced to being a community of farmers and fisherfolk. The city rings the ancient ruins, surrounding but never entering them. Whatever awful thing the Arclords released still roams the darkened depths below Padiskar, howling in the blackness.

Unlike many other old ruins, Padiskar has yet to be reclaimed by the Thakur, and the locals whisper that there may be some fell reason for this. Some have even claimed the Arclords released a bound deity which had been transported to the island by Khiben-Sald thousands of years ago.

Aelyosos.title: Aelyosos
Aelyosos.extract: Aelyosos is the westernmost city of note in the archipelago of Iblydos in the Obari Ocean. It serves as the gateway to Iblydan culture and hospitality to most travelers on the Obari Crossing, and due to its many gardens and the tireless work of its citizens, is an overall peaceful place to rest and conduct trade.

Prada Hanam.title: Prada Hanam
Prada Hanam.extract: Prada Hanam is a small port city on the eastern coast of the nation of Jalmeray. The settlement's defining feature is the Murmur Dome of Prada Hanam, an enormous, multi-building structure that suddenly appeared in the town centuries ago.

Sadiyeh.title: Sadiyeh
Sadiyeh.extract: Sadiyeh is a city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.

Heger.title: Heger
Heger.extract: Heger is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Heger is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean, between the cities of Izzet to the north and Husanah to the south.

Husanah.title: Husanah
Husanah.extract: Husanah is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert, the barren region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Husanah is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean and is the southernmost city in Qadira. The city of Heger is the nearest large settlement and located north-west along the coastline.

Bailax.title: Bailax
Bailax.extract: Bailax is the capital of the city-state Pol-Bailax of Iblydos.

Kalios.title: Kalios
Kalios.extract: Kalios is the capital of the nation of Midea located on the southern Kardaji Bay coastline of the country looking out over the Iblydan archipelago.

Tirchin.title: Tirchin
Tirchin.extract: Tirchin is the capital of the nation of Mishyria located on the southern Kardaji Bay coast of the country.

Lopul.title: Lopul
Lopul.extract: Lopul is a large caravan city in the southern desert of Qadira.

Sedeq.title: Sedeq
Sedeq.extract: Sedeq is a city in southern Qadira and the center of the nation's lucrative slave trade. It is located on the Alavah Peninsula south of the Zho Mountains, and is also known for its warm breezes and lush gardens.

Okeno.title: Okeno
Okeno.extract: The harbor city of Okeno, located on the southern coast of Stonespine Island off the eastern shore of Katapesh, is the third-largest city in Katapesh. Also known as the Yellow City, its bustling slave markets host up to 1,000 slavers, buyers, and travelers at any given time and is the largest slave market in the Inner Sea region.

Ayesh.title: Ayesh
Ayesh.extract: Ayesh is a city on the Alavah Peninsula in Qadira.

Ushumgal.title: Ushumgal
Ushumgal.extract: Ushumgal is a wealthy city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.

Merev.title: Merev
Merev.extract: Merev is a city on Yanimere Island, off the western coast of Qadira across from the Ketz Desert.

Khundurai.title: Khundurai
Khundurai.extract: Khundurai is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira that is renowned for its luxurious and exquisite carpets that are exported across the whole of Golarion. While most of these carpets only serve as gorgeous floor coverings, if one knows the right merchant, it is possible to purchase one of the fabled flying carpets so well known outside of Qadira.

Ihalar.title: Ihalar
Ihalar.extract: Ihalar is an underground city located between the Obari Ocean and the Meraz Desert. It is the westernmost example of the Khattibi-style underground settlements. The residents emerge only to graze their herds and trade with passing merchants. The city boasts many underground gardens sustained by the city's light wells although the lowest levels of the city have quietly been abandoned.

Qaharid.title: Qaharid
Qaharid.extract: Qaharid, also called the Turquoise City, is a major mining city in the Maharev region of Qadira.

Sanmeshul.title: Sanmeshul
Sanmeshul.extract: Sanmeshul is a Qadiran city that lies to the west of the centre of the Maharev region along the Meraz River. This region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira and Sanmeshul is the second largest city of the region, behind only Qaharid in terms of size.

Sothis.title: Sothis
Sothis.extract: The Osirian capital city of Sothis is located just south of the River Sphinx delta, and is the largest, and most prosperous city in the kingdom of Osirion. The city is built around the molted husk of Ulunat, a colossal beetle that was one of the spawn of Rovagug and slain by Ancient Osirion's first pharaoh, Azghaad. Except for a short time under the Four Pharaohs of Ascension, it was also the Osirian capital city before the millennia of Keleshite rule.

Azir.title: Azir
Azir.extract: Azir, the capital of Rahadoum and the birthplace of the Laws of Mortality, is a bustling trade port whose appearance has been greatly modified recently.

Merab.title: Merab
Merab.extract: Merab is the largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states, and is the closest thing the land has to a capital city. It is ruled by Ilepodus, the Patron of Merab, and has adopted Sarenrae as its patron deity, whose Temple of the Redeeming Sun is the largest temple to the Dawnflower—or to any deity—in the country.

War Cage.title: War Cage
War Cage.extract:
The War Cage is a weapons and armor shop located in the Yolubilis Harbor district of Kintargo that is a favourite amongst adventurers. It is named for its arena-like structure, with a central sparring area where customers can try out any of the weapons for sale, including a frost lucerne hammer that is claimed to be from distant Irrisen.

As of 4724 AR the War Cage was run by Chuko 'Sharp Beak and Hooks', a tengu ex-adventurer who offers discounts to other adventurers but also possesses a well-known hatred of thieves. He is equally renowned for telling stories to his customers and name-dropping his friend Shensen, the famed opera diva and Silver Raven.

Jayat-Von.title: Jayat-Von
Jayat-Von.extract: Jayat-Von is the largest city in the northern region of Vudra known as the Crying Jungle and is that region's capital city. The Crying Jungle is one of the densest, most verdant jungles in all of Casmaron, and like the rest of the region the city is overgrown with jungle foliage.

Azad.title: Azad
Azad.extract: The city of Azad is located on the northern shores of the Betul Sea in the Western Ghats region of Vudra. Despite being a city of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra, in terms of aesthetics, Azad is much closer in appearance to a Kelesh city as its architecture is dominated by beautiful domes and soaring minarets.

Shamara.title: Shamara
Shamara.extract: Shamara is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located north of the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's southern border with the neighbouring region of Southern Zho Mountains.

Shileh.title: Shileh
Shileh.extract: Shileh is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located north of the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's south-western border with the neighbouring region of Tapur.

Erastil's Alehouse.title: Erastil's Alehouse
Erastil's Alehouse.extract: Erastil's Alehouse is a popular establishment run by clergymen of Erastil on the edge of the Avenue of the Hopeful, so there is plenty of traffic to offer courage in the form of beer to those trying to become gods.

Far Market.title: Far Market
Far Market.extract: The Far Market is located in the basement of a warehouse, accessible only to those with specialized knowledge. Embassies from Axis, the Elemental Planes, the Netherworld, and various demiplanes fill the subterranean floors, where extraplanar materials are traded with beings from the Great Beyond. Even rarer than extraplanar items are also available from vendors from Akiton, Castrovel, and Aucturn.

Korvosa.title: Korvosa
Korvosa.extract:
Korvosa (pronounced kohr-VOH-sah) is the largest city in Varisia, and was once the capital of Chelaxian Varisia. It now serves as the seat of one of three city-states that claim independent authority over their individual holdings in the region. Though its citizens and traditions have strong ties to the nation of Cheliax, with many of its people tracing their ancestry directly to servants of the Empire, the city's location at the mouth of the Jeggare River and the presence of a highly-defensible harbor have contributed to the establishment of Korvosa as a primary hub for trade. Various cultures and peoples live within the city's walls as goods move in and out of its gates.

It is currently ruled by Queen Cressida Kroft, who was selected to rule by the city's seneschal upon the death of the city's previous monarch, Queen Ileosa Arabasti.

Glimmerhold.title: Glimmerhold
Glimmerhold.extract: Glimmerhold is a small, independent dwarven city. As of 4715 AR, it had been located within the borders of the human country of Nirmathas, near its border at the time with Nidal and prior to the establishment of Oprak.

Ipeq.title: Ipeq
Ipeq.extract: Located along the banks of the Crook River, Ipeq (pronounced ahy-PECK) is the third-largest city in southern Osirion. According to legend, Ipeq was conjured into existence in a single day by Pharaoh An-Hepsu VII. Ipeq stands on the southern bank of the river and completely blocks the Shallows of Ipeq, one of only three ways of crossing the Crook on foot.

Botosani.title: Botosani
Botosani.extract:
The Rahadoumi city of Botosani is located on the Arcadian Ocean on the west coast of the Kingdom of Man.

As with the rest of the nation, religion is completely banned in the city but persistent rumors of a cult of Iomedae have recently gained enough credence to be considered a threat, resulting in an increase in activity among the Pure Legion. The ongoing famine besetting Botosani makes fully devoting the resources needed difficult for the Legion, however, and they now look for outside help to either rout the cult by force or to create a public spectacle which they could then clean up.

Famine and cults are not the only threats to the well-being of Botosani, though, as three times in the last half century the city has been the victim of a terrible plague and pirates from the nearby Shackles continue to make trouble for the nation's navy and trading vessels going to and from the busy port. Being the major Rahadoumi port nearest the Eye of Abendego, where the pirates often flee for refuge, it is here that able seamen and naval guides are most in demand to help track down and dispatch the seafaring threats.

Corentyn.title: Corentyn
Corentyn.extract: The Chelaxian city of Corentyn (pronounced KOHR-ehn-tin) is located on the far southwestern tip of the country, as the capital of the Archduchy of Longmarch. Founded as a military outpost, the Arch of Aroden forms the western border of the city, and a 50-foot-high limestone wall continues on around the northern and eastern landward sides. Also known as Port Indomitable and the City of Nine Forts, Corentyn has survived dozens of wars, and remains a major trade center and military outpost in the new, infernal Cheliax of House Thrune.

Duwwor.title: Duwwor
Duwwor.extract: Duwwor is the fourth-largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states. It is situated in the south of the country on the edge of the Barrier Wall mountains, near the headwaters of the Turvanar River. Its ruler is Kharane, Defender of Duwwor.

Courtesan and Unicorn.title: Courtesan and Unicorn
Courtesan and Unicorn.extract: The Courtesan and Unicorn is a combination harrow parlor and brothel run by Vanyavi Czanji in the Lowcleft district of Magnimar. The harrow readings are often performed by non-Varisians dressed up as a caricature of true members of this ethnicity. Shoanti guards fanatically loyal to Czanji monitor the premises. Their pay consists of credit with the prostitutes.

Palace of the People's Council.title: Palace of the People's Council
Palace of the People's Council.extract: The many chambers of the Palace of the People's Council serve as the meeting places and offices of the People's Council, Andoran's legislative body. Located in the Liberty District, at the western end of the Field of Concord in the capital city of Almas, the ground floor of the palace contains a massive amphitheater packed with marble benches in which the council members meet to debate Andoren laws. The rest of the building is filled with the council members' offices and other personal spaces.

Lasinavel.title: Lasinavel
Lasinavel.extract:
Lasinavel is a city situated on the north bank of the Kyonin River about 30 miles upstream from Iadara, famous for its countless feasts. The city was founded by Lasina, a renowned chef who wanted to create a place to host others.

The kitchen is at the heart of Lasinavel culture, where magical processes create an environment in which food and ingredients remain fresh for a long time. Chefs are invited to prepare their dishes in the city's ovens. Although it boasts many festivals, none has become as famous as the feast in which Lasina created a dish resembling a dragon.

Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters.title: Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters
Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters.extract: The Bargecrew's Guild Headquarters is among Jawbone's fancy businesses, drawing attention for being a bare, undecorated building. The guild members have no interest in participating in local entertainment; their job is to guide people who are using the city's canals and are interested in what the city can offer them.

Gurat.title: Gurat
Gurat.extract:
Gurat is a citadel-city nestled high in the Southern Zho Mountains. The imposing city clings to the edge of a precarious mountain spire and is home of the Mouthpiece of Gurat, an ancient cyclops who serves as oracle for the emperor of Kelesh. Visitors are not allowed near the Mouthpiece, who is guarded by a thousand deafened eunuchs. It is believed that the town is named after one of the Mouthpiece's ancestors.

Gurat is considered a holy place to most Qadirans, and pilgrims visit in search of enlightenment. The settlement itself is relatively small, resting on the smallest and flattest of the Zho Mountains, and is surrounded by an 8-foot wall. Gurati locals, known for their open-fire cooking and their weaving, often sit outside their homes, selling their wares to passersby.

The city also has scholarly colleges, with locals traveling there to engage in philosophical and mystical studies. Students are often seen in the streets, discussing such teachings with crowds and passer-byes. Aside from education and basic trade, specialty carpet weavings are often used for flying carpets and it is claimed that some of the woven patterns of their carpets hold secret prophesies, if one can find and interpret their own life thread path.

Shiman-Sekh.title: Shiman-Sekh
Shiman-Sekh.extract: Shiman-Sekh is the northwestern-most city of Osirion, located about 100 miles inland of the Inner Sea on the western edge of the great Golden Oasis, and along a trade route connecting Osirion with its western neighbor of Thuvia.

Eto.title: Eto
Eto.extract: Eto is the largest city of central Osirion, located just north of the mountain range known as the Pillars of the Sun. It sits at the crossing of numerous overland trade routes, including the major one between Sothis to the east and Shiman-Sekh to the west, and has seen its fortunes wax with the increase of foreign trade since Osirion's independence. In addition to benefiting from this increased trade, it has also become a launching pad for adventurers seeking to plunder the country's many ancient tombs.

Lamasara.title: Lamasara
Lamasara.extract:
Lamasara is the third largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states and is situated on the Junira river. Its ruler, Queen Zamere, is known for her clever diplomacy.

Lamasara is famous for its performers, artisans, and hedonists in general, and is popular with tourists year-round. It is said that there is always music in the air, although the most well-known of its artists usually perform in whichever city is currently hosting the sale of the sun orchid elixir.

El-Shelad.title: El-Shelad
El-Shelad.extract:
El-Shelad is a small city on the Scorpion Coast of Osirion. It was once just a slave camp housing a part of the hordes of slaves that built Tumen. The city flourished under the Qadiran rulership of the country mainly because of the resulting influx of Keleshites that strengthened its economy.

El-Shelad is a city of fierce political struggle and racial tension. When the first Osirian ruler of the country after 3,000 years of Keleshite rulership, Khemet I, the Forthbringer, installed a loyal governor in El-Shelad, he tried to dissipate the political tension. He allowed the city's ruler to retain the traditional Qadiran title of "wali" but the struggle between the Osirian loyalists and Keleshite sympathizers has never ceased. The city is known for its stormy elections which until recently produced Garundi governors loyal to Sothis. Its current ruler, Sarita Senbi is Keleshite and thus distrusted by Garundi citizens.

Tekeh.title: Tekeh
Tekeh.extract: Tekeh is a city in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.

Nirfan.title: Nirfan
Nirfan.extract: Nirfan is a Qadiran city in the south of the Maharev region, which is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Nirfan itself sits on the eastern bank of the Meraz River a few miles upriver of the town of Isa; the Maharev Jungle lies along the opposite bank.

Rashiz.title: Rashiz
Rashiz.extract: Rashiz is a Qadiran city that lies near the centre of the lush Maharev region. This region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Rashiz itself sits on the southern bank of the Meraz River, with the Maharev Jungle just to the north upon the opposite bank.

Kharif.title: Kharif
Kharif.extract: Kharif is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, nestled in the foothills between two different spurs of the Zho Mountains. It is on the northern bank of the River Ladan, which separates the Northern Zho Mountains region from Qadira's Pashman region. The Northern Zho Mountains are the poorest region in Qadira and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor across the Jalrune River. As such, it is the least hungry for war with their northern neighbors.

Salav.title: Salav
Salav.extract: Salav is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, sitting at the head waters of the River Ladan in the foothills of the Zho Mountains. The Northern Zho Mountains region is the poorest of all Qadira's regions and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor just across the Jalrune River, as such it is the least hungry for war with the proud kingdom.

Sandpoint.title: Sandpoint
Sandpoint.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Rise of the Runelords Adventure Path and Jade Regent Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Sandpoint is known as the 'Light of the Lost Coast' and is a small town in south-western Varisia under the influence of the city of Magnimar to its south. Sandpoint remains the only place of note along the Lost Coast, but it has grown considerably in the past few years and is now Varisia's sixth-largest settlement; nevertheless, it still maintains the air of a sleepy fishing town. Sandpoint is most famous for the arts: particularly theatre and high-quality glass-blowing.

Wartle.title: Wartle
Wartle.extract: Though it is not a new settlement, the town of Wartle remains something of a frontier trading post, primarily populated by swampers and trappers who do not mind the inhospitable environment amidst the Mushfens. The buildings of the town rise out of the murky swamp water on stilts, connected by wooden boardwalks. Major exports of the town are peat, fungus, and furs, mostly sent down the Yondabakari River to Magnimar. The town sports a few successful brothels and a precariously tilted tavern called the Lean-To, which serves a local liquor called Bog Grog.

Galduria.title: Galduria
Galduria.extract: Galduria is a town in central Varisia, located on the western shore of Ember Lake. Generally thought of as a dependent of the city-state of Magnimar, most of the town's wealth is generated by the trade of grain and lumber across the lake and along the Lampblack River. Its true claim to fame is its arcane college, the Twilight Academy, the founding of which in 4629 AR by Galdurin Barmier was the establishment of the town. The Twilight Academy rivals the larger colleges in Varisia, the Stone of the Seers in Magnimar, and Korvosa's Acadamae. The college was founded specifically to avoid the political pressure and intrigues of those two cities. The Twilight Academy has a reputation for undertaking many experimental and unconventional practices. Nevertheless, locals turn the other way regarding its strange ways in exchange for the money that the college is able to provide for various civic projects.

Wolf's Ear.title: Wolf's Ear
Wolf's Ear.extract: The town of Wolf's Ear is located in western Varisia where the Lampblack River empties into Ember Lake. It gains its name from the unusually large population of lycanthropes who once called the community home.

Omash.title: Omash
Omash.extract: Omash is a fortress-city in northern Qadira near the border of Taldor that lies on the eastern end of the Satrapy's patrols. The city contains a dozen schools of war from which the satrap personally chooses warriors for special duties. Each of them specializes in a different tactic or troop type: there are schools for horse cavalry, camel cavalry, carpet cavalry, archery, polearms, siege warfare, and castle-storming. Some of the Peerless, the personal advisors to the satrap, have come from these schools.

Roderic's Cove.title: Roderic's Cove
Roderic's Cove.extract: Situated on the Varisian Gulf at the mouth of the Chavali River, Roderic's Cove is a small town that serves as an alternative destination for merchants too nervous to dock at the pirate-controlled city of Riddleport.

Crying Leaf.title: Crying Leaf
Crying Leaf.extract:
Crying Leaf is an isolated elven village in Varisia's Mierani Forest. It is a day's travel from the ruins of the former elf city of Celwynvian, where the elves are engaged in a bitter struggle with the drow. As a result, it is used by the elves as a staging ground for their warriors, including contingents of Lantern Bearers, and is not entirely safe from drow raids.

The village consists mainly of simple, low stone and wooden structures, decorated with graceful carvings. It is protected by a wall of thorns. Most of the village's trade and supplies arrive from Kyonin through the use of magic—there is little contact with Riddleport and the rest of neighbouring Varisia. Non-elven visitors are rare.

The leader of the village is the sorcerer Eviana Nirgassan, who was appointed by Kyonin a decade ago following the assassination of her predecessor by the drow.

Arsmeril.title: Arsmeril
Arsmeril.extract: The cliff-side elven village of Arsmeril can be found where the northwestern Mierani Forest meets the Steaming Sea. Along with the settlement of Crying Leaf, it is the only elven settlement in Varisia, and like its companion on the far side of the Mierani, it is an outpost of the elven nation of Kyonin who wish to keep a closer eye on the ruins of Celwynvian.

Peddlegate.title: Peddlegate
Peddlegate.extract: The city of Peddlegate is located in the northeast of the nation of Druma, hard against the Five Kings Mountains.

Orolo's Quay.title: Orolo's Quay
Orolo's Quay.extract:
Orolo's Quay is a nearly forgotten seaside town on Nidal's Conqueror's Bay.

Before the founding of the Varisian city-states of Korvosa and Magnimar, it was the last port before the long voyage through the wilderness to the cities of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. These days, the town and its Chelaxian fortress dating back to the Everwar have fallen into stark neglect, as very few ships dock here anymore. The only ships who make anchor at the crumbling wharf are those either desperate for supplies, or are smugglers bringing goods into Nidal who wish to avoid the harsh authorities in Nisroch farther south.

The first floor of the fortress has been converted into a shabby inn and trading post, with an attached stable of little note. Recently, rumors have begun to circulate that smugglers pay handsomely to store contraband in the fortress' old dungeons.

Fort Ramgate.title: Fort Ramgate
Fort Ramgate.extract:
Fort Ramgate is a frontier town of the nation of Molthune, in the Marideth River valley. It has impressive fortifications, which are tested on an almost daily basis by the forces of neighboring Nirmathas. In fact, the town takes its name from the repeated attempts to destroy its gates. Despite this, the town's population continues to grow, as Molthune's Imperial Army concentrates its forces here in preparation for the almost inevitable invasion of Nirmathas. Indeed, most of the few elite troops of the Imperial Army are currently stationed in Fort Ramgate.

The town is commanded by General Hakar. General Lord Resper Cyrno ostensibly rules the Border region of Molthune from Fort Ramgate as its territorial seat, but spends very little time there.

Tar-Kazmukh.title: Tar-Kazmukh
Tar-Kazmukh.extract: Tar-Kazmukh is the northernmost settlement in the Five Kings Mountains. It's famous for its rather impressive arcane library which not only draws dwarven wizards from all over the region, even distant Janderhoff, but also human and elf visitors. The librarians, called the Blue Warders, serve all visitors, but pay particular respect to the local Sages' Guild, whose members are dedicated to study of arcane and planar knowledge.

Coralesian.title: Coralesian
Coralesian.extract: The small town of Coralesian sits high in the northeastern Five Kings Mountains, and even though it is considered to be within the territory of the dwarves, it has been settled by mostly elves from neighboring Kyonin, who live here among the snowcapped peaks and pine trees of the mountains. Some live in small huts in the high alpine meadows, while others have built cliff-dwellings connected by ladders, bridges, and tunnels. No matter where they make their home, however, they all band together to support each other through the long, cold mountain winters.

Folarth.title: Folarth
Folarth.extract: The town of Folarth is located in southern Nirmathas near the Deepcut River.

Phaendar.title: Phaendar
Phaendar.extract: The town of Phaendar is located in southern Nirmathas on the southern bank of the Marideth River near the Chernasardo region of the Fangwood. Despite its small size, it is one of the largest settlements in the Nesmian Plains. The town grew out of a Chelaxian stronghold which itself was built on top of an outpost of the Shining Crusade. The residents of Phaendar, most of whom are skilled craftspeople, mistrust authority and the town has little to no standing government. Most of the town's law enforcement needs are provided by the Chernasardo Rangers who frequent the town to trade for supplies.

Westsher.title: Westsher
Westsher.extract: Westsher is an important military garrison in the Plains territory of Molthune.

Tathandyr.title: Tathandyr
Tathandyr.extract:
Tathandyr is an elven town nestled in the foothills of the Five Kings Mountains in the far north of that country on the border with Kyonin. The town was founded by the mystic Eninaleryl, who lends her aid in sacred rituals and ceremonies.

The proximity to the ruined city of Shevaroth presents a challenge for traversing the region, though the presence of the Green Faith helps combat demonic corruption. The druids say that an artifact capable of fighting corruption lies beneath the town.

Rivercroft.title: Rivercroft
Rivercroft.extract:
Rivercroft is a fishing town located at the confluence of the Usk and Gjurn Rivers in northern Nidal. Besides fishing, the town earns some of its income from river tolls, placing it in a better economic position than other towns in the region.

The town leader is Beldiore Chaintongue, who became interested in Rivercroft after a merchant discovered a shadow giant inside a massive shadowglass crystal and brought it to town. The shadow giant holds a book with runes similar to those found in Voyages into the Void. Beldiore has issued a call for adventurers to solve the mystery of this crystal and in the hopes of potentially earning a promotion for his efforts.

Whitemound.title: Whitemound
Whitemound.extract:
Whitemound is a town nestled in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains, on the border between the Atteran Ranches and the Uthori Steppes in the far north of Nidal. The town boasts a thriving economy thanks to its granary and serves as a popular stop for ranchers to sell their produce or to hold ceremonies, such as weddings. It is also famous for its tanners' leather goods.

Umbral Court agent Insevier of Nisroch is officially the town's leader, though this is more of a punishment imposed upon him due to several failed missions. The citizens of Whitemound generally avoid him, only interacting with him during sacred celebrations of Zon-Kuthon.
