Insevier is tasked with patrolling the Uthori Pass to prevent citizens from escaping to Korvosa, but in recent years he has not had much to do, as a leucrotta known as the Ascian, has been hunting down anyone attempting to cross, especially those from Whitemound.

Bailey's Perch.title: Bailey's Perch
Bailey's Perch.extract: Bailey's Perch is a small village on the shore of Lake Encarthan in Druma, far enough north not to be on the Gulf of Accord. It's a quiet, simple fishing community that recently caught an undead fish, which is the talk of the town.

Harse.title: Harse
Harse.extract:
Founded in 4536 AR by Sarwin Harse, the town of Harse is an important gateway between Korvosa's inland holdings and its seaward settlements. Positioned at the junction of the Sarwin and Falcon Rivers, the town's twin ferries provide the easiest means of crossing both rivers for travelers in the area.

The town is built on a narrow strip of land between the Sarwin and Falcon rivers, and has been a river crossing since the time before the Chelaxian arrived. It contains only a few dozen buildings, including a church of Erastil run by Mother Tasa Seganni, two grocers, and two taverns (the Spotted Pony, and the Nag Bag). Most of the inhabitants do not live in the village proper, but on isolated farms and ranches spread about the local countryside. The town is known for its horse and livestock breeding, and holds a large rodeo at the Harse Market each year to determine Varisia's top riders and animals.

Veldraine.title: Veldraine
Veldraine.extract:
The town of Veldraine is perhaps the most important strategic holding of Korvosa, and the second-oldest Chelaxian settlement in Varisia.

Located at the mouth of Conqueror's Bay, Veldraine's ships make up most of the Korvosan Navy, and its naval academy trains most of the city-state's sailors and captains. It also contains a giant winch that allows the military to pull an immense chain taut across the entrance to the bay, keeping invading navies or pirates out of the waters—or trapped within it to be dispatched one by one. The chain is inspected monthly by Korvosan officials, and has only been used once in 4685 AR in order to fight pirates from Riddleport.

Palin's Cove.title: Palin's Cove
Palin's Cove.extract:
Named for Keyra Palin, naval heroine of early Korvosa, Palin's Cove is one of the most important holdings of the city-state's realm. Originally a military outpost, Palin's Cove played a vital role in early Chelaxian victories over the Shoanti. Located approximately 80 miles northwest of Korvosa at the mouth of the Falcon River, Palin's Cover served as a naval garrison until 4485 AR, when it became an official settlement of the city.

Over the years, a number of extensive Shoanti sieges were laid on the town, but it never fell, and was rewarded by Korvosa with a strong industrial presence. Modern-day Palin's Cove can be identified for miles around by the cloud of thick black smoke which rises from its many factories. The pollution caused by the industrial epicenter of the town have caused great animosity and even violence from druids, Gozreh worshipers, and even ordinary smiths and craftsmen pushed out of their niche in the market by the overwhelming workhouses.

Not only do the catapults, ballistae, and trebuchets it produces serve as major exports both for the town and Korvosa, but they remain ever ready for another attack, a remnant of the town's military origins. Despite these defenses, Palin's Cove has long been plagued by the red dragon Glarataxus, who has periodically tormented the town since his first attack in 4571.

Elistia.title: Elistia
Elistia.extract:
The Hold of Elistia, or Fortress of the Bluffs, is a military outpost of the elven kingdom of Kyonin, located in the north-eastern part of the dwarven nation of the Five Kings Mountains. Elistia has a garrison of 500 elves commanded by Lord Alavar.

The Hold is protected by the Sentinels of Elistia, led by Lord Alavar in his role as Captain of the Sentinels. The Hold's elves are unfriendly to visitors, particularly orcs and dromaars, but assist mountaineers in need that they encounter in their patrols.

Avennara.title: Avennara
Avennara.extract:
Avennara is an elf village located near the western edge of the Fierani Forest halfway between the settlements of Riverspire and Erages. An elf gate within the village opens to a large number of other gates around Golarion. This gate has been activated thanks to the Godsrain connecting it to unknown locations, with strange animals that have come though causing havoc and bounties for their capture have been place on them.

The Lantern Bearers, an elven secret society that hunts the drow, use a site close to the gate as their main meeting place.✝︎

Visheksrad.title: Visheksrad
Visheksrad.extract: The largest city on the Crown of the World, Visheksrad is the capital of the dwarven nation of Zavaten Gura. The iron citadel is warmed by powerful coal forges, and is where the nation's ruler, Utograf Voislav, makes his home. Visheksrad is located on the coast of the Shining Sea.

Bovodport.title: Bovodport
Bovodport.extract: Bovodport is a dwarven city in the nation of Zavaten Gura on the coast of Cholpod Gulf. It is Zavaten Gura's primary center of trade and commerce.

Riverspire.title: Riverspire
Riverspire.extract: The elven town of Riverspire is located on the Kyonin River in the southwest of the country. It grew from a single family, and the majority of its inhabitants today are still distantly related to one another.

Longshadow.title: Longshadow
Longshadow.extract: Longshadow is a large town in western Nirmathas dedicated primarily to processing the ore brought out of the nearby Hollow Hills and Mindspin Mountains and turning it into valuable commodities useful for Tamran or the Molthuni front.

Valanyne.title: Valanyne
Valanyne.extract: Valanyne is a town located in the far east of Kyonin, near the Sellen River. The village was unremarkable until Godsrain, which severed all communication. Anyone who has gone looking for the town or its inhabitants has returned empty-handed.

Karpad.title: Karpad
Karpad.extract: Karpad is a small village approximately 100 miles north of Nidal's capital, Pangolais, just south of the region known as the Atteran Ranches. It has been ruled for centuries by the Barons of the House of Boroi.

Acorn's Rest.title: Acorn's Rest
Acorn's Rest.extract:
Acorn's Rest is a small village located in the portion of the Fangwood south of the Marideth River in Nirmathas. Most of its residents are druids attempting to maintain the forest during the nation's war with Molthune.

The druids of Acorn's Rest are friendly and open to visitors who respect the Fangwood.

Purt.title: Purt
Purt.extract: Purt is a small settlement located along the Eldress River in Nirmathas. Protected on all sides by a wall of thorns 20 feet high and eight feet thick, the hamlet's human farmers grow the largest cabbages in the region. Purt's citizens know the several hidden passages through the wall of thorns. The druids of Acorn's Rest helped build the wall to protect the inhabitants from Molthuni mercenaries.

Twingate.title: Twingate
Twingate.extract: The city of Twingate is located in the north of the nation of Druma on the Gulf of Accord. It is a semipermanent city of tents where the curious come to be initiated into the mysteries of the Prophecies of Kalistrade.

Siavenian.title: Siavenian
Siavenian.extract:
Siavenian is a small town in the northern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, just east of the Endowhar River. It grew up around a disused elf gate that was activated by a group of adventurers fleeing the Darklands in 4708 AR.

After the Godsrain the elf gate melted into a pile of strange metallic liquid that none have dare touch yet.

Greenglade.title: Greenglade
Greenglade.extract: The Greenglade is a way station on the Marideth River near the city of Tamran in southern Nirmathas. Once a month, it also hosts a bustling market in smuggled weapons and intelligence, particularly for valuable information about the Molthuni Army.

Cavlinor.title: Cavlinor
Cavlinor.extract: Cavlinor is an elven outpost in Nirmathas, located on the southern bank of the Deepcut River where it joins the Marideth River. A few dozen mostly transient elves live in this settlement, which resembles a hunting lodge more than an actual town. The elves are mostly concerned with exploring elven ruins in the area and making diplomatic contact with the fey of the Fangwood. Sivron Nal, Cavlinor's manager, was an outspoken advocate for aiuvarins in Kyonin, and their appointment here—far from the other elves—might be construed as exile.

Ash Hollow.title: Ash Hollow
Ash Hollow.extract: Ash Hollow is a small mining village in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains in northeastern Nidal. It is known for being the primary supplier of coal for Pangolais, and for its annual Festival of Night's Return, held on the nearby mountain of Aghor Thal.

Redburrow.title: Redburrow
Redburrow.extract: Redburrow was a mining hamlet in the Hollow Hills specializing in iron ore mining. Several mine shafts surrounded the settlement, providing some defense against attacks, but in 4717 AR the Ironfang Legion overwhelmed the defenders, burning the houses and its inhabitants to the ground.

Skirgaard.title: Skirgaard
Skirgaard.extract:
Skirgaard was a frost giant village in the Mindspin Mountains whose leader, Skirkatla, raided the region centuries ago, both during her life and even after her death. The village sits at an elevation of 9,000 feet, resulting in permanently snow-covered terrain perfect for the giants who inhabit it.

At the base of the mountain, the temperature allows for cultivation, and there are several plantations of potatoes, onions, and wheat, as well as grasses that are harvested to feed the village's mammoths. Several giants from Volstus's armies trained in the village after Skirkatla allied herself with him during his conquest of Avistan.

Fort Thorn.title: Fort Thorn
Fort Thorn.extract: Fort Thorn a small settlement with a stone keep beset by feral fey, owlbears, flame drakes, lizardfolk, bugbears, and other threats in the Bloodsworn Vale. Around 4707 AR, then-King of Korvosa Eodred Arabasti II appointed Sir Gyrad Tolgrith to establish Fort Thorn and a trade route through the vale.

Mzali.title: Mzali
Mzali.extract: Mzali is the largest Garundi city south of Katapesh in the Inner Sea region and is ruled by the undead god-emperor named Walkena. Having risen from death to destroy Avistani looters who sought to plunder Mzali's riches, Walkena became the absolute ruler of the city. The child-god rules Mzali with tyranny and cruelty, killing any who question his rule or even trade with non-Mwangi peoples. The city is an immense metropolis, with numerous ancient crypts and tombs beneath the city.

Senghor.title: Senghor
Senghor.extract: The port city-state of Senghor is located on the Bay of Senghor, which is part of the Fever Sea on Garund's western coast. It is on the western end of the peninsula known as the Kaava Lands (the kaava are a pygmy tribe of kech) in the southern Mwangi Expanse.

Umnyango.title: Umnyango
Umnyango.extract: Umnyango, known as Kalabuto during colonial times, is the largest settlement in Vidrian. It is a crumbling city built by Mwangi tribesmen in the pre-colonial era, and the overwhelming majority of the inhabitants are Mwangi who, until 4717 AR, were ruled over by a minority of Sargavan colonialists. It was the main target for the anti-colonial sentiments of the Mwangi city of Mzali to the east, and in time Mwangi both inside and outside of Umnyango united to expel the Sargavans from the city.

Anthusis.title: Anthusis
Anthusis.extract: Anthusis, formerly called Eleder, is the capital of Vidrian and the country's only deepwater port.

Mechitar.title: Mechitar
Mechitar.extract: Mechitar (pronounced meh-KEE-tar) is the capital of the undead-ruled realm of Geb, and its second-largest city. Its architecture consists of Osirion-style pyramids, jet black in colour, each home to one of the Blood Lords or influential local families. The largest, the Cinerarium, serves as the palace of the nation's ruler, Geb, who conquered the city in -1108 AR to found his kingdom. Mechitar's busy harbour is the main outlet for the country's food exports. The city's largest temple is devoted to Urgathoa, and has both living and undead staff.

Mount Rein.title: Mount Rein
Mount Rein.extract: Mount Rein is a large, snow-topped mountain in the western end of the Fog Peaks, on the border between the nations of Galt and Taldor. It is used as a guide to the border between those two nations.

Anuli.title: Anuli
Anuli.extract: Anuli, also known as 'The City of New Beginnings', is the northernmost city of the celestially empowered nation of Holomog, a distant nation in southern Garund, and is close to the southern border of Geb.

Radya's Hollow.title: Radya's Hollow
Radya's Hollow.extract: Radya's Hollow is a mining town that was built near mineral deposits in the Hollow Hills, but after a mining accident that killed several people, the mine was closed, and all attempts to reopen it have resulted in further accidents. During the Ironfang Invasion, the town was taken over by hobgoblins who, ignoring the warnings of the locals, reopened the mine, killing several of their own in the process.

Bloodcove.title: Bloodcove
Bloodcove.extract: The city-state of Bloodcove is a decadent haven of crime located where the Vanji River meets the Fever Sea on the edge of the vast Mwangi Expanse.

Osibu.title: Osibu
Osibu.extract:
The streets of the southeastern city of Osibu are literally paved with gold. Located in the Screaming Jungle within the greater Mwangi Expanse, the city is protected by a ring of statues called the Circle of Twice-Honored Women, which somehow reduces the cacophony of noise from the jungle and also keeps out the jungle's carnivorous plant life. Osibu is incredibly well hidden, with many maps not even acknowledging its existence, or misleading readers to another city or ruin within the Mwangi Expanse. Finding the city is an ordeal that involves many trials with the dangerous flora and fauna along the way.

The city is a democracy, and life is a veritable utopia for its inhabitants. There are plentiful supplies of fruit and water, and the city's wise-women are able to mix rare herbs to produce an ointment which greatly extends life and staves off diseases.

Osibu is home to possibly Golarion's oldest living creature, the hunched arboreal regent Dimari-Diji, Final Tree of the Elder World. The ancient arboreal regent's first memory is the impact of Earthfall nearly ten thousand years ago. Dimari-Diji guards the Nemesis Well, a way between worlds.

Ollo.title: Ollo
Ollo.extract:
The town of Ollo is one of the largest settlements in the Shackles. It is ruled by the cruel pirate Avimar Sorrinash, a member of the Pirate Council.

Ollo is on Shark Island and is plagued by raids from the sedacthys who live in the sea near the island. These raids are carried out with Sorrinash's permission, and the people of Ollo live in daily fear of a sedacthy attack.

Port Peril.title: Port Peril
Port Peril.extract:
Port Peril is the largest port in the Shackles and its de facto capital. It is situated on the mainland, rather than on one of the outlying islands, on the edge of the Terwa Uplands and overlooking Jeopardy Bay. The city is protected by walls, and the stronghold of Fort Hazard. All manner of vices can be found in its twisting streets, and its busy markets thrive on the sale of stolen goods. Rumour has it that the plunder from decades of piracy is hidden in sea caves beneath the city.

Despite being the capital of the Shackles Pirates, merchant ships sometimes dock here to trade. Those flying the flags of Inner Sea region nations are not welcome, and gain no protection, but unaligned vessels are permitted to dock.

Quent.title: Quent
Quent.extract: Quent is the second-largest settlement in the Shackles after Port Peril. It is on the north coast of Motaku Isle, the Shackles' largest island. Sheltered from the storm by the large barrier islands of its picturesque bay, Quent is known as the finest deepwater port in the region. Moreover, Quent is also known among the sailors as the liveliest and most welcoming of the ports.

Alkenstar City.title: Alkenstar City
Alkenstar City.extract:
Alkenstar City is the capital city of the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar and houses the whole of the Duchy's governmental infrastructure.✝︎ The city is located on top of the Hellfallen Cliffs, where the Ustradi River falls seven hundred feet down Alken Falls. Monumental works of engineering and architecture, including the Bridge of the Gods, connect the city to the vassal communities of Martel and Dongun Hold. As a result of the urban development of the Duchy, many incorrectly consider these vassals mere districts of Alkenstar City proper, rather than distinct communities in their own right.

Alkenstar City is also the nation's center of industry, housing the Duchy's infamous Gunworks, the source of mass-produced firearms used both for the protection of the city-state and for trade throughout the Inner Sea region. As a result of the industrial advancement of Alkenstar, a constant cloud of white steam and sooty smoke hangs in a haze over the city, generated by the gears and pistons of countless factories operating around the clock.

To the west of the city are the Karggat Hills, a rocky region dotted with mines and factories.

Yled.title: Yled
Yled.extract: Yled (pronounced EH-lehd) is the largest city in the land of Geb and also home to most of its undead armies.

Graydirge.title: Graydirge
Graydirge.extract:
Graydirge is a small, remote, and somber ossuary-city in the foothills of the Shattered Range in north-central Geb. The normal custom of Geb is to re-animate its citizens as undead once they die. Graydirge is literally built out of the bones of those Gebbites who were unwilling or unable to be reanimated. Its inhabitants, no doubt, have their own reasons for living in a city-sized ossuary. Its one notable feature is the Empty Threshold, a temple of Zon-Kuthon. It is home to only one Blood Lord, Berline Haldoli.

Surrounded by a 30-foot wall of fused stone and bone, this city has three main gates to the east, south, and west.

Neruma.title: Neruma
Neruma.extract:
Unusually for the Shackles, the city of Neruma is not on the coast. It is one of the few major settlements on the mainland, and is situated in the northeast along the Terwa River close to the borders with the Mwangi Expanse and the Sodden Lands. Neruma is a centre for trade between the Shackles and those two neighbouring regions. The main trading items purchased here are slaves, but other items of value also occasionally make their way along the river to Neruma.

The city's current ruler is Cousotta Shald. She is assisted by the Steel Cordage, a group of mercenaries supplied by the Shackles' ruling Pirate Council.

The city is built on lizardfolk breeding grounds (although the townsfolk are unaware of this), and is subject to regular attacks by lizardfolk seeking to reclaim their lost territory.

Free Station.title: Free Station
Free Station.extract:
Free Station (sometimes spelled "Freestation") is a town built on the confluence of the Little Vanji and Vanji River in the Mwangi Expanse.

To the west of the city lies the Gutblood Labyrinth: a mighty fissure in the Rechiend's Plains, which the locals of the city call Calistria's Grin and which contains Ghol-Gani ruins.

Jaha.title: Jaha
Jaha.extract: Jaha is a great crumbling city in the northern part of the Mwangi Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse.

Nantambu.title: Nantambu
Nantambu.extract: Nantambu, also called the Song-Wind City, is a colourful, cheerful city-state in the western Mwangi Expanse. A haven of peace, learning, and culture within the Expanse, it is home to the Magaambya, one of the oldest, if not the oldest arcane schools on Golarion.

Usaro.title: Usaro
Usaro.extract: The majority charau-ka city of Usaro is the former domain of the Gorilla King who served Angazhan, demon lord of beasts. The city is located on the Blood River where it flows into Lake Ocota from its southern shoreline. Its inhabitants are feared throughout the Mwangi Expanse, and the former king's treasure vaults are reputed to contain priceless riches, including the mighty bombard Worldbreaker, but the ominous sound of human-skin drums carrying across the lake deters all but the bravest or most foolhardy of treasure hunters.

Drenchport.title: Drenchport
Drenchport.extract:
The city of Drenchport is on Tempest Cay in the Shackles. Its proximity to the dreaded Eye of Abendego means the city is constantly lashed by storms. The city itself is constructed from driftwood and the remains of wrecked ships.

Drenchport's most infamous tavern is the Drowned Dwarf. It serves surprisingly tasty black kelp beer, but is notorious for its very high murder rate.

The ruler of Drenchport is the pirate lord known as the Master of the Gales.

The city is the home of the gnome salvager Tho Rimplethember, who is well known as an expert on the currents and winds of the dangerous Eye of Abendego.

Quantium.title: Quantium
Quantium.extract:
Quantium, a port city on the Obari Ocean, is the capital of the nation of Nex. The city prides itself on its diversity; xenophobia is frowned upon. The city has many palaces, hanging gardens, and open-air mazes, as well as several reputable universities of arcane magic and lore. The city's magnificence has been compared with Oppara in Taldor and Sothis in Osirion.

There are many statues of the archmage Nex, who took control of the then-city-state in -987 AR, as well as of the heroes who helped him forge the kingdom that still bears his name.

Ecanus.title: Ecanus
Ecanus.extract:
Ecanus is a fortress-city in southern Nex that was established during the centuries-long war with neighbouring Geb. It is a sprawling city that serves as the hub of Nex's arcane-powered military. Here the battle mages that make up the backbone of the army are trained in a mixture of potent evocation and battle tactics. The city is also famous for its fleshforges: building-sized artefacts created by the archmage Nex himself that provide the terrifying war beasts which support the battle mages. The fleshforges are responsible for many of the nightmarish creatures which roam the wastelands of Nex, the Barrier Wall mountains, the Mana Wastes, and even the Mwangi Expanse.

The city is the terminus of a trade route that winds its way south through the Mana Wastes, ending at the Gebbite town of Graydirge.

Oenopion.title: Oenopion
Oenopion.extract:
The Nexian city of Oenopion (pronounced oh-NOH-pee-ohn) is known as a center for alchemical production. Here they brew potions and elixirs which factor heavily in Nex's economy. The homunculi they create have a reputation for cunning and dependability, and the city also has a well-known golemworks. Within laboratories, mages and alchemists also work together in their perfection of the so-called art of fleshwarping.

The central lake is the site of a massive ooze colony that has developed a hivemind. As well as being a useful means of disposing of unwanted items and people, the ooze has proved to be useful in divination magic.

Oenopion is represented on Nex's ruling body, the Council of Three and Nine, by master alchemist Borume.

Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie.title: Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie
Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie.extract:
Located in the Docks section of Katapesh Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie is a unique wharf-side attraction that is part museum, part aquarium. For only a single silver piece, anyone can enter and see live fish from all of Golarion's seas, including live piranha from the Mwangi Expanse, a variety of squids and octopuses, and an overweight bunyip. The stuffed attractions are even more impressive, including several intelligent underwater dwellers like ulat-kinis, sedacthys, athamarus, and merfolk. The stuffed collection also includes larger creatures such as a water naga, a young kraken, a mighty alghollthu master, and even an enormous mottled worm.

The Fishy Menagerie is run by Annexa Palanthea, an expert on aquatic life who is said to pay very well for any new and interesting samples—though due to her expansive knowledge, it takes a rare specimen to qualify as either. Annexa sometimes even funds expeditions to search the Inner Sea for new specimens; this interest puts her in competition with the operators of Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval.

Hell Harbor.title: Hell Harbor
Hell Harbor.extract:
Hell Harbor is the largest settlement on the island of Devil's Arches, in the Shackles. It is ruled by the pirate lord Arronax Endymion, a member of the Pirate Council. Endymion is a fan of traditional Chelaxian opera, and the town boasts the Shackles' only opera house, the Three-Horned Hall. It is not clear whether the other residents share his passion for the arts, but those seeking the pirate lord's favour make a point of sitting through performances.

The city takes its name from the numerous imps which live in the area. Endymion is a mutineer from the Chelaxian Navy, and is worried that some of the imps may be Chelaxian spies. As a result, he has recently started trying to eradicate them. Ethnic Chelaxians (apart from members of Endymion's own squadron, the Devils' Own) are not welcome in the town, for the same reason. Any who arouse Endymion's suspicions are thrown into the city's prison, the Wretched Furnace.

Pirates established the port town of Hell Harbor in the early years of the Shackles, as the natural harbor is deep and easy to defend. They used limestone from ancient quarries and Ghol-Gan ruins to build tall watchtowers flanking its narrow harbor entrance. The Faded Seahorse is a tavern renowned for its cyclops bouncer Eriboe.

Murdered Manticore.title: Murdered Manticore
Murdered Manticore.extract: The Murdered Manticore is a pub run by the retired adventurer Shark from Andoran, making it an ideal place to hire mercenaries or adventurers. Even with all the armed people inside, the atmosphere is surprisingly calm, because if a fight breaks out, that customer will be banned from the pub for life.

Solku.title: Solku
Solku.extract: The large town of Solku is located near the western mountains of Katapesh (the Barrier Wall mountains), where it is relatively cut off from the rest of the nation. The settlement is frequently besieged by kholo slavers, and thus rests within a tall wall, though this does not always provide enough protection to keep the townspeople safe. Today the town is guarded by a small band of paladins of Sarenrae known as the Dawn Vigil who prepare for the rumored war ahead, as kholo amass in the mountains under the banner of Noor, the Red Sultana, who has her eyes set on Solku as the starting point of a massive campaign eastward.

El-Fatar.title: El-Fatar
El-Fatar.extract: A Pathfinder discovered an enormous set of catacombs beneath a ruined city of Ancient Osirion, in northern Katapesh. Adventurers came to the ruined city to explore the ancient tunnels and over time the small town of El-Fatar sprang up amidst the ruins. The intriguing artifacts discovered attracted many researchers and historians as well.

An.title: An
An.extract:
An is the first of Sothis' trio of southern sister cities, along with Tephu and Wati. It was founded in -107 AR by Pharaoh Hirkoshek I as a worker city housing the laborers and artisans working to construct his pyramid.

The City of Triangles is so-called because of the similar shapes of distant Mount Na-Ken and the surrounding peaks, as well as the pyramids visible on the southern horizon. Aside from the pyramid of Hirkoshek I, the region also holds the pyramids of Hirkoshek II, Zahur I, and Kamaria the Brazen. Kamaria's pyramid, thought plundered long ago, continues to attract followers of the cult of Rovagug, and some whisper that there may be unopened levels beneath those already explored.

Driftwood.title: Driftwood
Driftwood.extract: Driftwood is a small town in the coast of Katapesh sheltered from the weather but with little trade thanks to its ragged terrain.

Bronze Hook.title: Bronze Hook
Bronze Hook.extract: Bronze Hook is a small trading town located in the foothills of northwestern Katapesh's Brazen Peaks, just a short distance north of the Pale River. Once a stop on a now-defunct caravan route from Ipeq to Solku, the town no longer makes the profits it once did ferrying goods and passengers across the river and charging tolls for the use of the Hook Ford. Since the trade route fell into disuse, the people of Bronze Hook have learned to survive off of what little trade trickles along the old road or the river. With their town's coffers always in mind, the local constabulary has increasingly turned a blind eye as slavers, smugglers, and traders of exotic creatures from the Brazen Peaks bring their goods through the town, despite the moral implications and frequent disturbances that come with them.

Manaket.title: Manaket
Manaket.extract: The port city of Manaket on Rahadoum's northern Inner Sea coast was once famous for its lush gardens, but these all dried up a hundred years ago as the desert encroached ever further into the settlement.

Totra.title: Totra
Totra.extract:
Totra, founded in -2587 AR by the Conquering Pharaoh An-Hepsu II, is the largest of Osirion's ports outside Sothis. The city is often seen not only as a symbol of the country's power, but a symbol of its excesses as well. When the An Dynasty subjugated what would become Thuvia and Rahadoum with its fleets (known as the Great Atoqua), Totra saw an influx of slaves who would build the country's pyramids and monuments. The city's under-classes and nobility are still almost exclusively Garundi, and harbor suspicion and prejudice against Keleshites for their millennia of foreign rule. Following the death of the Fallen Pharaoh and the Qadiran invasion of 1532 AR, many of Totra's ancient monuments were toppled by the invaders. In a turnaround, the city's harbor now hosts a red granite statue of An-Hepsu II, built on a new island created from the toppled statues of Qadiran satraps.

The city is loyal to Khemet III, but the nobility bemoan the heavy taxation they suffer.

Aspenthar.title: Aspenthar
Aspenthar.extract: Aspenthar is Thuvia's second largest city-state and is the country's second port city on the Inner Sea after the capital, Merab. Generally seen as more dour in character than the capital, Aspenthar is under the rulership of the ambitious Prince Zinlo, who has decreed that martial training is mandatory for all citizens. Some worry that this training is in preparation for an invasion of one of the other city-states, or perhaps even an attempt to kidnap Artokus Kirran, the inventor of the sun orchid elixir.

Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep.title: Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval
Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep.extract: Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval is located in a large warehouse along the docks of Katapesh and does exactly what its name suggests. Founded by the adventurous sailors Darg Jexler and Wilbur Young, nowadays they limit themselves to wreck salvaging and tourist tours, they also sell items useful to adventurers heading to aquatic environments. Whenever news reaches Katapesh of a wrecked ship or nearby sunken treasure Jexler and Young are normally the first to investigate. Their ship the Iron Mermaid is specially designed for salvage operations and is a common sight in the water around the city. Currently word has reached them of a new opportunity, the Star Runner was attacked relatively nearby by a huge sea monster suspected to be a kraken. Supposedly the cargo holds of the Star Runner were stuffed to the brim with trade goods and ancient relics from the Mwangi Expanse. Jexler & Young are desperate to get there before the expedition from Annexa Palanthea's Fishy Menagerie who are after the sea monster but are none the less rivals.

Izzet.title: Izzet
Izzet.extract: Izzet is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Izzet is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean, between the cities of Ihalar to the north and Heger to the south.

Pashow.title: Pashow
Pashow.extract: Pashow is the smallest of Thuvia's five allied city-states. It lies in the barren south of the country, in a location conveniently close to the Citadel of the Alchemist but lacking in natural resources. It was ruled by Emir Guldis until very recently. The city's inhabitants are known for their magical talents and revere Nethys as their patron deity. They use magic to help compensate for the harshness of the local environment.

Haldun.title: Haldun
Haldun.extract:
Haldun is a town on the southern edge of the nation of Rahadoum, situated on the main pass linking Rahadoum with the southern lands. At one time it was a thriving centre of trade, dealing with Lirgen and the other lands to the south. The advent of the Eye of Abendego destroyed Lirgen, turning the area into a hurricane-blasted wilderness.

As of 4711 AR, however, Haldun had become a fortress town guarding Rahadoum against incursions of monsters and cultists, and is the centre of the defensive network known as the Rainwall. Its inhabitants are hardy folk who take a perverse pride in their harsh lives, and see the devastation to the south as a warning as to what happens to those who trust their lives to deities.

Chiron.title: Chiron
Chiron.extract: Chiron is a small Katapeshi town on the small island of Sand Haven.

Erekrus.title: Erekrus
Erekrus.extract:
Erekrus is a subterranean necropolis in southern Osirion, west of Mount Osiki. It is built on the side of the mountain and serves as a necropolis for pharaohs who were noble and worthy for Pahmet's blessings. In Ancient Osirion it was a great honor to be blessed by the Pahmet.

Two of the pharaohs who earned the blessings of the Pahmet of Erekrus were Osezis II and Menedes XVII. Osezis II became famous for his policies which ended a nationwide famine. He was entombed in Erekrus in -652 AR. Menedes XVII earned Pahmet's favor because he managed to abolish the slavery system during his reign. He was entombed in Erekrus in 1262 AR.

Erekrus is ruled by a pair of priests who claim to be siblings, Garzukk and Trelka. Together they guide the Pahmet in their prayers and rituals to honor the deceased god-kings of Osirion. They have disparate features for siblings as Garzukk who is the older of the two, has long golden locks of hair and bronze skin while Trelka is dour with dusky skin.

Fort Fang.title: Fort Fang
Fort Fang.extract:
Fort Fang has been the headquarters of its region's kholo for decades. This fortress is built on top of Ancient Osirian ruins and was founded by the kholo slaver Hlasho. It houses around 500 soldiers and slavers and 1,500 slaves at any given time.

The fort is the target of frequent attacks by the Eagle Knights of Andoran as part of their abolitionist activities.

The status of this fortress is unknown since the widespread abolition of slavery in the Inner Sea region since 4719 AR.

Aphet East.title: Aphet East
Aphet East.extract: Aphet East is a small town in the Footprints of Rovagug that sprung up to support the Aphet Mines. It provides goods and services to the miners, although not all residents are supportive of the practices of the mine's owners, the Aspis Consortium.

Oe-Tet.title: Oe-Tet
Oe-Tet.extract: Oe-Tet is a dwarven settlement in the Brazen Frontier area of Osirion. The Pahmet dwarves created this settlement to facilitate trade, without allowing foreigners into their other settlements. Traders are grudgingly let into the settlement, provided they do not stray too far from the bazaar and inns.

Safani.title: Safani
Safani.extract:
The settlement of Safani is a small town near the Crook River, in Osirion. Its leader is Efni Raan.

Safani is too far from major cities to receive the benefit of their protection against kholo raiders, so the town's residents came up with a unique defense strategy. The town is fully camouflaged: the buildings are built into the hills and covered in sod. Other vegetation has been carefully cultivated so it can cover more conspicuous aspects of the buildings, like doors and windows.

An-Alak.title: An-Alak
An-Alak.extract: An-Alak is a small community on Osirion's Scorpion Coast that includes the Temple of An-Alak, dedicated to the god Irori.

Djefet.title: Djefet
Djefet.extract: Djefet is a small town on the Sphinx River of Osirion that bears a terrible curse. At first glance, it looks like many other Osirian towns with reed-and-wood homes built on stone foundations, but this is where the similarities end. All of Djefet's residents bear a curse that causes their skin to turn ash-colored as they enter adulthood and then wrinkle and wither away. Residents who try to escape Djefet find that their paths inevitably always turn back to the town and are never able to escape. Legend tells of an artifact in the Pillars of the Sun that could put an end to the curse, but none of the residents have ever been able to leave to verify the validity of this claim.

Westpool.title: Westpool
Westpool.extract:
The small port towns of East Rikkan and Westpool, located north of Westcrown in Cheliax, are known collectively as the Rikkan Ports. These settlements house large numbers of merchants who trade goods up and down the River Adivian, and as of 4709 AR (prior to the abolition of slavery in Cheliax) also housed the numerous slaves required to operate the Stavian Arches and portage stations. Westpool is larger due to the presence of the Arch.

Since 4674 AR, Chelaxian forces frequently occupy the town looking for rebels and traitors. The current mayor of Westpool, Vismargrave of the Adivian Falling Tibalt Khrocini openly engages in politics and has gained much power and many enemies by controlling the transport route to Lake Sorrow and the capital Egorian.

Dekarium.title: Dekarium
Dekarium.extract: The Chelaxian settlement of Dekarium rests on the northeastern bank of Lake Sorrow near the mouth of the Tomarsulk River. Across Lake Sorrow lies Cheliax's capital, Egorian, while the Barrowood begins just west of the city. The city is renowned for its highly skilled fishermen and abundant fey-blooded sorcerers. Local legend tells that the townsfolk of Dekarium are descended from nereids. A druid patrols the land between the town and the forest, mounted on a griffon named Ironwing Kazi, seeking out evildoers and those wishing to make sacrifices to their diabolic masters in the Barrowood. Much of the village secretly worships Gozreh, including its mayor Lalita Jireen, who manages the town's image and tithes to House Thrune.

Halmyris.title: Halmyris
Halmyris.extract: Halmyris is a settlement in the Halikarnassos Hills of southern Cheliax, just north of the Cliffs of Fury and located at the eastern end of the Archduchy of Longmarch.

Longacre.title: Longacre
Longacre.extract: The town of Longacre in central Cheliax stands on the Fields of Chelam near the western edge of the Whisperwood and forms the regional centre of the Archbarony of Longacre within the Archduchy of the Heartlands. Some believe that it is a gathering place for Chelaxian military veterans who have been censured for openly (or privately) criticizing the government or House Thrune.

Dimayen.title: Dimayen
Dimayen.extract: Dimayen is a sizable farming settlement located in southern Qadira in the Meraz Desert south of the Meraz River.

Odd Stories.title: Odd Stories
Odd Stories.extract: Odd Stories is a bookshop located at 100 Roseguard Road in Otari, on the Isle of Kortos. Its proprietor is the human wizard Morlibint.

Isa.title: Isa
Isa.extract:
For another meaning of "Isa", please see Isa (person).

Isa is a Qadiran town in the south of the Maharev region located on the banks of the Meraz River where the river acts as the border between Maharev and the Meraz Desert region. The Maharev region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira.

Rikhist.title: Rikhist
Rikhist.extract: Rikhist is a rough border town in the far east of Qadira's Maharev region. Located near the headwaters of the Meraz River and just west of the Zho Mountains that mark Qadira's border, Rikhist is the largest centre of Rovagug worship in all Qadira despite the best efforts of Satrap Xerbystes II and his predecessors to completely eliminate it. Like a cockroach, Rikhist has withstood all attempts to stamp it out and it is supported by both orc and cave giant tribes that dwell in the surrounding mountains. The town is home to all manner of outcasts, thieves, blasphemers, and murderers of many races, including not just humans and orcs but also numerous bugbears and kholo. Rikhist is ruled by an axe-wielding sadist named Magyarsh, a riftmarked cambion warpriest devoted to Rovagug.

Crystalcrag.title: Crystalcrag
Crystalcrag.extract: Crystalcrag is a town of geniekin in the remote southern Zho Mountains of Qadira. Due to prejudices against geniekin, the town is protected by illusions created by Tygora Cinderfury, an ifrit exile from Medina Mudii'a.

Jezonna Oasis.title: Jezonna Oasis
Jezonna Oasis.extract:
Jezonna Oasis, or sometimes just Jezonna, is an oasis town located in the Meraz Desert of Qadira and is a rare source of both water and safety. It is headquarters to the Amellas al-Hekbah, an organization of desert giants granted imperial Kelesh citizenship as thanks for ensuring the safety of travellers crossing the Meraz Desert.

Jezonna Oasis has several encampments of desert giant tribes spread between the oasis' three lakes, which are known as the Three Wisdoms. The most prominent of these tribes is led by Janar Malakra, who also leads the Amellas al-Hekbah's mercenary organization.

Khoka.title: Khoka
Khoka.extract:
Khoka is a town in north-eastern Qadira on the southern banks of the Pashman River. It is the last stop within Qadira for merchants traveling eastward into the heart of the Empire of Kelesh, and the first way station for caravans entering the satrapy. Khoka is often full of caravans and mercenaries, many bargains take place in the town, and there are more rumors than could fill a hundred ears.

Khoka's permanent population is small, only 920 people, and residence permits are rarely granted. It is adjacent to a garrison for the Keleshite Imperial Forces, which enforces customs taxation under the command of Captain Jathbiyyah al-Istiyaz. It also contains a temple to Falayna.

Khoka is surrounded by a smattering of small villages in the foothills of the Zho Mountains, including the ratfolk village of Khurbresh located in a canyon less than a day's travel away. The former way station Pilgrim's Rest, a temple to Sarenrae abandoned due to an earthquake-induced shift in the nearby river, is located on the old road between them.

Caravans arriving from the Golden Path east of the town sometimes bring in travelers with a mysterious ailment that causes them to speak in tongues and their eyes to glow bright blue.

Slaves who are particularly devout worshipers of good-aligned deities fetch top coin in Khoka. The slaves are taken from town to the banks of the Pashman River and never seen again. They are destined for Nar-Voth, where they are part of the plans of the archdevil Moloch.

Demgazi.title: Demgazi
Demgazi.extract: Demgazi is a small Taldan town located on the Jalrune River in Sophra Prefecture, on the border between Taldor and Qadira, south of the Border Wood. It is also across the Wood from the town of Heldren, and contributes inexperienced fighters to the High Sentinels.

Elbistan.title: Elbistan
Elbistan.extract: Elbistan is a small town in the western foothills of Taldor's Southern Range mountains.

Elsekulp.title: Elsekulp
Elsekulp.extract: Elsekulp is a large Taldan town on the banks of the Porthmos River, approximately 90 miles upriver from the capital, Oppara.

Lionsguard.title: Lionsguard
Lionsguard.extract: The small town of Lionsguard is located on the western edge of the Southern range mountains of southeastern Taldor, south of the Jalrune River.

Ortalaca.title: Ortalaca
Ortalaca.extract: Ortalaca is a small town in the southwestern-most foothills of Taldor's Southern Range of the World's Edge Mountains, located near the intersection of the Ligos, Lingian, Sophra, and Whitemarch Prefectures.

Sardis Township.title: Sardis Township
Sardis Township.extract:
Sardis Township is a large town on the upper reaches of the River Porthmos in eastern Taldor's Porthmos Gap.

The prefectural capital of the Porthmos Prefecture, Sardis extends for well over a mile in every direction as it was formed as a dozen small mining communities that grew together around rich veins of lead and other minerals. The settlement sits on the chaotic, polluted landscape that stretches just a few miles inland from the South River. Technically, the piers, refineries, and warehouses are also part of the township's domain. Despite being depressing and miserable, Sardis generates a great deal of wealth, trading high-quality metals for foodstuffs to supplement their own meagre crops.

Although not known as a trade hub, Sardis does profit from trade caravans and travelling merchants. Centaur tribes from the Whistling Plains sometimes make the trek this far into Taldor for trade and some even choose to remain. Many are enlisted as enforcers by the local Grand Duke.

Sardis Township is home to both the infamous Porthmos Prison and the governor of the Porthmos Prefecture, Grand Duke Thestro Briarsmith. The former is populated with the enemies of the latter.

Tribulation.title: Tribulation
Tribulation.extract: The small town of Tribulation can be found in the eastern edge of the Southern Range mountains in southeastern Taldor, on the far side of Emperor's Pass.

Hanpa.title: Hanpa
Hanpa.extract: Hanpa is a town in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.

Qadlus Mavari.title: Qadlus Mavari
Qadlus Mavari.extract: Qadlus Mavari is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, and is the north-easternmost settlement in Qadira. Qadlus Mavari is a flourishing oasis town built around a shrine dedicated to Lugalisimaru, the so called "Palm Tree King". The town does a healthy trade in selling offerings that can be left at other shrines to Lugalisimaru that are built at each of the oases that dot the desert beyond. Qadlus Mavari also boasts a booming trade in water-producing magic items.

Finderplain.title: Finderplain
Finderplain.extract:
Near the center of Katapesh, the small gnome town of Finderplain provides temporary home for travelers exploring the area.

Finderplain has stood 300 years as a way-point for gnomes traveling south of the Inner Sea. It holds almost 2,000 residents, but the constant influx and departure means that the number could change completely every few years. Families rarely establish permanent homes in Finderplain; the lure of adventure and desert beckons, and the gnomes move on, leaving their houses empty for the next travelers. The transitory nature of the gnomes means that no individual consistently rules over Finderplain. The current ruler is a gnome bard named Wexlis Bean.

Kibwe.title: Kibwe
Kibwe.extract: Kibwe is a large, walled city-state of trade and mining with a diverse population of Mwangi peoples and non-humans, situated in the eastern Mwangi Jungle west of the Ndele Gap.

Gunworks.title: Gunworks
Gunworks.extract: The Gunworks of Alkenstar was built in 4620 AR and has been the primary source of firearms in the Grand Duchy and the rest of Golarion ever since.

Tiven's Reed.title: Tiven's Reed
Tiven's Reed.extract: Tiven's Reed is a small gnome settlement in central Katapesh, very similar to Yavipho. It is located near an oasis and began as a stopover for merchant caravans.

Stavian's Hold.title: Stavian's Hold
Stavian's Hold.extract:
Not to be confused with the Stavian Barony.

Stavian's Hold is a large town on the Whistling Plains, located on Taldor's eastern Porthmos Prefecture. The establishment of the settlement was motivated by Grand Prince Stavian III, who saw the project as a means to strengthen the power of the crown when he came to power, as well as divert the attention of his critics.

Khulo.title: Khulo
Khulo.extract:
The seaside town of Khulo, the only settlement on Valkus Isle, was once a favorite pleasure resort of the nobility of Nex. Failed experiments carried out in the island's interior by the archmage Nex saw the island overrun by dangerous planar forces and monsters, with unfortunate results for the town's population. The chaos meant that the settlement was run by crime lords and spirit nagas vying for power.

Today, the town (like the rest of the island) is cut off from civilization by the protective Stalwart Wall, so it is unknown what creatures (if any) currently dwell there.

Corpselight.title: Corpselight (disambiguation)
Corpselight.extract:
"Corpselight" might refer to:

  • Corpselight (creature), a type of undead being.
  • Corpselight (Geb), a farming settlement in Geb.
  • Corpselight (weapon), a magical blade.

Pagked.title: Pagked
Pagked.extract: Pagked is a small town in decline, that was once full of bustling factories.

Sallowshore.title: Sallowshore
Sallowshore.extract: Sallowshore is a port town on the coast of Geb directly east from Thornhearth. The town appears to be sinking into the ocean, with half the buildings already partially sunken under the water. The upper floors that poke above the water are connected by shoddy wooden bridges.

Gitna.title: Gitna
Gitna.extract: Gitna is an iruxi settlement that has existed here since before Geb and Nex began their conflict.

Fort Longjaw.title: Fort Longjaw
Fort Longjaw.extract: Fort Longjaw is located northeast of Solku, near the Brazen Peaks. It is a wooden stockade that stands as a symbol against kholo aggression. Its proud red flag displays a bleached white jawbone divided by a silver sword. The fort's residents are desert warriors who dedicate themselves to driving back kholo raiding parties, destroying kholo settlements wherever they lurk, and hunting down any other desert dangers such as hyenas. Led by Giana Secondstride, every day they scout the area for miles, hunting for slaver tracks, setting up false caravans to act as decoys, and interrogating travelers about any signs of kholo activity.

Commonfield.title: Commonfield
Commonfield.extract:
Commonfield is more of a collection of pesh-farming homesteads in western Katapesh than a town, but it represents the nation's densest concentration of pesh farming. Twenty-two farmers, their families, and their hired hands call Commonfield's 36 farms home. These farmers are, for the most part, hardworking and honest; their crops are precious, requiring them to post guards at night.

The five wealthiest farmers meet weekly to discuss coordinating guard patrols, how the season fares, any insect or rodent pests affecting the crop, and other topics. Two notable members of the council are the aiuvarin Richele Gerst and the halfling Kerek Cullstone.

Bug Harbor.title: Bug Harbor
Bug Harbor.extract: The town of Bug Harbor is a gathering of huts perched on stilts at the edge of swamp Evergrowth. It got its name from the swarms of dragonflies, water striders, and other insects that buzz over the swamp. The town supplies herbs and plants for alchemists, herbalists, wizards, and druids who travel here in search of rare components. The unofficial mayor of Bug Harbor is the aiuvarin Bastin Irriden.

Monastery of the Seven Forms.title: Monastery of the Seven Forms
Monastery of the Seven Forms.extract: The Monastery of the Seven Forms is an underground city built around a Tian monastery, located in the prefecture of Whitemarch in the nation of Taldor. This settlement is nestled in the World's Edge Mountains, and overlooks the lands of the empire all the way to the Inner Sea.

Djeneg.title: Djeneg
Djeneg.extract: Djeneg is a village in the Footprints of Rovagug area of Osirion. It is located near the mineral spring known as The Great Pool. The Great Pool has been visited by Osirian nobles for millennia, and the village of Djeneg grew up around the pool to support its visitors.

Saruhk.title: Saruhk
Saruhk.extract: Saruhk is a small religious community and temple complex located in the northwestern jungles of the Mwangi Expanse. It is protected from the outside world by Anwigasi, the divine herald of the empyreal lord Winlas.

Kalit.title: Kalit
Kalit.extract: Kalit is a small village on the River Sphinx in Osirion.

Nyshire.title: Nyshire
Nyshire.extract: Nyshire is a small town in the Archduchy of the Heartlands between the capital of Egorian and Halmyris.

Slither Cove.title: Slither Cove
Slither Cove.extract: Once a reasonably prosperous village, Slither Cove is now nothing but a sinking ruin being rapidly consumed by the surrounding swamp. Slither Cove's current name comes from the wide variety of snakes, lizards, and other reptiles that now inhabit it.

Drowning Stones.title: Drowning Stones
Drowning Stones.extract: The ruins of the city of Drowning Stones, located in the northern Mwangi Jungle of the Mwangi Expanse, were abandoned by its population when it began to sink into the swamp. It sits about halfway between the Olongo River to the north and the Vanji River to the south. Today, only the central fortress remains relatively dry. The city takes its name from the massive standing stones that served as pillars for the central forum.

Nagisa.title: Nagisa
Nagisa.extract: Nagisa is a ruined city on the eastern part of the Bdonge River in the Mwangi Expanse. It is also known as the White City, as its buildings—a number of terraced pyramids, surrounded by smaller structures—are built entirely from white stone.

Karakuru.title: Karakuru
Karakuru.extract:
The keyword exceeded the maximum length of 150 characters.

Karakuru is a small city located on the banks of Taldor's Porthmos River, just on the southern edge of the foothills of the southern World's Edge Mountains.✝︎ Known as the City of a Hundred Fountains, Karakuru sits atop a high-pressure natural spring that brings water up to the surface. The citizens of Karakuru have harnessed this water source to power both the fountains that give the town its nickname as well as bathhouses that draw tourists of every class from throughout Taldor. Due to this constant influx of noble visitors arriving to soak in the ochre-colored mineral water in the hopes of alleviating aches and pains and treat chronic illnesses, the small tourist city is one of the wealthiest communities in Porthmos.

Karakuru is also home to Taldor's iconic fighting style known as rondelero, which represents a combination of peasant dances and noble weapons, a mixture that is a direct consequence of the interactions between people of all classes that happens at the bathhouses. The lightly armored fighting style uses an off-hand buckler to deflect blows and distract the opponent, while delivering precise strikes with a falcata. A small academy specializing in this combat art, known as the Rondelero School, is located in Karakuru and accepts up to a hundred students at any given time. Students typically come from a talent pool that includes locals, sons of the nation's wealthier families, and the most promising military officers in the empire. Though it is possible to learn rondelero at other combat schools that operate without the same exclusivity or punishing footwork drills, no institution has produced more skilled or respected masters of the fighting style.

Geb's Rest.title: Geb's Rest
Geb's Rest.extract: The small settlement of Geb's Rest, sardonically called Bitter End by the locals, is located on the northern border of the nation of Geb, on the edge of the Spellscar Desert. It is little more than a ruin, but this dusty hamlet serves as a supply post on the road which connects the city of Graydirge to the south, through the Mana Wastes and all the way to the city of Ecanus in Nex.

Jalunth.title: Jalunth
Jalunth.extract: Jalunth is a village of Ekujae in the central Mwangi Jungle region of the Mwangi Expanse approximately 100 miles southeast of Nantambu. Due to years of difficult experience with outsiders, the residents rarely share even the name of their village with non-Ekujae. Finding Jalunth in the dense jungle terrain is difficult without appropriate local guides or skilled trackers.

Thornhearth.title: Thornhearth
Thornhearth.extract: Thornhearth is a small thorp in Geb just south of the Axanir River.

Pol.title: Pol
Pol.extract:
Pol is the capital of the Taldan prefecture of Whitemarch after the previous capital of Cydonus was destroyed during the Grand Campaign, Taldor's last war with neighbouring Qadira. Pol remains a work in progress, with much of the settlement existing only on the drawing board of its architects. Pol serves more as a crossroads between the Monastery of the Seven Forms and various settlements and military bases across the rest of the Whitemarch Prefecture.

It is also a source of contention amongst the local aristocracy who vie mercilessly for who gets the rights to build what where within the city, for influence within its burgeoning local political scene, and ultimately for who will rule this new city.

Nine Walls.title: Nine Walls
Nine Walls.extract: Nine Walls is a heavily defended nation of Matanji orcs located in the Mwangi Expanse. Its name is derived from its nine concentric cities, each ringed in tall, fortified, hexagonally shaped walls. Despite being beset by demons throughout its existence, the inner sanctums of Nine Walls are among the safest locations of the Expanse as well as some of the most cultured centers of orc civilization in the Inner Sea region.

Krihirik village.title: Krihirik village
Krihirik village.extract: The Krihirik village, located on the eastern edge of the Kaava Lands, is home to the Krihirik tribe of tripkees. The tribe has been in conflict with the lizardfolk of the region for years, losing more and more territory. In 4715 AR, members of the Aspis Consortium attacked the village in search of a fragment of the Sky Key, but agents of the Pathfinder Society arrived in time to stop them.

Kho.title: Kho
Kho.extract: The ruins of Kho were once the first flying city of the Shory Empire. Located in the Kho-Rarne Pass of the Barrier Wall Mountains between the Mwangi Expanse and Osirion, the former Shory capital fell to earth millennia ago.

Cuttle.title: Cuttle
Cuttle.extract:
Cuttle is a small settlement on the west coast of Mediogalti Island. It is much smaller and less well-known than the island's main settlement of Ilizmagorti.

It consists of a collection of huts on the edge of the beach and serves as a way station for the whalers of the Arcadian Ocean. The whalers pay the Red Mantis for the use of the site, normally in the form of whale oil, and several of the villagers are Red Mantis members in disguise. The whalers resupply here and also render the catches.

Ganda-Uj.title: Ganda-Uj
Ganda-Uj.extract:
Ganda-Uj is the treetop lair of the monkey goblins of Mediogalti Island. It consists of precariously balanced bark huts, linked by swaying vines and bridges. Scholars believe that the current site is the fifth lair to bear the name; the other four were accidentally burned down by the fire-loving goblins.

The goblins delight in attacking travellers, using nets and poison darts to take prisoners, who are then presumably sacrificed to whatever demons the goblins worship. It is best to travel through monkey goblin territory in large, well-armed groups.

El-Amara.title: El-Amara
El-Amara.extract:
The ruins of el-Amara are in western Osirion, close to the border with Thuvia. The ruins lie close to where the Junira River and the Scarab River meet.

On the other side of the Junira, in nearby Thuvia, are the Ruins of Akhenaten.

El-Amara was once the provincial capital of Thuvia, when Thuvia was still part of the Ancient Osirion. It was abandoned and fell to ruin when pharaohs started to ignore their distant vassal state. It was in el-Amara that High Theurgist Fentet-Pesu, the last Osirian governor of Thuvia, was assassinated in -841 AR. Since its fall, few had visited the half-buried spires and crumbling bell towers of el-Amara, until a few centuries ago when disciples of Ahriman took to the ruins to establish a base near Osirion. As of 4712 AR, el-Amara was populated by sepid divs led by general Karesh-Ekhial and hordes of ghul and edimmu thralls.

Mirnura.title: Mirnura
Mirnura.extract:
Mirnura was once the capital of Jistka Imperium, founded by the legendary hero Arustun. The city was named after Arustun's beloved wife. It was situated on what is now the northern coast of Rahadoum, on the Fallen Peninsula north of the Path of Salt.

Mirnura was the main target in Ancient Osirion's campaign to destroy the Jistkan empire. When Osirians burned the city to the ground, they used millions of tons of salt to eradicate all signs of life around it. The Path of Salt region was named for this epic razing.

Powerful sandstorms sometimes reveal hints of monuments, and the vaults of Jistkan emperors are thought to show themselves to individuals of royal Jistkan descent. One of these vaults, known among the treasure hunters as the Halite Crypt, lies in tremendous depths and is rumored to still be protected by a Jistkan behemoth golem by the name Helatoros.

Ekbet.title: Ekbet
Ekbet.extract: The ruined city of Ekbet in Osirion is known as Gralgor-Ot by its sphinx residents. The ancient ruins are protected by the timeless guardians called living monoliths. These magically enhanced sentinels, powered by the sphinxes, protect the ancient ruins.

Erspurn.title: Erspurn
Erspurn.extract: Little is known about Erspurn, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Rahadoum.

Anglemire.title: Anglemire
Anglemire.extract: Anglemire was a coastal town in Cheliax and stronghold of House Davian until their defeat during the Chelaxian Civil War. House Thrune, who had defeated House Davian's forces at the nearby city of Corentyn in the Battle of a Hundred Kings in 4639 AR, then starved House Davian's nobles to death in a year-long siege of the town's keep of Emberhold, torched the keep, and withdrew all support for the town. Its surviving residents persisted until orcs, trolls, and other dangers drove them away, leaving the town an abandoned ruin of stone structures.

Hekastor.title: Hekastor
Hekastor.extract: Hekastor was a city and golemworks of the Jistka Imperium known to be active ca. -3600 AR. It was destroyed and buried by earthquakes of divine nature millennia ago. Golems still roam the site, and its former residents slowly evolved into morlocks who remain active in the area. These Jistkan morlocks obsessively work to power 17 glowing magical pylons out of fear of releasing a destructive threat. The morlocks have been unable to power the pylons by any means other than the sacrifice of arcane magic spellcasters beneath the pillars.

Hapkani.title: Hapkani
Hapkani.extract: The town of Hapkani was famous for being the location of Hakan Mutalib's Clockwork Vault, situated on a nearby plateau, but after a sandstorm and subsequent desertification of the nearby farmlands have done damage to the city's population. The town is slowly dying, being buried by the sands with fewer and fewer inhabitants each year.

Hyrantam.title: Hyrantam
Hyrantam.extract: Hyrantam, the Drowned City, was once the capital of Lirgen, in what today is known as the Sodden Lands. The Saoc Brethren built hundreds of towers for use as observatories. The coming of the Eye of Abendego drowned the city, but the tops of the towers remain above the water. The city's remaining population live atop the towers, which they have connected with a crude network of rope bridges and pulleys. For some reason, aberrations from the depths of the Arcadian Ocean are drawn to these observatories, so the city is a dangerous place.

Oagon.title: Oagon
Oagon.extract: Oagon was a city in the now-lost kingdom of Lirgen. Its location at the mouth of the Black Flow river (in what is now known as the Sodden Lands) ensured it was completely covered by floodwater on the advent of the Eye of Abendego in 4606 AR.

Hanging City of Teskra.title: Hanging City of Teskra
Hanging City of Teskra.extract: The Hanging City of Teskra is an ancient ruin of the Jistka Imperium, located in the modern-day nation of Rahadoum in the Napsune Mountains.

Deng.title: Deng
Deng.extract: The ruins of the city of Deng can be found on the on the southern coast of Cheliax on the Inner Sea. The dead of the city are said to rise at night and reenact the lives they lived as if nothing had changed.

Rachikan.title: Rachikan
Rachikan.extract:
The ruins of the ancient Jistkan city of Rachikan were discovered in 4709 AR by the Pathfinder Eldis Grone among the Pillars of Anferita in southwestern Cheliax.

It is the site of a Grand Athenaeum expedition led by Marianix Karn.

Pridon's Hearth.title: Pridon's Hearth
Pridon's Hearth.extract: Pridon's Hearth marked the most southerly point of expansion of the historical Chelaxian colony of Sargava, now known as the independent nation of Vidrian.

Resa.title: Resa
Resa.extract:
Once a fertile village in central Thuvia, Resa is now little more than a flat stretch of desert with a few scattered signs of civilization.

During the decline of Osirion's first age, an Usij necromancer named Aryana Tahari took sanctuary beneath the village and used its inhabitants as test subjects in experiments to create a new plague. These experiments eventually lead to the downfall of the village and its citizens.

Deromas.title: Deromas
Deromas.extract: Little is known about Deromas, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Rahadoum. The city was known for its lawful nature and situated on the southern banks of the Uta River.

Magarai.title: Magarai
Magarai.extract: Little is known about Magarai, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Thuvia. The city was known for its zealous nature.

Wetan.title: Wetan
Wetan.extract: Wetan was a lirgeni city that was swallowed by the waters when the Eye of Abendego opened. A tribe of athamaru found the ruins in the Abendego Gulf and claimed them as their territory. They began to disrupt the city's magical defenses until they reached an observatory with copper plates bearing strange symbols. When they activated the arc within it, fiendish energies lashed out at them. The once peaceful tribe roams the region, attacking anyone who approaches, and the malevolent energies continue to spread throughout the waters of the gulf.

Mbaiki.title: Mbaiki
Mbaiki.extract:
In the past, the folk of the city of Mbaiki revered the animals of the jungle, and the jaguar above all others. The people all wished to emulate the jaguar—and something answered their call. The people rejoiced until it became clear that instead of being shapechangers they were condemned to live in jaguar form forever. In their rage they destroyed their city and then they moved into the jungle in small groups. It is said that some of the jungle's jaguars retain the knowledge of the Mbakai tribe, and can offer sage advice to those who know how to ask.

Today, the remains of the city are home to the most numerous tribe of halflings in the Kaava Lands, the Hazh'a. They revere Lamashtu and Zura, and collect the heads of intruders.

Boali.title: Boali
Boali.extract: The ruined city of Boali stands on the northern shore of the Bay of Senghor on Garund's western coast, north of the city-state of Senghor and south of the pirate haven of Bloodcove.

Bogsbridge.title: Bogsbridge
Bogsbridge.extract:
Bogsbridge is a small port on Motaku Isle in the Shackles. It is built in a marshy wetland completely unworthy of any development.

Falgood Boles, captain of the brig Bitter Wind, founded the small port after he had a vision of Besmara. He believed Besmara had tasked him to build a port in this marshy wetland. The devout pirate developed the settlement using his personal wealth, despite the boggy location and the fact that no large ship could use this harbor. Boles lived there alone with his wife for two years.

The marsh was gradually silting the harbor when Falgood Boles discovered a unique breed of snail in the surrounding marshes that produced a rare purple dye. The discovery made Falgood Boles a fortune and since then the town has grown. The town still continues to harvest the lucrative snails, transporting it via a sandy road to Lilywhite where the dye is shipped far and wide.

Boles's cheerful granddaughter, Tendry Boles, is the town's mayor. She also tends the inn her grandfather built, the thriving Besmara's Favor.

Little Oppara.title: Little Oppara
Little Oppara.extract:
For another meaning of "Little Oppara", please see Little Oppara (Absalom).

Little Oppara is the only settlement on the Taldas Isle in the Shackles. The little town was a small pirate haven but, in 4656 AR, Danziul Maxeme took control of the island and remade the town to mirror the opulence of far-off Taldor. Little Oppara's fine taverns serve delicacies from across Golarion, and richly attired men and women frequent plays and operas at the famed Blooming Rose Theater.

Ngozu.title: Ngozu
Ngozu.extract:
The picturesque port of Ngozu sits on the long cove that gave Mgange Cove island its name.

Ngozu is home to a sizeable fleet of pirate vessels crewed by sailors of Mwangi heritage, nearly all of Bonuwat descent. Residents of the town are loyal devotees of the ancient Mwangi faith of juju, a religion committed to congress with the spirit world of wendo. Ajuoga Baas is the chief wendifa and ruler of the settlement.

Ngozu tolerates non-Mwangi ships, but Ngozu's rules are strict for non-natives, and most laws involve "blood price" punishments.

Fort Benbem.title: Fort Benbem
Fort Benbem.extract: Fort Benbem is a small port fortress on the Pangalley Atoll in the Shackles. It has been the home port of the Screaming Reaver since the ship was banned from using even Shark Island ports. Black-Eye Benbem had the fortress built using slaves that he drowned soon after its completion. Fort Benbem consists of no more than a dozen wooden buildings surrounded by a log palisade, and is hidden by thick palm trees.

Genzei.title: Genzei
Genzei.extract:
Genzei, the City of Lanterns, is the capital of the Ushinawa Isles. It was established nearly two centuries ago, when samurai patriarch Tanagowu Useme conquered the islands.

The fiercely independent samurai and his rowdy followers fought the murderous kuru natives who populated the stony Ghol-Gani ruins that dotted the island. The bloody fighting lasted one month and, at the end, every kuru was put to the sword. The warriors and the Tian-Min sailors and servants who accompanied them established the new, limestone town of Genzei.

The settlers went to great efforts to erase any signs of the original Ghol-Gani builders, painting over the depictions of cannibalism and bloody alien rituals and removing the limestone carvings portraying their ravenous gods.

Rickety's Squibs.title: Rickety's Squibs
Rickety's Squibs.extract:
Rickety's Squibs is a village at the mouth of a nameless river on the Slithering Coast in the Shackles' mainland. It is concealed from passing ships by a protruding headland and jungle, and is difficult to find if one does not know where to look.

Rickety's Squibs refits and modifies the lines and profiles of ships to provide a new identity for a price. These rebuilt vessels are referred to as "squibs." Rickety's Squibs is run by Rickety Hake, a retired pirate; he was the former mate of the Bearded Whore, whose Captain Ella Gurnett was killed at the battle of Nolis Point.

As of 4720 AR, rumors suggested that the Chelaxian Navy had taken over Rickety's Squibs in order to coerce its crew into refitting a warship in such a manner that it could defeat Hurricane Queen Tessa Fairwind's Luck of the Draw in battle.

Azlant Ridge.title: Azlant Ridge
Azlant Ridge.extract:
Azlant Ridge is a ruin in the Terwa Uplands discovered in 4710 AR by the pathfinder Juliet Dias. An ivory sphere stands outside the structure, which illuminates when it detects the presence of the charau-ka, blocking teleportation magic. A palidade was erected to protect the site from attacks. Inside, a doorway displays symbols that no Azlant scholar can recognize, and beyond it, a technomagical object grants access to a defense system capable of protecting the structure through metal soldiers called Azlanti Monoliths, which can be controlled by whoever operates the defense system.

Years of investigation later, the purpose of the ruins was discovered: laboratories and armories for weapons of war used in the war with the serpentfolk, a conflict in which Azlant and Ghol-Gan collaborated. The site's engineers did not trust their cyclopean assistants and programmed the machines to activate only when someone contaminated by the Outer Rifts was present.

Oyster Cay.title: Oyster Cay
Oyster Cay.extract:
The port of Oyster Cay is in the Mainland region of the Shackles.

It is currently suffering as two Free Captains vie for control of the port, namely Havalas Grudd and his estranged lover Wide Olga.

Parley Point.title: Parley Point
Parley Point.extract:
The surprisingly welcoming port of Parley Point is on the coast of the Garundi mainland region of the Shackles. It is a popular port of call for merchant captains travelling north, who often stop off here before braving the storms of the Eye of Abendego. It is renowned for its friendly citizens and fine inns (and brothels).

The Parley Point lighthouse warns passing vessels of the nearby reefs and dangerous surf. The crews of those vessels that do run aground are rescued by the town's fleet of galleys.

However, what the sailors fail to realise is that the town also has a second, false lighthouse further inland, that is sometimes used to fool captains into running aground. The citizens rescue these sailors in the same way as the others, but also surreptitiously make off with as much of their cargo as they can salvage.

Alendruan Harbor.title: Alendruan Harbor
Alendruan Harbor.extract: Founded by the elven witch called Kaala Raingold around 4622 AR, Alendruan Harbor is as of 4712 AR the main port on the Shackles' island of Kepre Dua.

Plumetown.title: Plumetown
Plumetown.extract:
Plumetown is the only major settlement on the island of the Smoker in the Shackles. Willa Cloudbuster is the de facto mayor of the town, and oversees Plumetown's sometimes impatient and disruptive visitors with a small army of constables: former pirates who carry massive clubs. The entire town seems designed solely to house, feed and entertain its visitors who seek the wisdom of the mysterious soothsayers, Keeba's Eyes, living in the caverns of Mount Keeba. It boasts a large number of taverns, hostels, gaming houses, and brothels.

In 4717 AR Whark won the Free Captains' Regatta and was granted this town which she works to modernize into a hub of trade and entertainment.

Ganagsau.title: Ganagsau
Ganagsau.extract:
Ganagsau is the largest set of Ghol-Gan ruins in the Cannibal Isles of the Shackles. It is also the location of their so-called deity, the Blood Queen. The Blood Queen is actually a powerful outsider of astounding power and evil who has transformed the kuru—people who were forced onto the islands—into primitive monsters.

The Blood Queen's current high priest is Bukrugsor. He serves in the Sanguine Cathedral as the intermediate between the kuru and their "goddess", making demands that include human sacrifices and other acts of repugnant devotion.

Dragonsthrall.title: Dragonsthrall
Dragonsthrall.extract: Dragonsthrall is the only settlement on the island of Dahak's Horn. The island of Dahak's Horn was chosen by the great dragon-priest Aashaq when she first came to the isles of the Shackles, for her loyal humanoid worshipers to inhabit.

Ghrinitshahara.title: Ghrinitshahara
Ghrinitshahara.extract:
Ghrinitshahara is the capital of the Rampore Isles in the Shackles. The busy town is located on the central island of the archipelago, and as of 4712 AR it was famous for its large harbor and shipyards, where captured ships are repaired and refitted for sale to buyers across the Shackles, Sargava (now known as Vidrian), and the Inner Sea region.

As of 4712 AR, the cruel rakshasa Bedu Hanji ruled the Rampore Isles from his sprawling palace in Ghrinitshahara. The town once resembled the hamlets of the distant Kingdom of Vudra, home to the Vudrani mariners who established the settlement. When Bedu Hanji infiltrated Rampore's loose organization and became the master of the isles in 4662 AR, Ghrinitshahara became the center of a thriving business around the trade of humanoid slaves.

Beachcomber.title: Beachcomber
Beachcomber.extract:
The town of Beachcomber on Bag Island in the Shackles is mainly inhabited by halflings, but also a large number of liberated dromaars live in its Lurker District. The port is rowdy and shady characters are often attracted to the town.

An increasingly bold temple to Norgorber is located at the north edge of the town, and it is said that secret tunnels beneath the temple lead to the vast catacombs under the town. Despite the freedom of religion on Bag Island, most of Norgorber's faithful still hide their identity. The two priests of Norgorber in town are Antal Armskeep and Black Gnarea and they are almost open about their faith. They jointly command the ketch Darkbow. The priest-captains are brutally efficient at hunting and destroying slave ships, and often sacrifice the captured slavers to Norgorber.

Fort Holiday.title: Fort Holiday
Fort Holiday.extract:
Fort Holiday is a refuge built by the marooned survivors of Raptor Island in the Shackles. It is located atop a rocky hill of the island, at the fork of the Broken Hope River and its main tributary. It consists of a number of huts surrounded by a wooden palisade.

The castaways continue to strengthen the palisade of their small refuge with stones and welcome any new survivors who can make it to the gates of Fort Holiday.

Cho-Tzu.title: Cho-Tzu
Cho-Tzu.extract:
Cho-Tzu is the main settlement on the Shenchu Bay island in the Shackles. It is built around a large triangular harbor.

Most of the harbor is used by the Shenchu pirate vessels and the merchants ships. A second city is formed in the remaining part of the harbor consisting of 50 or more junks, rafts, and other ramshackle watercraft occupied by over 200 Tian-Sings, descended from sailors from Zo Piaobo, the floating city of Minata. These folk rarely set foot on dry land and are known to provide services from fortune-telling to midwifery but can also be hired as thugs. Most of the inhabitants of Cho-Tzu are Tian-Sings but the ramshackle neighborhoods on the northernmost side of the city are populated by tengu who escaped from Imperial Lung Wa long ago.

Jula.title: Jula
Jula.extract: Jula is a coastal village in the southern Sodden Lands, populated by hardy folk who defy the Eye of Abendego and refuse to leave the Sodden Lands for more temperate climes.

Elokolobha.title: Elokolobha
