Elokolobha.extract: The city of Elokolobha is carved out of a massive limestone formation on the eastern edge of the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse. It is inhabited by fey bilokos and elokos.

Port Freedom.title: Port Freedom
Port Freedom.extract:
Situated on the northern bank of the Korir River delta, Port Freedom is Vidrian's second port, behind the capital Anthusis.

The sandbanks of the delta mean the port cannot accommodate large, deep water ships, and are also a serious hazard to the river trade with Kalabuto. Barges travelling the river normally call upon the services of the Rivermen's Guild, native Mwangi pilots who are the only people skilled enough to reliably navigate the river.

M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch.title: M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch
M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch.extract:
M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch began as a cattle ranch on the edge of the M'neri Plains in Sargava. It was established by Olgran Macini under the name Freehold as a holiday spot, but has since grown into the equivalent of a small town. Olgan always tried to be fair to the native Mwangi ranch hands, and since then they have been paid a fair wage and offered a share of the profits.

It is currently run by Olgran's great-great-granddaughter Mindra Macini, although her cousin Salgarth is trying to gain control. He would like to run it as a "normal" Sargavan business, without any benefits for the Mwangi workers.

In the aftermath of Sargava's fall and the establishment of Vidrian, Freehold was renamed to M'neri Cattle Ranch, while Mindra appointed two native siblings who work as a herder and breeder to handle daily operations. Some members of the Combined Mercantile Interests and Field Unions say Mindra should waive all of her claims if she believes the Mwangi to be her equals, while certain people who wish to bring back colonial rule seek to harshly punish Mindra and her ranch.

Silvertree.title: Silvertree
Silvertree.extract:
This article covers the town in Vidrian. For the village in the Forest of Spirits, see Silvertree (Minkai).

Silvertree is a small settlement in northwestern Vidrian. Under the rule of Sargava, it was known as Crown's End.

Fort Bandu.title: Fort Bandu
Fort Bandu.extract: Fort Bandu is a fortification in eastern Vidrian, located in the Bandu Hills on the banks of the River of Lost Tears near the border with the Mwangi Expanse.

Stark Point.title: Stark Point
Stark Point.extract:
Stark Point is a trading post on the River of Lost Tears in the extreme east of Sargava. It is southeast of Fort Bandu and north of Kalabuto.

It was founded in 4414 AR as a mining community under the leadership of the church of Aroden. As a result, in addition to an inn, stable, and general store, it also boasts a surprisingly large cathedral. However, the mineral deposits were eventually exhausted, and the death of Aroden led to the cathedral being closed down and boarded up. Most of the population soon relocated to Kalabuto, and Stark Point become little more than a general store.

The abandoned cathedral might be haunted, and some have claimed to have seen strange lights and heard chanting coming from within.

In 4690 AR, the Sclerizi brothers—Arno, Carlo, Gudo, and Ikhaia—bought the store and converted it into the Korir Crossing Trading Post.

Greenblood.title: Greenblood
Greenblood.extract:
The Free Captains of the Shackles established a pit fight on a rock in the sea just off the coast of the Sodden Lands located northwest of Neruma. It became known as Green Blood on a Black Rock, and its fame spread as far as Avistan.

The popularity of the event led to the settlement of Greenblood growing up close to the site. Its population of brigands are considered to be rough and lawless even by Shackles standards.

Falchion Point.title: Falchion Point
Falchion Point.extract:
Falchion Point is a small town in the Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. Falchion Point has a reputation for unshowy fighting. The town is known for the Bloody Twins event, in which two combatants are manacled together and fight one another with glass shards. Usually, both opponents in a battle of Bloody Twins end up bleeding to death. Lefty Kemya, Falchion Point's mayor, is famous for having actually survived the Bloody Twins, albeit at the cost of his right hand.

Other attractions include Beaten Eyes, wherein four combatants are temporarily blinded with magic and pitted against one another in a bare-knuckle battle. Another unnamed game of wits is also played that involves two heavily armored combatants who duel in a small pit the ground of which is covered in hidden deadly traps.

Sumitha.title: Sumitha
Sumitha.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Island of Empty Eyes.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Sumitha is an ancient Ghol-Gani ruin on the Island of Empty Eyes in the Shackles.

Saventh-Yhi.title: Saventh-Yhi
Saventh-Yhi.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: City of Seven Spears.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The ruined city of Saventh-Yhi (pronounced saa-vinth-YHEE) is located in the southern Mwangi Jungle, east of the Ocota River, just north of the Bandu Hills. The city was founded more than 10,000 years ago during the Age of Legend by followers of the Azlanti hero Savith, who died while defeating the serpentfolk god Ydersius in the Darklands' tunnels below.

Arzikal.title: Arzikal
Arzikal.extract:
Also known as the City of Hungry Spires, the ruined city of Arzikal is located in the Mwangi Expanse's Bandu Hills, overlooking the Screaming Jungle.

The city appears as if an angry god ripped up the walls and buildings and stacked them on top of each other to form hundreds of precarious spires. It seems as if each spire could fall at any moment, and some do—yet the next day the fallen spire stands once more, but not necessarily in its previous location.

Strangely, sounds from the nearby jungle do not carry into the ruins. The only noise is the creaking of the towers.

The stone surface of the city's floor is marked by countless handprints, varying from the size of a human child's hand to massive prints over twenty feet across. All of these hand prints have seven fingers.

Those who dare to spend the night in the city are killed by some unknown force, their crushed bodies and scattered belongings added to the spires.

Bloodsalt.title: Bloodsalt
Bloodsalt.extract: Bloodsalt is the largest of several ruined cities on the shore of Terwa Lake, at the northern edge of Rechiend's Plains in the Mwangi Expanse. It takes its name from the region's red, iron-rich silt (similar to that which gave Bloodcove its name). Much of the city is now buried beneath the silt. The local tribes view the ruins as taboo. Treasure hunters who survive the creatures living within the ruins also have to escape the wrath of the natives. Few clues remain to identify the city's original inhabitants, but visitors tell of uncovering skeletons whose various locations imply the entire population died at once. In fact, the lake occasionally releases a cloud of deadly volcanic gas, which kills everything in the vicinity. The city is also sometimes known as the City of the Dragon-Men, after iconography found there which depicts people sprouting dragon-like wings and flying off to commune with spirits.

Darkreach.title: Darkreach
Darkreach.extract:
The settlement of Darkreach was established on the Vanji River around 30 years ago. It was intended to serve as a trading post between the mines of the Bandu Hills and the markets of Bloodcove in the Mwangi Expanse and of western Sargava.

At first it thrived—only to be razed to the ground by forces unknown. The people vanished, and all that was left of the buildings was the charred outline of the foundations. No natural fire cuts buildings off smoothly at ground level, and leaves no ash behind.

Some suspect the Aspis Consortium of wiping out a competitor; others wonder if the diamond miners had taken action against the tariffs charged on river traffic.

Rastel.title: Rastel
Rastel.extract:
The now-extinct nation of Rastel was known for conflict with its neighbours, which at the time included Holy Xatramba. At its height its territory included the Korir Plains and part of the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse.

It included humans, dwarves and lizardfolk among its members, and they worshipped a cross-species pantheon of gods.

They used dark magic in their conquests, but in summoning the horde of demons which destroyed Xatramba they may have inadvertently hastened their own downfall.

In any event, all that remains today are ruins, most notably the ruined cities of Rastel and Liclac. These ruins show signs of fire, conflict and cannibalism, and all the temples have been desecrated. However, it is not clear whether the nation fell as a result of civil war, out-of-control demons, or both.

Liclac.title: Liclac
Liclac.extract: The ruined city of Liclac was once a thriving settlement of the now-extinct nation of Rastel. The ruins stand upon the banks of the Upper Korir River on the edge of the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse. Just as with all other ruins of the Rastel culture, Liclac shows signs of fire, conflict, and cannibalism.

Kembe.title: Kembe
Kembe.extract:
The ruins of Kembe are in the southern part of the Mwangi Jungle, in the Mwangi Expanse.

It consists of three concentric rings of buildings. The buildings of the inner ring are sized for giants, those of the middle ring are sized for humans, and those of the outer ring are sized for halflings.

According to the myths carved on the city walls, a race of giants arrived from the sky in silver craft. They built the inner ring of buildings, but over generations found themselves getting smaller and smaller, requiring the building of the middle ring, and then finally the outer ring. They eventually shrunk too small to be seen.

Tazion.title: Tazion
Tazion.extract: Tazion is an ancient Azlanti ruin located in the southern Mwangi Jungle of the Mwangi Expanse.

Kiutu.title: Kiutu
Kiutu.extract: Kiutu is a small village in the Mwangi Expanse near Terwa Lake. Most of the locals make a steady living through fishing, although most of the town's income is generated from building boats. Bonuwat tradition and Mbe'ke innovation have combined over the generations to create some of the regions most in-demand boats. Kiutu is also known for producing a surprising amount of artisans who become explorers and adventurers, though they remember to send money and other gifts back to their families.

Ashkurhall.title: Ashkurhall
Ashkurhall.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: the Scions of the Sky Key arc of the Pathfinder Society Roleplaying Guild Year of the Sky Key, particularly encounters in The Golden Guardian.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Ashkurhall is an ancient abandoned dwarven settlement in Mount Nakyuk, located in a jungle vale of the Mwangi Expanse known by as the Valley of Broken Blood. It was the subject of the final expedition by the Pathfinder Society Venture-Captain Nieford Sharrowsmith, which led to his death.

Holy Xatramba.title: Holy Xatramba
Holy Xatramba.extract:
The ruins of Holy Xatramba (sometimes also spelled Xatremba) lie in the eastern jungle of the Mwangi Expanse, southwest of the city of Kibwe.

The city was founded in 3699 AR, at the site of Volnagur's first defeat (which happened in 909 AR). Its religion was an unique mix of ancestor worship and worship of Pharasma and its memory is revered by the Mwangi people.

Kokutang.title: Kokutang
Kokutang.extract:
Kokutang was the capital of Yamasa, one of the two lost kingdoms that now make up the Sodden Lands. The city was destroyed by the Eye of Abendego. Today there is a town on the surface, with a complex of caves and passages beneath, most of which are flooded.

The site is a seasonal gathering place for the Koboto, the cannibalistic descendants of the people of Yamasa; visitors risk being eaten.

Ilizmagorti.title: Ilizmagorti
Ilizmagorti.extract:
The city of Ilizmagorti (pronounced ihl-izz-mah-GOR-tee) is the only settlement of any size on Mediogalti Island.

Known to its foes as Scum Tide City, and to its friends as the Black Pearl of the Tropics, the city's exact location is unlikely to appear on any map thanks to the efforts of the Red Mantis to remove it from all records. Many of the ships that dock in the harbour are pirates, but the port is also used by a number of (comparatively) honest merchant vessels.

The population is also assumed to include a large number of Red Mantis assassins, which means a certain wariness is probably advisable when dealing with strangers.

Dokeran.title: Dokeran
Dokeran.extract:
The ruined city of Dokeran, sometimes written Dokaren, stands on the Defaka River in the Mwangi Expanse. The city was destroyed several centuries ago by Tyruwat the Lost One using the artifact known as the Song of Extinction.

Tyruwat now lives beneath the city's grand temple, his life greatly extended by the years stolen from his cult and the local tribes using the Song. He is guarded by packs of howlers and a tribe of wild elves he has subjugated.

The people of Dokeran built the city of Saruhk nearly a thousand years ago as a place of pilgrimage for followers of Winlas, an empyreal lord, and Aroden.

A tapestry of the city hangs in Venture-Captain Nieford Sharrowsmith's office, which obscures the secret entrance to the Pathfinder lodge hidden within Sharrowsmith's Exports in Nantambu.

Besmara's House.title: Besmara's House
Besmara's House.extract:
Besmara's House is the temple to Besmara in Ilizmagorti on Mediogalti Island. It is sited in a converted warehouse in the city's Wharves district. It is the second largest temple in the city: only the Pagoda of the Mantis is larger.

Few of Ilizmagorti's established citizens revere Besmara, but visiting pirates make offerings at the temple before setting out on voyages. The temple's message board is a convenient place for captains to leave their details, in order for potential employers or potential crew to contact them.

The clerics, led by Priest-Captain Ramos Blackarm, seek to cajole or frighten visiting sailors into making a donation to the goddess. They sell "placations" to the gullible, claiming they will protect the bearer from the perils of the sea. They also offer to sell letters of marque and flags of convenience, seeking to increase strife on the high seas in honour of their goddess.

Pagoda of the Mantis.title: Pagoda of the Mantis
Pagoda of the Mantis.extract: The Pagoda of the Mantis is the temple to Achaekek in Ilizmagorti on Mediogalti Island. Situated in the Redshore district, it is the city's largest temple. It is open to all and unguarded during the daytime, but unbelievers are magically marked with the deity's religious symbol on their foreheads as soon as they step over the threshold and are promptly attacked by the rest of the congregation.

Temple of the Sunken Sign.title: Temple of the Sunken Sign
Temple of the Sunken Sign.extract: The Temple of the Sunken Sign is a Ghol-Gani temple complex dedicated to Azathoth, hidden in the Sodden Lands.

Hospice of Serenity.title: Hospice of Serenity
Hospice of Serenity.extract: The Hospice of Serenity is an abbey nestled among the slums of the Bilges, dedicated to a saint of healing. The nuns who run the hospice are easily recognizable by their shaved heads and white robes, and they offer healing services to the poorest of the poor. Sister Maeri is the leader here and secretly a member of the Red Mantis, offering healing services in exchange for information, new recruits, or to sell the location of fugitives to parties that can pay. Since there aren't many temples in the city, most adventurers go to her, which keeps her informed of potentially dangerous groups in the city.

Seaside Betty's.title: Seaside Betty's
Seaside Betty's.extract: Seaside Betty's is the smallest brothel in Ilizmagorti, consisting of a single room run by the elderly Seaside Betty. Clients seek the information she provides, as Betty knows the comings and goings of the city's crews and serves as their intelligence network; even the Red Mantis utilizes her services.

Mantis Stone.title: Mantis Stone
Mantis Stone.extract: The Mantis Stones are a series of menhirs outside the city of Ilizmagorti, which are linked to the predatory forces that dominate the island. When the Achaekek cult arrived on the island and found that these monuments were linked to giant mantises, they saw it as a sign from their god that they were on the right path by relocating to the island.

Mirazha.title: Mirazha
Mirazha.extract: Years ago, the town of Mirazha in Katapesh was a waypoint for weary travelers, then an mysterious disaster struck the town. Mirazha was deserted and travelers found the bodies of the townsfolk in the oasis on the edge of town. The bodies were desiccated and withered, all the moisture seemingly drained. For a time, Mirazha stood abandoned. Eventually the desert sands covered over the town, the oasis dried up, and the town was forgotten. Recently, an evil has revived the memory of Mirazha and its doomed inhabitants. Travelers report seeing a little town on the edge of a sparkling oasis, but if someone enters the cursed town, the town and the new victim vanish from sight. Adventurers in the area have yet to spot the cursed town, but sometimes they find the mummified bodies of the victims, buried in the sand.

Ulduvai.title: Ulduvai
Ulduvai.extract:
Ulduvai was the last of the Shory flying cities in Garund and the Inner Sea region. In a misguided effort to restore the Shory Empire, the sorcerers of Ulduvai consorted with the powers of the Dark Tapestry, which ended up with the entire population being driven mad by a shoggoth servant of Azathoth. The city crashed upon Mirrshock Glacier in the southeastern Mwangi Expanse, south of Mount Dowama in the Shattered Range in -507 AR.

Three ancient Shory sorcerers survived to the present, albeit as undead creatures: Yakmar, the insane adventurer-mage; Yithdul, the arcane diplomat; and Banderak, former head of the city's aeromantic corps.

Tumen.title: Tumen
Tumen.extract:
Tumen was once Osirion's largest city and briefly its capital. Located 75 miles from Sothis in the eastern Underdunes far from the River Sphinx, the city relied upon pure water drawn forth "by the grace of the Opaline Satrap whom the Radiant Pharaoh had favored, and in turn was favored by", which contemporary Osirionologists speculate, referred to a massive aquifer or portal to the elemental planes. The city's water supply began to ebb shortly before the deaths of the Four Pharaohs of Ascension, and the city descended to abandonment and an endless cycle of burial in the khamsin sandstorms. The lifeblood of the city was the geyser that erupted from a vertical crater in the mountain, providing its inhabitants with drinking water. The pharaohs used a figurine-shaped artifact, imbued with magic, to make the geyser erupt, though it became inactive after their deaths. The artifact possesses its own consciousness, and if it were to be reactivated, it could once again make life easier in the ruined city, though it would likely begin plotting its own schemes now that its masters are gone.

Despite its remote location, the city remains a popular destination for scholars and treasure hunters.

Czarny Las.title: Czarny Las
Czarny Las.extract: The small town of Czarny Las sprang up around the Bloodstone Conservatory in the middle of the of the Irrisen province of Feyfrost. The town and school sit atop a conjunction of arcane energy.

Shotep-Kara.title: Shotep-Kara
Shotep-Kara.extract:
Shotep-Kara on the Scorpion Coast of Osirion was a military and commercial hub connecting Tumen to the Obari Ocean. The port city lost its prominent role after the death of the Four Pharaohs and the ruin of Tumen. A number of earthquakes and tsunamis finally drew the city into the sea in 1687 AR.

Shotep-Kara's ancient monuments and temples are now part of the underwater ruins, and the place remains imbued with powerful magic. At the height of Four Pharaohs' reign, conjuration magic connected the port to the far corners of the empire and beyond. The magic is believed to persist to this day, though in wildly unstable form. Oceanic horrors and unquiet spirits infest the waters, but Shotep-Kara might still offer magical passage to faraway places, or even to other planes.

Jawafeeq.title: Jawafeeq
Jawafeeq.extract: Jawafeeq is a city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.

Sukri.title: Sukri
Sukri.extract: Sukri is a city on the Alavah Peninsula of Qadira.

Avrossi.title: Avrossi
Avrossi.extract: Avrossi is a hamlet located 50 miles south of Mechitar, filled with estates granted to loyal servants of Gebbite leaders. From this unassuming place, Avrossi's mayor Furghol Rennold has been plotting against his own family for decades. Its population, consisting primarily of human wizards and clerics of Urgathoa as well as bodak, mohrg and ghoul wizards, employs mindless skeletons and zombie as field workers, and regularly receives human chattel from Corpseblight.

Jungle Runners.title: Jungle Runners
Jungle Runners.extract: Jungle Runners is a shop where Erandlon and Bujkis Tallwoman offer their services as guides for explorers or nobles who wish to venture into the jungle, whether to find a ruin or bring a dinosaur skull back home. No one knows their way around the dangerous inland jungle better than they do.

Obari Imports.title: Obari Imports
Obari Imports.extract: Obari Imports is located in the center of Mainmast, offering goods from all over the world. Its owner, Sanjeet Ralama Pakhesh, comes from Jalmeray and has connections with Absalom, Katapesh, Nex, Vudra, and even Tian Xia. Custom orders can be placed for a fee, and he is always protected by his Vudrani monk bodyguards.

Holvirgang.title: Holvirgang
Holvirgang.extract: The frost giant town of Holvirgang is located in the far north of the witch realm of Irrisen, hard against the icy tundra of the Crown of the World. Built into the side of a glacier, the town is composed of an outer walled bailey open to all, and the caverns of Holvir carved into the ice, where only the frost giants are allowed.

Sojourner's Rest.title: Sojourner's Rest
Sojourner's Rest.extract: Sojourner's Rest is a village in the southern Grungir Forest of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, situated in the foundations of Thornwall Castle.

Tomgruv.title: Tomgruv
Tomgruv.extract:
Tomgruv is located to the south of the Grungir Forest and to the north-east of Jol (the capital of Southmoor) in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Today, it is officially ruled by Southmoor's Linnorm King Opir Eightfingers, but the local leaders will take control when they feel they can.

The town is on the road between Jol and the Kopparberget mine. It is built around the entrance to an old abandoned mine. The locals who are mainly farmers and herders use the mine as a stronghold and storage facility. A few miners work on meager copper veins in the surrounding hills.

Kobolds and other underground enemies dwell in the lower levels of the Tomgruv's abandoned mine but so far the residents have managed to keep the upper levels safe. Troll raiders find nothing worth outside the heavily fortified mine entrance.

Justnoque.title: Justnoque
Justnoque.extract: Justnoque is a sunken city underneath the Steaming Sea, off the coast of Varisia and the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. In the times of ancient Thassilon, Justnoque was a center of learning and metallurgy in the kingdom of Cyrusian, but it now lies ruined under the waves; only about a dozen slick black stones jutting from the water mark its presence. The sunken city is feared by the Linnorm Kings, but adventurers sometimes set sail from the Linnorm King city of Jol to explore Justnoque; the few that return tell tales of aquatic giants inhabiting the ruins.

Xin-Cyrusian.title: Xin-Cyrusian
Xin-Cyrusian.extract:
Xin-Cyrusian was the capital of Cyrusian, the Thassilonian realm of pride, and that empire's largest city. It was said to have been protected by legions of bound angels, dragons, and enslaved giants. It was located in the western Kodar Mountains.

Most of this once magnificent city was destroyed during Earthfall but the wards of the core were so powerful that even when the city was buried in the Darklands most of it survived and with time denizens of the caverns and tunnels of this subterranean realm moved in. The only access from the surface is a crater that is filled with illusion magic and the region trolls aren't the most inclined in the magical arts to discover the city.

Belila.title: Belila
Belila.extract: Belila is a small Irriseni town of the Iceflow in the northwestern corner of the province of Bleakmarch. Much of the town recently converted to new faith known as the Way of Transcendent Bliss, much to the consternation of its ruler, the Baroness Frederyka.

Lachka.title: Lachka
Lachka.extract: The village of Lachka was founded in 4637 AR by Queen Elvanna as a place to banish her brutally stupid twenty-second daughter, Baroness Lachka.

Ysborg.title: Ysborg
Ysborg.extract:
Ysborg is located just below the snowline on the northern slopes of Mount Gnarlfang, in the western Kodar Mountains at the far south-eastern corner of the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor, where borders provide no real barrier to the eternal winter of neighboring Irrisen.

Ysborg, the "Steading of the Ice Trolls", is something between a town and a fortress, consisting of crude wooden buildings and hide tents, all sized for giant occupants, surrounded by a stout wooden palisade. The entire fortress is surrounded by a moat of nearly freezing water inhabited by numerous river drakes.

Frost trolls, ettins, ogres, and other giants come from all across the Linnorm Kingdoms to join the army of the Blue Jarl, a huge powerfully muscled frost troll with a giant battleaxe curved from magical ice.

The streets of Ysborg are dangerous, with brutal fights and fresh blood on the snow to be a common sight. The Blue Jarl's "Winter Palace" is one of Ysborg's few stone buildings. It is nothing more than a blocky pile of icy rocks but it is well-fortified. The throne of the Blue Jarl is made from carved mammoth tusks and is decorated with human skulls. The Blue Jarl's kennels, just outside the palace are filled with winter wolves who have sworn allegiance to the jarl and the Bear Pits host ferocious polar and grizzly bears armored and trained to fight.

The Blue Jarl is always accompanied by his loyal frost giant shield maidens, as well as by the huge, white-furred dire bear that serves as his mount.

Frost troll marauders from Ysborg frequently raid neighboring human settlements for slaves, or more often, fresh food, for frost trolls relish the taste of human flesh. The villages have appealed to Trollheim for aid but for now usually defend themselves or abandon their villages. Rumors hold that Irrisen has been courting Ysborg as an ally, but so far, the trolls remain independent.

Zharchovsk.title: Zharchovsk
Zharchovsk.extract: Zharchovsk is a small town in southern Irrisen, located at the end of a mountain pass near the southeastern border of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.

Glacier's Rest.title: Glacier's Rest
Glacier's Rest.extract: Glacier's Rest is a small town in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains of northwestern Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is five miles south of a long-abandoned dwarven fortress, which as of 4724 AR had been claimed by General Ordulf Bladecaller and named Crownhold.

Delmon's Glen.title: Delmon's Glen
Delmon's Glen.extract:
Delmon's Glen is a small gnome town located in the far east of the Grungir Forest, in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, at the point where the Summermelt River flows into the Thundering River; the town's current mayor is Bavakanka Winterlisp.

Delmon's Glen is located along the most important overland trade route that connects Jol and Varisia to the south with the northern lands. The town is unusually mundane for a gnome settlement. Most of its buildings are like Ulfen buildings except for their small size. A few larger buildings catering to visitors are located on the edge of the town, like a trading post and the popular Raven's Best inn.

The small town has had trouble in the past with monsters from the southeastern badlands, especially bulettes, and Bavakanka Winterlisp many times recruited visiting adventures to protect her town.

Losthome.title: Losthome
Losthome.extract: Losthome is an Ulfen logging town in the far east of the Grungir Forest in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Located on the banks of the Thundering River, though not actually within the Linnorm Kingdom of the Thanelands, Losthome is protected by Kalsgard. Its people, many of them descendants of refugees from the Winter War, constantly watch the border with Irrisen for signs of incursion, and provide sanctuary to anyone fleeing the realm of the Witch Queen.

Solskinn.title: Solskinn
Solskinn.extract:
Solskinn is a small town in the Linnorm Kingdom of Southmoor, located to the south of Grungir Forest and to the north-east of Southmoor's capital of Jol.

As of 4711 AR it was officially ruled by Southmoor's Linnorm King Opir Eightfingers, but local leaders would take control whenever they felt that they could. Pleasant during the day, the small town was under forced curfew at night, as anyone leaving their homes is abducted by the visiting shadows.

Eightfingers went missing after attempting an ill-fated series of raids against New Thassilon in 4719 AR, vacating his throne. His successor was Ostog the Unslain. The town's relationship with Ostog is unknown.

Whiterook.title: Whiterook
Whiterook.extract: Whiterook, sometimes denoted White Rook, is located in the east of Southmoor in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings hard against the border with Irrisen, from which nation the people of Whiterook are defended by their own fierce fighters and a magically-enhanced vine and birch wall. The settlement lies on the banks of the Thundering River.

Dalun.title: Dalun
Dalun.extract:
Located in the south of the Irriseni province of Wintercrux on the Thundering River, Dalun is a small trade town that does business with the outside world.

Though Dalun strongly encourages trade with the outside world, which is essential for Irrisen's survival, its diplomatic efforts are ruined because of the Irriseni raids launched from Redtooth and Saarbotten in the Verge to the west. Indeed, Dalun is derisively called 'Gentle Dalun' by the people of Saarbotten despite the fact that Dalun supplies most of Saarbotten's food.

As of 4713 AR, Baroness Nadya was the ruler of Dalun. She had tried to foster stable trading relations with Southmoor and Varisia to little success. Dalun was responsible for supplying Redtooth and Saarbotten with grain and other foodstuffs. Nadya's bloodthirsty sister, Urgalaena, baroness of Saarbotten, often sabotaged Nadya's diplomatic attempts. Lady Nadya's chief steward, Betrona Harcatha, was actually a spy for her sister Urgalaena.

Saarbotten.title: Saarbotten
Saarbotten.extract:
As of 4713 AR, Saarbotten was a border town on the Thundering River in the Irriseni province known as the Verge.✝︎

Saarboten is organized entirely toward the support of the raiding parties that cross the border south and west into the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The town hosts permanent armories, camps and warehouses. The Wintercrux town of Dalun has the responsibility to supply Saarbotten's food while Morozny and Vasterborg provide weapons and armor. Baroness Urgalaena is the bloodthirsty, enthusiastic ruler of the town, mocking publicly her sister Baroness Nadya of Dalun for her diplomatic efforts in order to improve trade with the Ulfen people. Baroness Urgalaena hosts the vicious "Crimson Tournaments" every week.

Saarbotten was opposite the lands of Southmoor and Ysborg to the south and the eastern part of the Grungir Forest to the west. The nearest Ulfen settlements are the village of Whiterook in Southmoor and Losthome in the Grungir Forest. Saarbotten is also close to the gnome village of Delmon's Glen in the forest.

As of 4719 AR, the region around Saarbotten was within the borders of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.✝︎

Sascha.title: Sascha
Sascha.extract:
For other meanings of "Sascha", please see Sascha (disambiguation).

Sascha, once called Ermalin, is a small Irriseni town in the Verge with a troubled past.

Three-Troll.title: Three-Troll
Three-Troll.extract: Three-Troll is a small town in the Irriseni province known as the Verge which holds a great number of ice trolls. Three-Troll began as a camp for ice trolls raiding the neighboring Grungir Forest and over time grew into a small town. Three rock troll statues stand in the town square; these were rock trolls petrified when they were caught in the sun during the Winter War and have since given the name to the town. The ruler of Three-Troll is Baroness Nedyalka but she prefers to stay in the duke's court and rarely visits the town leaving the ice trolls to govern themselves.

Saraby.title: Saraby
Saraby.extract:
Saraby is a small town on the Thundering River in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. It is a bustling peasant town which accepts a number of Varisian merchants that have the courage to travel the few treacherous passes across the Kodar Mountains into Irrisen. The brave merchants mainly deliver grain and foodstuff and are richly rewarded. Saraby is ruled by Baroness Zoja who carefully accounts the volumes of trading goods moving through her town. Much of the precious cargo moves on to Dalun to the west and eventually is delivered to the militant settlements and camps of the Verge. The rest, however, is sent east along a route known as the Saraby Trail.

The Saraby Trail to the east follows no visible roads. It follows the Thundering River until reaching the Kodar Mountains and then heads north towards Hope Lost. From Hope Lost some experienced groups head directly east to Sosulka fighting the beasts of the tundra and the fearful white-furred perytons that prowl the skies but most of the groups take one of the well traveled roads that meander through the Skala Foothills to Zlatomesto. There they join a heavily guarded caravan of ore and travel to Veshtak. Bendiks Poljarny and Velna Elishky are veterans of the Saraby Trail.

Naldak's Point.title: Naldak's Point
Naldak's Point.extract: Naldak's Point is an abandoned dwarven town near Dalun in Irrisen.

Eldentre.title: Eldentre
Eldentre.extract: The town of Eldentre can be found in the south-western corner of the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, on the banks of the Thundering River. The town is famous for the Lightning Oak, and the weapons that are made using the electrical energy from this tree.

Hellirinn.title: Hellirinn
Hellirinn.extract: The town of Hellirinn is located on the banks of the Rimeflow River a few miles downstream from Trollheim in the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is near a warm spring located in a cave that serves as an ancient Pharasmin shrine.

Helkgen.title: Helkgen
Helkgen.extract:
Helkgen is a small town on the shores of Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Bleakmarch.

The Ulfen residents of Helkgen are struggling to support the merchant ships hugging the shores of Glacier Lake as Jadwiga overlords demand, but also to collect sufficient tribute throughout the year to give to the unforgiving scrags of Scrag Deep.

As of 4713 AR, Helkgen's mayor was Valka Dagsdottir, who earned the ire of townsfolk while working hard to keep them safe from the scrags.

Ledenica.title: Ledenica
Ledenica.extract:
Ledenica is a small, yet busy town on the Marbleflow River in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold. The town is fortified against the wintry terrors originating in the Frozen Fog across the river.

Baroness Helka is the efficient ruler of the town and her stone keep is located in the center of Ledenica. The Baroness, convinced by her Jadwiga guard captain Vlad Omelinsky, hires mercenaries to deal with the threats from the Frozen Fog, rather than risk her soldiers. Vlad Omelinsky handles the contracts with the mercenaries and is notorious for cheating and filling his own coffers. The Baroness knows nothing about this and, if she were ever made aware, she would execute her captain.

Ledenica is famous for three of its inns: Natalya's Riverside Inn, the Spirit's Caress and the White Raven. The travellers to and from Whitethrone enjoy visiting these inns. The White Raven is owned and run by sisters Kira Brodovsky and Kaleena Brodovsky and is a secret meeting place for the underground anti-government movement called the Heralds of Summer's Return.

Riekamesto.title: Riekamesto
Riekamesto.extract:
Riekamesto is a small town on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Thronehold that has seen quite a bit of recent trouble.

As of 4713 AR, the Cold Sisters had recently discovered that Baroness Urszula and 13 of her councilors had been cheating Whitethrone by hiding tax revenues. Cold Sisters viciously killed these councilors by throwing them from the top floor of the Council House. Urszula was instead buried alive in a great brass coffin wrapped with bands of iron inscribed with strange sigils. They placed the coffin in the center of the town, and as of six months later Urszula's desperate cries and frantic pounding continued from within.

Sister Arminaela was temporarily made the town's administrator, and achieved hard work and discipline from the peasants and increased tax revenues.

Gojko.title: Gojko
Gojko.extract: Gojko is a tiny Irriseni village located directly on the border with the Lands of the Linnorm Kings in the province of the Verge.

Redtooth.title: Redtooth
Redtooth.extract:
For another meaning of "Redtooth", please see Redtooth (person).

Redtooth is the provincial capital of the Irriseni border province known as the Verge.

Zelen.title: Zelen
Zelen.extract: Zelen is a peasant settlement on the Rimeflow River in the westernmost Irriseni province known as the Verge. It handles merchant barges heading eastward to Algidheart or Whitethrone, or westward to Iceflow. Most river captains choose Zelen to sleep in a warm bed for at least one night. Baroness Pribuska is the ruler of Zelen. She was a paramour of Duke Arvanoff, and nourishes great hatred for Baroness Slavena of Skrata who has replaced her in the duke's affections in 4710 AR. The waters of the Rimeflow near Zelen are rich with fish and the town sends a large portion of its catch as far as south as Redtooth. The Vasterborg Run is a caravan of dogsleds led by Raisa Gevenovna and Zavid Menavich that makes the challenging monthly trip to Redtooth.

Vasterborg.title: Vasterborg
Vasterborg.extract: Vasterborg is a heavily fortified town in the Irriseni province known as the Verge. It is located close to the border with the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, and a large part of its population consists of soldiers, ice trolls, and winter wolves.

Zaplava.title: Zaplava
Zaplava.extract:
Zaplava is a little village on Rimeflow River just before reaching Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Wintercrux. Few travelers stop at the village, inhabited by as many as seven intermarried families. The miserable superstitious peasants live in fear of the dreaded Frozen Fog to the east. The fog's icy tendrils and the strange horrors that sometimes wander close to the village make the frightened peasants hide in their stone hovels. Arko Arkosson is the mayor of Zaplava.

Todora Navanya, a renowned adventurer in Avistan, comes from this tiny village.

Atvan.title: Atvan
Atvan.extract:
Atvan is a small, peasant town on Glacier Lake in the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch. It is ruled by Baroness Baelara, whose husband, Gregouri Urvalane, is a notorious womanizer. The couple spend a great deal of time arguing, which is what got them banished to this remote settlement from Whitethrone.

The young women of Atvan who attract Gregouri's amorous attentions became targets of the baroness' extravagant rage. To avoid the baroness's wrath, Vaclav Konstantin proprietor of the Sealskinner Inn, has closed down his brothel and employs only local grandmothers in the inn's common room.

Atvan main business is to provide shelter, food, and entertainment for merchants sailing to and from Whitethrone. It is also well known that Atvan has coveted its neighbor Trezira's ice cleaving monopoly.

Soduras.title: Soduras
Soduras.extract:
Soduras is a small fishing community on the Iceflow River, which forms the border between the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch and the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach. Due to its location, it is frequently used as a stopover and resupply point for raiding parties of ice trolls, witchwargs, or mercenary bands conducting cross-border raids.

As of 4713 AR, the town's ruler Baroness Vjera encourages the martial visitors and orders the townsfolk to provide housing, food, and entertainment to them. Soduras' north end houses a large number of inns, as well as fighting pits that cater to ice trolls and witchwargs. Human mercenary companies usually stay in the south end of the town and enjoy themselves at the brothels of Madame Plana Vectenstyn. Life is hard for Soduras' residents, but these hardships have also inspired tough adventurers.

Trezira.title: Trezira
Trezira.extract:
Trezira is a small town on the Frozen Road River in the eastern part of Irrisen's Bleakmarch province. It single distinguishing feature is that it was granted a monopoly on "ice cleaving" (the process of keeping a river navigable year-round) the Frozen Road in 4623 AR by Queen Elvanna. Nearly a hundred years later, fur-wrapped citizens of Trezira still patrol the river in their "breaker barges", using any means at their disposal to maintain a navigable channel through the ice.

The ruler of the town is Baroness Marcia who is not very oppressive of the peasants provided they do whatever is necessary to fulfill their obligations and especially do nothing to endanger the "ice cleaving" monopoly. Most successful of the seven "ice cleaving" companies is the White Stewards led by Trepek Gorny. Trezira is also the main trading partner of the frost giant hold of Holvirgang. The frost giant guests in town usually prefer the Horned Helm Inn run by the Ulfen Ludmil Arronsson, a giant of a man himself.

Yensa.title: Yensa
Yensa.extract:
The border town of Yensa lies on the Iceflow River, separating the Irrisen province of Bleakmarch from the Linnorm Kingdom of Hagreach. Its ruler, Countess Marcelyna, does her best to encourage trade between the two nations, even going so far as to bar entrance to the town to mercenary companies and other ruffians intent on conducting cross-border raids.

Countess carefully cultivates a friendly image is order to foster commerce but the Yensa's inhabitants know their mistress' cruel nature. None dare to reveal the truth to visitors who hear nothing but praise for the countess from her secretly brutalized subjects. The blacksmith Vanak Usztony lost his wife from Marcelyna's cruelty and he still does not speak a word of complain in order to protect his four children.

Kizobran.title: Kizobran
Kizobran.extract:
As of 4713 AR, Kizobran was a small town on the Gullik River in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood.✝︎ The town caters to merchant traffic on the river. Since a few years prior to 4713 AR, the ruler of the town was Baroness Veviane, who had quickly proven to be a harsh mistress for Kizobran by squeezing outrageous taxes from its peasants. She was also known to take a fancy to a man or woman, and when her carnal appetite was sated her victim was usually found frozen in a snow bank or floating in the Gullik River.

Baroness Veviane was in fact the offspring of a boreal green hag and a charmed Jadwiga man, substituted for Hedvika's real child by a midwife who was also in thrall to the hag. She murdered these "parents", Baroness Hedvika and Baron Gorney, by setting fire to their home, then succeeded them to the rulership of Kizobran.

The true daughter of Baroness Hedvika and Baron Gorney is Kataryne Vilimanov, an officer in the local peasant militia. Kataryne had recently discovered her true identity as of 4713 AR, and was secretly planning to pursue her revenge and restore her proper position in the aristocracy.

Sigvor Yovenovich was an assistant priest at the local temple of Pharasma. She was a member of the underground anti-government movement Heralds of Summer's Return and approached Kataryne in 4712 AR. Kataryne refused to join the Heralds, and Sigvor lives in fear that Kataryne might expose her.

As of 4719 AR, Kizobran was within the borders of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.✝︎

Badelund.title: Badelund
Badelund.extract:
Badelund is a well-fortified town on the Frozen Road, in the north of the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.

Badelund is surrounded by high walls to protect the small peasant town from uninvited fey visitors. Feyfrost fey are notorious child-stealers. Badelund is the first village on the Frozen Road west of Isseld and hosts a number of inns and taverns to serve the tired merchants sailing the Frozen Road. Baroness Pavlina is the ruler of the town. Because of her kindness and her love for a peasant, Pavlina was sent to Badelund as a punishment. The Baroness and her Ulfen husband Oddvar Pavelsson are wise governors and do their best to make peasants' life bearable. It is well known that Pavlina sends mercenary groups north to retrieve stolen children. Such groups are the Home Again Safe led by Helena Oukarnion and the White Dawn Hunters led by Mikhail Shalanov.

Baldachin.title: Baldachin
Baldachin.extract: Baldachin is a small town at the mouth of the Frozen Road on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.

Hagby.title: Hagby
Hagby.extract:
The small trading town of Hagby is located on Glacier Lake in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold.

Hagby is the last stop for all trade headed to Whitethrone from the west. It is ruled with paranoid scrutiny by the cruel Baroness Ladina. The peasants do not trust anyone knowing that even a hint of the slightest misdemeanor results in harsh punishments. A lot of peasants work their daily duties with missing fingers, eye patches and worse. Yefrem Chelnitsky is the despicable agent of the baroness. He follows her everywhere, with parchment and quill in hand to record every demand and directive his mistress utters.

Despite the overbearing atmosphere, the Heralds of Summer's Return have a lot of members in Hagby including warehouse worker Nakia Serebrynakova and blacksmith Yuri Patasil.

Skrata.title: Skrata
Skrata.extract: Skrata is a small town on the Rimeflow River, in the westernmost Irriseni province called the Verge that acts as a waystation of goods shipped to the capital of Whitethrone from the north, through the Iceflow River, and as a supply depot for the army. Skrata's docks are always busy, and its warehouses are full with goods destined for the army or the Rimeflow route.

Frostbreach.title: Frostbreach
Frostbreach.extract: Frostbreach is a small but self-sufficient town in the northern Lands of the Linnorm Kings, on the Path of Aganhei. Once a more prosperous settlement, its population has since shrunk to about a third of its peak. It was the site of the Jarlsblood Witch Trial in centuries past, in which the prospective Linnorm King Kolvi killed the reigning Alvard the Wolf in claimed self-defense, but was accused of being an Irriseni witch and executed.

Temple of the Endless Step.title: Temple of the Endless Step
Temple of the Endless Step.extract:
The Temple of the Endless Step is a non-denominational monastery located atop the Storval Rise in the Varisian city of Kaer Maga.

Founded by the philosopher-monk Yen Hazai, it is located in the Cavalcade district, on the outer edge of the city walls. A highly ascetic order, the monks of worship no deity, but instead believe in purification of the body through the mortification of the flesh. To this end they engage not only in rigorous martial training and meditation, but also in self-flagellation. This has led students to be referred to as Brothers of the Lash, a name which the monks now use themselves.

Because of their mortification practices, outsiders sometimes mistake the brothers for worshipers of Zon-Kuthon, or Skin Artists from the Oriat district. This greatly upsets the monks, as they see their flagellation as a path to enlightenment, not as worship of a god or the pursuit of artistic expression. Even though their conservative opinions on this subject have led them to be unpopular in the Cavalcade district, they are not unwelcoming to newcomers seeking true spiritual growth.

Monks of the temple who believe they have achieved true enlightenment sometimes seek to emulate their founder, Yen Hazai. It is recorded that Hazai voluntarily stepped out of a window overlooking the Rise and simply touched the edge of the cliff as he fell to slow his descent. Many have tried to duplicate his feat, but none so far has succeeded.

Bosorka.title: Bosorka
Bosorka.extract: The small town of Bosorka is located on the Frozen Road River, in the southeast corner of Irrisen's Feyfrost province. As with many towns along the Frozen Road River its main purpose is to assist those conveying goods and traveling down the river. It is located within sight of the border with the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, and therefore of a land not eternally dominated by the ravages of winter. No non-Jadwiga are allowed to cross the border out of Irrisen and the Jadwiga guards of Bosorka have to be ever vigilant with the border so plainly visible and enticing.

Isseld.title: Isseld
Isseld.extract: Isseld is a minor town on the Frozen Road in Irrisen's Feyfrost province where most people earn their living from the trade on the river. The settlement has recently become known as the center of the trade in "primitive wares", mostly carved fetishes and talismans crafted by the Kellid barbarians of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords.

Coldwater.title: Coldwater
Coldwater.extract:
Coldwater is a small town on the western bank of the Gullik River in the Irriseni capital province of Thronehold. Baroness Wilimina is the ruler of the town.

The peasants of Coldwater look with envy the changing seasons in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords but no peasant is allowed to cross the river. They have to work the docks and house and entertain the merchants traveling up and down the Gullik River. Some young and rebellious Jadwiga have formed a stilyagi gang and call themselves the Lords of the Tusk. They are led by Paval Turosky, a foolish youth, and enjoy crossing the river and imitate the Kellid barbarians. On some occasions the Lords wander deep into the foreign lands and they say they want to forage deeper into the wilds to hunt bison and the prehistoric animals that roam those lands.

Quarrimac's Curatives.title: Quarrimac's Curatives
Quarrimac's Curatives.extract: Quarrimac's Curatives is a well-known and respected apothecary and alchemist's shop located in the Bis district of the Varisian city of Kaer Maga. Its proprietor, Kylos Quarrimac, is a former adventurer who can usually be found working in the shop coming up with new salves and tinctures or asleep on one of his workbenches. Shoplifters long ago discovered that despite his age, Kylos has lost none of his quickness.

Tashagrot.title: Tashagrot
Tashagrot.extract: The ruins of Tashagrot are all that remains of the capital of the former Irriseni province of Rimetusk, wiped out by predators coming from the Realm of the Mammoth Lords that were eager to regain their old territories. It is said that the site, which lies on the eastern bank of the Gullik River south of Coldwater, is haunted by its massacred residents.

Flickering Tower.title: Flickering Tower
Flickering Tower.extract: The Flickering Tower is the residence of a wizard who has never been seen in the city. Its previous owners sold it in 4645 AR, but although they were compensated for the sale, they don't remember to whom they sold it. Strange lights are seen inside, and opaque windows block the sunlight and view of the interior. The tower sometimes disappears for months or years, returning either newer or older than before, and those who have sought to uncover its mysteries and enter are later found in the markets, either older or younger, with no memory of their attempts to enter the tower.

Cliffside Millworks.title: Cliffside Millworks
Cliffside Millworks.extract: Cliffside Millworks is a factory that has abandoned grain production and focused on ambitious machine part manufacturing projects. Complex clockwork designs allow the factory to create most of its parts without human intervention, and its quality rivals that of Korvosa. The owner is Jerich Ableton, although Obo, the former slave, is the true mastermind behind the factory's designs.

Heaven's Ladder.title: Heaven's Ladder
Heaven's Ladder.extract: Heaven's Ladder is a crematorium that offers an alternative to Godsmouth Ossuary, providing services at various prices to prevent bodies from ending up in the streets. Owned by Geph Hinsmen, it allows the families of the deceased to decide on funeral rites and priests before placing them in the crematorium.

Therassic Spire.title: Therassic Spire
Therassic Spire.extract:
The Therassic Spire, also known as the Great Library, is one of the oldest repositories of knowledge in all of Varisia. A cabal of librarians who worship Nethys, Irori and the Peacock Spirit cares for the tomes within, some of which are illegible due to their age. The structure rises six stories above and below, and because no knowledge is forbidden here, mages, paladins, and thieves pay handsomely for access to these books and the power they may contain.

In recent years, the librarians have made contact with the caulborns of Xavorax, with whom they exchange information and maintain a relationship of mutual interest. In 4712 AR, the librarians sent mercenaries in search of the Shard of Gluttony, but after losing contact with them, they had to ask the Pathfinder Society for help in resolving the matter discreetly.

Kaer Maga.title: Kaer Maga
Kaer Maga.extract: Standing atop one of the highest spots of the Storval Rise, the cliff-top city of Kaer Maga (pronounced kare MAH-gah) is built inside the ruins of an ancient fortress: a six-sided ring of 80-foot-high seamless stone, stretching more than a half mile in diameter and topped with towers of every shape and design. Also known as the Asylum Stone, Kaer Maga has served as a refuge for exiles, misfits, and ne'er-do-wells fleeing persecution and prosecution for thousands of years, and is known throughout Golarion as a place where anyone can fit in, and where anything can be bought and sold.

Last Rites.title: Last Rites
Last Rites.extract:
Last Rites is a store in the Ankar-Te Ring district of the Varisian city of Kaer Maga. It is owned and run by Horus Ilaktya, who also oversees this unusual neighborhood with High Priestess Shamalay Kasan. Horus is a talented necromancer, and Last Rites is a supplier of custom-created undead for use as menial servants, mounts, and laborers. He prefers to refer to his merchandise as "Twice-Born", rather than undead, and thinks there is nothing wrong with his chosen profession. Since his creations are usually homicidal after being imbued with unlife, Horus has created special control-amulets known as death's head talisman, which allow the purchaser full control over his "reborn" minions.

Horus is always looking for fresh "raw materials" for his work, and will readily purchase fresh corpses. However, he is careful not to be seen to encourage grave-robbing, so he always checks the provenance of the bodies he buys.

Augur Temple.title: Augur Temple
Augur Temple.extract: The Augur Temple is the headquarters of the Augurs, a group of civilized trolls who perform divination and fortune-telling by supposedly reading the future in their own entrails: a grim, self-haruspicy.

Gadka's Magical Oddities.title: Gadka's Magical Oddities
Gadka's Magical Oddities.extract: Gadka's Magical Oddities is a business in the Tarheel Promenade district of the Varisian city of Kaer Maga. Owned and run by the dwarf Gadka Burtannon, the shop is actually just a covered cart which is semi-permanently parked on the corner of Fever Street and Half-a-Chicken Walk. It deals in minor magical trinkets, although Gadka will readily admit that many of his charms are worthless baubles with which to fool the gullible. Perhaps because of this, his prices on genuine magical items is quite reasonable. Potential customers, however, are warned to keep their wits about them while dealing with the dwarf, for he is willing to do nearly anything to turn a profit.

Reanimations.title: Reanimations
Reanimations.extract: Reanimations is a store in the Ankar-Te Ring district of the Varisian city of Kaer Maga. It is owned and run by Gerik Mubb, and sells mindless undead to customers, who control them via death's head talismans. The shop also sells potions and other clerical services, which are generally of a higher standard than the undead.

Duskwarden Guildhouse.title: Duskwarden Guildhouse
Duskwarden Guildhouse.extract: The headquarters of the Duskwardens is the Duskwarden Guildhouse: a squat building in the district of Bis marked with a placard of the Duskwardens' Twisted Door badge. Within are a few offices for ranking officials of the group, barracks for new recruits (or any member with no other place to sleep), workshops, and a modest store of equipment.

Sorry Excuse.title: Sorry Excuse
Sorry Excuse.extract: The Sorry Excuse is a cheap inn outside the Twisted Door in the Hospice district of Kaer Maga in Varisia.

Canary House.title: Canary House
Canary House.extract: The Canary House is a rather exclusive inn in Kaer Maga, owned by Alaeh A'kaan, who wields considerable influence in the area. The establishment only admits the wealthiest of guests, and those whose attire displeases Alaeh are turned away. Expensive bird meat is the main delicacy here, and several murals of Garundi birds adorn the walls. Hanging from the ceiling are cages with golden bars where "Songbirds", slave girls who sing and dance, hop from cage to cage for the enjoyment of the guests below.

Pillars of Dream.title: Pillars of Dream
Pillars of Dream.extract: The Pillars of Dream is a strange monument associated with dreams, capable of putting anyone who touches them to sleep. The pillars float thirty feet in the air and are made of an unrecognizable, strange metal. Those who look through the center between the two pillars see a night sky that doesn't appear to be that of Golarion, its shape constantly shifting and composed of various stars and galaxies.

Gap.title: Gap
Gap.extract: The Gap is a massive opening in the wall of The Warren, considered the city's main gateway. Dozens of merchants and guides offer their services to travelers, and the city employs guards to protect against the district's more undesirable elements, though they allow pickpockets to do as they please.

Immaculate Repository.title: Immaculate Repository
Immaculate Repository.extract: The Immaculate Repository is a highly visible temple of Abadar in the Dawn Gate district of Katapesh. It is packed with merchant stalls and tents, each of which hangs a decorative key in the front as homage to the Master of the First Vault. They also keep a tithe box, into which customers are expected to drop spare change following a transaction, and from which the clergy collects to fund the daily routines of the temple. Interestingly, none of the merchants take security precautions against theft from the tithe, as few would be foolish enough to rob a tithe box of Abadar in Katapesh, the city of merchants.

Gilded Shell.title: Gilded Shell
Gilded Shell.extract:
The Gilded Shell is a gigantic tortoise-shaped building situated in the Dawn Gate district of Katapesh. The surface of the building is covered with gleaming metal plates, designed and installed by a master armorer named Malthus Feyhew.

Feyhew and his staff of apprentices and journeymen craft high-quality and greatly valued suits of armor, all of masterwork quality. The master armorer tasks special interest in unusual materials, and keeps a standing offer to his clients to build custom armor from such materials at a 50-percent discount. He also keeps an inventory of special and unusual armors, including giant scorpion shells, blink dog hides, gorgon hides, gray render hides, Naga scales, bone mail, and wyvern hides.

Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation.title: Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company
Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation.extract:
A nondescript building in the city of Katapesh serves as the office of Truther & Trathis Mining and Excavation Company, a mining and excavation enterprise maintained by two dwarves, Gravlet Truther and Kip Trathis. The pair are notoriously spendthrift with their money and refuse to pay anyone else to do work that they can't do themselves.

The company usually has a variety of operations in process at any given time, and are working on reopening the Zolurket Mines despite the rumors of undead there, and excavating the region around the Crouching Jackal. They claim to have come across an ancient scroll depicting other structures around the statue, and think that their excavation efforts will reveal other lost structures buried beneath the sand.

The truth behind the Zolurket Mines operation is that it is, in fact, haunted by the two dwarves' dead business partner, Tibbler Rocksledge, abandoned decades ago to a mummy attack when the trio was first exploring the mines. The ghost of Tibbler haunts the mines in anticipation of the return of Truther and Trathis, who have returned several times searching for rich platinum deposits buried there.

The current mining operation is their most ambitious to date: they've employed diggers to tunnel through the rock around their ghostly foe, but the work goes slowly and the two dwarves are growing increasingly frustrated. They are ready to hire a group of specialize ghost hunters to take out Tibbler permanently, despite the extra costs and hassle involved in employing adventurers. What they haven't realized is that the only way to permanently destroy the ghost is to satisfy its need to bring the pair to justice.

Golden Scarab.title: Golden Scarab
Golden Scarab.extract:
The Golden Scarab is a grand and magnificent building, residing in the shadows of the Dockside Giant in Katapesh. The walls of the Golden Scarab are made of alabaster and speckled with sparking seashells. Gold painted scarabs make their way across its surface, giving the building its name. The Scarab is perhaps the most famous gambling hall in the city, and its owner, Krebble-Jeggle adds new games to the hall at a breakneck pace. The selection of games includes roulette, cards, darts, dice games, and more exotic (and disreputable) games can be found upstairs in the Upper Hall.

The basement of the building is the home of the bloodiest games in all of Golarion: Mig-a-Mug-Tug, Hatch-a-Catch, Knivesies, and Killgull for example. Games involving undead are common as well; Zombie Melee, for example, is a "to the death" game where necromancers pit teams of zombies against each other.

Interestingly, there are no pit fights or duels to be found in the basement of the Golden Scarab. This is because the Pactmasters have granted the Guild of Gladiators sole rights to these sorts of sports.

Smoking Ruin.title: Smoking Ruin
Smoking Ruin.extract:
The Smoking Ruin is a unusual sight in the otherwise gilded and wealthy region of Katapesh's Dawn Gate: a smoking ruin of ash and burnt out buildings, once the site of businesses and homes. According to rumor, one night a portal opened up to the Elemental Plane of Fire, and through it a host of angry fire elementals poured. They destroyed the area, killing everyone and everything in the region. Locals have since referred to that night as the "Dawn Gate Apocalypse", and are quick to avoid the area, as the restless souls of the victims of the event haunt the ruin.

Businessmen and residents near the area have pooled together a fund to sponsor a brave group of adventurers to put to rest these poor wailing souls by finding and burying their bones, but thus fair none have been brave enough to take them up on it.

Dockside Giant.title: Dockside Giant
Dockside Giant.extract:
The Dockside Giant is a massive copper statue located in Katapesh's Docks district that depicts a bearded man with horns and a large trident staring menacingly out to sea. The statue has been there since at least 2589 AR and is seen by the locals as a ward against bad weather and bad luck. The locals must constantly battle against the elements to keep the statue clean and decent.

There are various theories as to who or what the statue depicts. Some believe it depicts an ancient hero, while others believe it is a now-dead deity of the sea. Whatever it depicts, the Dockside Giant has become a landmark of the Docks.

Succoring Muse.title: Succoring Muse
Succoring Muse.extract: The Succoring Muse is a drug den and flop house in Kaer Maga's Oriat district, run by the Sczarni Parfus Erigol. It offers a wide assortment of recreational drugs, and a place to use them out of the public eye. To this end, Erigol offers a range of price options for short-term stays, ranging from extravagant and luxurious accommodations, catering to the client's every need, to dingy floprooms for those on the lower end of the economic spectrum. Erigol's larger-than-life persona and reasonable prices make him a favorite of local students and artists looking for a short escape from their troubles. Young women are generally advised not to visit the Succoring Muse by themselves, as the owner is not above renting out his customers by the hour while they are incapacitated by his narcotic fare.

Kallow Mounds.title: Kallow Mounds
Kallow Mounds.extract: The Kallow Mounds are the burial grounds of the Shoanti clan known as the Skoan-Quah, and are located on the Storval Plateau approximately 50 miles east of Kaer Maga.

Lyceum.title: Lyceum
Lyceum.extract: The Lyceum is a college for bards and entertainers that produces the finest in Varisia, though Magnimar and Korvosa never acknowledge this. The college's egalitarian policy was established by its founder, Tharius Giovée, and his great-grandson, Thaddeus Giovée, continues the practice. The college houses the largest altar to Shelyn in the city and is a popular meeting place for lovers.

Ivory Wands.title: Ivory Wands
Ivory Wands.extract: The Ivory Wands is a series of tents that form a bazaar run by a family of wizards renowned for creating magical wands. This family is quite paranoid, and their shop contains multiple traps for any thieves who dare to try to steal from them.

Red Pyramid.title: Red Pyramid
Red Pyramid.extract: A prominent landmark within the Twilight Gate area of the city of Katapesh, the Red Pyramid is an ancient structure that dates back to the "Golden City" age of Katapesh. In recent decades, it also served as the headquarters of the Scarlet Triad.

River Inn.title: River Inn
River Inn.extract: The River Inn is a prominent building in the Sprawl district of Katapesh. It was built in 3748 AR aiming to attract a higher cast of locals and visitors. It is a three-story building made of river stones and mortar with strong timber beams. The lanterns that flank its wide doors are lit with magical flames and the interior is suitable for nobles and rich merchants. Unfortunately rich merchants and nobles were never arrived in the district and the ownership of the River Inn has changed hands many times since its construction. The River Inn is still a magnificent building and has some of the best rooms in the city of Katapesh. Marvius Anquen, the current owner, works hard to keep the inn in good condition. Caravans, laborers, and adventurers are the most common customers. They enjoy having a nice place to stay without actually entering the city. A variety of fresh sea fish or river fish from River Scorpius, caught by Marvius's son Eldron, is always in River Inn's menu.

Rabid Dog.title: Rabid Dog
Rabid Dog.extract: The Rabid Dog is a tall, ramshackle tavern that sits amidst warehouses and mining offices east of the road through the Dawn Gate in Katapesh. Its owner is an old kholo named Moss Pelt who caters to kholo visitors and locals. The food served is distinctly "kholoish" in nature. The Rabid Dog is a dangerous place to frequent but it is said that a kholo named Shank Whitelock who spends much of his time in the Rabid Dog, is one of the greatest guides in the city. For a fee of 5 gp per day Shank can guide his clients to just about anywhere they want to go from Katapesh all the way to Solku.

Castle Clarion.title: Castle Clarion
Castle Clarion.extract:
This article is about the castle in Katapesh. For the district in Katapesh, see Castle Clarion (district).

Castle Clarion was built in Katapesh by Oslynn Clarion, a now-retired explorer, about 4600 AR. A small set of docks, positioned under the cliffs of Castle Clarion, are used by the Clarion family for any shipping they may need to do and can hold nearly all of the Clarion fleet of ships (nearly a dozen blacksailed vessels). The castle, made up of slim towers that rise high above the castle, are connected by covered bridges. The sparkling walls and red roofs of Castle Clarion make it an easy landmark to spot from land or sea. Lord Clarion is loyal to the government of Katapesh and dutifully adheres to all of the city's laws and requests. He is also one of the city's largest merchant of slaves.

Despite its elegance and lack of fortifications, the castle is well defended against Lord Clarion's many enemies, most notably agents of the Gray Corsairs. Traps riddle the castle and his servants include veteran warriors and mages. Also, due to Lord Crarion's ties with the Pactmasters, several aluum guard his property.

Cliffside Tavern.title: Cliffside Tavern
Cliffside Tavern.extract: The Cliffside Tavern in the Castle Clarion district of Katapesh is perched at the edge of the cliffs overlooking the ocean. It is a popular tavern for locals and a romantic spot for couples. Azhmeer Murlawden is the owner of the tavern who often entertains his customers playing soft music on his dulcimer.

The Grand Alchemist's Tower.title: The Grand Alchemist's Tower
The Grand Alchemist's Tower.extract: The Grand Alchemist's Tower is located in one of the towers of the Twilight Gate, and is the residence of the most influential and powerful alchemist in the entire city.

Ruined Monastery.title: Ruined Monastery
Ruined Monastery.extract: The Ruined Monastery is an abandoned temple dedicated to Lamashtu in Katapesh. It was built during the reign of the kholos, though it is now in disrepair and is only visited for its eerie atmosphere as a place of worship for this ill-fated goddess. Several guides offer tours of the building for a small fee.

Gladiators' Guildhall.title: Gladiators' Guildhall
Gladiators' Guildhall.extract: The Gladiators' Guildhall is a structure of bone and hide where the city's gladiators train with strange weapons and in unique styles, practicing day and night. Battles are held constantly, with fierce stakes and high mortality rates.

Charred Meats.title: Charred Meats
Charred Meats.extract: The Charred Meats is a butcher shop located on the edge of Dog Town, selling scraps of meat from other butcher shops, which the owner, Chargut, sells to fishermen and farmers. The establishment's secret business is selling corpses, with humanoids fetching the most money, and several disguised undead guard the shop at all times.

Jerg's Repair Shop.title: Jerg's Repair Shop
Jerg's Repair Shop.extract: Jerg's Repair Shop is a repair shop with a pier on the River Scorpius due to the high volume of traffic it receives daily. Retired adventurer Jerg Alshelner runs the shop, using his adventure funds and magic to repair items and vehicles brought to him.

Katapesh Lodge.title: Katapesh Lodge
Katapesh Lodge.extract:
The Katapesh Lodge, the Pathfinder Society's local base of operations in the city of Katapesh, is an elegant three-tiered building located among cyclopean ruins and other ancient marvels in the Twilight Gate district. Venture-Captain Aurora Steelbloom oversees the operations of several teams of Pathfinders.

The lodge maintains a library full of field agents' chapbooks, historical treatises, maps, rare tomes, and other items. The Pactmasters agreed to post two aluum outside the lodge for its protection. The building itself is thoroughly warded against any intrusion.

There are three other lodges in Katapesh: Farseer Tower Lodge, Kotargo Lodge, and the Winding Road Lodge.

College of Dimensional Studies.title: College of Dimensional Studies
College of Dimensional Studies.extract:
One of the many magical fraternities that make their home in the city of Katapesh, the College of Dimensional Studies specializes in magic relating to interdimensional travel or visiting truly distant parts of the Universe. The domed building of the college is located in the crowded Inner City district of Katapesh and contains not only the normal dormitories and laboratories expected of a wizards' college, but also a huge telescope open even for the general public's use on some nights. The College of Dimensional Studies is also, therefore, one of the foremost schools of astronomy in the Inner Sea region.

One of the most unique features of the college is its lack of doors. All students are expected to possess an array of travel magic and are expected to be able to teleport between rooms. Students of the college are also expected to create scrolls of transport magic as part of their payment for the college's unique education. The College of Dimensional Studies is also one of the few institutions on Golarion to house one of the rare and mysterious astralabes, devices of enormous utility to those seeking to explore the planes of the Great Beyond.

Grand Coliseum.title: Grand Coliseum
Grand Coliseum.extract:
Nestled in Katapesh's Inner City district, the Grand Coliseum is one of Golarion's largest arenas, capable of comfortably housing 10,000 spectators. The arena is home to many forms of entertainment, from parades to gladiatorial bouts, but by far the most popular is a ball game called ruk.

Many of Katapesh's guilds field, fund, and promote highly competitive ruk teams, and many former players return to the Coliseum to sell their services as trainers and coaches.

Ahkmed's Carpets.title: Ahkmed's Carpets
Ahkmed's Carpets.extract:
Ahkmed's Carpets is a shop in the Lower City's bazaar of the city of Katapesh. The rugs and tapestries of the shop are famous all over the world. The area of the shop is a maze of hanging rugs, tapestries, sleeping pallets, and other woven creations. The owner of the shop, Ahkmed is a high-ranking member of Katapesh's Guild of Weavers. He enjoys roaming his woven lair each day, serving the serious buyers.

An intelligent flying carpet named Cloud Cutter has hidden itself here among the other carpets. It is trying to evade his previous owner, a kholo assassin known as Spider.

Azure Star.title: Azure Star
Azure Star.extract: Azure Star is a popular restaurant deep in the Lower City district of Katapesh. From the outside it is a large gray tent covered in sky-blue stars. Inside the tent, private rooms are formed using silk partitions, and all rooms surround a large open common room with overlapping carpets covering the floor. Patrons lounge around small tables, on soft comfortable pillows. Musicians and singers entertain the guests who come to taste the exotic cuisine served there such as honeyed loci, curdled camel milk, pesh-sprinkled dhabba liver, pickled scorpion, grilled adder, thrice-poisoned oysters, and others. A bound air elemental creates a soft breeze to comfort Azure Star's guests.

Council Hall and Gaol Block.title: Council Hall and Gaol Block
Council Hall and Gaol Block.extract: The Council Hall and Gaol Block in the city of Katapesh is a walled compound standing atop a low bluff overlooking the Lower City. It was known as Aktep Keep and was the estate of Massein Aktep an extremely wealthy businessman in Katapesh. When he was involved in a huge scandal, in 4701 AR, implicating him for tax evasion, fraud, and murdering of several prominent guildmasters, the Pactmasters seized all of his holdings and turned his expansive personal dungeon into the city's new gaol, where he is currently imprisoned. His estate has been converted into the Council Hall where the city's guildmasters and other officials meet. It is also houses the barracks for the Zephyr Guard.

Cindra's Exotic Pets.title: Cindra's Exotic Pets
Cindra's Exotic Pets.extract: Cindra's Exotic Pets is a shop that promises to sell the most diverse animal species from the Inner Sea, including monkeys from the Mwangi Expanse, dinosaurs from Mediogalti Island, snakes from the Mana Wastes, crocodiles from Osirion, toads from the Sodden Lands, white ravens from Irrisen, wolves and foxes from Nirmathas, and much more.

The Doomsayers' Stand.title: The Doomsayers' Stand
The Doomsayers' Stand.extract: The Doomsayers' Stand is a stone dais amidst the maze of tents in the Lower City, once used by musicians to entertain. Starting in 4707 AR, various preachers of strange cults and faiths began using it to attract new followers to their mysterious dogmas, and the resulting clashes between these faiths have forced the Zephyr Guard to post guards around it at all times.

Jangley's Shop.title: Jangley's Shop
Jangley's Shop.extract: Jangley's Shop is a store run by the halfling brothers Pasker and Trill Jangley, who sell all sorts of curiosities. They use their extensive network of contacts to update their inventory daily, acquiring a wide variety of items at varying prices.

Sisters of the Quill.title: Sisters of the Quill
Sisters of the Quill.extract: The Sisters of the Quill is a congregation of women dedicated to preserving the written word, living in this two-story building. They are hired to translate books into other languages, and the business is run by Sister Masequa.

Marble Sphinx.title: Marble Sphinx
Marble Sphinx.extract:
The titanic statue of the Marble Sphinx in the city of Katapesh is 120 feet tall. It depicts a sitting creature with the body of a lioness and the head of a long-haired female human with sharp tusks jutting up from the corners of her mouth. The statue is not made from marble, but rather from sandstone.

When the first settlers arrived to Katapesh, they discovered this enormous statue of a sphinx sitting as if it was guarding the valley. Those settlers built the ancient Golden City at this site in 2218 AR.

Sand Dragon Inn.title: Sand Dragon Inn
Sand Dragon Inn.extract: The Sand Dragon Inn sits along the southern side of the Golden Oasis. Travelers often stop here before exploring the city of Katapesh further on. A serpentine dragon perches above the main entrance of the inn.

Jerikal's Ales.title: Jerikal's Ales
Jerikal's Ales.extract: Jerikal's Ales is a cantina and brewery located in the Docks of Katapesh city and is uniquely run by a cleric of Shelyn called Jerikal Ashworth who has picked brewing as his chosen medium. The interior is dedicated to Shelyn: filled with beautiful statuary, fresh flowers and other beautifications that his patrons can enjoy along with his unique brews.
