Despite being run by a worshipper of Shelyn, it is like many traditional breweries, a site of worship for followers of Cayden Cailean, the Drunken Hero; this mini congregation is led by Tomlin Orlof who gets along well with Jerikal. Jerikal's Ales is renowned for it unique "priest in a bottle" brews which are combination of magical potions and alcoholic beverages. Jerikal is also a source of endless rumours especially adventure related ones, in his adventuring days he even acquired a map leading to a catacomb beneath Pale Mountain filled with treasure.

Zandrek's Pesh Palace.title: Zandrek's Pesh Palace
Zandrek's Pesh Palace.extract: Zandrek's Pesh Palace is a famous pesh parlor in the city of Katapesh. It is a round, domed building surrounded by bronze columns. Its massive copper door is a sculpted face of a laughing man. There are no other doors in the interior but only silk and bead curtains. Multiple private chambers line the first ring of the building while a second ring is filled with statuary, cushions, couches, and wandering minstrels and dancing girls. The center of the structure is a lovely courthouse filled with plants, marble flagstones and a central pool full of colorful fish. Zandrek's Pesh Palace is the most popular den of iniquity in a city full of sin and vice. Master Zandrek cares to satisfy all of his customers' desires. A small army of exotic slaves are especially trained to see to the needs of Master Zandrek's guests.

Golden Oasis.title: Golden Oasis
Golden Oasis.extract:
Golden Oasis might refer to:

  • Golden Oasis (Katapesh)
  • Golden Oasis (Osirion)

Datorro's Brewery.title: Datorro's Brewery
Datorro's Brewery.extract: Datorro's Brewery is an extravagant stall in the Daystalls, the shantytown of vendor stalls, tents, and kiosks in the city of Katapesh. Ano Datorro sells casks, bottles, skins, and mug samplings of his various ales. His workspace is full of still and alchemist's equipment and smokes all day long, churning out a variety of ales.

Aromas and Aphrodisiacs.title: Aromas and Aphrodisiacs
Aromas and Aphrodisiacs.extract: Aromas and Aphrodisiacs is a pavilion of brightly colored tents connected to each other, with a scent that attracts anyone who approaches. The herbalist Chiaong runs the establishment with his slaves, offering herbs that can be inhaled, drunk, or applied to the skin to create a relaxing sensation in the user.

The Ramps.title: The Ramps
The Ramps.extract: The Ramps are a series of timber and stone blocks that connect the Inner City and the Lower City. They start at the gates and are designed to collapse in the event of a siege. The ramps are 50 feet wide, and both sides have safety rails to prevent falls, although three or four people die each year from falls here; whether these are accidents or something else is unknown. Various illicit activities take place in the shadow of the ramps, and many criminals take refuge here in search of new victims to rob.

Temple of Nethys.title: Temple of Nethys
Temple of Nethys.extract: The Temple of Nethys in Katapesh is an enormous pyramid with a multicolored flame that burns without any fuel. Inside, the vast space features walls decorated with the tenents of Nethys, while guards in masks and half-white, half-black robes watch to prevent thieves from entering. One of the rooms contains a library of arcane knowledge that the priests share among themselves to further their research into magical creations or their principles and their head priest Bale Politiz watches over these experiments.

Cheeho the Talking Dog.title: Cheeho the Talking Dog
Cheeho the Talking Dog.extract: Cheeho the Talking Dog is, as the name suggests, a stall where Cheeho entertains travelers with his stories, as he can communicate like a person. The truth is, he was once a human man, but after stealing an amulet from a burial site, he was cursed to live out his days as a dog, and here he is earning money to see if he can break the curse.

Katapesh.title: Katapesh
Katapesh.extract:
This article covers the nation of Katapesh. For information on the city of the same name, see Katapesh (city).

Katapesh is a nation of trade located on the eastern coast of Garund that sells all conceivable goods, no matter how mundane or bizarre. Traders converge on the nation's bazaars from around the world, even from other planes. Pesh is among its major exports. It had long been a major vector of slaves in the Inner Sea region until its mysterious Pactmasters abolished the practice in 4722 AR.

Glass Pyramids.title: Glass Pyramids
Glass Pyramids.extract:
The Glass Pyramids are a pair of transparent pyramids located in the Inner City district of Katapesh. The pyramids are made of transparent metal plates, a unique design created by Gezbria and Tralvar, the two dwarven wizards interred within who had arrived to Katapesh in 2219 AR.

Unknown to almost all, the two wizards discovered a confluence of magic ley lines beneath the Golden City and built two structures atop it, but too late realised that the magic was corrupt and uncontrollable. To stop this powerful magic being used for evil they built the Glass Pyramids on top of their previous structures, and after warding themselves with powerful magic they interred themselves alive within the pyramids. The magic of the corrupted ley lines soon overwhelmed them, transforming them into evil liches.

The site is regularly investigated by curious adventurers and scholars, but those smart enough to bypass the magic wards inevitably fall victim to the evil liches dwelling within. The Glassclaw tribe of kobolds consider it their sacred mission to ensure that no one ever breaks the Glass Pyramids, claiming to have guarded the structures for more than 100 generations, a longer span of time than the pyramids have existed.

Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant.title: Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant
Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant.extract:
Doubtlessly one of Katapesh's strangest weapon shops, Al'Faran's Steel Restaurant is located in the Inner City area, but bears more resemblance to an eatery that a swordsmith's. Customers are taken to a table and presented with a selection of beverages and food, then given a list of the unique weapons that have been created by the master weaponsmith Diyab Al'Faran. He crafts bespoke weapons from the finest Drumish steel to give them near-perfect balance. As a result, his weapons cost substantially more than normal weapons.

While Al'Faran doesn't sell magical weapons, he does sell magical oils and alchemical substances that enhance weapons, such as silversheen or oil of keen edge.

Screaming Obelisk.title: Screaming Obelisk
Screaming Obelisk.extract:
A landmark of the Inner City district of Katapesh, The Screaming Obelisk is visible for miles around. The Screaming Obelisk is a massive spire nearly 400 feet tall and made of solid red carnelian.

The structure is covered in symbols; the one nearest the bottom seems to be times and date, while the higher, more mysterious symbols are unfanthomable. This does not stop people trying, and every year the Obelisk attracts scholars, linguists, engineers, and wizards from around the Inner Sea region who try to unravel its mysteries. The Screaming Obelisk's name comes from the holes cut into the top of it which, when the winds pick up, radiate an eerie series of whistling sounds.

As well as an impressive tourist attraction, the Obelisk also serves as a giant sundial. Surrounding the Screaming Obelisk are three belltowers that use the shadow cast by the Obelisk to measure the passage of time, sounding a bell at every hour of the day.

Trillia's Bathhouse.title: Trillia's Bathhouse
Trillia's Bathhouse.extract: Trillia's Bathhouse is often one of the first stop for sail crusted sailors or weary desert travels, it sits on the dock of Katapesh and is easily recognised by its sign of a cooper tub above the door. It offers over a dozen private bathing chambers, a basic bath cost one silver piece for fifteen minutes so a traveller can bath for two hours for less than a gold piece additional soaps and amenities are available for more. Unlike many other bath houses Trillia's, still run by Trillia herself, does not offer any sort of lewd services. It is however a hotbed for information as many travellers loosen their tongues in the relaxing waters of the bathhouse.

Nexian Embassy.title: Nexian Embassy
Nexian Embassy.extract: The Nexian Embassy in the city of Katapesh is located in the Inner City district. Katapesh and Nex are natural allies, sharing similar views and business models. The embassy has been built after the dominant architectural style in the city of Quantium. A number of domed buildings topped by narrow spires surround a central courtyard where shrines to the gods form a central ring. Nexian flags cap the spires. The Nexian ambassador in Katapesh is Narlaguut Haraxis. He and his slaves and advisors reside in the embassy. The ambassador spends much of his time in court at the walled Palace of the Pactmasters maintaining goodwill between his country and Katapesh. He spends most of his spare time in Zandrek's Pesh Palace and the Golden Scarab.

The Slace Block.title: The Slace Block
The Slace Block.extract: The Slace Block was located in the Lower City and guarded by two squads of the Zephyr Guard. While vendors displayed slaves to potential buyers on the stone platform, intelligent creatures of all kinds were sold. Some, like ogres or trolls, could not be purchased carelessly, as they could tear their owners apart. However, there was always someone arrogant enough to end up as pieces of meat shortly after buying them. The Steel Falcons operated by assassinating and sabotaging slavers, and the Pactmasters offered bounties on their heads for a time. In 4722 AR Katapesh outlawed slavery, and this market ceased operations.

House of Whips.title: House of Whips
House of Whips.extract: The House of Whips is a shop catering to sadomasochists, run by a woman who wears red silks and is an open secret to be a succubus who drains the energy of her more healthy customers through the lashing of her whips. She uses magic to control the minds of her "employees" and sometimes struggles to resist acquiring a beautiful new toy that enters her establishment.

Alseen's Camels.title: Alseen's Camels
Alseen's Camels.extract: Alseen's Camels sells Katapeshi camels at the Daystalls, breeds perfectly suited to this desert region. They are also very docile, making them easy for any rider. The owner, Alhamad Alseen, sells the camels along with all the necessary equipment to ride them properly.

The Smoke Lady.title: The Smoke Lady
The Smoke Lady.extract: The Smoke Lady is a black and purple shop where the Smoke Lady tells fortunes for travelers for a fee. Children can be heard outside advertising, and inside, the woman is hidden behind a smokescreen that obscures her face as she foretells the destiny of the clients.

Kelmarane.title: Kelmarane
Kelmarane.extract:
Kelmarane is a ruined battle market village in northwestern Katapesh. The village was one of several in the Uwaga Highlands of the Brazen Peaks situated around the battle market. In 4689 AR the Pactmasters abandoned it to ruin. By 4707 AR the Kulldis tribe of kholo had claimed Kelmarane as its own. The village has since become a haven of kholo slavers, human bandits, and disreputable brigands from the northlands.

Aside from the ancient battle market and the ruined church of Sarenrae, most of the structures in Kelmarane are abandoned ruins. Most lack roofs or sturdy walls, but others are solid enough to provide temporary shelter.

The Pactmasters hired Almah Roveshki to reclaim the village.

Dongun Hold.title: Dongun Hold
Dongun Hold.extract:
The dwarven Sky Citadel of Dongun Hold, located in the Mana Wastes of southeastern Garund, is part of the magic-bereft Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. While the nation as a whole has a primarily human population, Dongun Hold is almost exclusively inhabited by dwarves.

The ancient forge within Dongun Hold is surrounded by gargantuan dwarven statues, seemingly gazing at the forge within. A flaming inverted hammer is engraved into the handle of every item forged in Dongun Hold to show its provenance.

Yavipho.title: Yavipho
Yavipho.extract:
The village of Yavipho is a tiny, predominantly gnome settlement in central Katapesh, very similar in nature to Tiven's Reed. It grew up around an oasis stopover for merchants and continues to serve as a place where one can sell almost anything to its curious inhabitants.

Constant harassment by slavers (especially from the kholo population) eventually forced the gnomes to migrate beneath the oasis, where homes and workshops are dug from the rock. Most dwellings have secret escape routes, and the settlement itself is carefully monitored by guards for fear of discovery from hostile outsiders. Few visitors are accepted into the community, and those select few are searched before allowed entry. Paranoia is the price of security.

Tar Kuata.title: Tar Kuata
Tar Kuata.extract:
The monastic settlement of Tar Kuata, dedicated to the god Irori, is located among the peaks of the Barrier Wall in southwestern Osirion near the Footprints of Rovagug, and the borders of Thuvia and the Mwangi Expanse. The Monastery of Tar Kuata was founded by Narmek Tar Kuata, a Sothan priest of Irori now venerated as a saint.

Many among the Pahmet, the sand dwarves, claim that Tar Kuata is home to some of their contemplative brethren, the Ouat, who are scholars of the martial arts taught in the monastery. The Ouat are in fact an isolated caste of dwarven monks based in the mountain valley near Tar Kuata. They are led by Menkha Helg.

Zulran's Equines.title: Zulran's Equines
Zulran's Equines.extract:
Zulran's Equines is a sprawling horse ranch belonging to An'Aldane Zulran, guildmaster of the Imperial Union of Breeders. His horses and camels are regarded as some of the finest in all of Katapesh, if not the entire Inner Sea. The horsemaster's animals are bred for one thing: speed, and for this reason they are greatly valued by collectors. Zulran's reputation for breeding fast horses is matched only by his reputation for being a legendary haggler, marking his horses up to well above the usual rate.

The ranch is also a profitable riding school. An'Aldane's riding school is shockingly expensive but well worth the price, as those who complete his school ride away with unique and enviable skills.

A recent theft of one of his finest chargers by a kholo bandit named Bristlemane has left An'Aldrane infuriated and offering a 1,000-gold-piece reward for anyone than can return the horse and the head of the offender.

Merishai.title: Merishai
Merishai.extract: Merishai is a town in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.

Dead Villages.title: Dead Villages
Dead Villages.extract:
The Dead Villages are a collection of abandoned villages in the Brazen Frontier section of southern Osirion. They were once inhabited by Pharasmin death cults who believed that one's afterlife could be positively influenced by choosing the time of one's demise. This led the villagers to engage in ritual combat to the death as well as suicide. These practices guaranteed that the cult soon ran out of members, leaving the villages empty.

As of 4714 AR bones could still be found in the central squares of these empty settlements, and distressed undead such as allips haunt them at night. Although the cultists believed they were worshiping Pharasma, its leaders secretly were devotees of Sifkesh, demon lord of heresy and suicide.

Ophidian Estate.title: Ophidian Estate
Ophidian Estate.extract: The Ophidian Estate is located outside the walls of Kerse and serves as the Aspis Consortium's base of operations in the city. They wish to improve their relations with the Prophecies of Kalistrade, perhaps even by placing one of their members in the order, or vice versa. Currently, operations are being led by Brinesie Iliesco, who is seeking guards to protect the trade routes connecting to Andoran.

Sublime Square.title: Sublime Square
Sublime Square.extract: The Sublime Square is one of the best open-air markets around, boasting an excellent variety and unparalleled quality, selling everything from fine clothing and magical items to delicious ingredients and unmatched weapons. The stalls are run by Kalistocrats who have obtained permits from the city; those of lower rank have to settle for simply setting up a cloth to sell their wares. Blackjackets patrol the square all day, but since crime is very low in Kerse, there are few problems here.

Kerse.title: Kerse
Kerse.extract: Kerse (pronounced KERS) is the capital of the Kalistocracy of Druma and also its largest city. A native of Kerse is known as a Kersite.

Citadel Vaull.title: Citadel Vaull
Citadel Vaull.extract:
Citadel Vaull, located in the Castle District of the city of Kintargo in Ravounel, housed the otherwise nomadic Hellknight Order of the Torrent until 4715 AR, when new Lord-Mayor Barzillai Thrune stripped the order of its charter and had the Order of the Rack loot the citadel of its contents.

After Barzillai Thrune was removed from power and Kintargo and Ravounel won their independence from Cheliax in 4716 AR, Citadel Vaull was returned to the Order of the Torrent, who began renovating its facade to be more approachable. However, work has been slowed by the Order's simultaneous work caring for Castle Kintargo.

Silver Span.title: Silver Span
Silver Span.extract:
The Silver Span, formerly known as Bleakbridge, is the sole bridge connecting the two halves of Kintargo across the Yolubilis River and links the districts of Yolubilis Harbor with Redroof. The Silver Span also serves as one of the city's greatest markets, as it is lined with wooden shops from end to end on both sides with smaller markets nestled round either end of the bridge.

The bridge is 475 feet long, consisting of stone arches that peak 10 feet above the water, and are supported by eight large stone towers that also serve the local Dottari as guardposts. All types of shops line the bridge, including food stores, a cobbler, a hatter, curio shops, and a florist.

The Silver Span is also home to unique gang of tengu toughs called The River Talons who act as cutpurses, thieves and general nuisances who never quite made the leap from street gang to thieves' guild.

Kintargo Keep.title: Kintargo Keep
Kintargo Keep.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: A Song of Silver.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Kintargo Keep is a part of the larger Castle Kintargo that dominates the Castle District of the city of Kintargo. The keep's five-storey spire juts from the north-western corner of the castle, and under the rule of Cheliax it served as Kintargo's most notorious prison. As of 4724 AR under independent Ravounel, it had been sealed since the Ruby Massacre in 4716 AR.

Sunset Imports.title: Sunset Imports
Sunset Imports.extract: Sunset Imports is a harborfront import house in Kintargo that was among the city's wealthiest companies until losing its contracts with House Thrune in 4715 AR. Its fortunes have improved as of 4724 AR due to its role in fostering trade with the distant continent of Arcadia.

Thrashing Badger.title: Thrashing Badger
Thrashing Badger.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: In Hell's Bright Shadow.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The Thrashing Badger was a rough-and-ready bar in the Yolubilis Harbor district of Kintargo that burned to the ground during the Night of Ashes. Before its destruction, The Thrashing Badger was home not just to rowdy sailors and labourers but also served as a drinking hole for a myriad of malcontents and rebels, most of whom now drink at nearby Clenchjaw's instead.

As of 4724 AR, many attempts to rebuild the tavern have variously failed.

Gems of Kalistrade.title: Gems of Kalistrade
Gems of Kalistrade.extract: The Gems of Kalistrade are five towers surrounding the Everlasting Palace, each containing a piece of the nation's history. These can be viewed by travelers recognized by the prophets and their curator, Werden Luddrecht. Two of the towers are inaccessible, and their purpose is unknown.

Planepierce Spire.title: Planepierce Spire
Planepierce Spire.extract: The Planepierce Spire is a tower filled with laboratories and warehouses, all belonging to Cyran Androtus. Only those invited by him can enter; the doors are only opened rarely to obtain supplies from the outside. There are rumors that extraplanar creatures are also bringing supplies from other planes inside, and given Cyran's involvement, this is quite likely.

Mercenary League Headquarters.title: Mercenary League Headquarters
Mercenary League Headquarters.extract: The Mercenary League Headquarters is where the organization's generals, its most powerful and influential members, reside. This black building stands in stark contrast to the district's opulence, and mercenaries can be seen and heard planning and coordinating global campaigns, as well as patrolling the city as guards. Commander Sarissa Kurst directs operations here, and the Whispering Tyrant's presence on the lake is creating a noticeable shortage of resources.

Immaculate Path.title: Immaculate Path
Immaculate Path.extract: The Immaculate Path is a bridge connecting the Everlasting Palace to the rest of Kerse. It is a structure filled with fountains and golden statues of prophets of the past. Its open-air state might suggest a lack of defense, but that would be a misconception, as many of the statues are actually guardians that awaken to defend the bridge if it is invaded.

Kintargo.title: Kintargo
Kintargo.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Hell's Rebels Pathfinder Adventure Path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Kintargo, also known as the Silver City, is the capital of Ravounel and is situated on Nisroch Bay at the mouth of the Yolubilis River. It is the main harbor for ships heading north to Varisia and west to Anchor's End.

Sarini Estate.title: Saringallow
Sarini Estate.extract: The Isgeri town of Saringallow is located on the Conerica River, south of the capital of Elidir.

Vashnarstill Shipyard.title: Vashnarstill Shipyard
Vashnarstill Shipyard.extract:
The Vashnarstill Shipyard is a centuries-old fixture of the Yolubilis Harbor area of Kintargo. Named for House Vashnarstill, it has remained the most reliable dockyard for over 10 generations due to its management. Ownership is handed down on the basis of merit rather than blood ties; each owner has tried to select the best employee to succeed him, which has worked well so far.

Its owner as of 4724 AR was Kohl Draksitus. In 4715 AR, Draksitus' most significant concerns were the stiff competition from new yards in the rival Chelaxian cites of Westcrown and Corentyn. However, her more recent struggles are the result of keeping up with the demands of Sendi Vashnarstill, whose ambitions to make Kintargo a hub of trade with the distant continent of Arcadia are taxing the shipyard's capacity.

Lady Docur's School for Girls.title: Lady Docur's School for Girls
Lady Docur's School for Girls.extract:
Lady Docur's School for Girls is the oldest of the many private schools in the Villegre district of Kintargo, but is somewhat overshadowed by the larger, more famous Alabaster Academy.

Lady Docur's School was founded approximately 300 years ago by its forlorn elven headmistress Lady Mialari Docur, who as of 4724 AR still operates the school. She founded the school when educating young ladies in elven ways was fashionable. Tastes changed over the centuries, however, and as of 4724 AR its students are primarily female aiuvarins from across Cheliax, Isger, and Molthune.

The seemingly innocent school harbours a strange secret, however, for Lady Docur educates the most talented of her students in not only etiquette but also espionage. Those she deems her most promising students are recruited into a secretive organisation of spies and messengers called the Lacunafex that operates across Cheliax and often cooperates with the Kintargo-based Sacred Order of Archivists.

Cebarinne Estate.title: Cebarinne Estate
Cebarinne Estate.extract: The Cebarinne Estate, located in the Villegre district of Kintargo in Ravounel, is the extravagant home of retired opera divo Ricovial Cebarinne, who now tutors students from the estate.

Aulamaxa Estate.title: Aulamaxa Estate
Aulamaxa Estate.extract: Aulamaxa Estate, located in The Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Aulamaxa.

Aulorian Estate.title: Aulorian Estate
Aulorian Estate.extract: Aulorian Estate, located in The Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Aulorian.

Delronge Estate.title: Delronge Estate
Delronge Estate.extract: Delronge Estate, located in The Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Delronge.

Tanessen Estate.title: Tanessen Estate
Tanessen Estate.extract: Tanessen Estate, located in the Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Tanessen.

Aeldervenk Manor.title: Aeldervenk Manor
Aeldervenk Manor.extract: Aeldervenk Manor, located in The Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of the Aeldervenk family. As of 4715 AR, this minor aristocratic family was not considered to be among one of Kintargo's great noble houses.

Devil's Nursery.title: Devil's Nursery
Devil's Nursery.extract: The slums of Devil's Nursery, located in the Redroof neighborhood of Kintargo, are named for the large proportion of its residents who are cambions, most of whom fled here from less tolerant corners of Cheliax.

Rust Gate.title: Rust Gate
Rust Gate.extract: The Rust Gate straddles the border between Kintargo's districts of Old Kintargo and Redroof and is the city's most heavily trafficked gate. Most of the traffic crossing through it comes from the Ravounel Road, the busiest roadway in the city, which leads to Kintargo from the south and over the Argo Bridge.

Fair Fortune Livery.title: Fair Fortune Livery
Fair Fortune Livery.extract: The Fair Fortune Livery is a horse stable in the Redroof neighborhood of Kintargo, Ravounel. As of 4715 AR it had been abandoned and fallen into disrepair, but by 4724 AR it had been recently reopened by the Delronge family.

Humbright House.title: Humbright House
Humbright House.extract: Humbright House was one of a number of smaller shrines to Aroden that once dotted Kintargo's Temple Hill district but has been abandoned since the god's death.

Holding House.title: Holding House
Holding House.extract: The Holding House, located in the Old Kintargo district of Kintargo, was the city's original jail. It fell out of use when Castle Kintargo and Kintargo Keep began to be used to hold the city's prisoners.

Vespam Artisans.title: Vespam Artisans
Vespam Artisans.extract: As of 4724 AR, Vespam Artisans is one of the larger businesses still in the Old Kintargo district of Kintargo. It serves as both a metalworking workshop and tinkers' guild. Vespam Artisans is owned and operated by Vespasio Vespam, a fifth-generation Kintargan and lifelong resident of Old Kintargo.

Lucky Bones.title: Lucky Bones
Lucky Bones.extract: For centuries, Lucky Bones was a notorious gambling house and drug den sat in the heart of Old Kintargo, but it was burned down in a raid by Hellknights of the Order of the Torrent. Unbeknownst to most, Lucky Bones had long served as a legitimate front for the notorious Gray Spiders guild which operated out of the basement below it.

Cloven Hoof Society.title: Cloven Hoof Society
Cloven Hoof Society.extract: The Cloven Hoof Society is an organisation that helps cambions throughout the city of Kintargo, the nation of Ravounel, and lands beyond. Its headquarters are located in the slums of the city's Redroof neighborhood.

Shrine of Saint Senex.title: Shrine of Saint Senex
Shrine of Saint Senex.extract: The Shrine of Saint Senex is not dedicated to a deity but instead to the centuries-dead Senex, an oracle who devoted her life to recovering the drowned dead of Nisroch Bay and the Yolubilis River. The shrine is now all but forgotten by the modern world; Saint Senex fell in popularity after the Chelaxian Civil War, lying unremembered on a stretch of swampy coast on Argo Isle, just south of the city of Kintargo. The driftwood shrine is devoted to drowned sailors, including a buried sanctum that is home to the bodies of dozens of unknown drowned. The shrine is shielded from magical divination and has sometimes been used as a secret safe house by Hellknights of the Order of the Torrent. Thanks to its decline in prominence, the shrine's only inhabitants are now a pair of rag-clad oracles.

Little Miss Drurie's.title: Little Miss Drurie's
Little Miss Drurie's.extract: The Little Miss Drurie's is a dance hall located in the Jarvis End district of Kintargo. Dance halls in Kintargo combine the functions of a musical venue, tavern, and brothel. As of 4719 AR, it was one of Kintargo's most recently established dance halls and had garnered a following for its dance contests and experimental musical performances.

Kintargo Opera House.title: Kintargo Opera House
Kintargo Opera House.extract: The Kintargo Opera House in the city of Kintargo, Ravounel, is beloved by the local population and has been the stage for such famous soloists as Shensen. It is one of the most esteemed opera venues in Avistan.

Green Market.title: Green Market
Green Market.extract: The Green Market is a small, enclosed indoor market run by Zeeva Foxglove in Korvosa's South Shore neighborhood. It specializes in food, clothing, and other necessary items, but also carries jewelry and other luxuries for the upper classes. Its merchants are expressly forbidden to sell magic items.

House of Golden Veils.title: House of Golden Veils
House of Golden Veils.extract: The House of Golden Veils is the largest bank in Kintargo and a temple to the god Abadar. It is located in the northeastern corner of the city's Temple Hill district. As of 4724 AR its high priest was Mhelrem Gesteliel.

Andos Hall.title: Andos Hall
Andos Hall.extract: Andos Hall, the city hall of Kintargo, is located in its Castle District and thoroughly dwarfed by the neighbouring Castle Kintargo, next to which it appears understated and quaint.

Administrative and Civic Center of Ravou.title: Administrative and Civic Center of Ravounel
Administrative and Civic Center of Ravou.extract: The Administrative and Civic Center of Ravounel, colloquially called the People's Temple and formerly known as the Temple of Asmodeus and Temple of Aroden, is the largest temple in Kintargo and government capitol of Ravounel. It dominates the Temple Hill district.

Silver Star.title: Silver Star
Silver Star.extract: The Silver Star, once a music store in the foreign quarter of the Jarvis End district of Kintargo, is as of 4724 AR a vacant lot.

Sallix Salt Works.title: Sallix Salt Works
Sallix Salt Works.extract: The Sallix Salt Works are the largest and oldest such business in all Kintargo and are located in the oldest part of the city, Old Kintargo.

Ruba's Shop.title: Ruba's Shop
Ruba's Shop.extract: Ruba's Shop is a scrivener's shop in the Old Kintargo district of Kintargo that also serves as a notary and translator. Unbeknownst to most, the owner—the elderly Varisian, Ruba Fenquay—is also an accomplished forger whose other illegal sideline business is copying forbidden texts.

Silver Spindle.title: Silver Spindle
Silver Spindle.extract: The Silver Spindle, formerly known as The Devil's Threads, is one of the many tailor's shops located adjacent to the Redroof Market in the Redroof district of Kintargo.

Hocum's Fantasmagorium.title: Hocum's Fantasmagorium
Hocum's Fantasmagorium.extract: The long-shuttered and storied building once known as Hocum's Fantasmagorium, located in the northern end of the Redroof district in the city of Kintargo, Ravounel, was purchased by Maercin Kelimber at some time recent to 4724 AR. Its renovation into a dry goods warehouse has been delayed by infestations of unusual vermin and imps.

Kendall Amphitheater.title: Kendall Amphitheater
Kendall Amphitheater.extract: The Kendall Amphitheater, located in the Heights District of Korvosa, hosts open-air performances.

Palin's Lighthouse.title: Palin's Lighthouse
Palin's Lighthouse.extract: Palin's Lighthouse can be found on the small Jeggare Isle in Conqueror's Bay, and is part of the Varisian city of Korvosa. It is the only structure on the island, and is presumably named after Captain Keyra Palin, a hero from the early years of Korvosa's history.

Bard's End.title: Bard's End
Bard's End.extract: Bard's End is the city of Korvosa's largest inn, sitting in South Shore district where it provides welcome respite to weary travellers. Bard's End lies a block south of the Kendall Amphitheater and as such normally plays host to a variety of musicians and performers—both famous and non-famous. A small stage dominates the common room of Bard's End and there is a performance there every night. For many bards and performers leaving Varisia (via Korvosa), their final performance will be for a handful of extra coins in the Bard's End common room.

Pantheon of Many.title: Pantheon of Many
Pantheon of Many.extract:
The Pantheon of Many is a temple devoted to almost all of the main deities of the Inner Sea region located in the South Shore district of the Varisian city of Korvosa.

The Pantheon of Many contains shrines to all the traditional core deities of the Inner Sea except for Gorum, Lamashtu, and Rovagug. Neither Cayden Cailean nor Desna had a shrine within the Pantheon when it was built, but when huge crowds of their worshippers blockaded the Pantheon before its opening, their shrines were swiftly added.

Each deity's shrine is equally sized and equally respected. Clerics and priests of each religion are allowed to practice their personal rites and rituals without interference as long as they abide by the laws of Korvosa, and regardless of whether the religion is evil or chaotic. The only restriction is that no sacrifices, human or animal, are allowed.

Gatefoot.title: Gatefoot
Gatefoot.extract: Gatefoot sits as the western end of the Pillar Wall in the Korvosan district of the Heights. The massive foot is one of the remnants of ancient Thassilon that still dot Korvosa and was clearly part of some ancient wall. Many scholars think it most likely also serves as part of some sort of enormous gate.

Orkatto's Feathers and Fur.title: Orkatto's Feathers and Fur
Orkatto's Feathers and Fur.extract: Orkatto's Feathers and Fur caters to those nouveau riche of Korvosa who have a taste for exotic pets. The unique store is located in the newer South Shore district of the Varisian city. The store is a strange wonderland of exotic animals, from caged songbirds to gem-hued snakes, all the way up to dream spiders which are valued by Orkatto's more shady clientele for their venom, which can be refined into a powerful drug called shiver. The store's owner, Orkatto, served as a well-respected master of the hunt to a powerful lord in the nearby city of Magnimar, before finally saving enough money to open his own namesake store.

High Bridge.title: High Bridge
High Bridge.extract:
For another meaning of "High Bridge", please see High Bridge (ward).

High Bridge is the southernmost of the two large bridges that connect the city of Korvosa with the rest of Varisia. High Bridge crosses the Jeggare River and its western end opens onto Eodred's Square in the Midland district of the city.

Great Tomb of Leadership.title: Great Tomb of Leadership
Great Tomb of Leadership.extract: The Great Tomb of Leadership is a vast vault beneath Korvosa's Gray district that is the final resting place for many of the city's most decorated military leaders. Located near the Grand Cathedral of Pharasma, the Great Tomb is guarded at all times by a soldier of the Korvosan Guard and a marine from Sable Company. The position is considered a high honour and is often assigned to those who are expected to earn a place in the Great Tomb in time. These guards are not allowed to go further than fifty feet from the building, so they cannot interfere in anything happening within the rest of Gray.

Pestico's Dolls and Figurines.title: Pestico's Dolls and Figurines
Pestico's Dolls and Figurines.extract: Pestico's Dolls and Figurines is a shop located in the south of the Midland district that sells dolls and figurines to the delight of children all across the city of Korvosa. The shop is run by Vadid Pestico: a kindly, but fragile, old man, who builds the store's dolls. His late wife, Kiristi, used to sculpt the figurines, until her death over a decade ago. Recently Vadid unveiled a project he had been working on for a long time: a group of six soulbound dolls he named "Pestico's Daughters". These 'daughters' help Pestico run the shop and have become quite the draw for tourists and locals alike. However, recently one of these dolls has gone missing and, shortly after she disappeared, a string of bodies were found nearby. So far, the Korvosan Guard has kept these killings under wraps, as the victims were vagrants who have yet to be missed.

Castle Korvosa.title: Castle Korvosa
Castle Korvosa.extract: Easily the most imposing structure in a city of monumental architecture, Castle Korvosa is the fortress-citadel home of the monarchs Korvosa. The castle itself is built on an ancient four-sided, flat-topped pyramid known as the Grand Mastaba, built millennia ago by the Shoanti people. Built on the mainland's Citadel Hill, it can be seen from anywhere in the city.

Marbledome.title: Marbledome
Marbledome.extract:
Marbledome is the central opera house of the Varisian city of Korvosa, and can be found in the upscale Heights District, just south of Castle Korvosa. Productions at the Marbledome, which is owned by House Jeggare, are always extremely lavish, but the operas performed there as of 4708 AR were often mediocre and rarely broke even. Nevertheless, it is one of the centers of Korvosan high society, with wealthy families clamoring for the best box seats.

As of 4708 AR, the Marbledome was (poorly) managed by Touran Palastus, a somewhat sadistic man who has driven more than one performer to relocate to Magnimar, or even the Sandpoint Theater.

Tenna's.title: Tenna's
Tenna's.extract:
Citadel Crest ward, in the Heights District, is the only ward in Korvosa having no inns by law. People requiring overnight stays, instead, have the choice from three bed and breakfast establishments, all of which are owned by nobles. In order of price, they are:

  • the Frisky Unicorn, the cheapest;
  • Upslope House; and
  • Tenna's, the most expensive.

In order of size, Tenna's is the smallest and Upslope House the largest.

Tenna's has four luxurious rooms able to comfortably house 11 guests, providing more pampering than anywhere else in Korvosa. The bed and breakfast is situated on Volshyenek Loop opposite the estate of the powerful House Ornelos.

Theumanexus College.title: Theumanexus College
Theumanexus College.extract: The Theumanexus College is a small wizard school located in the East Shore district of the city of Korvosa.

Creaky Hammock.title: Creaky Hammock
Creaky Hammock.extract: The Creaky Hammock is a tavern and inn in the Midland district of Korvosa with a distinctly nautical theme. It often serves ships' crews that are ashore for only a few days, as the tavern is so noisy that the only way most customers can get any rest is by drinking themselves to sleep. The Creaky Hammock is run by Destin Smish, who frequently has to deal with drunken sailors passing out on the floor.

Bailer's Retreat.title: Bailer's Retreat
Bailer's Retreat.extract: Bailer's Retreat is a rough and tumble tavern near Citadel Volshyenek in Korvosa's Midland district. Bailer's Retreat serves as a drinking hole for many recently released criminals as well as many members of the Korvosan Guard. Brawls between the two groups are not uncommon and normally result in the former convicts returning to jail. Still, Bailer's Retreat serves as a hotbed of rumours concerning every sort of illicit activity in the city.

Shoreline Drinkhall.title: Shoreline Drinkhall
Shoreline Drinkhall.extract: Shoreline Drinkhall is the most popular tavern in the East Shore district of Korvosa. Its main clientele are off-duty Korvosan Guard members who come through three times a day when the shift changes and longshoremen who visit every evening. Many other East Shore residents also stop by for drinks and, due to Shoreline Drinkhall's close proximity to the Thief Camp area, it also attracts many Shoanti and Varisians. Bar brawls occur fairly regularly but are not quite as dangerous as those at some of the city's other establishments like, for example, the Sticky Mermaid. The Shoreline Drinkhall has a reputation for fair prices, stiff drinks, and meals that are closer to military rations than to civilian food. The establishment is owned by House Endrin. For those requiring more privacy, private areas are available in the tavern.

Gold Market.title: Gold Market
Gold Market.extract: Gold Market is the largest marketplace in the city of Korvosa and the central attraction of the Midland district. Located between Eodred Circle and Eodred's Walk, people flock from across the city to visit its stalls and stands. Gold Market features local goods from not just Korvosa and it holdings but from across Varisia. The market is rife with food stalls of every sort, it has been speculated that as many as half the population of Korvosa eats here each month. One of the semi-regular features of the market is Sagitar Tiguan, a merchant who sells a range of trinkets and elixirs that he claims are magical. Whenever Sagitar's brightly coloured stall is there, adventurers are sure to be found nearby.

Horse Shop.title: Horse Shop
Horse Shop.extract: The Horse Shop is, as its name suggests, a no-frills livery stable and equestrian supply shop that offers fair prices. It is located in the East Shore neighborhood of the Varisian city of Korvosa.

Leftover's.title: Leftover's
Leftover's.extract: Leftover's is the only inn in the East Shore neighborhood of the Varisian city of Korvosa. It offers a dozen rooms big enough for two people to sleep in as well as a large, comfortable common room and charges standard rates. The innkeeper, Portenus Gaskelinni, is an old soldier who lost an arm in a fight with a Shoanti; this tale is often told by the innkeeper and re-enacted fortnightly by Gaskelinni's Korvosan Guard comrades in the inn as entertainment for the inn's guests.

Acadamae.title: Acadamae
Acadamae.extract: The Acadamae is a wizard's college in the Varisian city of Korvosa which attracts students from as far away as distant Geb. Although it teaches all eight modern schools of magic, it specializes in conjuration and requires its pupils to pass a difficult exam in conjuration in order to graduate. It is currently run by its headmaster, the powerful Toff Ornelos.

Jeggare Museum.title: Jeggare Museum
Jeggare Museum.extract: In the city of Korvosa, located in the region of Varisia, the Jeggare Museum is only rivaled by the Museum of Ages in Magnimar.

Gilded Orrery.title: Gilded Orrery
Gilded Orrery.extract: The Gilded Orrery✝︎ is a magic shop located in the Heights District of Korvosa. Gasta Weagra is the owner of the Gilded Orrery. A plump woman with gray hair, Gasta is the niece of Toff Ornelos, the headmaster of the Academae, the city's most prestigious school of magic, and rarely sells to anyone not connected with it.

Great Tower.title: Great Tower
Great Tower.extract: As a dominant point on Korvosa's skyline, the Great Tower rivals some of the ancient buildings of the city for height. This modern tower is used by the Sable Company as a military base and training centre for hippogriff riders.

Upslope House.title: Upslope House
Upslope House.extract:
Citadel Crest ward, in the Heights District, is the only ward in Korvosa having no inns by law. People requiring overnight stays, instead, have the choice from three bed and breakfast establishments, all of which are owned by nobles. In order of price, they are:

  • the Frisky Unicorn, the cheapest;
  • Upslope House; and
  • Tenna's, the most expensive.

In order of size, Tenna's is the smallest and Upslope House the largest.

Upslope House has 10 rooms able to house 22 guests at a time. That this number is large for a mere bed and breakfast establishment, in an area where inns are forbidden, seems to be overlooked by the authorities.

City rumours hold that Upslope House hides a door to an area of the Vaults used by followers of an unnamed dark power. To add spice to the rumours, the involvement of dero is often thrown in.

Sanctuary of Shelyn.title: Sanctuary of Shelyn
Sanctuary of Shelyn.extract: The Sanctuary of Shelyn is the smallest independent temple in the city of Korvosa. Dedicated to Shelyn, the goddess of beauty and art, it also ranks as amongst Korvosa's most beautiful buildings. The sanctuary sits in the heart of the North Point district; it is a square building set around a central courtyard filled with beautiful statuary and murals, as well as an array of enticing plants. While the building itself is also replete with fine artwork, it never feels cluttered.

Three Rings Tavern.title: Three Rings Tavern
Three Rings Tavern.extract: The Three Rings Tavern is a tavern in the Five Corners district of Korvosa that belongs to a retired Pathfinder named Theandra Darklight. Theandra is a good-natured bartender and is always hungry for any tales of adventure or daring-do. The Three Rings Tavern is comparatively quiet, serving as much coffee as it does alcoholic beverages. The Three Ring's bouncer Tauk Par keeps a close eye on things and barely manages to conceal his dislike for all of the taverns clientele. As well as a wide range of drinks, the Three Rings Tavern is renowned for its breakfast services which include local staples like Varisian sweetbreads all the way to exotic pastries whose recipes come from distant Geb.

North Bridge.title: North Bridge
North Bridge.extract: North Bridge, also known as Longriver Bridge, connects North Point and the neighbourhood of Northgate with mainland Varisia. North Bridge is one of the first sights that many people entering Korvosa see, as it connects Endrin Isle with the rest of Varisia and is one of the city's main entry points.

Dock Trade.title: Dock Trade
Dock Trade.extract: The Dock Trade is Korvosa's smallest marketplace, built into a repurposed warehouse and it is probably best described as "dubiously legal". A lot of their goods, while fresh, appear to have been mysteriously damaged in transit and for years the Korvosan Guard have been trying to link the various merchants of the Dock Trade to a range of robberies. The Hellknights are also regulars at the market and this intense scrutiny has kept the Dock Trade from growing in size. Beyond this, the Dock Trade is also often disrupted by a family of pseudodragons that live in the rafters of the building. For the most part the pseudodragons hunt rats and other vermin, having minimal interaction with the market below, but once in a while an object will take their fancy, often drawing in the entire colony of diminutive dragons. While the pseudodragons are not violent, normally when this happens anarchy ensues, often to the point that the local guard have to be called. When this happens, the pseudodragons will often leave for a few weeks, normally until the rat problem gets so bad that the merchants invite the tiny dragons back.

Citadel Vraid.title: Citadel Vraid
Citadel Vraid.extract: Citadel Vraid is the fortress-headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Nail, indeed it is sometimes referenced as the Fortress of the Nail. It was constructed by the city of Korvosa in an effort to successfully entice the Order to move to Varisia. It is located about 50 miles southwest of Korvosa on a narrow plateau named after one of Korvosa's founders, Montlarion Jeggare, and is on the edge of the southern Mindspin Mountains.

Cicatrix.title: Cicatrix
Cicatrix.extract:
For another meaning of "Cicatrix", please see Cicatrix (weapon).

The Cicatrix is a blasted wasteland on the southwestern edge of the Tanglebriar in the elven nation of Kyonin. Crowded with camouflaged oozes, it is avoided by both elves and the nearby demons.

Jeggare Light.title: Jeggare Light
Jeggare Light.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Curse of the Crimson Throne.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The Jeggare Light is a lighthouse located in Varisia where the Jeggare River spills into the Varisian Gulf, just across from the city of Korvosa. Jeggare Light is operated by Veranada Mezzerae, who was recently transformed by a cult of Urgathoa into a type of vampire known as a penanggalen. After assuring her compliance, the cult ordered her to allow the free passage of the plague ship Direption into the harbor of Korvosa.

House of Sky.title: House of Sky
House of Sky.extract: The House of Sky is an aerie high in the Five Kings Range that is the home of the giant owls of the Fierani Forest. These intelligent creatures have been allied with the elves of Kyonin for millennia, but are quite mistrustful of any other humanoid who makes the dangerous trek to their home. They hate Treerazer and his demonic minions, and can be summoned by the rulers of Kyonin in times of need via enchanted whistles. They are led by Winglord Kreiagh, who oversees a council of owls who frequently disagree with one another, but whose every word is taken to heart. Travel to the House of Sky is quite dangerous on foot, and is most easily accomplished by flight.

Reefclaw Run Market.title: Reefclaw Run Market
Reefclaw Run Market.extract:
The Reefclaw Run Market is a seafood market in the Old Korvosa district of Korvosa. The Reefclaw Run Market has existed for just under three centuries and is host to a range of seafood from Alikan oysters all the way up to jigsaw sharks. Fishing boat captains also often sell other things dredged up by their nets from the depths, including ancient artefacts and the remains of strange deep-sea creatures.

The Reefclaw Run Fountain provides water for the locals.

Ironworks.title: Ironworks
Ironworks.extract:
The Ironworks is one of the largest buildings in Old Korvosa and indeed the entire city. The Ironworks gets pig iron from Janderhoff and recycled slag from local sources and churns out wrought iron of a variety of qualities. Much more a factory than a conventional smithy, the Ironworks focuses on quantity over quality and so never clashes with the city's more quality-focused blacksmiths.

Many of the employees at the Ironworks are also members of the secret guild called the Ironsoots.

Orisini Academy.title: Orisini Academy
Orisini Academy.extract: A famous sword-training school built in the center of the Old Dock ward of the Varisian city of Korvosa's Old Korvosa district, the Orisini Academy, features a grand archway and balcony with ornate oak doors. Located at 16 Hillcrest Street, its door knockers feature the shape of an imp and pseudodragon seemingly locked in lethal combat. Mostly young heirs of noble houses of the city enroll in the academy to learn and practice the art of fencing. The school's namesake, Vencarlo Orisini, rarely teaches the classes himself and leaves the responsibility to one of his senior students named Dengaro, instead.

Arkona Palace.title: Arkona Palace
Arkona Palace.extract:
Arkona Palace, or Palace Arkona, is a massive black manor atop the highest point of Endrin Isle that as of 4708 AR housed Old Korvosa's ruling family, House Arkona. Surrounded by an iron-wrought and black-marbled wall, the manor is adorned with strange ornamentation hailing from Vudra, such as doors bearing depictions of Chamidu and decor of elephants with three heads, peacocks holding sabers, and tigers with carved eyes that leer from the palace's walls and over the gardens of the mansion. Ruthless guards patrol the palace.

Beneath Arkona Palace is a famous cyst drop, an occurrence when a sea cave collapses in on itself to form a sinkhole. The cyst drop is formed from the natural caves that compose the Vaults of Korvosa. The palace's underground levels also include the Vivified Labyrinth, a sprawling and complex obstacle course and dungeon.

Exemplary Execrables.title: Exemplary Execrables
Exemplary Execrables.extract: A former temple of Aroden, Exemplary Execrables now serves as a theatre devoted to all things foul and shocking. Redecorated in gold paint and tacky fake gems, Exemplary Execrables now puts on plays solely designed to shock and scandalise, they feature faked rape, murder, and bloody torture; these shows are always filled with gore and are normally pornographic. The owner of the theatre, Pilts Swastel, is a sore-covered man who is every bit as repugnant as the shows the theatre puts on. The most popular attractions are the so called "death plays" where a volunteer is pulled from the audience and then 'murdered' in front of the audience by a masked performer. Given how realistic these murders appear, some have wondered if they are even staged at all. Many of the volunteers go missing shortly afterwards, as do a startling number of Pilts's assistants. As such, the theatre has drawn investigations by the Korvosan Guard, Sable Company, and even Hellknights of the Order of the Nail but none has been able to catch Pilts doing anything untoward. Exemplary Execrables also has an attached 'museum': a side-show of the macabre that includes severed heads, fetuses, and a large collection of torture implements. It also includes a Galtan guillotine whose sharp blade has claimed many a finger from overly curious guests.

Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactor.title: Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactory
Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactor.extract: Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactory is an art supplies shop with a sagging store front that sits in the Old Korvosa district of Korvosa. The store, which was founded in 4600 AR, is filled with art supplies, canvases, easels, pots, and brushes. The paints from Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactory vary from the cheapest, a one silver piece white paint, all the way up to a dazzling shade of violet—Newby Violet—that costs forty gold pieces a jar. Regardless of price, their paints are renowned as being the best in all Korvosa for colour, consistency, and permanence. The owners of the shop, Hessim, Newby, and Sage, are a trio of very old men who all wear glasses and are very ingratiating towards their customers.

Witchbole.title: Witchbole
Witchbole.extract:
The Witchbole is a giant, twisted thorn tree which Treerazer uses as a fortress and palace. As if its appearance were not harrowing enough, the bloated, gnarled tree is completely mobile, crawling slowly through the forest on its writhing roots. The Witchbole's true nature remains a mystery, though the arboreal regents of the Fierani Forest avoid the topic, leading some to believe that the massive demonic arbor was one of their kind before being corrupted to Treerazer's evil will.

The shattered crest of the Witchbole sometimes twists into the outline of a face, although this visage is vague. On its branches are the impaled corpses of those elves who sought to confront Treerazer directly.

This page contains spoilers for the following products: A Voice in the Blight.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

In 4725 AR as part of his offensive Treerazer teleported the Witchbole straight to Iadara where he began a ritual to grow in power and destroy the city. Heroes where able to infiltrate the structure and the ripples of magical power within destroyed the tree making it an empty husk now.

Tanglegate.title: Tanglegate
Tanglegate.extract:
Tanglegate is an elf gate in the demon-corrupted forest of Tanglebriar. It can be reached via Lichgate, which is the only known example of a working elf gate that links to Tanglebriar. Its portal key is a sapphire.

Treerazer, the lord of Tanglebriar, hopes one day to be able to use Tanglegate against the elves, and so he has ordered his minions to refrain from destroying it. The gate itself is a stone arch covered in moss, and it stands on a rare patch of dry ground in the middle of a stinking bog.

Shevaroth.title: Shevaroth
Shevaroth.extract: The ruins of the city of Shevaroth, once one of Kyonin's most populous pre-Earthfall cities, are located on the plains southeast of the Fierani Forest. Abandoned by the elves fleeing Earthfall to Castrovel, Shevaroth fell under the dominion of the nascent demon lord Treerazer in 2633 AR, and as of 4725 AR it had not been retaken.

Dimcrater.title: Dimcrater
Dimcrater.extract: Dimcrater is a mysterious crater located in the southernmost tip of Kyonin's Fierani Forest, well within the Tanglebriar, the domain of the nascent demon lord Treerazer. It was once known as Arabrecht, the manor home of the timeless and powerful Archmage Aelthian, prior to its disappearance during Aelthian's attempt to banish Treerazer in 2988 AR.

Lightning's Call.title: Lightning's Call
Lightning's Call.extract:
Lightning's Call, also known as Lightning Top, is a mountain of the Five Kings Range located at the border of the Five Kings Mountains and Kyonin in central Avistan. It bears one of the Carved Kings sculptures featuring the visage of King Harral, which overlooks the southern side of the Great Pass.

The mountain derives its moniker from the perpetual gathering of storm clouds around its peak, a weather phenomenon that wizards of Tar-Kazmukh have determined is not magical in nature but remain mystified by.

Thorn's End.title: Thorn's End
Thorn's End.extract: Thorn's End is a hidden fortress of the Winter Council in the demon-infested Tanglebriar of Kyonin.

Mourning Burrows.title: Mourning Burrows
Mourning Burrows.extract: The Mourning Burrows were once a mass burial ground for Kellids who lived in Druma long ago. Today, Razmiran insurgents who survived their failed raid on Twingate have settled in the interconnected tunnels between the many mounds here.

Vault of the Impostor Goblin.title: Vault of the Impostor Goblin
Vault of the Impostor Goblin.extract: No one is entirely sure what is inside the Vault of the Imposter Goblin. A few even doubt its existence, though it certainly does. The vault has one large stone entryway that connects to a larger underground complex.

Fort Dagh.title: Fort Dagh
Fort Dagh.extract:
Fort Dagh is located at the summit of a mountain peak south of Tar-Kazmukh and overlooks the Great Pass of the northwestern Five Kings Mountains. It is renowned for its view despite a reputation among soldiers as a hardship posting, and its commander is First Captain Hannah Drasten, a rare human among the Five Kings Mountains' military leaders.

A nearby peak bears the Carved Kings sculpture of King Kazmukh of Doggadth, which also overlooks the Great Pass.

Miluvabrecht.title: Miluvabrecht
Miluvabrecht.extract: Miluvabrecht is a ruined fortress in the Tanglebriar region that once served as a retreat for members of the magical order led by the elven wizard Aelthian. After he disappeared while fighting Treerazer, the place was abandoned and has been used as a lair by various demons and monsters for centuries.

City of Thorns.title: City of Thorns (disambiguation)
City of Thorns.extract:
City of Thorns can refer to:

  • The City of Thorns in Kyonin
  • Egorian, Cheliax, also known as the City of Thorns

Endless Cairn.title: Endless Cairn
Endless Cairn.extract: The Endless Cairn is an ancient elven mausoleum located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin near the border with the Tanglebriar.

Imvrildara.title: Imvrildara
Imvrildara.extract:
This article covers the temple of Calistria. For the organization, see Imvrildarai.

The ancient Calistrian temple of Imvrildara, located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, is the base for the goddess's most militant sect of worshipers: the Imvrildarai, defenders of the forest against Treerazer and its demonic armies.

Lethaquel.title: Lethaquel
Lethaquel.extract: Lethaquel is a site in the eastern hills of the elven nation of Kyonin, halfway between the Fierani Forest and the Sellen River.

Sun Dagger.title: Sun Dagger
Sun Dagger.extract: Sun Dagger is a ruined solar observatory in the eastern Fierani Forest of Kyonin that was abandoned by the elves when they abandoned Golarion just before Earthfall.

Wandering Spheres.title: Wandering Spheres
Wandering Spheres.extract: The Wandering Spheres is a massive ruined orrery located in central Kyonin with human-sized planetary spheres that once must have risen above the treetops. Its mechanism shattered long ago, and the many models of celestial objects now lie scattered about the glade, or are attached to metal beams that are twisted. No one knows how the orrery was destroyed, but what is stranger is that it models a solar system that is not that of Golarion.

Cutter's Holdout.title: Cutter's Holdout
Cutter's Holdout.extract: Cutter's Holdout is a small village near the borders of Druma and Kyonin, and has been disputed territory between the two nations since the return of elves to Golarion from Castrovel in 2632 AR.

Shaping Bluffs.title: Shaping Bluffs
Shaping Bluffs.extract:
The Shaping Bluffs are a two-mile-long geological feature along the Sellen River and the border it forms with Galt, and is known for the mysterious tendency of its bluffs to constantly reshape their faces. The steep cliffs expose red stone that changes erratically regardless of any winds or waters and can hide or reveal caves at water level.

The cliffs house a camp of dwarven stone shamans and geomancers, led by Hromcul Stetruk, who have stabilized their location enough to continue studying the phenomenon. The Shaping Bluffs are also used by river pirates who take advantage of the transient nature of caves that appear and disappear along the water level to raid traffic along the Sellen River.

Bloomwreath.title: Bloomwreath
Bloomwreath.extract:
Bloomwreath is a town situated in western Kyonin around a greenhouse called the Great Blossom. Botanists recognize the plant species preserved in the greenhouse as species lost during Earthfall.

The village's activities revolve around botany, from growing flowers to cutting topiary. The process is overseen by the founder, known as the High Gardener, who considers it her mission to preserve plants from around the world.

Thorngate.title: Thorngate
Thorngate.extract: Thorngate is a disabled elf gate near Tanglebriar in Kyonin. It was deactivated to prevent demons from corrupting it upon Treerazer's arrival and has remained inactive since then.

Gensmaren.title: Gensmaren
Gensmaren.extract: The ruins of an ancient Taldan supply post, Gensmaren is located in the Vergan Forest of Razmiran and is now the lair of a stranded group of driders who try not to attract the attention of the local human authorities.

Walking Man.title: Walking Man
Walking Man.extract:
The Walking Man is a 30-foot-tall tree-like construct sculpted to resemble a human man. It consists of at least four different trees bound together with ivy and other vines and covered in herbs and flowers. The flowers include amaranth, bluebell, buttercup, celandine, clover, creeping bellflower, daffodil, dragon's teeth, eyebright, goldenrod, hawksbeard, hyacinth, iris, knotweed, lily, mouse-ear, mudwort, nightshade, orchid, shepherd's purse, and tansy. It was most likely created by elves, but no one remembers when or how it was made.

Many have climbed inside of the construct's hulking body through an entrance in its shoulder and written notes. One such note, from Variel Morgethai, states that the burial mound Erithiel's Hall may somehow be connected to The Walking Man. In this room are panels that can act as controls for the construct, which were tested by Varian Jeggare. On midsummer each year, it takes one step towards Erages.

The druids have tried to communicate with the Walking Man to asks for its help to fight the Whispering Tyrant but the voice from within the construct was unintelligible. The elves have asked the Wildwood Lodge of the Verduran Forest for assistance in making their pleas hear by the ancient being.

Berm.title: Berm
Berm.extract: The Berm is a miles-long stand of semi-sentient trees that defend Kyonin's northern border with Razmiran along the Glass River. Nominally part of the northern Fierani Forest, and capable of independent movement, the Berm is greatly feared by the soldiers and agents of Razmir, who have never seen how it travels from place to place. Those who enter the forest are rarely seen again and their blood-soaked clothing is sometimes found downriver. The Berm's sudden morning appearance on what had been gentle plains the day before, is enough to give the stoutest Razmiri pause.

Caruskei.title: Caruskei
Caruskei.extract: Caruskei is a ruined temple to an unknown god, located in the far-eastern Fierani Forest of the elven kingdom of Kyonin, less than 50 miles west of Kallas Lake.

Dabril.title: Dabril
Dabril.extract: The town of Dabril is located on the Sellen River in the tumultuous nation of Galt, just south of Kallas Lake. Despite being on the river, the residents rarely work as river traders or fishermen. Instead, the people generally work as gardeners or perfumers, and the town has become known for its immense fields of roses and its manufacture of exotic perfumes.

Century Root.title: Century Root
Century Root.extract: The Century Root is a massive, half-mile-wide tree stump located in the southern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, just south and east of a bend in the Kyonin River. As of 4709 AR it was the location of a arboreal regent moot, a meeting of these woodland protectors from across the forest.

Erithiel's Hall.title: Erithiel's Hall
Erithiel's Hall.extract: Erithiel's Hall is a serpentine earthen mound located in the western Fierani Forest of Kyonin. Several stories in height, between 20 feet to 100 feet across and several miles in length, the mound is said to predate the elves and is rumoured to be connected to the fey of the First World. According to the most popular myth, Erithiel's Hall is named for an ancient fey hero, Erithiel, who in the dawn of history fought against the earth serpent Garukresh. Their epic battle is said to have left deserts and seas in its wake before Garukresh was finally slain where he now lies at Erithiel's Hall. The victorious Erithiel supposedly then built a vast barrow-palace within the remains of his defeated foe. Those few elves who have tried to excavate Erithiel's Hall have found themselves plagued with bad luck and earning the hatred of the region's fey creatures.

Moonpool Causeways.title: Moonpool Causeways
Moonpool Causeways.extract:
The Moonpool Causeways are a series of magical pools ranging in width from a few feet to half a mile located in the northeastern Fierani Forest of Kyonin. The pools have properties that are similar to common uses of chonomancy and the dragon Ekhazarean has been studying it for decades.

The pristine pools were besieged by omoxs sent by Treerazer to corrupt and destroy the fey that were allied with Kyonin but the magical defenses proved too much and the fey were able to withstand the assault.

Bieralei.title: Bieralei
Bieralei.extract: Bieralei is a fishing village located on the Kyonin River. The village is famous for its monthly fishing festivals at the time of the full moon, where locals use the occasion to show off their largest and most impressive catches. A crayfish called the Kavakex lives in the swamp's waters and is respected for its role in cleaning the waters of pollution.

Ryvanthnyl.title: Ryvanthnyl
Ryvanthnyl.extract:
Ryvanthnyl is a memorial located in the eastern part of Kyonin. The site is dedicated to the lives and traditions of people and cultures that perished and are remembered by the elves who preserve their memory by leaving behind works of art and objects of significance.

Ryvanthnyl is protected by gravekeepers and preserved through magic that safeguards the objects within, though the origin, era, and historical significance of many of these objects remain unknown.

Celwynvian.title: Celwynvian
Celwynvian.extract:
The ancient elven capital city of Celwynvian (pronounced sehl-WIN-vee-enn) sits amid the Mierani Forest in northern Varisia. Abandoned by the elves when they departed Golarion prior to the Earthfall, the city became overridden with drow, and was the site of one of the most vicious conflicts between the two elven races upon their return from Sovyrian. Taking considerable losses from the drow's evil tactics, the elves once again departed the city, and it remains empty and forbidden to this day.

The elves of the Mierani Forest strictly prohibit outsiders from entering the city, under the guise of wanting to preserve it as a refuge from the rest of the world, but those more familiar with the region sense that the elves themselves avoid the city and are still involved in an ongoing war to reclaim it.

Ravenmoor.title: Ravenmoor
Ravenmoor.extract: The Varisian village of Ravenmoor is a holding of Magnimar located on the shores of the Lampblack River, well off the main roads connecting Magnimar and Riddleport.

Brinewall.title: Brinewall
Brinewall.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Brinewall Legacy.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Brinewall was an abandoned settlement and castle (see Brinewall Castle) built during the early part of the Chelaxian expansion into Varisia (see Everwar). Situated in northwestern Varisia where the Steam River flows into the Bunyip Bay, it was designed to encourage trade with, and fend off attacks from, raiders of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Recently, it gained a new lease of life as a port in the new kingdom of Eurythnia in New Thassilon.

Mordant Spire.title: Eysuna
Mordant Spire.extract: The island of Eysuna, located in the Steaming Sea on the southern tip of the Ironbound Archipelago, is home to several thousand aloof and alien Mordant Spire elves who are hostile towards outsiders. The island is one of the few inhabited remnants of Azlant, and the elves closely guard the secrets of the once-great continent in fear that trespassers would bring a second Earthfall upon Golarion.

Averaka.title: Averaka
Averaka.extract: The town of Averaka is located on the southeastern coast of the island of Flintyreach in the Linnorm Kingdom of the Ironbound Islands and is predominantly populated by dromaars.

Frembrudd.title: Frembrudd
Frembrudd.extract: The town of Frembrudd is located on the south-western cliffs of the island of Battlewall in the Linnorm Kingdom of the Ironbound Islands.

Irrere.title: Irrere
Irrere.extract:
For another meaning of "Irrere", please see Irrere (town).

Irrere is an island in the Ironbound Archipelago in the Steaming Sea. Once an independent island—it does not form part of the Linnorm Kingdom of the Ironbound Islands—it was claimed by Belimarius in 4718 AR after the founding of New Thassilon. It is located between the islands of Chakikoth Isle and Syranita's Aerie.

Its population grew when a group of gnomes established the monastery of Shay Citadels, and gnome pilgrims often visit the island.

Taal Mornat.title: Taal Mornat
Taal Mornat.extract: Approximately six centuries ago, dwarves constructed the ossuary of Taal Mornat especially for the servants of the Cathedral of Magrim in Larrad as well as other faithful servants of the dwarven god. The necropolis lies some twenty miles west of the city in a hidden valley within the peaks of the Five Kings Mountains. Over time, due to the fame of many laid to rest there, the site is now a holy and cultural centre for the dwarven community.

Alstone.title: Alstone
Alstone.extract: Alstone is a fishing and farming village located on Flintyreach in the Ironbound Islands of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Alstone also has a nearby quarry.

Pediment Building.title: Pediment Building
Pediment Building.extract: The Pediment Building contains the Office of the Lord-Mayor, plus Magnimar's primary court and the Halls of Virtue, as well as its only prison. The latter is located beneath the building: a prison known to all Magnimar as "The Hells". The Pediment Building is located atop the wealthy Summit region in the Capital District near the Usher's Hall. The building itself is large and intimidating, its vast exterior topped with imposing architecture and scowling gargoyles. Its inhabitants are scarcely less intimidating: the stern faced judges of Magnimar's Justice Court.

Asleifar.title: Asleifar
Asleifar.extract: Asleifar is a large whaling village on the coast of the Arcadian Ocean in the Thanelands of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.

Skjoldmur.title: Skjoldmur
Skjoldmur.extract: Skjoldmur is a small whaling town in the south of the island of Aegos, the larger island comprising Broken Bay in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.

Seer's Home.title: Seer's Home
Seer's Home.extract: Seer's Home, located on the western coast of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, is Icemark's capital but no more than a large village by population. However, it is the largest and only permanently inhabited Varki shelter, and home to the Varki Seers, wizened elders who have retired to this more permanent residence.

Sunken City.title: Sunken City
Sunken City.extract: Sunken City is a Thassilonian ruin under the waves in the Howling Straits in the northern reaches of Broken Bay. Known as Udeomel to historians, the sunken city would have been a northerly settlement of Cyrusian.

Fifth Wind.title: Fifth Wind
Fifth Wind.extract: The Fifth Wind is a stone weather vane that rises from the Dockway district in Magnimar. The sides feature carvings of air elementals and cloud dragons. All of Outcast's Cove can determine the speed and direction by looking up at the windsocks. Those who spend time observing this monument are rumored to avoid seasickness.

Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages.title: Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages
Founder's Archive and Museum of Ages.extract: The Founder's Archive and the Museum of Ages are cultural and historical institutions that share a joint campus in the Capital District of the Varisian city of Magnimar. In Varisia, the museum is rivaled only by the Jeggare Museum in Korvosa.

Lost Lane.title: Lost Lane
Lost Lane.extract: Lost Lane is a street in Rag's End, one of Magnimar's poorest neighborhoods in the city's Beacon's Point district. It is also the location of the headquarters of Magnimar's premier thieves' guild: the Night Scales.

Harbormaster.title: Harbormaster
Harbormaster.extract:
For other meanings of "Harbormaster", please see Harbormaster (disambiguation).

In Magnimar, the Harbormaster refers to the building housing the city's harbormaster. As of 4712 AR, the harbormaster was Vert Aigerd, who replaced Odie Phexen by displaying an incredible ability to memorize ship schedules and names.

This three-story stone building is located in the Beacon's Point district. The distinctive red skiffs of the Harbormaster guides ship traffic through the complexities of Outcast's Cove.

Osprey Club.title: Osprey Club
Osprey Club.extract:
For another meaning of "Osprey Club", please see Osprey Club (Otari).

The Osprey Club, located in the Naos district of Magnimar, caters to a restrictive clientele of the richest families in the city. Gambling, drinking, and smoking are common activities here; there is also a brothel, for those so inclined. The exterior of the club is guarded by an iron fence to keep the onlookers outside. Green and gold magical lights illuminate the outside every night. The club is currently run by Kayasi Zivatchi.

Golemworks.title: Golemworks
Golemworks.extract: The Golemworks is one of the most iconic industries in Magnimar. As its name implies, it produces golems.

Bazaar of Sails.title: Bazaar of Sails
Bazaar of Sails.extract:
The Bazaar of Sails is the largest free market in Varisia. Located in Dockway, in the Shore region of Magnimar, the Bazaar of Sails is adjacent to the waterfront, wedged between the Seacleft and the Varisian Gulf, and nearly under the Shadow of the Irespan.

The Bazaar of Sails is probably the busiest district of the city, filled with the hustle and bustle of traders from dozens of nations all competing for space, attention, and profit. From Osirian spices to Andoren wood to Chelaxian clothing, the markets are filled with goods of every sort. With such a variety of wealth on display one might imagine the district to be riddled with crime, but it is well policed, though not by the Magnimar city watch.

Sabriyya Kalmeralm bears the title "Princess of the Market", which she inherited from her missing father Nazir Kalmeralm; with the title comes the responsibility for keeping the markets reasonably safe through whatever means necessary. Some critics view Sabriyya as little more than Magnimar's most open crime boss, but those who trade in the Bazaar of Sails understand the service she provides even if it sometime requires unsavoury methods.

Wyrmwatch.title: Wyrmwatch
Wyrmwatch.extract:
Both a functioning lighthouse and a tribute to Magnimar's founder Alcaydian Indros the Wyrmwatch, or Wyrmwatch Tower, is a one of the city's most prominent landmarks. The Wyrmwatch sits at the western most edge of Outcast's Cove at the very tip of Beacon's Point jutting out into the Varisian Gulf warning ships away from the dangerous spur of rock and welcomes them to the busy harbour of the Dockway and the Bazaar of Sails.

It supposedly sits upon the very site where Alcaydian Indros slew the mighty Sea Dragon known as Vydrarch which was one of the events that lead to the founding of Magnimar.

No-Horn.title: No-Horn
No-Horn.extract:
The No-Horn is a low quality tavern in Magnimar's Dockway neighbourhood. It typifies the sort of establishment that pops up periodically near the Bazaar of Sails, only to close again in short order. It is named for its sign, which depicts a hornless unicorn being tormented by drunken devils.

The structure itself is a rickety and leaning thing constructed from an old ship's hull. The establishment's barkeep is an ornery man missing both of his ears. Patrons can expect cheap food and drink here, but both are of an extraordinarily low quality.

Shadow Clock.title: Shadow Clock
Shadow Clock.extract:
The Shadow Clock is a 250-foot-tall clocktower in Magnimar's Underbridge district. It is a rusting and leaning structure of stone, wood, and metal. Like the rest of Underbridge it is in constant shadow from the Irespan. An angelic statue is positioned atop the tower, and the clock is frozen at 3 o'clock.

At least 10 people have died trying to climb the inside stairs, which earned them the nickname "Terrible Stairs". While ordered closed by the city, this tower still stands.

Local rumors say ghosts or other nasty inhabitants reside in the tower. However, as of 4712 AR it was the lair of the Scarecrow, a flesh golem made over 100 years ago by the Brothers of the Seven.

Versade Villa.title: Versade Villa
Versade Villa.extract: Versade Villa is the headquarters of House Versade, one of the aristocratic families of the Varisian city of Magnimar best known for throwing amazing parties. It is located at the end of a long line of upper-class villas in the Alabaster District, directly beside Valdemar Villa.

Mindurian Villa.title: Mindurian Villa
Mindurian Villa.extract: Mindurian Villa is home to House Mindurian of Magnimar. Filuvia Mindurian, one of the biggest gossips within the Magnamarian aristocracy, lives here.
