Vanderale Villa.title: Vanderale Villa
Vanderale Villa.extract: Vanderale Villa is the home of Magnimar's House Vanderale, one of the city's oldest families.

Battle of Charda.title: Battle of Charda
Battle of Charda.extract: The Battle of Charda was the first, and remains the most well-known, naval battle fought between Magnimar and the pirates of Riddleport.

Osprey.title: Osprey
Osprey.extract:
The druid Osprey is a member of the Pathfinder Society, although he tends to work outside of the organization's standard hierarchical framework. He gets his name from his habit of wildshaping into dark-eyed raptors.

The enigmatic Pathfinder is rumored to be writing a secret report that he guards jealously; only bits of it have been fed to the Decemvirate. Osprey is known to be an expert on the Kortos Consortium and their activities in Absalom and its sister-city, Diobel, most likely because he is a native of the latter settlement.

He was kidnapped in 4712 AR by the succubi of the Moonscar for some sinister purpose that had to do with the Pathfinder Society but was rescued by heroes sent by a member of the Decemvirate.

Fort Indros.title: Fort Indros
Fort Indros.extract: Fort Indros is located at the north-easternmost point of Magnimar, amongst the riches of the Alabaster District on a spur of land that juts out into the Varisian Gulf. The fort is named after its designer Alcaydian Indros, the founder of Magnimar and a watchful paladin of Aroden. The fort gives an unmatched view of the surrounding Gulf and serves as a deterrent to any pirates (from Riddleport or anywhere else) who might think of attacking the city. The fort bristles with siege weapons and bowmen. As of 4707 AR, it was overseen by the dwarven commander Vanns Wynmerd.

Serpent's Run.title: Serpent's Run
Serpent's Run.extract:
Serpent's Run is the largest building in Magnimar, quite a feat in the so-called city of monuments, and is located in one of the city's most prosperous areas, the Marble District. Serpent's Run is Magnimar's arena and most popular entertainment venue; the hippodrome can seat up to 5,000 people, over a quarter of Magnimar's total population. Unlike many other arenas, the Serpent's Run specialises in non-bloodsports like dog and horse racing, decathlons, and magic displays. However, the late-summer rodeos sponsored by the Matador's Lodge are also held here.

The arena is named for the carving of a gigantic serpent that runs around the upper rim of the arena, a tribute to the serpent slain by Alcaydian Indros. It is run by Master of Games Jorston "Axetongue" Droaeb.

Nirodin Villa.title: Nirodin Villa
Nirodin Villa.extract: Nirodin Villa is a stately townhouse in the northwest of Magnimar's Alabaster District. Situated directly next to Kaddren Villa, it is the seat of House Nirodin, and run by Cheiskaia Nirodin, the family's matriarch.

Whale.title: Whale
Whale.extract: Whales are massive aquatic cetaceans with fins, a tail with horizontal flukes, and blowholes on top of the head.

Rat.title: Rat
Rat.extract: Rats are small, prolific rodents found in urban areas. They are popular familiars among spellcasters.

Arvensoar.title: Arvensoar
Arvensoar.extract:
Arvensoar is the tower built by local Varisians and the citizens of Magnimar to replace the Seacleft Spire after it was destroyed by an angelic being. It took over 18 years to create the tower, but it now stands as a symbol of the city's receptiveness to the guidance of good spirits.

At approximately 400 feet in height, the tower is the tallest structure in Magnimar and stands over the districts of Lowcleft and Naos. The tower serves as the barracks and fortress for the local city watch and is defended with eight powerful trebuchets able to target any attackers regardless of the direction from which they attack.

Mistress of Angels.title: Mistress of Angels
Mistress of Angels.extract: The Mistress of Angels is a fine marble sculpture located in Magnimar's southern Ordellia district. It depicts Ordellia Whilwren, one of Magnimar's founders, scanning the horizon of the Varisian Gulf, seemingly looking out for danger. Whilwren was a cleric of Desna and one of Alcaydian Indros' colleagues at the founding of the city, and the statue is an occasional gathering place for Magnimarian Mystery Cultists.

Red Kestrel Imports.title: Red Kestrel Imports
Red Kestrel Imports.extract:
Red Kestrel Imports had existed in Magnimar since 4702 AR. Its red warehouse in the northern portion Ordellia district flies the flags of the six nations it operates out of—Absalom, Cheliax, Osirion, Katapesh, Sargava, and, of course, Magnimar.

As of 4712 AR, it was run by Anton Ghanafini.

Bent Rib Alley.title: Bent Rib Alley
Bent Rib Alley.extract: Bent Rib Alley is a small street that ends in a cul-de-sac in a poor part of Magnimar's Keystone district.

Dome of the Savored Sting.title: Dome of the Savored Sting
Dome of the Savored Sting.extract:
The Dome of the Savored Sting is a church of Calistria in the Varisian city of Magnimar, and is located on the northern edge of that city's Keystone district. The building is topped by a yellow and black dome with a black spire, and is considered somewhat garish.

Access to the temple prostitutes is only granted by the priests and priestesses to those whom they deem worthy. The church also offers paid revenge services, which the temple mistress—Ayamyra, as of 4712 AR—personally oversees, and dispatches patrols of leather-clad ruffians to keep the peace in the Keystone district.

These patrols often run afoul of the archers sent out by the Deadeye Lodge, Magnimar's temple of Erastil. Conflict between the two groups is exacerbated by territorial disputes, compounded by differences in how each group accomplishes their goal of keeping the streets safe.

Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgen.title: Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs
Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgen.extract: Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs is a curious shop run by the eccentric Billiver Billivin, and is located in the southwest of the Ordellia district in the Varisian city of Magnimar. A magic shop of sorts, Billivin's is overflowing with every sort of potion, elixir, scroll, alchemical supply, ingredient, book, papyrus, piece of sculpture, spell component, and other bric-a-brac imaginable, due to the fact that its gnomish owner is a compulsive collector (some would say hoarder) whose organizational skills leave something to be desired.

Boria's.title: Boria's
Boria's.extract: Boria's is an innocuous-looking wine shop on Lemnius Lane in the northern Naos district of the Varisian city of Magnimar, and is run by Todoban Boria. Numerous types of wholly unremarkable wines are displayed around the shop and a small statue of Cayden Cailean stands upon the front counter, all giving the impression of a perfectly ordinary establishment that sells wines. The truth is that Boria's is the city's premier supplier of poisons, manufactured or procured by the owner, and sold to a very selective clientele. Only with the proper references (such as from the Night Scales) does Boria display his true wares. The poison maker earns enough money from his illegal trade that he doesn't have to actually sell any wine. Because of this, Boria only makes enough of an effort to move his legal inventory so as not to arouse suspicion.

Embassy Building.title: Embassy Building
Embassy Building.extract: The Embassy Building in Magnimar is set aside for officially invited ambassadors from other countries. Many nations rejected the offer, but those who accepted exchanged functionaries and also receive lowered tariffs and increased trade. As of 4712 AR, the nations with representatives in Magnimar were Absalom, Andoran, Cheliax, Katapesh, Osirion, Qadira, Sargava (now known as Vidrian), Taldor, and Ustalav. Korvosa maintains offices here, but they are rarely used and remain empty most of the time. This two-story building is located in the city's Ordellia district.

Shark.title: Shark
Shark.extract: Sharks are a group of fish found throughout the seas of Golarion characterized by pectoral fins and five to seven gills on the sides of their head. Dangerous predators, they had evolved over the eons into primordial aquatic killing machines and have not changed much since.

Triodea.title: Triodea
Triodea.extract: The Triodea is by far the most important playhouse and concert hall in the whole of Magnimar, and possibly all of western Varisia. It sits in the Summit region in Magnimar's Naos district, and its entrance is located on Starsilver Plaza. The venue is owned by Durstin Versade; although the aiuvarin counter-tenor and dancer Kassiel Iylmrain is the one who makes all the important decisions.

Defiant's Garden.title: Defiant's Garden
Defiant's Garden.extract: Defiant's Garden is the mayoral residence of the city of Magnimar, located in the Vista district directly next to the Seacleft. It is the largest and most impressive home in all the city. Defiant's Garden became the official mayor's residence only with the rise of the decadent Lord-Mayor Haldmeer Grobaras; before Grobaras, the mayor's residence was a humbler place within the city that kept him in touch with the people. Grobaras, however, wanted nothing so humble and took Defiant's Garden, which had previously been a palace for visiting dignitaries, as his personal home. The building itself is closer in scale to a small castle than a manor, at eight stories tall and filled with room after room of salons and lounges all devoted to the mayor's pleasure.

Stone of the Seers.title: Stone of the Seers
Stone of the Seers.extract: Magnimar's most respectable school of arcane instruction, the Stone of the Seers, stands next to the Seerspring Gardens and its legendary spring.

Aquaretum.title: Aquaretum
Aquaretum.extract: A truly unique building along the wealthy water's edge district of Silver Shore in Magnimar, the Aquaretum serves as a guide to the underwater world of the Varisian Gulf. The Aquaretum is somewhere between a museum, a trophy room, and an aquarium, and combies stuffed trophies, regular exhibits, and live creatures from the depths of the Varisian Gulf. The Aquaretum is run by the eccentric gnome Nireed Wadincoast, a renowned expert on life below the waves, although few locals take his claim of "ancient cities beneath the waves" too seriously.

Burn.title: Burn
Burn.extract:
Located in the western part of the Keystone district, the area now known as the Burn was once home to the Varisian Council. When esteemed painter Goren Andosalu went mad and set his studio on fire, the fire spread to several other fine buildings in the area, killing over 48 people. Andosalu was never confirmed as one of the dead.

This destruction so close to their home building caused the Varisian Council to change where it convenes. Between this and the ghosts of these 48 residents that are rumored to haunt the location, most locals avoid the area. A local group of cat burglars named the Gargoyles uses the old Varisian Council building as a headquarters.

Cynosure Tower.title: Cynosure Tower
Cynosure Tower.extract:
Cynosure Tower is the temple to the goddess Desna's within the Varisian city-state of Magnimar and is located in the south of the poorer Keystone district. Even though it is unusual for Desna's priests and priestesses to build permanent structures in her honor, they chose to do so in Magnimar because it is home to so many of her devotees, especially among the Varisians.

The building is circular with a central courtyard open to the sky, and is maintained by a single priestess, Bevaluu Zimantiu. The temple is named after the goddess' fabled home in the heavens, the Sevenfold Cynosure.

Deadeye Lodge.title: Deadeye Lodge
Deadeye Lodge.extract:
The wooden Deadeye Lodge is the city of Magnimar's church of Erastil, and is located in the northeast of the Keystone district on the edge of Seerspring Garden.

Looking more like a rustic hunting cabin, Deadeye Lodge seems quite out of place among the townhouses of the city, but that does not seem to bother the devotees of Old Deadeye, who attend in large numbers. Its head priest, Father Lorgell Fendus, holds services three times-a-week, and is known for his welcoming nature. The Lodge also hosts a dozen archers who serve their god by keeping the streets of Keystone safe for all. This brings them into occasional conflict with the similarly armed followers of Calistria's Dome of the Savored Sting, whose methods and morals while maintaining the peace are quite different.

Pug's Contraptions.title: Pug's Contraptions
Pug's Contraptions.extract:
Pug's Contraptions is a clockwork-creation business and tinker shop in the northwest of Magnimar's Ordellia district that generally deals in small contraptions such as toys, unique locks, and other baubles. It is run by old Morgamer Pug, a halfling who is in direct competition with the much-larger Golemworks.

Pug has carved himself out a small niche in only dealing in smaller, clockwork machines, but would love to cut into the Golemworks' business. To this end, he has an ongoing agreement with Venture-captain Sheila Heidmarch of the city's Pathfinder lodge to buy any construct her agents may come across in their journeys. The guildsign for the store is a pig whose mouth is stuffed with lockpicks, a remnant of the owner's first business (which he still practices when he can't sell clockworks) of creating locks and other small metalworks such as bird cages.

House of Lords.title: House of Lords
House of Lords.extract: The House of Lords is a municipal building of Magnimar's city government in Varisia. Located in the wealthy Alabaster District, the temple-like structure holds offices for each of the city's seven great aristocratic families. Members of the public can appeal to the representatives of each of these families here in order to air grievances, ask for favors, or otherwise deal with the needs of the citizens at large. Magnimar's lesser aristocratic families do not have permanent offices here, but may rent them for a small fee should they so choose.

Dreaming Dryad.title: Dreaming Dryad
Dreaming Dryad.extract: The Dreaming Dryad is a high-class drug den in Magnimar. Situated in the Lowcleft district, customers receive privacy and uncontaminated drugs. Proprietor Vert Klydus designed the building to be a mix of maze-like corridors leading to variously sized rooms both above- and below-ground. The exterior's illusory appearance is of a beautiful glade. However, the glade appears to be sun-filled at night and nighttime during the day.

Lord-Mayor's Menagerie.title: Lord-Mayor's Menagerie
Lord-Mayor's Menagerie.extract: The Lord-Mayor's Menagerie is a public park located in the Capital District district of Magnimar. The park's unusual name stems from the collection of bizarre taxidermy and living creatures on display. Canayven Heidmarch and Sheila Heidmarch, a pair of local Pathfinders who run the Heidmarch Manor Lodge, use the park as a place to display their unique menagerie. This strange collection includes an eyeless, and therefore relatively harmless, basilisk, a tame 14-foot-tall ape named "Prince Mandali", and the egg of a tarn linnorm.

Heidmarch Manor.title: Heidmarch Manor
Heidmarch Manor.extract: Home of the noted Pathfinders Canayven Heidmarch and Venture-Captain Sheila Heidmarch, Heidmarch Manor is the first Pathfinder lodge in all Varisia, serving as the Pathfinder Society's base of operations into the rest of Varisia. Heidmarch Manor is located in Magnimar, the so-called "City of Monuments", within the prosperous Alabaster District of the city's wealthy Summit area. The manor is located just north of the Triodea in the neighboring Naos District and contains all the usual amenities of a Pathfinder lodge—including some with a Varisian twist.

Lady's Light.title: Lady's Light
Lady's Light.extract: The Lady's Light is an enormous statue located on the western end of Lady's Cape, in the south-westernmost reaches of Varisia's Mushfens approximately 50 miles from the city of Magnimar as the crow flies. Almost 200 feet in height, the statue resembles a woman in flowing robes with one breast bared, holding a staff aloft.

Fancy Reefclaw.title: Fancy Reefclaw
Fancy Reefclaw.extract: The Fancy Reefclaw is a gaudily painted, but successful, brewery located in Magnimar's Lowcleft district. The front is painted red and yellow, with an image of a well-dressed reefclaw. Owned by House Deverin, it is operated day-to-day by Golliver Haskinswurth. The brewery is best known for its raspberry ale and lambics.

Seven's Sawmill.title: Seven's Sawmill
Seven's Sawmill.extract: Seven's Sawmill is the oldest of the six major sawmills on Kyver's Islet in Magnimar and has never changed ownership. Production occurs here, but not in significant volumes, because it is used as the meeting place of the secret society called the Brothers of the Seven. Few know of the group or the meetings.

Derexhi Villa.title: Derexhi Villa
Derexhi Villa.extract: Derexhi Villa is the seat of House Derexhi, one of the Varisian city-state of Magnimar's founding families. Located in the upscale Alabaster District, it was run by the family's head, Randred Derexhi, who was a member of the Justice Council.

Bronze House.title: Bronze House
Bronze House.extract: The ornate Bronze House is the regional headquarters for the international corporation known as the Aspis Consortium in the Varisian city of Magnimar. Located in the city's working-class Dockway district, it was established in the early years after Magnimar's founding by the Aspis Consortium's executive board in the Chelaxian city of Ostenso, in the hopes of looting many of the ancient Thassilonian ruins that dot the Varisian landscape. The house's executive director is Gold Agent Maiveer Sloan.

Deverin Villa.title: Deverin Villa
Deverin Villa.extract: Deverin Villa is the main residence of House Deverin, an aristocratic family in the Varisian city of Magnimar. Located in the Naos district, Deverin Villa is currently occupied by Hobart Deverin, the head of the family. A walled compound with a central main building and several secondary structures, Deverin Villa is sandwiched between Kaijitsu Manor and the House of Welcome, Magnimar's most exclusive brothel.

Pig.title: Pig
Pig.extract:
This article is about the domesticated animal. For its wild relative, see boar. For the tavern in Magnimar, see Pig (tavern).

Pigs are small, omnivorous, four-legged animals often domesticated for their meat and hide, but are also clever, sociable, and trainable on their own. Some spellcasters even take pigs as familiars, especially halflings and gnomes and spellcasters in Varisia.

Large pigs are sometimes called hogs, female pigs sows, and infant pigs piglets. Boars are their wilder ancestors, and part of a broader family of animals known as swine.

Magnimar.title: Magnimar
Magnimar.extract: The city of Magnimar (pronounced MAG-nih-mahr) stands as one of the two major city-states of southern Varisia, and sits in the shadow of the most awe-inspiring ruins of ancient Thassilon, the crumbled cyclopean bridge called the Irespan.

Windsong Abbey.title: Windsong Abbey
Windsong Abbey.extract: Windsong Abbey is a pacifist refuge for followers of all religions—good and evil—to come and resolve their doctrinal and political differences in a non-violent manner and to peacefully further their own goals.

Foxglove Manor.title: Foxglove Manor
Foxglove Manor.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Skinsaw Murders.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Foxglove Manor is a haunted mansion on the western coast of Varisia (also known as the Lost Coast), approximately halfway between Magnimar and Sandpoint. Constructed in 4624 AR by minor Magnimarian noble Vorel Foxglove, and funded by the Brotherhood of the Seven, Foxglove Manor was intended to be the ancestral home of Foxglove's family for 100 years before reverting to the Brotherhood's ownership. Three generations of Foxgloves have met tragic ends in the Manor, from Vorel and his wife to Aldern Foxglove, who perished in 4707 AR. The manor consists of three stories overlooking a high sea cliff, a basement and natural cave network, and a ruined outbuilding.

As of 4710 AR, Foxglove Manor is believed to still be haunted by Vorel's malign spirit and is currently uninhabited.

Silver Mount.title: Silver Mount
Silver Mount.extract:
The jagged metallic peak of the Silver Mount is the above-ground remnant of the largest of the fallen starships that crashed in Numeria during the legendary Rain of Stars thousands of years ago. The edifice is honeycombed with chambers and was once heavily guarded by servants of the since-defunct Technic League.

This scarred monolith looms over the capital city of Starfall and the strange knowledge and technology found therein is the source of much of the Black Sovereign's (and Technic League's) power. Even though Silver Mount is closely guarded by the soldiers of the Black Sovereign, certain brave scholars and adventurers continue to try and enter the structure in the hopes of discovering untold wealth or technology from beyond this world.

Wisher's Well.title: Wisher's Well
Wisher's Well.extract:
Wisher's Well is one of the lesser known Thassilonian ruins in the greater Sandpoint area. This circular stone tower is 30 feet high, and the interior drops away into a 100-foot-deep shaft, ending in an underwater cavern connected to Sog's Bay.

This submerged complex was formerly the laboratory of Mazmiranna, who later abandoned it when she became the fifth runelord of greed. It has since been taken over by the albino alghollthu master Vorimorath, who has been drawn to Wisher's Well by the promise of Thassilonian treasures and the alghollthu master has restored Mazmiranna's wards and guardians. Myriad monsters also dwell in the complex, including Vorimorath's ulat-kini and faceless stalker minions, the latter of which have been modified to be able to breathe water.

Paupers' Graves.title: Paupers' Graves
Paupers' Graves.extract:
Paupers' Graves is a small graveyard three miles east of Sandpoint along the old Varisian Kaspakari (traditional coastal route along the Lost Coast). It was used as a graveyard by the poor of Sandpoint and the laborers from Magnimar who built the town. Usage dwindled once the Sandpoint Boneyard was opened to all regardless of ability to pay.

Several years ago Jediah Kheln robbed the cemetery of its medallion of the Empyreal Lord Ashava. Kanker, ghoul priest of Kabriri, tricked Kheln into this theft. Kheln was repaid by Kanker with his death and transformation into a ghoul. Since then Kheln has turned the graveyard into his personal domain, building an underground warren for himself, his ghoul thralls, and his feral ghoul hounds.

The warrens extend throughout the region, reaching under Devil's Platter and even to the old smuggler's tunnels beneath Sandpoint. Kanker presides over an ancient temple to Kabriri somewhere in the tunnels.

Battle of Grasyhot.title: Battle of Grasyhot
Battle of Grasyhot.extract: The site of the Battle of Grasyhot can be found northeast of the Numerian capital of Starfall, just south of the Egelsee River near the border with Mendev. In it, the forces of the Kellid Queen Boliga Bharsolm fought with the 30 tribes of the hill giant chief Urgo Axbiter. The battle began in 1288 AR and continued on and off for three years, with both sides experiencing advances and retreats. When Axbiter was killed, his followers erected a huge earthen mound over his grave near the battlefield, so that he might continue to inspire them in death. The climactic confrontation of the battle ended in victory for the Kellid tribes. Queen Bharsolm met her death during it, and was interred in a similar earthen tomb at the battle site, so that she might eternally delight in the hill giants' defeat. Both burial mounds still stand today, a lasting tribute to heroism and determination.

Plain of Ten Thousand Swords.title: Plain of Ten Thousand Swords
Plain of Ten Thousand Swords.extract: The Plain of Ten Thousand Swords in Numeria's Felldales is the haunted site of a bitter conflict from centuries past. The swords of thousands of Kellid warriors jut from the ground, each cursed with the bitter spirit of a warrior slain in the pointless war and bound to their weapon.

Licktoad Village.title: Licktoad Village
Licktoad Village.extract: The Licktoad Village was where the Licktoad goblins of Brinestump Marsh lived in huts. The atmosphere was foul, and since it blocked a road, they harassed travelers who tried to pass through the area. The leader of the tribe was Rendwattle Gutwad, who didn't speak because his deep voice would terrify his subjects.

Threshold.title: Threshold
Threshold.extract:
For other meanings of "Threshold", please see Threshold (disambiguation).

Threshold was a prison tower in the High Cairns of Sarkoris reserved for those who practiced arcane magic. It became the epicenter of the Worldwound upon its opening in 4606 AR.

Herne's Oak.title: Herne's Oak
Herne's Oak.extract: Herne's Oak is a barrow deep within the Estrovian Forest of Mendev and the final resting place of the ill-fated hunter Herne Vilhaur. It is guarded by his descendants that were transformed by the curse of the winterthorn: the herne.

Star Keep.title: Star Keep
Star Keep.extract:
Star Keep is a fortress on the Mendevian banks of the West Sellen River that overlooks the southeastern plains of Sarkoris. As of 4716 AR, prior to the Worldwound's closing, it was staffed by a number of holy paladin orders who worked independently from the national command in Nerosyan. Rumors along the Crusader Road claimed that the soldiers of the keep launched strikes deep into the Worldwound to track demonic troop movements, or made precise incursions to take down particularly troublesome opponents.

The fate of Star Keep since the closure of the Worldwound is unknown.

Severed Spiral.title: Severed Spiral
Severed Spiral.extract:
The Severed Spiral is a circle of 43 standing stones surrounding seven dolmens located deep within the Estrovian Forest of Mendev. The western half of the circle's stones are made of dark, rough-hewn basalt and other dark-colored stones, while the eastern stones are made of white, smooth dolomite.

The circle is the only permanent holy site of the Children of Balance, an organization of religious two-headed trolls led by Romrix who worship deities that most closely embody the duality and dichotomy of their lives—especially Pharasma, Nethys, and Gozreh.

Chapel of the Argent Shield.title: Chapel of the Argent Shield
Chapel of the Argent Shield.extract: The Chapel of the Argent Shield is an Iomedaean temple located on the road between Kenabres and Egede. The chapel's walls are marked by many shrines and tributes honoring the fallen Mendevian Crusaders, as well as art mapping the region's numerous battlefields.

Devil's Rest.title: Devil's Rest
Devil's Rest.extract: The Devil's Rest is a centuries-old trading post located on the southern edge of the Sarkoris Scar in Sarkoris. Its name is said to be derived from its purported founder, a Chelaxian noble.

Icerift Castle.title: Icerift Castle
Icerift Castle.extract: During the First Mendevian Crusade, the government of Mendev decided to establish a stronghold on its northern border with the Worldwound. As a result, work began on building Icerift Castle at the source of the West Sellen River near the Crown of the World. Before the castle was finished, however, the Mendevian forces had a change of heart. Conscious of the difficulty in supplying and reinforcing such a remote outpost, they decided to cancel the construction. A party of soldiers was sent north to escort home the construction crew and their guards, only to find that they had all been slaughtered and their hearts had been torn out. Even stranger, the attack did not appear to have been carried out by demons. The superstitious indigenous people of Mendev blamed the legendary wendigo for the killing, but no investigation was carried out; instead the site was declared anathema and removed from the nation's records.

Monastery of Tala.title: Monastery of Tala
Monastery of Tala.extract: Located in central Mendev, the Monastery of Tala caters to many faiths of the crusaders going to fight the demons in the Worldwound. Run by a cloistered order of monks from numerous faiths named the Brotherhood of Tala, it was founded in 4702 AR as a place where the various churches could conduct research on how best to combat the demonic forces of the Worldwound.

Saintsgrove.title: Saintsgrove
Saintsgrove.extract:
Saintsgrove is a small area of ancient olive trees in the south-east of the county of Sinaria, in Ustalav, lying near the southern banks of Lake Prophyria, and to the south-east of Karcau.

Legends speak of a priestess of Urgathoa who performed a sacrilegious ceremony to transform herself into a Daughter of Urgathoa at this spot, perverting a shrine of Desna and sacrificing the followers of the Song of the Spheres as part of her ritual. Such blasphemy apparently stirred Desna to direct action against the priestess by immediately ageing the olive trees by a thousand years, trapping the blasphemer and preventing her ritual from succeeding, while providing a grave for her dead worshippers.

Circle of Hierophants.title: Circle of Hierophants
Circle of Hierophants.extract:
The Circle of Hierophants is a circle of stone menhirs created in Old Sarkoris and located in the Stonewilds of modern Sarkoris. Druids of the Green Faith worshiped here, as this was the greatest of their stone circles.

Hundreds of stones of varied sizes make up the many circles located at the site. Many of the circles has stones placed at exact angles beyond the known ability of man to build repeatedly. Each stone has rituals, legends, and secrets carved into them. Unlocking these carvings is beyond those who do not practice the Green Faith. Some of these circles are on small hills overlooking the main circle, giving the area a feeling of depth. Six of the circles are around cave mouths that reportedly led to deeper caves and even more stone circles. While leaders of the faith once protected these caves, after the opening of the Worldwound corrupted siabraes lived within.

Pulura's Fall.title: Pulura's Fall
Pulura's Fall.extract: Pulura's Fall is a former waterfall located in Sarkoris near the Sarkoris Scar, northeast of the ruins of Iz. Maybe one of the only places not fully controlled by demons in the Wounded Lands while the Worldwound was open, a temple of Pulura led by the powerful cleric Eliandra stood in the dried-up waterfall.

Te Taspeh Valley.title: Te Taspeh Valley
Te Taspeh Valley.extract:
Te Taspeh Valley is a peaceful land dotted with the ruins of ancient wooden homes believed to be the surviving remnants of Mendev's once-nomadic society. Unusual carvings associate these buildings with these ancient people, who were believed to settle only in locations of great spiritual importance.

As of 4725 AR the valley's ruins had yet to be investigated in large part due to the possessive behavior of a nearby adamantine dragon.

Jundlan Wrecks.title: Jundlan Wrecks
Jundlan Wrecks.extract: The pirate lords of Issia (now part of Brevoy) once engaged one another in a massive naval battle on the Lake of Mists and Veils. During the battle, the fleets were caught up in a massive storm and wrecked off the coast of Brevoy in Jellicoe Bay. The wreckage remains there to this day, and is known as the Jundlan Wrecks. Presumably a considerable amount of pirate treasure can be found amongst the frost-covered tangle of wrecked ships and ancient, waterlogged corpses. However, numerous unfriendly fey now live amongst the destruction, building altars and structures made from the dead pirates' treasure. On moonless nights at midnight, ghostly bells toll here and gangplanks made of bone extend from the shipwrecks and invite all to board.

Scrapwall.title: Scrapwall
Scrapwall.extract: Scrapwall is a town in the Sellen Hills built out of a gigantic technological junkyard almost 3 miles in length. Having never had a formal leader in its thousands of years of history, the city is instead run by disparate rival gangs.

Sable Barrow.title: Sable Barrow
Sable Barrow.extract:
Sable Barrow is a hill that overlooks the Numerian city of Chesed. The Pathfinder Gojan the Sharp observed a golden statue appear on the Barrow the night before a seemingly inevitable slave revolt. The statue hefted a mighty hammer and seemed to be threatening the city with it, and carried the mark of the god Abadar on his chest armor. Gojan believed that the statue's presence prevented the slave revolt, and it disappeared four days later. Most agree that the golden statue was actually the Lawgiver, the herald of Abadar.

Lawgiver's appearance instead inspired the slaves to join with the city's defenders in order to repel an unprecedented, and since unequaled, assault upon the city by barbarians. After the city's successful defense against the raid, Chesed abolished its slave trade, emancipated its slaves, and decreed that any slave brought into the city would be declared free within an hour of arrival if not registered with the city's government.

Crowhollow.title: Crowhollow
Crowhollow.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Divinity Drive.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Crowhollow is a sinkhole in the Sellen Hills of northeastern Numeria, and the site of a piece of the Divinity that fell during the Rain of Stars. It is also the home of a half-mechanical lich named Alling Third, a former Technic League agent who sought immortality in order to fully exploit its technology.

Hollow Garden.title: Hollow Garden
Hollow Garden.extract:
The Hollow Garden is a large entertainment module of the spaceship Divinity which was detached from its deck and slammed into Numeria, in the area now called Sovereign's Reach, during the Rain of Stars. It is currently run by Celmak Toysiel, a malfunctioning mannequin robot, who turned it into a trap for adventurers who have to provide 'entertainment' by traversing a dungeon customised by Celmak that only ends with their deaths.

Celmak uses the entertainment module's remaining functional sensors to scan for potential victims. When someone approaches within a mile of the Hollow Garden, it begins to emit pulses and holograms (particularly idyllic gardens) to entice them. The doors to the bunker are always open with flickering lights, outwardly resembling a recently opened, malfunctioning, undefended section of the Divinity, ripe for plunder. Once the prey enters, the door seals and the only escape from that point is death; no teleportation is possible within the Garden.

Celmak then greets explorers and explains that they are destined to make history; if they survive, then the rewards will be great, turning them into heroes rich beyond imagining. At the same time, it also scans their brains for their emotions and abilities, and analyses them to optimise the entertainment it provides, before opening the doors to the Hollow Garden. As its guests traverse the dungeon, fighting robots, and overcoming obstacles, Celmak records their thoughts and actions via hidden cameras, and crafts each encounter to test the responses of explorers, sometimes releasing psychoactive chemicals to 'heighten the drama', or offering commentary if its victims are having a too easy or too hard a time. Occasionally, a minor item from Celmak's hoard is given to its current victims, hand-picked to prolong the entertainment but never allowing success.

In addition to Celmak and its robots, the Hollow Garden has recently become refuge to a group of demons fleeing the Worldwound. They accepted Celmak's offer to be occasionally sent into scenes as desired, but their leader Vlathherex found that they cannot teleport within the Hollow Garden, and now suspects that Celmak will never allow them to leave, and seeks to disable this lock.

Academy of the Reclamation.title: Academy of the Reclamation
Academy of the Reclamation.extract: The Academy of the Reclamation in Nerosyan is an academy focused on the preservation and restoration of knowledge and history from the lost nation of Sarkoris and its diaspora following the closure of the Worldwound into the Sarkoris Scar. This extends to pursuing secrets of Sarkorian gods and the Sarkoris Scar itself.

Ayin Lun.title: Ayin Lun
Ayin Lun.extract:
Ayin Lun is a long-lost ruined city in northern Mendev that had been overridden by undead in generations past. Survivors of the city erased all records of the city after its destruction, and its suspected site was revealed only after the death of Gorum in 4724 AR.

Possibly during the Godsrain that ensued, nine stony spires erupted across Mendev, each containing a gap at several hundred feet of elevation that purportedly lead to strange lands of hostile beings. These towers became known among Mendevians as Godsfall towers.

As of 4725 AR, the towers' purpose and origins are unknown, and Mendevian Chancellor Irahai had officially prohibited climbing the towers and posted guards at each. The towers nonetheless remained scaled by young native Mendevians who consider them to be rites of passage, and many have successfully returned from their attempts.

Saresp Mountain.title: Saresp Mountain
Saresp Mountain.extract: The cave-pocked Saresp Mountain, a peak in Mendev's far eastern reaches, was once the site of many ancient tombs as well as shelters for families seeking refuge from the harshest parts of winter. The caves hosted many Sarkorian refugees after the opening of the Worldwound, but all disappeared near the end of the Mendevian Crusades, their caves either sealed or apparently struck by grave robbers.

Starrise Spire.title: Starrise Spire
Starrise Spire.extract:
The Starrise Spire, the tallest building in Nerosyan, is located on the city's outer wall in the Confluence District neighborhood of Coopertown. It is topped by a celestial light that continuously shines in even the darkest of nights and never fades as long as at least one defender still resides in the tower. This beacon provided hope and relief to adventurers that crusaders still stood against the demons of the Worldwound, and continues to operate as a center of restoration efforts since the Worldwound's closure into the Sarkoris. Before the Pathfinder Society was tasked to protect the city, the city's crusaders defended its walls.

The Starrise Spire's venture-captain is Jorsal of Lauterbury, a well-decorated Mwangi man with over 50 advances into the Worldwound under his belt. Jorsal delegates divine magic training and consecration of the Spire's holy sites to Seeker Ereth Brightmane.

Stom's Claim.title: Stom's Claim
Stom's Claim.extract: Stom's Claim is a ramshackle silver mining camp deep in the Menador Mountains between Cheliax and Nidal.

Acisazi.title: Acisazi
Acisazi.extract: Acisazi is a small aquatic elven shoreline village in Ravounel, north of the Ravounel Forest. It was a part of Cheliax until 4717 AR, when the Archduchy of Ravounel seceded and became an independent nation.

Whiterock.title: Whiterock
Whiterock.extract: Whiterock is a city located on the bank of the Yolubilis River, 50 miles south of Kintargo. It has long been maintained by the wealthy House Jhaltero, and originated as a stop where rocks mined from Jhaltero quarries were smoothed and catalogued for shipment to Kintargo, but has since grown into a market town for local farmers and ranchers who do not wish to take the long trip to the capital. Its ruler, Canton Jhaltero, is casual and disinterested, viewing himself due an easy life after securing his family's place on the Silver Council.

Cypress Point.title: Cypress Point
Cypress Point.extract: The small town of Cypress Point, located about 40 miles northeast of Kintargo, on the point of land furthest north on the coast, is the largest settlement in Ravounel east of the Yolubilis River.

Auginford.title: Auginford
Auginford.extract: Auginford is a small village in southern Nidal near the southern Usk River.

Thrune's Chance.title: Thrune's Chance
Thrune's Chance.extract:
Thrune's Chance is a coastal hamlet on the edge of the North Plains in the west of Nidal on that nation's border with Cheliax. The hamlet is managed by the Chelaxian embassy in Pangolais. Most of its inhabitants are ambassadors or agents of House Thrune, under the command of Admiral Ferixia Rone. Each year, the embassy selects five Nidalese to work for a year at Thrune's Chance on joint activities between the two nations. If they complete the year successfully, the chosen ones can become Chelaxian citizens and live in the empire.

Thrune's Chance is an opportunity for the Umbral Court to rid itself of citizens dissatisfied with the government without provoking them to openly rebel. Those who take this opportunity are never allowed to return to Nidal.

Hurlandrun.title: Hurlandrun
Hurlandrun.extract: Hurlandrun was a Nirmathi village located near the source of the Deepcut River in the Mindspin Mountains in the south-west of the country. The disaster that led to the village's destruction was not due to war, but to the actions of individuals. A powerful mage named Mahalagris lived a quiet life in the village until he was killed and then returned as a dangerous undead being. This, combined with an attempted robbery of his tomb, unleashed his wrath, and the destruction he unleashed razed the village to the ground.

Scallion's Wrap.title: Scallion's Wrap
Scallion's Wrap.extract: Scallion's Wrap is a village in Nirmathas whose only reputation lies in the way they prepare for Winter Week. Many decades ago, a pack of wolves carried off the village's sheep and destroyed the crops in the weeks leading up to Winter Week. It was then that a witch, using her cauldron and a magic rock, created stews for the entire village. Since then, the preparation of this event has fallen to the witch's descendants, currently the halfling Kupyn Baro.

Ecru.title: Ecru
Ecru.extract: Ecru was a mining village in the Hollow Hills specializing in extracting valuable sulfur from nearby mines, which was sold at a good price to neighboring towns. The town fell under the control of the Ironfang Legion in 4717 AR and they used it as a base for transporting supplies throughout the region.

Pangolais.title: Pangolais
Pangolais.extract: Pangolais is the capital city of Nidal. Located deep within the Uskwood, the trees' heavy canopy makes even the brightest day nearly as dark as the blackest night in the softly glittering, city of shadows. Visitors to the city must seek permission to enter, and once inside must seek the services of a local guide. These guides come at great expense since visitors can easily become lost in the darkness of the city, and because it is illegal for outsiders to roam about unattended. Hidden worshipers of Desna and the rare diabolist also make their homes in Pangolais. These rare few provide the only possible assistance to visitors who arrive without the permission of the city's rulers, the mysterious Black Triune.

Albatross.title: Albatross
Albatross.extract: The small, coastal fishing town of Albatross perches precariously on a cliffside off the coast of Nidal on Conqueror's Bay. Although it is a settlement of less than 400 souls, it is home to many times that number of its namesake avians. Residents of Albatross claim to be able to predict the weather, foretell ill omens, and see the approach of death simply by observing and interpreting the flights of these birds.

Brimstone Springs.title: Brimstone Springs
Brimstone Springs.extract: Brimstone Springs is a small settlement high in the Mindspin Mountains of Nidal known for its hot springs: the Soulsheen Baths. Hundreds of people from all over the region come every year to soak in its sulfur-rich mineral waters that fall in natural cascades, giving the town a rather unpleasant smell. These waters then collect to form the source of the Sulfur River, which flows westward out of the mountains toward Usk Lake. Brimstone Springs has also gained a certain amount of notoriety from the golden dream, a special, day-long immersion in poisonous waters that, if it doesn't kill the user, gives him or her visions of the afterlife while permanently dying the skin a deep yellow.

Crosspine.title: Crosspine
Crosspine.extract: Located along the southeastern edge of the Uskwood, Crosspine is a village that stands out due to its distinctive practice of guiding a remarkably elevated proportion of its youth into the paths of arcane and druidic learning. Among its notable denizens are Isiem, a prominent shadowcaller, and Ascaros, a renowned sorcerer skilled in shadow magic.

Graybanks.title: Graybanks
Graybanks.extract: Graybanks is an abandoned Nirmathi town south of Kraggodan.

Elith Lorin.title: Elith Lorin
Elith Lorin.extract: Elith Lorin is one of the most pleasant settlements in Nidal, and is one of the most important transportation hubs in the nation.

Icebow Bridge.title: Icebow Bridge
Icebow Bridge.extract: The village of Icebrow Bridge is a darkly scenic and quaint little village that sits in the centre of the shadowy nation of Nidal. Icebrow Bridge would be an unremarkable little village were it not for the Library Without Light, a vast collection of ancient, pre-Earthfall Azlanti and Thassilonian texts that were brought by fleeing survivors of this ancient apocalypse to Nidal. The ancient scrolls and books cover a wide variety of often obscure topics but have zero cohesion as a collection, as they were whatever texts their long-dead owners valued enough to take with them when fleeing their homes. The head librarian Hale Craggox controls access to the Library Without Light. Due to the prestige of the library, Icebrow Bridge is considered quite a desirable posting for agents of the Umbral Court.

Kayalhi.title: Kayalhi
Kayalhi.extract:
Kayalhi is a village inhabited mainly by fetchlings, or kayals as they themselves prefer to be termed, located just outside the north-eastern limits of the Uskwood. The community is able to survive thanks to its self-sufficiency, and contact with human visitors is infrequent.

The village is led by Chancellor Zelvith, who regularly travels to Pangolais to demonstrate loyalty to the Umbral Court, although the locals are not particularly devoted to Zon-Kuthon or other deities. Zelvith also maintains an extensive network of spies abroad, and it is this service that keeps the Umbral Court happy and in check.

The village is famous throughout the region, and even fetchlings from other villages stay informed about its events. Several taverns have been built in these villages, bearing the name Kayalhi as part of their title, and fetchlings can gather and dine with others like themselves in these taverns.

Snowford.title: Snowford
Snowford.extract:
Snowford is a village nestled in the foothills of the Mindspin Mountains of eastern Nidal close to the border with Molthune. It is dedicated to goat herding and famous for producing Snowserpent's Kiss, a local alcoholic beverage. Every year during the winter solstice, an event called the Glassmoon Witch occurs, where a circle of obsidian stones five miles from the village is illuminated and an ancient shrine appears. It is said that those who offer gifts throughout the year can ask the spirit for a blessing, but only if the request is altruistic and helps others.

The inhabitants of Snowford avoid revealing the location or effects of the Glassmoon Witch, fearing the wrath of the Umbral Court, which would consider it heretical, especially as some associate the phenomenon with Shelyn. The village mayor, Wistea Glasswing, keeps the village's attention at bay by ensuring that visitors leave early and never discover the village's secret.

Stormhollow.title: Stormhollow
Stormhollow.extract: Stormhollow, formerly known as Lorra's Hollow, was a village located in the Umbral Basin that was destroyed by velstracs during a shadowstorm. The village was founded by halflings who escaped the empire of Cheliax and sought a more fulfilling life. Those hopes were shattered by the velstrac attack, which transformed the inhabitants into greater shadows that now roam the village. Their houses are now filled with spikes and nails, and the flora has vanished from the streets.

Pezzack.title: Pezzack
Pezzack.extract: Pezzack is a town in western Cheliax on the Sallow Coast of the Arcadian Ocean. Nestled in Devil's Perch among the Menador Mountains, the isolated town has long been a seedbed for sedition against the infernal government far way in Egorian. The crown has responded with several naval blockades and year-long sieges that have left the settlement quite isolated from the rest of the country, and moved the seat of the Archduchy of Hellcoast from Pezzack to Belde.

Belde.title: Belde
Belde.extract: Belde is a town in western Cheliax situated on the banks on the Maiestas River, between the Menador Mountains and the Barrowood. Despite being geographically located in the Archduchy of the Heartlands, it serves as capital of the rebellious Archduchy of Hellcoast.

Bodie's Haunt.title: Bodie's Haunt
Bodie's Haunt.extract:
Bodie's Haunt was once a settlement in the River Kingdoms, in what is now called the Protectorate of the Black Marquis.

Up until 4707 AR, it was home to one of the three rival Marquises vying for control of the Pirate Pact. However, in that year Urdal Bazzak arranged for the inhabitants to be slaughtered by Numerian soldiers as his first step to gaining control of the Protectorate.

Today, the town is a ruin haunted by the angry souls of the slain pirates.

Blackcove.title: Blackcove
Blackcove.extract: Blackcove is a small and very isolated fishing village on Cheliax's western Hellmouth Gulf. The ruined island of Nal-Kashel is located two miles off the coast from the village.

Narona.title: Narona
Narona.extract: The ruined city of Narona is located on Hellmouth Gulf on the western coast of Cheliax on the Arcadian Ocean. The city was destroyed by the Chelaxian Navy because of its resistance against the rule of House Thrune. Unlike the inhabitants of the nearby city of Pezzack, Narona's residents never returned after the settlement's destruction.

Blackridge.title: Blackridge
Blackridge.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Nightglass.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Blackridge is the young capital of the Chelaxian region of Devil's Perch, within the Archduchy of Hellcoast.

Ciricskree.title: Ciricskree
Ciricskree.extract: Ciricskree—the Screeching Spire in the Strix language—is the largest strix settlement in the Inner Sea region and perhaps in all of Golarion. It is located in the southern Menador Mountains of Cheliax, in the Archduchy of Hellcoast.

Bluestone.title: Bluestone
Bluestone.extract: Bluestone is a hamlet on the border of Molthune and Nirmathas that frequently changes hands between the two nations. It was originally built around a circle of stones and a shrine to Gozreh. Its frequent changes in ownership has weakened the town's economy considerably, and the village no longer contains many elaborate structures outside the original Gozren shrine.

Old Detmer.title: Old Detmer
Old Detmer.extract: Old Detmer is the nickname given by the locals to the underwater ruins of the city of an ancient civilization. Standing stones and antimagic wards scattered around the area make it difficult to explore fully.

Tradecross.title: Tradecross
Tradecross.extract: Tradecross is a small collection of farmhouses and an inn in the Plains territory of Molthune. The town is mainly known for the occasional appearance of the mysterious Tradecross Peddler.

East Rikkan.title: East Rikkan
East Rikkan.extract: The small port towns of East Rikkan and Westpool, located north of Westcrown in Cheliax, are known collectively as the Rikkan Ports. These settlements house large numbers of merchants who trade goods up and down the River Adivian, and as of 4709 AR (prior to the abolition of slavery in Cheliax) also housed the numerous slaves required to operate the Stavian Arches and portage stations. Although Westpool is larger, East Rikkan has more docks for fishermen and travelers.

Kantaria.title: Kantaria
Kantaria.extract: The Chelaxian town of Kantaria stands just west of the Sedna River, between the Barrowood and the Menador Mountains to the north. It is the capital of the Archduchy of Menador.

Khari.title: Khari
Khari.extract: Khari is the southernmost settlement of Cheliax, lying at the southern end of the Arch of Aroden and the Hespereth Strait that it once spanned. Since the independence of Sargava, Khari is the only Chelaxian city left on the continent of Garund. Formerly a Rahadoumi city, Khari is the largest settlement in the province of Kharijite in the Archduchy of Longmarch.

Windspire.title: Windspire
Windspire.extract: Windspire is an independent roost of the Windspire tribe of strix in Devil's Perch, within the borders of the Chelaxian Archduchy of Hellcoast.

Alabaster Academy.title: Alabaster Academy
Alabaster Academy.extract:
See also: Category:Images of Alabaster Academy

The Alabaster Academy is a widely renowned university in Kintargo's Villegre district, though most of its prestige lies in its past. The academy's reputation was ruined in 4599 AR when a professor was revealed to be a murderer known as the Temple Hill Slasher, and the Chelaxian Civil War ensured it never fully recovered.

The university's focuses include the sciences of botany, medicine, and zoology; practical training in apothecary and healing; and, as a recent addition, arcane magic untainted by Chelaxian requests to incorporate diabolism.

Since at least 4715 AR and as of 4724 AR, the university's headmaster was Iylvana Desdoros.

Everpine.title: Everpine
Everpine.extract: Everpine is an unusual fey settlement in the western Barrowood of Cheliax.

Tastikka.title: Tastikka
Tastikka.extract: Tastikka is a small village of the strix Kitkasiticka tribe in the Ravounel Forest. They traditionally isolated themselves from human contact until the defeat of the Grundlescorn sisters encouraged them to pursue careful and cautious negotiations. The tribe's rokoa Rarrnir, who came to power after the abdication of her predecessor Solmestria, had met with representatives from the Silver Council as of 4719 AR but as of that time had yet to secure an agreement concerning the new settlements along the Ravounel Forest's borders.

Crooked Cove.title: Crooked Cove
Crooked Cove.extract: Crooked Cove, often referred to as Crookcove or Crook Cove, is a small village on the southwest coast of Ravounel. It houses an abandoned observatory.

Sember Cove.title: Sember Cove
Sember Cove.extract: The village of Sember Cove is nestled just inside the Ravounel border at the point it meets the Hellcoast in Cheliax.

Wati.title: Wati
Wati.extract: Wati, also called the Half-City, is located in southern Osirion at the confluence of the Asp and Crook rivers and the birth of the River Sphinx. It is best-known for its enormous necropolis, or city of the dead, a relic of a bygone plague.

Fort Hazard.title: Fort Hazard
Fort Hazard.extract: Fort Hazard stands on the bluffs overlooking Port Peril, capital of the Shackles. Its strategic position allows it to control both the town and the harbour. It was the stronghold of the late former Hurricane King Kerdak Bonefist. The tallest of its seven defensive towers is also the location of the lighthouse known as Besmara's Beacon.

Gardens of Shepeska.title: Gardens of Shepeska
Gardens of Shepeska.extract: The Gardens of Shepeska are located on the northeastern tip of Osirion, where the Inner Sea meets the Obari Ocean. They are tended by druids who have granted many saplings to the crown prince at Sothis.

Nicyruse.title: Nicyruse
Nicyruse.extract:
Nicyruse was an ancient city of the Jistka Imperium located today in southern Avistan, on the Inner Sea coast. It housed one of the largest temple-forges in all the Imperium, rivaling even that of Rachikan. When Jistka's doom became imminent, divine earthquakes tore the region apart and city's forges were abandoned. Now the seaside ruins of Nicyruse have mostly eroded away by the Inner Sea.

In 4712 AR, reports from the Chelaxian city of Hinji to the east stated that a recent storm had cleared a new entrance to the buried site. Fisherfolk said that mechanical creatures began moving across the shore near the ruins, grabbing fish from the ocean with their bare hands, and taking them back into the ruins. The Pathfinder Society has called for adventurers to explore the ruins and bring back any information about lost Jistka.

Mystic's Redoubt.title: Mystic's Redoubt
Mystic's Redoubt.extract: Mystic's Redoubt is the only public arcane library in Port Peril. It is a six-winged circular tower in Crescent Harbor, and it charges fees of as much as 1,000 gold pieces per year for its use. A small number of apprentices join the library each year, while other scholars visit Mystic's Redoubt to study relics in its antiquities hall. Rare natural components also grow in an adjacent, well-maintained garden.

Qumarin.title: Qumarin
Qumarin.extract: The remnants of the lost and accursed city of Qumarin are located in the southern Meraz Desert of Qadira. Its location has been largely forgotten except for a few reclusive girtablilu tribes and it is avoided by everyone.

Harborhorn.title: Harborhorn
Harborhorn.extract:
The Harborhorn is the home of Port Peril's harbormaster, who is responsible for inspecting every ship that docks at the pirate port. It is located on its own small island just off the larger island of Crescent Harbor.

As of 4712 AR, Harborhorn was the home of the notoriously stern dwarven harbormaster Tsojmin Kreidoros. Little goes on within the bustling city that escapes the harbormaster's notice.

Latchmin's Folly.title: Latchmin's Folly
Latchmin's Folly.extract: Latchmin's Folly is a squat sea fort constructed on a rise in the High Eastwind district of Port Peril.

Mermaid's Bucket.title: Mermaid's Bucket
Mermaid's Bucket.extract: The Mermaid's Bucket is a famous tavern located next to the main docks of Crescent Harbor in the pirate city of Port Peril. A rowdy bar, the Mermaid's Bucket is decorated with numerous depictions of merfolk, fish masks, and other nautical paraphernalia of all sorts, including an enormous mosaic of a mermaid behind the bar. The Mermaid's Bucket's proprietor Nefti Unwesha also supplies rum to every bar, tavern, and brothel in town, buying out her competition with strangely minted silver coins.

Sea Wrack Chapel.title: Sea Wrack Chapel
Sea Wrack Chapel.extract:
The Sea Wrack Chapel is a small temple dedicated to Gozreh that serves the pirate city of Port Peril.

Like many seaside settlements, Port Peril has a small shrine dedicated to Gozreh for pious sailors to make offerings to the Lord of Waves. No larger or more impressive than the shrine of a typical fishing village, Sea Wrack Chapel is made from driftwood and discarded fishing nets but is still large enough to provide shelter to those seeking Gozreh's guidance.

Sea Wrack Chapel is tended to by Father Ignalus, a halfling priest who accepts Gozreh worshippers of any creed, including in the form of Shimye-Magalla, a dualistic deity worshipped in the Mwangi Expanse. Father Ignalus is also a meticulous record keeper and has kept track of every single shipwreck that has occurred in the vicinity of Port Peril.

Tolguth.title: Tolguth
Tolguth.extract: Like the Realm of the Mammoth Lords' largest settlement, Icestair, Tolguth is nestled against the icy walls of the Crown of the World. Unlike Icestair it is a warm, almost tropical town, surrounded by great ferns, rhododendrons, and green cycads. The reason for this vast difference in temperature is the volcanic vents and thermal springs that dot the area. Within this valley roam fantastic creatures that are not found anywhere else in Avistan. These include duck-billed dinosaurs, swift raptors, mighty tyrannosaurs and predatory terror birds like the axe beak. Unsurprisingly, the city is walled for the protection of its inhabitants, but these walls provide only a slight hindrance to the largest of the valley's creatures. Despite being surrounded by a dinosaur-infested wilderness, the biggest danger to the people of Tolguth comes from the Worldwound and its fell demonic influence.

Heldren.title: Heldren
Heldren.extract: The peasant village of Heldren is located in the Sophra Prefecture of southern Taldor, close to the Border Wood.

Al-Bashir.title: Al-Bashir
Al-Bashir.extract: Al-Bashir is a harpy-infested ruin and oasis in the central Pashman district of Qadira, located on the edge of the Zho Mountains near the Pashman River. It is avoided by the caravans that pass through the mountains. Despite attempts by the Qadiran government, the hundreds of harpies who roost there have never been driven off enough to explore the vast ruins and recover the substantial treasure believed to be within. The songs of the creatures have drawn entire herds of antelopes and camels to their doom.

Shadun.title: Shadun
Shadun.extract: The ruins of the Qadiran town of Shadun are located in the eastern foothills of the Zho Mountains, on the opposite side of the mountain range from Gurat. The hillsides around the town were once covered in terrace farms watered by the tributaries of the Pashman River, and criss-crossed with green trails. Tragedy struck when the nearby volcanoes Zhonar and Zhobl began spewing great clouds of dust and ash that covered the countryside, forcing the town's inhabitants to flee. No one is quite sure what happened to the citizens of Shadun, and today its desolate streets are stalked at night by strange shadowy figures with eyes like glowing embers.

Koor.title: Koor
Koor.extract: Koor is a ruined forge-city found in the northern part of Qadira in the Zho Mountains. Once known for producing incredible weapons and armor that were shipped throughout the Inner Sea region, Koor was abandoned in the 41st century AR.

Yalakheen.title: Yalakheen
Yalakheen.extract: Yalakheen is a sacred city of the deity Yahaiya located in the Alavah Desert of Qadira. Its residents are priestesses of the goddess who tend to visitors seeking their prayers to be fulfilled, usually through suicidal sacrifice.

Al-Hiraf.title: Al-Hiraf
Al-Hiraf.extract: Al-Hiraf is a Qadiran city in the south-western corner of the Maharev region, one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Al-Hiraf itself is located on the eastern bank of the Maharev River a few miles upriver of the metropolis of Qaharid.

Naamat.title: Naamat
Naamat.extract: Naamat is a luxurious tent city located in the Northern Zho Mountains region of Qadira on the banks of the River Ladan. The Northern Zho Mountains region is the poorest region in Qadira and faces the neighbouring land of Taldor just across the Jalrune River. As such, it is the least hungry for war with their neighbors.

Ishad.title: Ishad
Ishad.extract: Ishad is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located on the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's south-western border with the neighbouring region of Tapur.

Kerim.title: Kerim
Kerim.extract: Kerim is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located some miles east of the banks of the Pashman River. The river is Pashman's south-western border with the neighbouring region of Tapur.

Riddleport.title: Riddleport
Riddleport.extract: Riddleport is the third-largest city in Varisia and its northernmost port. The city is a haven for pirates and sea-faring brigands, who find themselves far to the north of the Arch of Aroden. Teeming with criminals and rogues of all kinds, Riddleport is full of potential danger and hard-to-avoid intrigue. Also known as the City of Cyphers, Riddleport takes its name from the Cyphergate, a giant stone arch spanning the natural cove around which the city is built. It is covered on both sides with ancient Thassilonian symbols, although their exact meaning is unknown.

Thistletop.title: Thistletop
Thistletop.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: Rise of the Runelords (Burnt Offerings).
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The island of Thistletop is located in the Varisian Gulf off the coast of the Nettlewood.

Jormurdun.title: Jormurdun
Jormurdun.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Secrets Stones Keep, Legacy of the Stonelords, The Sky Key Solution.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Jormurdun is one of the ten ancient Sky Citadels built by the newly emergent dwarves at the end of their Quest for Sky. Lost from history for thousands of years after invasion and disaster until 4713 AR, it is located in the Wolfcrags, a largely uninhabited range of hills in northwestern parts of Sarkoris's (formerly the Worldwound's) Frostmire region.

Shadow Spring.title: Shadow Spring
Shadow Spring.extract: Shadow Spring is one of the northernmost hot springs in the Frostmire Fen, which can be found in the region of Sarkoris known as Frostmire. A small village of degenerate humans surrounds Shadow Spring (the village and the spring share the same name), populated by the descendants of evacuees from the nearby city of Dyinglight.

Well of Tainted Virtue.title: Well of Tainted Virtue
Well of Tainted Virtue.extract: The Well of Tainted Virtue is a temple of Lissala in the Stony Mountains that survived the destruction caused by Earthfall. The temple has halls dedicated to each school of magic practiced in the time of Thassilon. A modern cult of the goddess protected the site, and the Aspis Consortium was able to make contact with them to reach an agreement in which they would open a backdoor to the Hao Jin Tapestry, which their enemies in the Pathfinder Society had obtained. In 4712 AR, a team of pathfinders raided the temple, eliminating their opponents and using the site to travel between Absalom and Varisia.

Paddlefoot Farm.title: Paddlefoot Farm
Paddlefoot Farm.extract: The Paddlefoot Farm is located near the Soggy River, and the halfling family that runs it doesn't make money from the farm itself, but rather from the illegal toilets they smuggle into the river and along the roads. Their illegal activities have been largely ignored because Sandpoint has more pressing problems to solve. The farm's appearance is terrible, and it's clear that no one here takes farm work seriously.

Eastern Lighthouse.title: Eastern Lighthouse
Eastern Lighthouse.extract: The Eastern Lighthouse is one of the most enduring structures in Devil's Elbow, having been built through the efforts of Yaris Neraken and being the spot from which the poor man jumped to his death. Even after decades of neglect, the exterior of this 40-foot-tall tower remains, covered in grime, yes, but still standing.

Devil's Elbow Harbor.title: Devil's Elbow Harbor
Devil's Elbow Harbor.extract: The Devil's Elbow Harbor is located in the only safe place for a ship to dock on the island, as the rest of the coast is full of reefs. The island's inhabitants built piers and buildings here, which are now just ruins and abandoned stone walls.

Western Lighthouse.title: Western Lighthouse
Western Lighthouse.extract: The Western Lighthouse at Devil's Elbow was destroyed by fire shortly after its completion, and although the inhabitants of Witchlight intended to rebuild it someday, that moment never came, and it remains infested with insects that have moved into the ruins.

Devil's Elbow Sea Cave.title: Devil's Elbow Sea Cave
Devil's Elbow Sea Cave.extract: The Devil's Elbow Sea Cave was the home of the legendary siren Virashi, who arrived on the island over six centuries ago. After her death, it was inhabited by her spirit until the drow arrived and took over the cave to investigate the effects of the meteorite that had struck the island in 4708 AR.

Valley of the Pyramids.title: Valley of the Pyramids
Valley of the Pyramids.extract: The Valley of the Pyramids, uncovered during a recent khamsin storm, is an unusual landscape of twisted, sand-carved canyons not fully seen since the time of the Four Pharaohs of Ascension. Rumors hold that the four pyramids contain magical portals that allow access to the extradimensional tombs of the Four Pharaohs. As of 4708 AR, artifacts from the tombs had already begun appearing in Sothis's bazaars.

Litran.title: Litran
Litran.extract: Litran is a Galtan city best known for being the headquarters of the mysterious executioners known as the Gray Gardeners, and because of their intimidating presence, it was one of the most stable cities in the nation.

Ahn'Selota.title: Ahn'Selota
Ahn'Selota.extract: The mysterious pyramid of Ahn'Selota, also known as the fabled Pact Stone Pyramid, is located near the ruins of Tumen in the Scorpion Coast region of northeastern Osirion. The pyramid is somehow linked with the magical pact that united the Four Pharaohs of Ascension more than 6,000 years ago. It is said that Ahn'Selota houses the secret of the pharaohs' powerful pact that joined their fates.

Thrylheim.title: Thrylheim
Thrylheim.extract: Thrylheim is the ruined city of the frost giant nation that once controlled much of the Fog Peaks. It was destroyed in a devastating war with the rime dragons of the mountains and is now largely in ruin. Underground portions of the city are still said to exist, and hold the frost giant sword Eldavar, used by the last konig of Thrylhim, Virtosk, to kill the elder rime dragon Keljanir.

Androsphinx of Zukebri.title: Androsphinx of Zukebri
Androsphinx of Zukebri.extract:
The Androsphinx of Zukebri is an ancient monument in Osirion's Underdunes in the form of a sphinx, with a bronze-cast face of an unknown pharaoh and massive ruby and sapphire adornments. Powerful magic protects it from the Underdunes' constantly shifting sands and leaves it at the center of a perfectly circular crater of flat ground, and any who approach it are assaulted by its piercing telepathic screams similar to mind quakes. The monument also seems to weep tears of unknown black fluid.

A cult of Lamashtu, comprised of the rare people unaffected by its telepathic attacks and possibly inspired by another Lamashtan cult near Eto, worships the monument and has sacrificed travelers who draw near. Occult researchers have attempted to link the androsphinx to the 11 gifts reportedly given to the Four Pharaohs by "Emissaries" from Aucturn in the Calosian Manuscript.

Lost Mine of Siwat.title: Lost Mine of Siwat
Lost Mine of Siwat.extract: The Lost Mine of Siwat is a lost silver mine located in the Sahure Wastes of central Osirion. It was originally founded in 4349 AR, but mysteriously disappeared only a few years thereafter. Few people today even remember that the silver mine, and the small village of Siwat that grew up around it, ever existed.

Water Gardens of Xerbystes.title: Water Gardens of Xerbystes
Water Gardens of Xerbystes.extract: The Water Gardens of Xerbystes were commissioned by Xerbystes I, the first Qadiran satrap of Osirion in the 16th century AR. Feeling that the delta of the River Sphinx was underutilized, the satrap ordered a complex of gardens and pools to be built on an island, and had it shored up against flooding. The new Keleshite elite frequented the garden, but it was avoided by the Osirians, who felt that the attempt to hold back the river was disrespectful to the water spirits. The river reclaimed the island in the great flood of 1535 AR, killing everyone who was there, and it has never been reoccupied. The island occasionally reemerges, leaving parts of the Gardens high and dry, but they are soon submerged in the Sphinx's floods again.

Kublan Grove.title: Kublan Grove
Kublan Grove.extract:
The Kublan Grove is no clump of woodland but a tangled mass of rafters, scaffolds, and masonry that serves as a testament to the flighty whims of the Taldan aristocracy. Located in Taldor's Lingian Prefecture, the Kublan Grove began life as a construction project that started soon after the prefecture's rise in popularity. Approximately twenty years ago, three Opparan baronets, all cousins from House Kublan, borrowed against their family's ancestral lands, buying up several miles of farmland to construct a grand retreat for the nobility. In their lust to provide every possible luxury, the project's scope increased massively, bloating from an original plan for a simple manor house to, eventually, being an entire community devoted to vice and hedonism, but it was not to be.

Funding dried up and construction halted. Now the half-finished buildings lie abandoned amidst a forest of scaffolding, empty save for a few wild animals and a community of squatters. House Kublan occasionally hires bands of thugs to sweep through the grove and chase off the squatters, desperately hoping to attract new investors as the Lingian Prefecture's popularity grows.

Pegaduor.title: Pegaduor
Pegaduor.extract: Located in the rural province of Kazuhn in Taldor, Pegaduor is a vast underground city that dates back to the Azlanti Empire and Earthfall.

Wreck of the Kaijitsu's Blossom.title: Wreck of the Kaijitsu's Blossom
Wreck of the Kaijitsu's Blossom.extract: The Wreck of the Kaijitsu's Blossom is hardly identifiable as such, since crashing into the swamp and the passage of decades have caused nature to consume much of the ship. When the crew members heard that the crew of their sister ship, the Kaijitsu Star, had drowned, they risked leaving by land before the ship sank.

Kravenkus.title: Kravenkus
Kravenkus.extract:
The dwarven hold of Kravenkus stands in the World's Edge Mountains, in Taldor. It is one of the original Sky Citadels, and still maintains a connection to the Darklands.

Like the other Sky Citadels, Kravenkus was built by the dwarves soon after their Quest for Sky as a fortress from which to continue their epic war against the orcs. Kravenkus is one of the four remaining in dwarven hands.

Kravenkus is something of a ghost town, consisting of around 2,500 dwarves protected by a small garrison of only 400. The inhabitants typically reside in the central "keep" area. Most of the outskirts of the citadel have been abandoned due to regular attacks from Darklands creatures, including hryngars, mongrelfolk, and worse. While a few stubborn souls eke out a living in the empty halls and corridors, most of the remaining population live in the citadel's nearly abandoned central keep. The mines that once provided the wealth of the Kravenkus have long since been played out, resulting in the departure of most of the populace to the Five Kings Mountains.

Despite these hardships, the people of Kravenkus are proud of their home and refuse to seal the Darklands tunnels, viewing them as a connection to their life prior to the Quest for Sky. The dwarves welcome adventurers who offer assistance against the underground menaces.

Dalaston.title: Dalaston
Dalaston.extract:
Dalaston is a large town on the southwestern edge of Taldor's World's Edge Mountains. Although members of all the major races reside in the town, dwarves are the second-largest group after humans. The three major employers are the local iron mine (owned by the Dalassenos family), the Morian Foundry, and the Irini brewery.

In 4709 AR, the red dragon Horranath attacked Dalaston.

In 4725 AR, the Godsrain awakened a dragon that devastated the village, and only a few inhabitants have not left the area.

Faldamont.title: Faldamont
Faldamont.extract: Faldamont is a small town in the western foothills of the World's Edge Mountains in northern Taldor.

Talamir.title: Talamir
Talamir.extract: Talamir, now known as Dragonscar, was a wealthy city whose prideful citizens once painted many of its buildings green and blue to match the flag of Taldor. It is now an ash-encrusted ruin charred by both volcanic and draconic flame.

New Oppara.title: New Oppara
New Oppara.extract:
New Oppara (pronounced new oh-pa-ra) is the capital of the nation of Linvarre, formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar, on the continent of Tian Xia. It was rechristened in 7108 IC (4608 AR) by its colonial founder, General Orphyrea Amanandar, who defeated the local warlords who had previously controlled it. Its original Tien name was Kamikobu (pronounced ka-mi-koh-bu).

Positioned near the Ilushe River and bathed in its mists, New Oppara features architecture blended between Tian and Taldane styles. The city also includes the Raocado Market of hawkers and the Ocallio Institute of magical practice.

Mewru.title: Mewru
Mewru.extract: Mewru (pronounced miu-ROO) is a city-state of outcasts, criminals, and scavengers of the underwater nation of Xidao, located in the Xidao Gulf of eastern Tian Xia. It is located in Western Xidao, the part west of the Aya-Maru trench.

Rippleden.title: Rippleden
Rippleden.extract: Rippleden is a small, isolated village located in the Verduran Forest region of Andoran, along a slower patch of the Sellen River. Its inhabitants are known for their friendly, chatty nature, and the town itself boasts cheery-looking houses covered in thatched roofing. The inhabitants of Rippleden are renowned for their distrust of the residents of nearby Whiterush, claiming that Rippleden and its residents are obviously the superior of the two rival villages.

Funako.title: Funako
Funako.extract: Funako (pronounced fu-na-ko) is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. It is home to most of the nation's elven population and known for its Jininese metalworking and dawnsilver trade.

Toduc Wa.title: Toduc Wa
Toduc Wa.extract: Toduc Wa (pronounced tu-duk wah) was once the largest city in the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. The Monastery of the Blended Forms, a renowned martial arts retreat inspired by Taldor's Tian-founded Monastery of the Seven Forms, is located in Toduc Wa.

Vigil's Rest.title: Vigil's Rest
Vigil's Rest.extract: Vigil's Rest is a small outpost near the border with the Whistling Plains in Avin Prefecture. The Taldan Horse offers no protection beyond this point, and at least one regiment can always be seen in the area. Although Vigil's Rest is not especially populated, the local market is well supplied as it is on a main trading route. In addition to the Taldan Horse, the Lion Blades have members at the outpost who interrogate a good number of caravan workers that arrive from Casmaron.

Goka.title: Goka
Goka.extract: Goka (pronounced go-kar) is a free city-state on the western coast of Tian Xia. Formerly part of Imperial Lung Wa, it broke free when the empire collapsed and is now enjoying a golden age of trade and prosperity. Goka is a vital port city that rivals Absalom and Katapesh in size and diversity, and serves as a gateway to Tian Xia for travelers from the west.

Zom Kullan.title: Zom Kullan
Zom Kullan.extract: Zom Kullan, the capital city of Nagajor, is located inside a volcano deep in the Kullan Dei mountain range. The volcano Zom Kullan is built around is still active and it is believed that it is only through rituals conducted by the royal naga queen, First Mother Vassath Shethagri and her naga royal family that stop the volcano's fury and prevent its eruption.

Thulsadus.title: Thulsadus
