Thulsadus.extract: Thulsadus is one of the many cities of the naga empire of Nagajor. Nagajor consists of 25 different kingdoms, each of them ruled over by their own naga monarch; the biggest cities of Nagajor each form the capital of a smaller naga kingdom.

Vannarak.title: Vannarak
Vannarak.extract: Vannarak is one of the many cities of the naga empire of Nagajor, and is located in its far western reaches on the nation's northernmost island. A relatively new city, it is built around and within the remains of a massive beached kaiju; its residents mine valuable materials from the beast's remains, and academics and wizards travel to study still-unexplored parts of the corpse.

Langkhu.title: Langkhu
Langkhu.extract: Langkhu is a city of inland Goka, near a gap in the Wall of Heaven in western Tian Xia.

Ngōn Hoa.title: Ngōn Hoa
Ngōn Hoa.extract: Ngōn Hoa (pronounced ng-ON hwah) is the ornate, soaring capital of Xa Hoi and one of Tian Xia's largest cities.

Surat Prakan.title: Surat Prakan
Surat Prakan.extract: Surat Prakan (pronounced sue-RA-ht pra-KHAN) is the traditional capital of Tang Mai, home of its ruling kamraten and their Cinnamon Throne, and is the Tian Xia nation's largest city.

Sangpo-Jong.title: Sangpo-Jong
Sangpo-Jong.extract: Cut into the side of a mountain, Sangpo-Jong is the nominal capital of Zi Ha in Tian Xia and an isolationist city dominated by samsarans. Most outsiders are forbidden from the city, but for those who can enter, Sangpo-Jong is full of scenic wonders and epic stone carvings.

Babschu-Jong.title: Babschu-Jong
Babschu-Jong.extract: Babschu-Jong is a major city in the samsaran country of Zi Ha on the continent of Tian Xia. It is located on a southern peak in the Kelsang Mountains and is a major trade connection on the route between Linvarre's capital of New Oppara and Zi Ha's nominal capital of Sangpo-Jong. Primary trade materials include artwork, gems, herbs, incense, livestock, and stone from Zi Ha, which are traded for grain, iron, and textiles.

Tuotuo.title: Tuotuo
Tuotuo.extract: Tuotuo (pronounced tow-tow) is a city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other deities other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.

Changdo.title: Changdo
Changdo.extract: Changdo (pronounced CHANG-dew), also called the Imperial City, is the capital of Po Li and was the original capital of Lung Wa, the empire that dominated Tian Xia until the death of Aroden.

He Ming.title: He Ming
He Ming.extract: He Ming (pronounced huu ming) is the rowdy capital of Quain, in southern Tian Xia, and home to the nation's temples and government. Its heroes often demolish property while testing themselves and each other in its bustling streets, to such a point that tradesfolk have a well-known saying about the city: "To grow rich, move to He Ming."

Nanzhu.title: Nanzhu
Nanzhu.extract: Nanzhu, an agricultural center of southeastern Quain in Tian Xia, is also known for its sprawling and supposedly haunted hilltop cemetery and the monkeys that roam its streets.

Xiwu.title: Xiwu
Xiwu.extract: The fortress city of Xiwu, one of the most populated settlements of Tian Xia—or indeed, on Golarion—is the capital of militaristic Lingshen. The city appears to be a military installation unlike anything else in the world.

Haseong.title: Haseong
Haseong.extract: Haseong is the cosmopolitan capital of Hwanggot in eastern Tian Xia. This bustling port city is known for its diverse crafts, artworks, and industries, and the many festivals that promote and celebrate them. Each of its many festivals feature competitions and are open to outsiders.

Maecho.title: Maecho
Maecho.extract: Maecho is a city in Hwanggot, a nation in Tian Xia. In contrast to Haseong, Maecho is a slower-paced city, making it a relaxing destination. The city's many hot springs are its largest attraction.

Jitai.title: Jitai
Jitai.extract: Jitai (pronounced gee-TAI) is a city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other gods other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.

Yuming.title: Yuming
Yuming.extract: Yuming is a massive city located in the former heartland of Imperial Lung Wa in the Successor State of Po Li. The nation is ruled by the powerful Oracular Council that allows no worship of other deities other than that of the idea of the Eternal Emperor.

Danjing.title: Danjing
Danjing.extract: Danjing is a vast metropolis in the eastern reaches of the Successor State of Lingshen and its inhabitants number almost 100,000 people. It is located near a unique, massive, impenetrable, unnaturally perfect pillar of resonant, humming crystal that predates the known history of Tian Xia. The crystal's mysteries have drawn large numbers of nagaji, magic users, and alchemists to the site, making the city one of Lingshen's most diverse.

Thap Samut.title: Thap Samut
Thap Samut.extract: Thap Samut (pronounced: Tahp sahm-oot) is the northernmost city of the sorcerous nation of Tang Mai, located in the far north-east of the country. Thap Samut also serves as Tang Mai's largest port, where fortunes can be rapidly made and easily lost.

Peijia.title: Peijia
Peijia.extract:
Peijia is the largely empty capital of the Tian Xia nation of Bachuan, built on the northern shore of Naikang Bay. Established by the country's founder Grandfather Pei, the city was designed to hold up to 200,000 inhabitants. However, as of 7211 IC barely 50,000 people had followed Grandfather Pei's commands and relocated there, leaving much of the city uninhabited.

As of 7224 IC, many of the city's buildings are overrun with monsters, magical plants, and squatters despite a recent influx of investment in the city.

Hisuikarasu.title: Hisuikarasu
Hisuikarasu.extract: Hisuikarasu (pronounced hee-soo-ee-Kah-rah-soo) is the capital of the tengu nation of Kwanlai, in Tian Xia. A relatively prosperous city in chaotic Kwanlai, Hisuikarasu is ruled by the half-celestial benevolent autocrat Lady Sutarai-Gongen. Lady Sutarai-Gongen's popularity is such that her power is beginning to spread beyond the capital to the rest of Kwanlai.

Khitai.title: Khitai
Khitai.extract: Khitai is a lively city in the Tian-Shu nation of Quain, on Tian Xia, that is well-known for its many festivals and dancers that attract swathes of foreign visitors. These dances are based on various creatures including dragons, lions and basilisks.

Mukinami.title: Mukinami
Mukinami.extract: Mukinami (pronounced moo-kee-nah-mee) was once a free city where people from the four provinces that make up modern Songbai could meet and trade freely. Now, it serves as the capital of the recently formed nation. The nation's ruler, the Sun Shogun, governs from the city but in order to try and assuage the former nobility of the realm, he takes advice from a quarrelsome group called the Four Notables—a quartet of aristocratic families that previously ruled Songbai's four provinces.

Zetang.title: Zetang
Zetang.extract: Zetang is capital of the empyrean-ruled Tian Xia nation of Tianjing. The Nyenreng Conclave rule from here, many of them too frail to leave the city. A port city on Point Aykishe along the nation's eastern coast, Zetang was victim of Zo pirate attacks when Lung Wa fell but has since been rebuilt. Rumors suggest the unhappy dead still persist among the shipwrecks in its harbor.

Okim.title: Okim
Okim.extract: Okim is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. Formerly a military outpost, Okim is now primarily known for its fireworks production, which is said to have drawn organized criminals from Seidoyaji and Todorokaze seeking to sell black powder across neighboring Kwanlai.

Nagaiyamatsu.title: Nagaiyamatsu
Nagaiyamatsu.extract: Nagaiyamatsu (pronounced nah-gie-yah-mah-tsoo) is a port city of the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. It is prosperous as the nation's only port due to its location straddling the Sanga River.

Seidoyaji.title: Seidoyaji
Seidoyaji.extract: Seidoyaji (pronounced say-doh-yah-jee) is one of the many cities that dot the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. The thieves' guild of Seidoyaji is responsible for the Autumn of Iron Tears, a conflict with the thieves' guild of the city of Todorokaze that escalated into a full-blown proxy war between the two cities.

Todorokaze.title: Todorokaze
Todorokaze.extract:
Todorokaze (pronounced toh-doh-roh-Kah-zeh) is one of the many cities that dot the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. It is the only city in the Kimu Mountains and a throughpoint for travelers and caravans crossing the range, and also is home to Tian Xia's largest shrine to the tengu deity Hei Feng. Todorokaze is known for its volatile weather, which its structures harness through myriad use of lightning rods.

The thieves' guild of Todorokaze is responsible for the Autumn of Iron Tears, a conflict with the thieves' guild of the city of Seidoyaji that escalated into a full-blown proxy war between the two cities.

Anjing.title: Anjing
Anjing.extract:
Anjing is a city located in the southeastern Heisong Forest of the Successor State of Lingshen. Anjing is a vast metropolis, its inhabitants numbering over sixty thousand.

According to legends, Anjing was the site of one of the challenges of the Tian Xia zodiac hero Tian Shen. Tales say the mythical hero lost an archery challenge due to his opponent's aggressive taunting, leading him to spend months mastering and balancing his qi through the practice of qigong. Tian Shen won the rematch through his renewed focus by striking the bullseye with every shot, each perfectly splitting his previous arrow. The constellation of the Archer is said to depict this event.

Gold Goblin.title: Gold Goblin
Gold Goblin.extract: The Gold Goblin Gambling Hall is a gaming hall and casino located in the Varisian city of Riddleport. Located in the bustling Wharf District, it hosts a number of games of chance and skill, including bounder, ghoulette, golems, and skiffs.

Xin.title: Xin
Xin.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: The Dead Heart of Xin.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

This article covers the founder of Thassilon. For the city named after him and the island on which it was located, see Xin (city).

Emperor Xin, an exile from the ancient nation of Azlant, was the visionary founder of Thassilon, an empire that would later be divided by the squabbling runelords that he created.

Corveilles.title: Corveilles
Corveilles.extract: Corveilles, sometimes spelled Corveiles, was a large city in the Thassilonian kingdom of Cyrusian. As of 4707 AR, its location had not been rediscovered.

Dhucharg.title: Dhucharg
Dhucharg.extract: Dhucharg is the capital of the hobgoblin nation of Kaoling in northwestern Tian Xia, and is by far the most impressive of all the country's cities.

Pobashabla.title: Pobashabla
Pobashabla.extract:
Pobashabla is a city-state in the underwater nation of Xidao, in the Xidao Gulf of eastern Tian Xia. It is located in Western Xidao, the part west of the Aya-Maru trench, and is sometimes referred to as the "western capital" of Xidao.

The city is dominated by the presence of the Xidao Army, which influences the city's culture but also generates tension with separatists who oppose them as an occupying force.

Waunomani.title: Waunomani
Waunomani.extract:
Waunomani (pronounced Wau-noh-MAA-nee), also known as Silungan, is the closest thing to a capital in the archipelago nation of Minata, off the eastern coast of Tian Xia. The diverse and heavily fortified city serves as a center of sea trade on the eastern coast of Belem and port for Minata's defensive fleet of warships.

"Waunomani" is a word of the Tau Nui people, a once-nomadic folk who claim descent from the ancient empire of Taumata and now primarily live in the Minatan isles of Rendah Pulu.

Vannisaria.title: Vannisaria
Vannisaria.extract: Vannisaria is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. Vannisaria was founded by Brutus Vannisar and has since been home to multiple generations of the Vannisar family, including General Jiayi Vannisar, Brutus's great-granddaughter and Linvarre's leader as of 4724 AR. The city is also host to the Temple of the Eternal and Exquisite Rose, the largest temple to Shelyn on the continent.

Sharpened Axe.title: Sharpened Axe
Sharpened Axe.extract: The Sharpened Axe of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen is a fighting school located on the southeastern side of the city's fourth level.

Halls of the Closed Fist.title: Halls of the Closed Fist
Halls of the Closed Fist.extract: The Halls of the Closed Fist, located in the northeastern third level of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen, serves as the headquarters of the Closed Fist, an elite unit that enforces the will of the city's overlord. Under the rule of Ardax the White-Hair, the Halls also effectively serve as his seat of government, as he leaves the Great Palace vacant save for his predecessor Grask Uldeth's corpse.

Great Palace.title: Great Palace
Great Palace.extract:
The Great Palace—located in the eastern first tier of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen, just inside the city's eastern gate—is the location of the vacant Throne of Urgir. The last overlord to sit upon the throne was Grask Uldeth, who was assassinated in 4716 AR; as of 4720 AR, his remains still sat upon the throne with a sword plunged through his chest. His effective successor Ardax the White-Hair, a former advisor to Uldeth, served as acting overlord after the assassination and refuses to fill the throne, instead claiming the title of steward and ruling from the Halls of the Closed Fist.

The exterior of the building bears massive letters in the Dwarven language that spell "Urgir", an Orc word meaning "first home". These letters were carved in -3708 AR by the city's conquering orc forces, led by Belkzen, after defeating its dwarven defenders in the Battle of Nine Stones; before the orcs, the city was the Sky Citadel of Koldukar. The palace is surrounded by the historic Plaza of Sky.

Delvehaven.title: Delvehaven
Delvehaven.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: plot elements of the Council of Thieves adventure path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Delvehaven is the Pathfinder lodge in the old Chelaxian capital of Westcrown. It was founded in the late 47th century AR by Venture-Captain Aiger Ghaelfin.

Taranik House.title: Taranik House
Taranik House.extract: Taranik House is a large mansion in the Rego Scripa section of the Chelaxian city of Westcrown, which serves as the city's garrison for the Hellknight Order of the Rack.

Citadel Rivad.title: Citadel Rivad
Citadel Rivad.extract: The oldest of the citadels of the Hellknights, Citadel Rivad squats in the Turanian Hills northwest of Westcrown, the Twilight City and former capital of Cheliax. It is a solemn jail to heretical scrolls, quartos, and banned lore, and is zealously guarded by the Order of the Rack.

Dusk Market.title: Dusk Market
Dusk Market.extract:
The Dusk Market is a wandering bazaar that sets up within the shells of fallen temples, theaters, and other opulent ruins within the Westcrown district of Rego Cader. Here the brave and foolish buy contraband, under the very eyes of bribed rundottari (local watch). Drugs, exotic poisons, strange beasts, and kidnapped slaves can all be found in this semi-secret marketplace.

Run by a secret syndicate, it takes place at a different ruin each day in the two hours before sundown—hence its name—to avoid Order of the Rack Hellknights and the city's dangerous creatures. For a price, almost any local ne'er-do-well can tell visitors where the market will set up next.

Dorjanala.title: Dorjanala
Dorjanala.extract: Dorjanala is a palace built in 4336 AR by order of Queen Dorjana to serve as her magnificent city residence, featuring solariums with crystals constructed from metal plates on the sides. In 4716 AR, a descendant of the monarch, the knight Bellinia Dorjana, took possession of the site for the Glorious Reclamation.

Ducotrion.title: Ducotrion
Ducotrion.extract: Ducotrion is a single-story stone structure located in the center of the city to respond to any emergency. It also serves as the offices and barracks of the duxotar. Duxotar Ariende Jharnavar is in charge of the guard and is usually in the building.

Canaroden.title: Canaroden
Canaroden.extract: Canaroden is the longest and deepest canal in Westcrown, physically separating Parego Spera and Parego Dospera. The canal walls in the southern area feature bas-reliefs of the city's founders, while those in the north depict faces of Aroden. Thanks to the magical properties of these walls, they have remained undisturbed for centuries after their construction. In the past, eight bridges spanned the canal, though only two remain: Bladewing Bridge and Truemount Bridge.

Jacovo's Stables.title: Jacovo's Stables
Jacovo's Stables.extract: Jacovo's Stables is a small ranch near town where good-quality horses are sold or loaned. Its owner, Jacovo, is an honest man with a good attitude who is helped in his business by his nephew, Gorvio.

Limehouse Theater.title: Limehouse Theater
Limehouse Theater.extract: The Limehouse Theatre in Rego Scripa is run by the infamous director Robahl Nonon, who has made actors cry after his harsh critiques. The theatre is quite small, and dress rehearsals are held more often than actual performances, which is preferred by the locals who can boo and throw tomatoes during them.

Blacknape Hideout.title: Blacknape Hideout
Blacknape Hideout.extract: Blacknape Hideout is located on one of the islands in the Westchannel, where a gang of thugs, the Blacknapes, released from prison in 4710 AR by the Council of Thieves, gathered to wreak havoc. The group specialized in kidnapping children and the elderly, who were easy to hold prisoner and could demand ransoms for their safe return.

Korradath.title: Korradath
Korradath.extract: The Korradath is the former royal castle and seat of Imperial Cheliax in the Rego Corna section of Westcrown. A massive fortified castle, it remains an impressive sight even though it has not seen any major upkeep since before the death of King Gaspodar in Neth of 4622 AR. It is still used by Wiscrani nobles for offices, temporary lodgings, or neutral meeting places for visitors to Westcrown that need to be impressed and protected from the rabble of the streets.

Trivardum.title: Trivardum
Trivardum.extract:
Trivardum, in Rego Laina, was a meeting place for the Triumvirate, the three most powerful families of Westcrown: House Drovenge, House Julistarc, and House Oberigo.

Today, it serves as a local market and inn, the latter called the Tricalista. The inn's suite was booked from at least [[4709 AR to 4716 AR by Vors Kyniar, the ambassador of Taldor to Westcrown.

Founder's Peak.title: Founder's Peak
Founder's Peak.extract: Founder's Peak is located on the east bank of Southrun, being the highest point in the area. It was named after Founder Adel, who is said to have cut down the trees he found there to create a boat to cross the river, and from its heights saw how the city should be built. The area is full of farms and olive orchards tended by generations of druids of the Arthrugge family, who maintain political neutrality.

Old Temple of Erastil.title: Old Temple of Erastil
Old Temple of Erastil.extract: The Old Temple of Erastil in Westcrown lies abandoned in the Rego Cader. It once served the lower-middle-class residents of the neighborhood, who ran simple shops and helped each other, but this spiritual center is no longer what it once was. In 4709 AR, it was used as a base of operations by a gang of cambions who were robbing and causing anarchy in the city.

Wave Door.title: Wave Door
Wave Door.extract:
This article contains spoilers for the following products: What Lies in Dust.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

The Wave Door is a dimensional storage space left as a hidden treasure in Cutlass Cove by the Pathfinders of Delvehaven as an additional way to enter the complex by disabling its wards beforehand. It was only accessible at night by following the area's geography and using a wayfinder at a specific point, making it nearly impossible to find by chance.

Bloody Tarn.title: Bloody Tarn
Bloody Tarn.extract: The Bloody Tarn is the base of a tribe of redcaps who serve the Mother of Flies in the Hagwood near Westcrown. Like most of its members, they are violent towards all outsiders, attacking them unless they are invited by their mistress.

Trollclaw Cleft.title: Trollclaw Cleft
Trollclaw Cleft.extract: Trollclaw Cleft is a cavern where a tribe of jinkins, under the orders of the Mother of Flies, cared for an enormous beetle that had been raised by the hag since it was young and was, to say the least, powerful.

Maggot Tree.title: Maggot Tree
Maggot Tree.extract: The Maggot Tree is in the center of the Hagwood, a huge, twisted tree that was once home to a coven of hags, of which only the Mother of Flies has survived. In 4710 AR, agents of the Council of Thieves attacked the tree, attempting to burn it down to destroy the hag who knew the only weakness in their new leader's plan.

Sunset Gate.title: Sunset Gate
Sunset Gate.extract: Sunset Gate is one of the exits from Rego Cader, but since the district was abandoned long ago, this gate now leads to overgrown vegetation with a path that is not regularly used by anyone.

Thesing's Tower.title: Thesing's Tower
Thesing's Tower.extract:
This article contains spoilers for the following products: Council of Thieves adventure path.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Thesing's Tower was the residence of Thesing Umbero Ulvauno, the popular but arrogant actor of Westcrown, who, after his death, rose as a vampire seeking revenge against the actors who stole his thunder in his last performance. The stone tower served as a guard post in the past and is in good condition, standing up to 30 feet tall.

Arodennama.title: Arodennama
Arodennama.extract:
The Arodennama, a massive statue of Aroden, stretches 90 feet above its base on Aroden's Rise in Westcrown. Created from massive blocks of white marble, it is the oldest structure in Westcrown and, perhaps, even in all of Avistan. The statue faces the river and Westcrown Island, with the chest aligned to stand if the first rays of sunlight in the morning and the last at night. A massive silver-inlaid winged-eye symbol of Aroden stretches to the sleeves and under the arms.

A variety of structures of many ages surround the statue, originally built in the expectation of his arrival, most now lie neglected and vacant. In contrast, even after a century's neglect, no signs of tarnish or wear mar the silver eye of Aroden across the breast. Within the statue are a number of chambers originally designed as places to pray and pay homage to Aroden. Most troubling to many of his clergy is that no residual magic or lingering curse remains here from Aroden's demise. After all, if Aroden could not even leave his blessing here, the place where his prophesies foretold he would return to the world, then his demise is even more definite and profound.

The area surrounding the statue is a large plaza known as the Vacant Throne, and encompasses several buildings (now abandoned). The area has been described as having a feel of ancient but abandoned majesty.

Qatada Nessudidia.title: Qatada Nessudidia
Qatada Nessudidia.extract: The Qatada Nessudidia is the largest temple to Asmodeus in Westcrown, and one of the largest buildings east of the Arodennama. Located in Rego Sacero, it stretches four stories tall and is capped with a red crystal skylight roof over its altar that glows with such sinister light that it is difficult to miss at night.

Vaneo Arvanxi.title: Vaneo Arvanxi
Vaneo Arvanxi.extract: Vaneo Arvanxi was a vast estate in the Rego Sacero of Westcrown that as of 4709 AR served as the home of Lord-Mayor Aberian Arvanxi, into which he had poured such immense wealth that it had earned the local nickname of Aberian's Folly.

Imperial Marina.title: Imperial Marina
Imperial Marina.extract: The Imperial Marina was established in 4634 AR in the easternmost part of Westcrown's mainland to gain naval superiority over river trade. In 4716 AR, it was used by the Glorious Reclamation as a prison for the nobility who opposed their rule.

Sanqatada Cinqarda.title: Sanqatada Cinqarda
Sanqatada Cinqarda.extract: The Sanqatada Cinqarda, the Cathedral of the Five Founders, is an immense cathedral dedicated to the five founders of Westcrown, which predates Cheliax and Andoran as provinces of the empire. The facade remains shining, refusing any attempt to deface it in any way. The 30-foot-tall limestone statues beside each doorway stand on Turanian greenstone columns and represent one of the founders: Dotara, Palmor, Rixana, Adel, and Crucisal.

College of Mysteries.title: College of Mysteries
College of Mysteries.extract: The College of Mysteries (formerly known as the House of Secrets) is the oldest school of magic in Absalom and is located in the Petal District. The college has a superb reputation in the city and is favored by a number of organisations, such the Javelin Gallery, whom they aid by enchanting weapons.

White Grotto.title: White Grotto
White Grotto.extract:
White Grotto is the premier bardic college in Absalom where the students learn the art of using magic to enhance all forms of entertainment. The school is located in the upscale Ivy District.

The college is not associated with any guilds, but pays the Ivy District Council and the district's larger businesses and establishments to allow their students to perform without worrying about repercussions. Students wear uniforms that reflect their rank—green for apprentices, blue for journeymen, and black for masters. Graduates can no longer enjoy the school's support, and have to figure out how they will earn a living themselves.

Spiralcross Cemetery.title: Spiralcross Cemetery
Spiralcross Cemetery.extract:
The Spiralcross Cemetery is the largest and most prestigious cemetery in all of Absalom, where Primarch]s, nobles, and high priests are buried. Located next to the Ahnkamen Estate, this cemetery is a labyrinth of vaults and shrines belonging to families with over four millennia of history. A large church of Pharasma stands among the tombs, and its presence, along with the proximity to Mother Jackal, means that few undead awaken in the area.

There are several ghost stories about the place, but only one seems true: white orbs are seen moving through the cemetery at night, and it is said that they may be codes used by spies in the city, although no one has confirmed their purpose.

Palace of Thirteen Spires.title: Palace of Thirteen Spires
Palace of Thirteen Spires.extract: The Palace of Thirteen Spires is a small but opulent palace in the Wise Quarter, divided into four wings, each in a Chelaxian, Osirian, Taldan, and Minkan style. For most of the year, the rooms are rented to merchants and nobles visiting the city. The White Grotto holds the contract to the estate, working with the Wise Council to manage the building exclusively, a fact that greatly displeases the Arcanamirium. When a head of state visits, everyone else is evicted so the best rooms can be reserved for royalty, and the money raised goes toward public works and scholarships.

Tempest.title: Tempest
Tempest.extract: Tempest is a nightclub in the Ivy District that was formerly a theater with a second floor containing apartments. Thanks to the illusions employed by the establishment, an esoteric and incomprehensible aesthetic is revealed, even though the common man is not permitted entry if they were the establishment would make no sense to them. Models pose in strange clothing, and niche artists use the stage for plays whose symbolism is convolutedly difficult to understand. The second floor is reserved for the elite, serving as the apartment of a painter whose disturbing murals cover the walls and who hated both affection and wealth. The sealed balcony is the residence of Zozzler Vondorun, the High Priest of Zon-Kuthon in Absalom, and with a touch of his hand, he can reveal the secret temple hidden behind the grotesque paintings.

Thistleguard Station.title: Thistleguard Station
Thistleguard Station.extract: The Thistleguard Station is the base from which the Ivy District guard operates. Located north of the Ivy District Park, it has a holding cell for up to 10 criminals to be detained until they are prosecuted. The Thistleguard does not concern itself with minor crimes, as it wishes to maintain a smooth relationship with the neighborhoods, and instead only goes after violent acts that disrupt the residents. One exception to this system is that performers who are not members of the Street Performers and Actors' Guild are more frequently arrested by the guard, and it doesn't take a genius to connect the conflicting interests with Nomarch Alain Always. They come into conflict with the Brotherhood of Abadar, which wants the law to be followed to the fullest extent, and nobles and merchants hire them to protect their merchandise since the guard does not investigate incidents of theft.

Wandering Monster.title: Wandering Monster
Wandering Monster.extract: The Wandering Monster is an irregular park maintained by the Ivy District's leshy population. It features an open-air amphitheater that circumvents the rules prohibiting unregistered artists outside the Street Performers and Actors' Guild from performing. It is therefore popular with traveling artists who enjoy nature, and because the Nomarch keeps a watchful eye on who performs here, artists often wear masks to conceal their identities. The Thistleguard used to patrol the area, removing anyone attempting to get on stage, but now the Brotherhood of Abadar maintains at least one member on site at all times to prevent people from being removed unless they actually break the law.

Uiry Manor Museum.title: Uiry Manor Museum
Uiry Manor Museum.extract: The Uiry Manor Museum was the residence of this noble family who held considerable influence in the city until 4673 AR. One night, a stranger arrived at the house, invited by the Scion Lord and when the butler went to another manor to get more wine, he returned to find a scene of carnage; everyone was dead, from the nobles to the children of the servants. The crime was never solved, and the family turned the house into a museum to avoid falling into poverty. Several guides explain the history of the artifacts and theories about how the murders might have occurred. Scion Lady Veridel was a schoolgirl when her family was murdered, and although she refuses to sleep in the manor, she also refuses to sell it for reasons that remain unclear.

Grand Council Hall.title: Grand Council Hall
Grand Council Hall.extract:
The Grand Council Hall is a magnificent, large white marble structure where the city's most influential people meet to discuss the city's laws and administration. The 49 members of the Low Council meet monthly in a large hall with alcoves and stone thrones, where the acoustics provide good communication over long distances, though paradoxically, they also offer privacy for quiet conversations. Above these alcoves are the 13 seats of the High Council, which are in every respect superior to the others, and its members rarely appear in public except on the rare occasions when the entire body must vote on a motion, as they prefer to hold these debates in private rooms around the city.

The general public can attend if they possess one of the tablets granted to the councillors, which in theory should be given to the city's military and heroes, but in practice end up in the hands of corrupt merchants and nobles who want to be close to those in power to see if they can plunder something like vultures. A series of underground passages connect to factories and scriptoriums where the council's bureaucracy is on full display, and the entire system is protected by a private guard at the service of the institution itself.

Kin Gate.title: Kin Gate
Kin Gate.extract: The Kin Gate is located in the northeast of Absalom. It was originally a siege castle, commissioned by the fey prince Juroveal during the Siege of Kin, but when he was banished, the fortification was merged with the rest of the city.

Arcanamirium.title: Arcanamirium
Arcanamirium.extract: The Arcanamirium is not only the largest and oldest school in Absalom, but quite possibly the most prestigious arcane magic academy in the entire Inner Sea region of Golarion.

Forae Logos.title: Forae Logos
Forae Logos.extract: Also known as the Wise House and Library of Kortos, the Forae Logos in Absalom's Wise Quarter is the city's oldest and largest library, and one of the most extensive archives of written texts west of the Pit of Gormuz.

Lantern Lodge.title: Lantern Lodge
Lantern Lodge.extract:
For other meanings of "Lantern Lodge", please see Lantern Lodge (disambiguation).

After being purchased by Venture-Captain Amara Li for a large sum of money, the Lantern Lodge in the Petal District of Absalom reflects the finest culture that the city has to offer. During her many trips to Absalom, she purchased many furnishings for the lodge and has brought them back to Goka, currently on display even today. The lodge is kept extremely clean and Amara Li expects her guests to conduct themselves professionally. Any Pathfinder who is heading on a long journey usually stops by this lodge before leaving.

Ahnkamen Estate.title: Ahnkamen Estate
Ahnkamen Estate.extract: The Ahnkamen Estate is a beautiful Osirian-style manor with magnificent gardens that are frequently visited for the parties held every two weeks. These are the most fun in town, and no one realizes that beneath them lie cellars with temples to Yog-Sothoth and other Outer Gods, the extensive residence of Mother Jackal, who uses illusions and abjurations to maintain the secrecy of her existence. The Eaters of Knowledge also operate in the area, taking bodies from the city's prisons and serving them to their mistress. Only the most influential in House Ahnkamen, such as Scion Lady Neferpatra, know of their progenitor's existence, and it is their knowledge, which predates the existence of Ancient Osirion, that allowed them to rise to masters in matters of law and death.

Gutless Griffon.title: Gutless Griffon
Gutless Griffon.extract: The Gutless Griffon is a tavern in the Ivy District that has become very popular thanks to its owner, Fronsac Shimm. He chooses the food and the music, but above all, it's his lively attitude that makes him so appealing. He introduces himself to new patrons by telling anecdotes or serving drinks. His popularity even earned him a seat on the Grand Council, giving him a rags-to-riches story that only makes him more likable. The establishment boasts a taproom and balconies furnished with the finest furniture, and bards who are excluded from the Street Performers and Actors' Guild are welcome. Shimm has a mutual animosity with Alain Always, even though they were once best friends. They respect each other and generally don't interfere in each other's affairs, but since the Brotherhood of Abadar often hangs out at the tavern, the rift between them only widens. One of the first things you notice upon entering is its large fireplace and hearth, where the craziest stories from all over the Inner Sea are told.

Withrun House.title: Withrun House
Withrun House.extract: The Withrun House is a school founded by Kalistocrats who instill their doctrine in the children of Absalom's wealthiest families, who are unaware of the cult's control. Due to its similarity to the Prophet's Academy, it maintains a rivalry with them. They also have their own team of ruk called the Withrun Wraiths.

Kyonin Embassy.title: Kyonin Embassy
Kyonin Embassy.extract: The Kyonin Embassy is located in the Ivy District, a gathering place for artists and a haven for the free-spirited elves of the forest nation. The manor house is built in the Castrovel style, and a number of ambassadors are rotating through, pursuing the nation's goals in Absalom. Rumors suggest that Queen Telandia Edasseril will visit the city to discuss something with Wynsal Starborn in person.

Madinani Manor.title: Madinani Manor
Madinani Manor.extract: The Madinani Manor is an imposing estate with a white wall, gardens with plants used for arcane or alchemical purposes, and a three-story building in the center. This impressive structure serves as the home of Lady Darchana, one of Absalom's most prominent political figures and a master of arcane magic.

Wisdom's Refuge.title: Wisdom's Refuge
Wisdom's Refuge.extract: Wisdom's Refuge is where the district's Wise Council conducts its administrative activities, focusing on education and the arts. To further their goals, many institutions dedicated to preserving history or knowledge receive reduced taxes, which are then passed on to students at the district's numerous schools and academies. Many council members have studied at the Arcanamirium, so they are aware of the dangers of magic manipulating elections and ensure that all processes adhere to the district's philosophy of fairness.

Daltus Academy.title: Daltus Academy
Daltus Academy.extract: The Daltus Academy is a boarding school that occupies an entire block of the district where its campus, dormitories, and classrooms are located. Students at this academy are often involved in fights and are viewed unfavorably by the other residents of the district who tend to avoid them.

Brine.title: Brine
Brine.extract:
For other meanings of "Brine", please see Brine (disambiguation).

The Brine is an imposing stone prison house located in the Puddles district in Absalom. Lady Seichya of House Tevineg serves as the prison's head warden.

Ever since the regular flooding of the district, The Brine has ensured a slow but certain death for the criminals within. The holding pens is usually soaked with at least a foot of water from the flooding, but also raw sewage washes in from time to time, typically resulting in outbreaks of filth fever and general sickness.

Although the guards use elevated catwalks to stay out of the muck, the prisoners below frequently are forced to stand, sit and sleep in the foul waters.

Especially important individuals are sent to Black Whale prison.

The Salt Cartel fills its stocks of slaves from prisoners fraudulently collected from the Brine.

Beldrin's Tower.title: Beldrin's Tower
Beldrin's Tower.extract:
Beldrin's Tower, sometimes known as the Tower of the Broken Shield, is the most famous monument in the Precipice Quarter of Absalom. Originally part of a three-spire magic academy established by the wizard Beldrin, it suffered considerable damage after the earthquake of 4698 AR. It has since teetered on the cliff's edge, shaken and cracked but unbowed.✝︎

Beldrin's Tower, as well as the shattered ruins of the other two towers, derive their name from the objects of power that Beldrin sequestered within: the Tower of the Candelabra for Beldrin's Candelabra, the Tower of the Horn for Beldrin's Horn, and the Tower of the Broken Shield for Beldrin's Shield. The three spires were also collectively known as the Tri-Towers, and the former name of the nearby Drownyard was the Tri-Towers Yard.

Silken Court.title: Silken Court
Silken Court.extract: The Silken Court is a brothel in the Petal District of Absalom. It is the city's most expensive establishment of its type, and among of the Inner Sea's most famous. Previously owned and managed by Elissa the Fair until her recent retirement, it has since been fully renovated by its new manager, the Calistrian Head Hetaera Dyrianna of House Avenstar.

Darkgate.title: Darkgate
Darkgate.extract: The Darkgate is a fissure in the Precipice Quarter, created during the earthquake that devastated much of the district. Months after it opened, undead and monsters emerged from the fissure and still roam the city streets. Taking advantage of the situation, the Nailfists moved into the area and keep the threats from below at bay. The gang doesn't do this out of charity, of course; they only allow access to the caverns if they are paid a price.

Wracked Rock.title: Wracked Rock
Wracked Rock.extract: The Wracked Rock is a stony structure in an abandoned block of buildings in the Precipice Quarter, inhabited not out of necessity but because they are drawn by the strange dreams that occur when sleeping near the obelisk. Thousands of years ago, a Rovagug cultist summoned a giant monster to destroy the city, but heroes managed to turn it to stone before it could ravage the neighborhood, killing it by shattering it into pieces. The only part that remained was this rock, a tusk protruding from the head that lies buried underground. After the earthquake a few decades ago, a small opening opened into the subsoil, from which all who have ventured never return. Rovagug cultists are often seen in the area, but since their violence doesn't spread beyond the block, the Knights of Lastwall see no reason to intervene.

Absalom Menagerie.title: Absalom Menagerie
Absalom Menagerie.extract: The Absalom Menagerie is a park where a wide variety of animals live in climate-controlled enclosures behind magical walls designed to reduce the danger to visitors. Almost anyone can enter, as its founder bequeathed the park to the city with the stipulation that no member of the Blakros family be allowed entry, though the reason for this is unknown. The government pays the park's caretakers, who are usually members of the founder's family, a matter of tradition rather than obligation and Alina Muraabe is the High Warden of the park. The druids are divided between those who consider the place an affront to the natural order and those who believe that exposing urban dwellers to nature can foster greater appreciation and respect for it.

Spiral Shrine.title: Spiral Shrine
Spiral Shrine.extract: The Spiral Shrine is a temple of Pharasma that was among the first to be rebuilt in the Precipice Quarter after the earthquake that shook the district. Hundreds of candles were placed in its alcoves to honor the memory of those whose bodies could not be recovered from the rubble, and the residents of Beldrin's Bluff donated significantly to consecrate the temple and enable them to hire undead hunters. It is one of the few places in the district where divine magical healing can be obtained, and the abandoned mansions in the area have been converted into apartments where a community of duskwalkers lives, fighting the undead. For this reason, the district's living residents hold them in great reverence, bowing their heads when they see one or offering them food and water when asked.

Palace Ormuz.title: Palace Ormuz
Palace Ormuz.extract: The Palace Ormuz is a white Thuvian-style structure where Scion Lord Yamthar invites the aristocracy to seminars where they can speak with researchers, scholars, and intellectuals, listening to or testing their theories. The south wing houses the laboratories where dozens of alchemists work and where Yamthar keeps his collection of Jistkan artifacts, which he has acquired and attempts to reactivate, including those that are normally dormant.

Mortecant.title: Mortecant
Mortecant.extract: Mortecant is a neighborhood in the southern part of the Precipice Quarter where followers of Urgathoa, seeking to be corrupted by the area's necromantic energies, move from abandoned house to abandoned house, weaving through a tangled web of people as if they were undead. Even the knights of Vigil's Hope have killed several mobs without realizing they weren't truly undead. Not all members of this community are inherently evil; some cannot move because the neighborhood's reputation would lead to their new neighbors killing them if they discovered their origins. Thus, they are trapped between the Knights of Lastwall and the wall of semi-dead mobs at their backs.

Sphinx Gate.title: Sphinx Gate
Sphinx Gate.extract: The Sphinx Gate is located in the northwest of Absalom and is usually open even at night due to the constant traffic in the area. It connects to the farms of the region and is used by the First Guard to patrol the Cairnlands, taking its name from the enormous sphinx statue erected there. During the Unraveling Siege, when the proteans attacked the city, dozens of riddles appeared at the gate, and after a guard solved one, the proteans were no longer able to dominate the city's skies, proving instrumental in the victory against them. Only those who have aided Absalom on countless occasions have access to the riddles, as no one knows what dangerous magic they may contain, and of all the city's entrances, this is the only one that has never been breached, partly due to its proximity to the Azlanti Keep.

Drownyard.title: Drownyard
Drownyard.extract:
The Drownyard is a location in the city of Absalom, in the (now mostly destroyed) Precipice Quarter.

Before the terrible earthquake of 4698 AR, the Precipice Quarter (formerly known as Beldrin's Bluff) was known as the Tri-Towers Yard, the premier finishing school for children of the rich and powerful. The unexpected quake destroyed the academy and the students within, but the quake also brought forth a spray from the sewer that contained the remains of an (previously unknown) ancient necropolis over which the school had been built. The lower reaches of the Drownyard access this old necropolis.

Now the Tri-Towers Yard is better known as the Drownyard, a desolate ruin filled with salty brine and haunted by undead. For a decade after the tragedy, the site has been off limits to outsiders, the families of the dead erecting a fence of black iron around the area to keep out morbid intruders and even archaeological groups. The Pathfinder Society was eventually permitted access to the area.

Arboretum Arcanis.title: Arboretum Arcanis
Arboretum Arcanis.extract:
The Arboretum Arcanis once contained a wonderland of exotic fruit and blossoms from beyond Golarion. It was housed in a magical crystal dome in the Precipice Quarter in Absalom.

The magic that protected and nurtured the flora was placed by Beldrin, but these wavered and collapsed when the earthquake of 4698 AR struck the district. The magical cloud that hovered overhead, designed to provide nutrient-rich rain, became corrupted. Now the purple-black cloud spills caustic gray and yellow rain on the plants below. The plants that once thrived either withered away or evolved into horrific versions of themselves, while new abominations of nature have since sprung into existence.

Vigil's Hope.title: Vigil's Hope
Vigil's Hope.extract: Vigil's Hope, located in the Precipice Quarter of Absalom, is the headquarters of the Knights of Lastwall and serves as an embassy for the exiled leadership of the destroyed nation of Lastwall. Watcher-Lord Ulthun II resides in Vigil's Hope, from where he coordinates the organization's diplomatic and fundraising activities.

Aysepir's Astounded Abyss.title: Aysepir's Astounded Abyss
Aysepir's Astounded Abyss.extract: Aysepir's Astounded Abyss is a carnival show of unusual, and often disturbing, beings in the Puddles district of Absalom. Despite having the trappings of fiction and making extraordinary claims, its employees are exactly what they are presented to be—beings of sometimes immense power afflicted by curses or misfortune, or simply being of unique parentage.

Red Drake Warehouses.title: Red Drake Warehouses
Red Drake Warehouses.extract: Red Drake Warehouses is a series of warehouses in The Docks run by Ptarnex Dexarion, the ruthless boss of Ogrekin Hall. He runs the business by offering merchants space to store whatever they want, with the caveat that if they fail to pay for even a single month, all the merchandise inside becomes his property. He makes sure his customers are aware of this situation and has made more money selling confiscated goods than renting the space. It is said that he uses infernal contracts and hired thieves to steal from merchants so they can't pay on time, but since the rent is so low, many take the risk.

Wondervale.title: Wondervale
Wondervale.extract: The Wondervale was created decades after Aroden's death by Absalom's intellectuals, who sought to showcase the city's inventions and demonstrate that it had not declined in relevance after the death of its patron god. They chose Eastgate and Green Ridge as the venue for they were the least populated areas with the largest open spaces, and proceeded to construct illuminated bridges, Tian Xia-style towers, Vudra pagodas, and Casmaron palaces. The resulting intellectual and cultural exchange was a resounding success, drawing attention to the nation. After the festival, the structure was abandoned, and the escaped plants and animals give the site a sense of ancient ruin, a place favored by lovers, poets, and followers of Brigh. The most striking features are the Jalmeray-style flying towers, teeming with bats.

Barque Bazaar.title: Barque Bazaar
Barque Bazaar.extract: The Barque Bazaar is a floating business where several barges have joined together, and merchants sell their foreign goods to customers all along the Absalom coast. Created by Market Master Annavi, she believed her business would be a success, but she hadn't anticipated that the residents of The Puddles, The Docks, and Precipice Quarter would be the poorest in the city, and she's struggling to keep the business afloat, both literally and figuratively. Her best customers are in The Puddles, where she's become involved with criminal gangs, acting as a fencer. However, she may be too deeply involved in the criminal underworld, and someone might eventually retaliate against her and the floating business.

Barterfall.title: Barterfall
Barterfall.extract: Barterfall is a neighborhood in the Precipice Quarter, named for its economy and precarious living conditions. It's one of the least dangerous parts of the district, though that's not saying much. Its inhabitants are the descendants of those who were either too poor to move or wanted by the law, and many don't even have jobs, earning their living by fishing, trapping for vermin, and farming whatever they can. Violence and disease are common, and the mansions converted into apartments are falling apart. While its residents want to move to other districts, they also resent the rest of the city for abandoning them. There's an enclave of Kalistocrats who trade coin for what the residents scavenge from the ruins, making them the object of envy and hatred from the inhabitants who barely see them behind their walls and clean houses.

Grislyfair.title: Grislyfair
Grislyfair.extract: The Grislyfair is an attempted amusement park that the Grand Council created in the Precipice Quarter to attract new residents and generate jobs. Most of the performers would be wanderers, thus not exposed to the district's spiritual corruption, and the use of clockworks unafraid of spirits should ensure the park's smooth operation. Due to sabotage by either cultists or smugglers, the construction suffered several accidents and was abandoned. The spirits moved about, enchanting the musical instruments and the carousels that now spin aimlessly, making it a place of eerie amusement. Reclaiming the Grislyfair is one of Wynsal Starborn's priorities for cleansing the district of corruption, as similar public works cannot be undertaken without serving as a constant reminder of past failures.

Rat-Taker's Palace.title: Rat-Taker's Palace
Rat-Taker's Palace.extract: The Rat-Taker's Palace is the base of operations for the city's cleaners, responsible for maintaining the sewers, repairing pipes, and pest control. The building was abandoned after the earthquake, but they quickly returned to reclaim it, as its ostentatious appearance was too tempting to not move back in. Unfortunately, during their absence, a rat infestation took hold and continues to be a problem to this day.

Green Ridge.title: Green Ridge
Green Ridge.extract: Green Ridge is a neighborhood in the Eastgate district of Absalom. It is one of smallest and least populated parts of the city. It contains a number of extensive gardens and parks, as well as several copses of trees scattered throughout it, that inspire the district's name. Green Ridge also contains many small farms a result of past sieges. In contrast to much of Absalom, the district tends to be peaceful and quiet.

Sea King Shipyards.title: Sea King Shipyards
Sea King Shipyards.extract: Sea King Shipyards is a small shipyard in the Docks district of the city of Absalom. The yard builds only one ocean-going vessel at a time, but it remains famous for the high quality and sturdiness of its fluyts and galleons that can sail the most dangerous waters in the Inner Sea.

Devil's Own.title: Devil's Own
Devil's Own.extract: The Devil's Own is a shipyard in the Docks district of the city of Absalom. The shipyard specializes in war galleys used by Absalom's navy and sold to the markets throughout the Inner Sea. Its owner, Lord Archych of House Dureanz is a loyal Chelaxian. He employs the worst thugs and pushes around his considerable weight at every opportunity. The machinations of Lord Archych and those of his rival Goodmiss Alidane of House Candren, manager of the Sea King Shipyard, are the main source of intrigue and violence in the Docks.

Beneath the Docks.title: Beneath the Docks
Beneath the Docks.extract: Beneath the Docks is a neighborhood beneath the wooden planks of the district, inhabited by the area's poorest residents, those with criminal connections, or those who prefer to live as close to the water as possible. The Seafoam Conclave, comprised of azarketi, is the largest community here, though they are generally poorer than those living in Gilltown, often working as garbage collectors or fishermen. Jossie Slimfin is one such garbage collector, selling items to surface dwellers as aquatic treasures and also acting as an informant between the merfolk and cecaelia communities living deeper underground. The communities here tend to be insular, with little interaction with the outside world, though in 4711 AR, many cecaelia arrived from Xidao in pursuit of a criminal who had already been killed by the Pathfinder Society, and many stayed to live here.

Broadfoot Marshals Station House.title: Broadfoot Marshals Station House
Broadfoot Marshals Station House.extract: Broadfoot Marshals Station House is a base for a private firefighting brigade in The Docks district of Absalom. Under the instruction of the dwarven engineer Bagara Broadfoot, they use water carts that, through pedals, can create pressure to expel water and extinguish fires, which are quite dangerous in a district made almost entirely out of wood.

Temple Row.title: Temple Row
Temple Row.extract: Temple Row is a neighborhood teeming with temples to various deities, most of them related to the sea and weather, located in The Docks. The temple of Gozreh is near tide pools that fluctuate with the high ot low tide, generating a good amount of wind, and the area is favored by water druids. Those unfamiliar with the faith consider Besmara's Boardwalk to be its temple, but that's a stretch, as true followers gather in a seedy tavern with a tradition of throwing out customers who lose bets by tossing a hand in the air as an offering. A group of tengus erected a temple to Hei Feng on the second floor of a warehouse that was struck by lightning twice during a storm. After convincing the owner, they repaired the roof and can now live there rent-free.

Kortos Watercrafts.title: Kortos Watercrafts
Kortos Watercrafts.extract: The Kortos Watercrafts is a shipyard that creates boats and small vessels using the finest quality wood on the island. They are owned by the Kortos Consortium, which uses the vessels to navigate the region's waterways.

Soggy Piper.title: Soggy Piper
Soggy Piper.extract: The Soggy Piper is a tavern perched on a cliff in The Puddles, a hangout for criminals and cultists. Violent crime is commonplace there, as bodies can be disposed of by simply pushing them off the cliff into the sea. The patrons avoid eye contact, creating a moody atmosphere and while the food is fairly average, it's a good place to gather information about the district's activities.

Consulate of the Platinum Band.title: Consulate of the Platinum Band
Consulate of the Platinum Band.extract: The Consulate of the Platinum Band is a temple dedicated to Apsu where his non-draconic followers gather and help others. Although the faith isn't widely understood by the residents of The Puddles, they won't refuse the magical assistance offered. The building also houses archives on the region's dragon lore. The Consulate has few members, distinguished by their pendants bearing the symbol of their god. The rest avoid leaving the temple and do not interact with the Muckruckers or other ruthless groups in the district.

Goblin King's Court.title: Goblin King's Court
Goblin King's Court.extract: The Goblin King's Court is a series of wooden walkways and towers resembling a castle amidst the flooded houses of The Puddles. It is where Zusgut resides, acting as king. The goblins' short stature makes them reluctant to walk through the wet streets, so they hop from house to house to reach the court. Zusgut has advisors from all over Golarion who come to the city following tales of his fame. The goblins have devised a system that repairs and removes planks depending on the tide, and they have prepared a large stage where they perform daily. Therefore, it is easy to find the court simply by listening for the tremendous noise they make shouting during theatrical performances.

Ogrekin Hall.title: Ogrekin Hall
Ogrekin Hall.extract:
For other meanings of "Ogrekin", please see Ogrekin (disambiguation).

Ogrekin is a transporting business located in the Docks district of Absalom. Ogrekin can move anything that needs moving anywhere within the city. Its characteristically strong employees cooperate with the Pilot House and Harbormaster's Grange to get to ships first and offer to move their goods quickly and securely. Its base of operations is a simple building called Ogrekin Hall, distinguished by two disturbing life-sized ogrekin sculptures that flank its main entrance.

Ogrekin is owned by the Second Harbormaster, Ptarnex Dexarion, who also owns the city's Red Drake Warehouses. It is managed by his second-in-command and labormaster Feldreth Noor, and its employees work with the city's Pilot's House and Harbormaster's Grange.

Gilltown.title: Gilltown
Gilltown.extract:
Gilltown is a secluded collection of crude surface buildings in southern Eastgate, a district of Absalom, where azarketi ply their public trades. The surface neighborhood is a facade for an underground and underwater tunnel community leading to the Bay of Kortos. Despite the lack of formal shops, items retrieved by its residents from underwater wreckage can often be bought here, particularly necromantic items.

The azarketi trade on the surface, while underwater they descend to worship their deities and conduct business amongst themselves. The surface is intentionally neglected to keep the existence of the underwater tunnels hidden from humans. Not only pearls, oysters, and marine materials are sold, but the azarketi also recover marine artifacts from past empires, with those from Azlant being the most sought after.

Purewater Home.title: Purewater Home
Purewater Home.extract: The Purewater Home is an orphanage that takes in the most unfortunate children in The Puddles while also serving as the base of operations for the local Firebrands cell, run by Hope, the building's owner. The place is relatively safe and dry thanks to Hope's efforts and reputation, and the children are instructed in political issues by helping with acts of vandalism that keep them occupied and away from more serious crime.

Tallavont School.title: Tallavont School
Tallavont School.extract: The Tallavont School was founded centuries ago by idealists seeking to foster a sense of community in Eastgate by creating an institution that would allow teachers persecuted in their home countries for their political beliefs to teach here instead of being entangled in the city's treacherous and infighting politics. Called "the Fortress" because it incorporates an old war keep from the city's early history, this structure defines the district's skyline and is visible for miles around. Teachers often use the height to give lessons from the balconies, offering views of the city and providing their students with a unique teaching perspective. A quarter of the students live in Eastgate, while the rest come from noble families in the city or abroad who send their children to study here.

House of Guile.title: House of Guile
House of Guile.extract: The House of Guile is a gallery in Eastgate where Parsin Guile exhibits his works—some artistic, others more practical, featuring mechanical designs in wood—to the general public. The commercial side is managed by his wife, Hilenda Guile. The building's facade features over 30 different types of wood sourced from the Isle of Kortos, some of which have been extinct for centuries.

Pickled Imp.title: Pickled Imp
Pickled Imp.extract: The Pickled Imp is a curio shop in The Docks run by the infamous Guaril Karela, though he's rarely seen there. It now operates more like a pawn shop, keeping no records of any transactions, making it impossible to know what's being sold, bought, or who the customers are. Captain Elmoira Taggart used to work as a clerk there, and when anyone tries to suggest the wares aren't entirely legal, the employees point out that she still frequents the shop and there have never been any investigations into the establishment.

Mithril Chef.title: Mithril Chef
Mithril Chef.extract: The Mithril Chef is a high-end restaurant that also serves as a second-mark Firebrands cell located in The Docks district. All its staff are members of the organization, led by Claudette Butterfoot, who is more focused on cooking than going on adventures, though she frequently gives her employees "vacations", after which news of their deeds is heard around the city. The food they serve is excellent, very varied, and with strong aromas that make a good impression and one can also see the chefs using juggling techniques to prepare it, which always entertains the customers. They have a rivalry with the Bladed Bastards, although their fights are staged as a distraction until agents from both groups collaborate to free prisoners and help the population.

Antler Lodge.title: Antler Lodge
Antler Lodge.extract: Antler Lodge is the temple of Erastil, located in the urban city of Absalom, offering respite in its gardens to those who long for a rustic life. In the Eastgate district, a modest two-story structure can be seen with aesthetically pleasing wooden atlers at the entrance. The walls are magically reinforced to block out outside noise, granting tranquility to those inside. Instead of windows, illusions of rural landscapes adorn the walls, and the lodge is maintained by High Priestess Loys Zepah, an elderly woman who feeds sweets and confectionery like a grandmother to her grandchildren. Lately, strange things have begun to appear in the illusions, such as sick animals or burning landscapes, and adventurers are being sought to investigate the cause of this phenomenon.

Beragel's Fur and Feathers.title: Beragel's Fur and Feathers
Beragel's Fur and Feathers.extract: Beragel's Fur and Feathers is a pet shop run by Beragel Tindertales, offering animals from all over Golarion, as well as food and specialized enclosures to keep them happy. As a rule, sentient animals, endangered species, and monsters are not sold in the shop, though, whether by mistake or due to her gnome tendencies for novelties, she sometimes acquires a larva or hatchling that later turns out to be something that shouldn't be anyone's pet.

Church of the Muted God.title: Church of the Muted God
Church of the Muted God.extract: The Church of the Muted God is dedicated to this would-be god of the Failed, who perished during the Test of the Starstone but still has followers. The Muted God provides no divine power or guidance, and the members of this benevolent organization are becoming more of a criminal group than a religious congregation.

Postern.title: Postern
Postern.extract:
The Postern Gate, or simply the Postern, is the eastern gate of Absalom. It is the smallest of the city's gates, only 50 feet high and 40 feet wide, and the only one that is normally kept closed and locked. It is a defensive building designed by Aroden and made of thick stone with balconies, and is used mainly to get goods to and from the Starwatch Keep.

The gate was often the focus of siege attacks, and was breached during the Siege of Prophets even if the enemy forces proved unable to penetrate the city. A carved stone set in the last hall within the Postern commemorates the deepest point the enemy reached. The Postern Gate also serves as the headquarters of Eastgate's district guard, the Post Guard.

Statue Street.title: Statue Street
Statue Street.extract:
Statue Street is a long stretch of road in the Absalom district of Westgate.

Appropriately named, the street contains dozens of scattered statues in various poses. These are the remnants of an Absalom tradition that has only recently been outlawed. In order to prevent prison overcrowding, nonviolent offenders were sometimes allowed to compete for a pardon in a race to outrun an enraged gorgon. The fortunate or skilful who evade the beast are granted a pardon for their crimes, while others now decorate the area with contorted expressions of shock, defiance, or terror.

Although ultimately considered unethical at best, there are many in the community who are upset that this tradition has been made illegal.

Grand Holt.title: Grand Holt
Grand Holt.extract:
The Grand Holt is a massive, multi-trunked fig tree located in the Green Ridge neighborhood of Absalom's Eastgate district. It is the oldest and largest tree on the Isle of Kortos, maybe even older than Absalom.

The tree is tended by a fanatical druidic sect known as the Circle of Stones, who also care for Iolanthe, the immortal dryad queen who lives within it. The Grand Holt has expanded greatly over the millennia of its life, and entire blocks of buildings have been demolished to facilitate its growth. Blights, such as those caused by the Welt and the Tyrant's Grasp, have not been able to deter the tree's growth, and in fact seem to cause it to grow stronger. The tree provides a refuge for nature-loving adventurers, who are hired by the Circle of Stones to further Iolanthe's cause within the city and beyond.

Hall of Commerce.title: Hall of Commerce
Hall of Commerce.extract: The Hall of Commerce is a magnificent building, constructed in the most modern and ostentatious style, which serves as the government office of the Coin Council as well as a bank. The various district taxes are administered here, and both the Foreign Coin Exchange and the church of Abadar maintain several offices in the building. It is one of the areas with the highest concentration of foreign visitors.

Band of the Palm Headquarters.title: Band of the Palm Headquarters
Band of the Palm Headquarters.extract: The Band of the Palm Headquarters is where the Bloody Barbers criminal gang gathers for their operations throughout the city. Under the guise of the Band of the Palm, a union of chimney sweeps, garbage collectors, and cleaners operate out of this series of buildings that include offices, sleeping quarters, bankrupt businesses, and warehouses, all connected by ropes and bridges that make it difficult to distinguish one building from another. Legitimate members live in the area closest to the entrance, while below, the Barbers operate in a series of catacombs, sewers, and caverns guarded by the Ghostguides, a group of albino chokers who show the way to the base where the leaders reside—an impenetrable fortress. In a room hidden even from the guides, Dr. Bensi Skule experimented on a troll prisoner who would become the gang's leader.

Gallery of Innovation.title: Gallery of Innovation
Gallery of Innovation.extract: The Gallery of Innovation is the Brigh temple in Absalom run by the impassive High Priestess Flindish Tanwhirl. Followers from all over the Inner Sea come to the temple, which is filled with engineers, blacksmiths, and metalworkers who push their skills to the limit working in the workshops inside. Constant classes and seminars are given by the clergy on the functions of inventions or new ideas that can be put into practice.

Five-Fire Pavilions.title: Five-Fire Pavilions
Five-Fire Pavilions.extract:
The Five-Fire Pavilions is a burnt-out structure with exposed blackened metal beams that can be considered Absalom's goblin embassy, ​​led by the explorer Skirma Toadlicker, who seeks to show that goblins are ready to join the "civilized" world.

Many goblins, hobgoblins, and others considered monsters have attended the pavilion's exhibitions, art displays, and fireworks, trading amongst themselves. However, not all cultural exchanges go smoothly, especially when a neighbor walks their dog, which some goblins interpret as a meat offering. There are also rivalries between tribes and differences between those who seek a more savage life, insulting hobgoblins who ride horses, and the more civilized, who call their less educated kin deformed. Even so, Skirma hopes that things will be better in the future and that this is a step forward.

Flying Alderman.title: Flying Alderman
Flying Alderman.extract: The Flying Alderman is a modest inn on the edge of the Foreign Quarter, serving as a safe house for Pathfinder Society agents. Drock Ovix, the innkeeper, is a Pathfinder himself and allows them to use tunnels beneath the inn that connect directly to the Grand Lodge—a closely guarded secret of the organization. Agents can spend three nights free at the inn, while others must pay two silver pieces, which includes bed, dinner, and breakfast the next day.

Little Lirgen.title: Little Lirgen
Little Lirgen.extract: Little Lirgen is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where the diaspora of the fallen nation of Lirgen gathers, and most of them maintain their nation's astronomical traditions. Mosaics of stars fill the streets, painted like the night sky, and although in the first few years after the Eye of Abendego opened they hoped the storm would dissipate, they are increasingly uncertain about whether to remain culturally separate from the rest of Absalom or assimilate. The death of prophecy has rendered many of their stargazing rituals and artifacts obsolete, and a feeling of emptiness fills the hearts of these residents. The neighborhood is also where the city's lizardfolk population gathers, with inns offering live fish to eat, hot showers to sleep in, and lights that provide solar warmth in the winter.

Saucy Wench.title: Saucy Wench
Saucy Wench.extract: The Saucy Wench meadhall is one of the very few of the permanent buildings in the Grand Bazaar in the Coins district of Absalom. The popular four-story edifice near the center of the bazaar serves roast meat, bread, and mead, and is well-known for its uniformed all-female waiting staff. The tavern's employees all work for owner Lady Kythes Finch, captain of the Token Guard, so unwelcome advances are often dealt with by one of the many off-duty guardsmen who number among the meadhall's patrons.

Anandari Block.title: Anandari Block
Anandari Block.extract: The Anandari Block is a Westgate neighborhood populated mostly by people of vudrani descent, and the architecture and food offered in the area reflect this style. The House Anandari are said to have been companions of Khiben-Sald in the past, and in just three generations, they became a prominent family and now control the entire neighborhood, which is patrolled by their Guild of Spears mercenaries.

Javelin Gallery.title: Javelin Gallery
Javelin Gallery.extract: The Javelin Gallery is the base of operations for the Guild of Spears, composed of veteran guards who have joined as mercenaries. The structure was a second-millennium manor house converted into a defensive fortification and boasts enough forges to equip an entire army if needed. The Guild has contracts with various mercenaries in the city and they are either highly respected or feared, depending on who sees them.

Guiding Hand.title: Guiding Hand
Guiding Hand.extract: The Guiding Hand is an establishment that offers food, shelter, and job opportunities in the poverty-stricken neighborhood of Mudhaven. Tern on the Wind is an elf from the Crown of the World who came to represent her insular people in the city, but upon witnessing the plight of the people in the streets, she began her religious mission to help the community. She follows Findeladlara, and the two-story building with stained-glass windows is kept clean thanks to magical wards amidst the filthy streets and buildings.

Mother Sphinx.title: Mother Sphinx
Mother Sphinx.extract: Mother Sphinx is a shop a few blocks from the Grand Bazaar where Horner Shan sells household gadgets at great prices. Behind secret doors, illusions, and traps in the underground lies a temple dedicated to Norgorber, where Horner is its high priest, praying to the Gray Master aspect of the deity. Rival gangs gather to settle disputes, recruit new talent, or profess their faith, but few know that Horner knows what goes on beneath his shop or that he is the leader of the city's most secretive thieves' guild, the Smoke Knights.

Eastgate Hall.title: Eastgate Hall
Eastgate Hall.extract: Eastgate Hall is an ornate stone structure that once served a group of adventurers fighting against the corruption spread by Rovagug's followers. Today, it is used by the Eastgate Council, which meets monthly to resolve issues arising in the district.

Broken Bastion.title: Broken Bastion
Broken Bastion.extract: The Broken Bastion was a clock tower maintained by a mystical sect obsessed with time, but three centuries ago the structure and the temporal artifact it protected were destroyed by the dragon Maejeryx Steeleye. Since then, strange temporal anomalies have occurred in the area, attracting the curiosity of Fourth Spell Lord Muar Gauthfallow, who resides in the complex. The sect constructed many chambers sealed with anti-divination magic to store dangerous magical artifacts, and Muar is putting them to good use by storing the dangerous contraband he acquires in the course of his duties, intended for use against any external magical threat. There is only one chamber that was already sealed before his arrival, and whenever a temporal anomaly occurs, the lock keeping it closed moves. Muar considers it his responsibility to be in the area in case whatever emerges from the chamber proves dangerous.

Tiantown.title: Tiantown
Tiantown.extract: Tiantown is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where most of the Tian Xia immigrants on Absalom reside. Although empires on that continent tend to be long-lasting, their fall results in great trauma for their inhabitants, who then move to Absalom and the most recent wave of migration came with the collapse of Lung Wa. Rival cultures and past enmities occasionally resurface, but the residents prefer the security of a familiar culture and language, blending with the more curious aspects of Absalom. Other unique residents include nagas, hobgoblins from Kaoling, and strange goblins never before seen in the Inner Sea.

Yemhasin.title: Yemhasin
Yemhasin.extract: Yemhasin is a neighborhood in Absalom where the Yamasan diaspora goes about their daily lives. Most are farmers, and even if they don't sell their vegetables, they likely have a garden where they grow them for personal use. Initial opinions about them were negative because they couldn't use their agricultural skills in an urban setting, and compared to the Lirgeni diaspora, they didn't assimilate well to the city. However, over time, they adapted, using whatever space they had to plant crops and preserve their culinary culture, while also leaving behind the caste system of their original nation.

Groggy Froggy.title: Groggy Froggy
Groggy Froggy.extract: The Groggy Froggy is a Westgate tavern that changed hands when its previous owner was petrified by a gorgon in 4714 AR, and the tavern was passed on to Annara Laskin and the other staff members. The statue of the cruel tavern keeper stands in the middle of the tavern with its mouth agape in a ridiculed expression of terror, and the tavern's popularity has reached unprecedented levels. A pastime was created where customers could paint on the statue, giving it a new look, and it was magically cleaned every week. This pastime stopped when the statue mysteriously disappeared one day, and there are conflicting accounts, with some saying that an enemy destroyed the statue while others say that an ally unpetrified it, although it's hard to believe he had any.

Grand Bazaar.title: Grand Bazaar
Grand Bazaar.extract:
For other meanings of "Grand Bazaar", please see Grand Bazaar (disambiguation).

Nearly anything can be found for sale in the Grand Bazaar of The Coins district in Absalom. The area has few permanent buildings, and most goods are sold from tents, carts, portable stages, and merchant wagons. Many merchants use dancers or illusionists to draw a sufficient crowd to make a sale. After dark, the Grand Bazaar becomes quite dangerous as criminals from the Docks and Puddles districts come here in search of heavy purses.

Grand Lodge.title: Grand Lodge
Grand Lodge.extract:
For another meaning of "Grand Lodge", please see Grand Lodge (faction).

The Grand Lodge is the world headquarters for the organization of explorers known as the Pathfinder Society. Located in the Foreign Quarter of the city of Absalom, it is a massive fortress surrounded by tall stone walls. Within the outer walls sit seven smaller fortified structures that each have a separate function, the largest of which is Skyreach, an enormous five-towered building, which also houses the Society's Grand Hall.

Clockwork Cathedral.title: Clockwork Cathedral
Clockwork Cathedral.extract: The Clockwork Cathedral, the youngest of Absalom's Great Schools, specializes in the studies of mechanical engineering and constructs. The Cathedral is located in the Merchants' Quarter, within The Coins district of Absalom.

Mithril Hall.title: Mithril Hall
Mithril Hall.extract: The Mithril Hall is a beautiful, traditional-style structure in Westgate that serves as offices and residences for the bureaucrats and diplomats of the Western Council, which administers the district. It is divided into hexagonal sections, resembling a honeycomb, with each office having a corridor that connects to an interior courtyard with a garden and fountain in the center. While undeniably beautiful, it can be disorienting for visitors. The Hall is open five days a week until sunset and later on weekends, and this schedule has drawn complaints from working-class residents who find it difficult to attend to administrative matters or file complaints during those hours.

Second Labyrinth.title: Second Labyrinth
Second Labyrinth.extract: The Second Labyrinth is a popular social and gambling club among chelaxians and gamblers in The Coins district, run by the beautiful Lady Azoria.
