The gothic exterior and gargoyles watching passersby are the first things one sees upon arrival, and inside, games of all kinds from Vudra to Riddleport fill the tables. Since Lord Gulv's return, the establishment has been experiencing a resurgence, and the profits earned by House Tevineg are on the rise.

Concerned Residents' Union.title: Concerned Residents' Union
Concerned Residents' Union.extract:
The Concerned Residents' Union is Eastgate's second council, unrecognized as official. However, because the district is such a close-knit community, decisions made by the residents are often final, while official recognition is merely a formality. Their headquarters are located in a red brick building in the district center, and landowners and shopkeepers coordinate their schedules to meet regularly. The union's operations could be considered organized crime, as they decide who to elect as Nomarch, which lieutenants to be promoted to the Post Guard, and what favors the council owes to the union's businesses.

The organization operates through donations to charitable causes, though this money tends to disappear as it passes through various channels, and any criminal investigation is terminated before it even begins, simply by one resident whispering something to another until the guard catches on. The union isn't interested in lining its own pockets; it aims to maintain the calm and supportive atmosphere of Eastgate, even if it means marginalizing some people. The two cults, the Children of Spring and the Circle of Stones, are a concern, as President Gelda Dellby's son, Rance, has joined the Children as a recruiter. This betrayal has hardened her heart, and she seeks to close the park and eliminate the cults. The more sons and daughters who join the cults, the more likely her proposals are to be approved.

Endiron School.title: Endiron School
Endiron School.extract: The Endiron School in Eastgate is a school founded by a noble family with the aim of using words to resolve conflicts between people. To this end, the institution places great emphasis on languages, and its students must know two non-native languages ​​before graduating. Instructors from abroad come to share their culture, language, and food with the students, who often become merchants, diplomats, or travelers. The student body is composed of three-quarters Eastgate children who do not have to pay for their education, while the remaining third consists of children from abroad from families who have heard of the school's reputation and pay exorbitant tuition fees for their children to study there.

Mama Shrog's Solutions.title: Mama Shrog's Solutions
Mama Shrog's Solutions.extract: Mama Shrog's Solutions is a shop that was bought years ago by a woman called Mama Shrog. The building has a grotesque history, having belonged to a painter who murdered his entire family to obtain the specific colour of red he sought. Mama Shrog is a curious old woman who claims to be able to answer her visitors' questions, and although she has no children, she calls everyone her own. The shop primarily sells alchemical products to address her customers' physical ailments, and although people view Shrog with suspicion because she doesn't hide her faith in Lamashtu, there is no evidence that she has committed any crimes. Several champions and holy warriors have tried to expel her from the city and close the shop, but they haven't made any progress through legal means. When some Firebrands have tried to forcibly remove her, demons appear to defend her with extreme violence, which she justifies as self-defense when questioned by the guards. Although the shop is the only obvious temple of the goddess, shrines are also springing up throughout the city, taking advantage of Absalom's religious freedom.

Utterhome.title: Utterhome
Utterhome.extract: Utterhome is the base of operations for the Sleepless Suns, the guard of Absalom's Foreign Quarter. The structure was a manor in the Osirian style, complete with Jadwiga ice statues, glass panels imported from New Thassilon, Tian Xia-style pools, and Vudrani area gardens, the fortress reflects the district's diversity. The very name Utterhome represents the many ways in which languages ​​refer to the concept of home, and all people are welcome regardless of their cultural background. The guard's name also has a similar concept, indicating that the sun never sets for them, as their members come from all over the world. Therefore, when it is night in Absalom, it is day in Tian Xia, and its members embody that devotion to citizen protection.

Grand Dance Hall of Kortos.title: Grand Dance Hall of Kortos
Grand Dance Hall of Kortos.extract:
The Grand Dance Hall of Kortos is an ornate, round theater on the district of The Coins in the city of Absalom.

The city's richest merchants and most ambitious nobles gather in the theater to organise deals in order to control the economy of the Inner Sea. Admission is free to those in the appropriate attire, but access to the hall's three balconies requires increasingly audacious fees as the guest ascends the levels.

The entertainment includes the finest Vudrani dancers and gymnasts from throughout the Inner Sea and beyond.

Evergreen Park.title: Evergreen Park
Evergreen Park.extract: Evergreen Park, located in the Eastgate district, is the city's largest park, maintaining a perpetual spring thanks to the efforts of the Children of Spring, a Gozreh cult led by Lady Evigail of House Wycomb. At the center of the park is an open-air altar, often visited by sailors before embarking on voyages to distant lands.

Devils' Garden.title: Devils' Garden
Devils' Garden.extract: Devil's Garden, also known as Cheliax's Garden, is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where both exiles from the nation and agents of House Thrune live together, enjoying wine, opera, and the company of each other most of which cannot return to their homeland. After the civil war, many nobles went to Absalom to escape persecution, although several were killed by the new regime, which did not provoke a strong response from Absalom beyond a letter of disapproval to their embassy. Tensions have eased in recent decades, and as long as these nobles do not act against Her Majestrix, they have nothing to fear. Throughout the district, hatred and detestance toward Ravounel can be observed.

School of the North Song-Wind.title: School of the North Song-Wind
School of the North Song-Wind.extract: The School of the North Song-Wind, located in the Foreign Quarter, is a branch of the Magaambya. It consists of a grove of trees with fabric draped around the trunks, creating natural walls within the "structure". This structure is characterized by geometric patterns carved into the vegetation, as well as crystals symbolizing Nantambu. The study of the arcane is paramount here, though it also serves as an unofficial embassy for those seeking to learn about Nantambu culture and way of life. Thanks to the area's natural magic, the leshy are drawn in droves, finding great comfort resting among the trees.

Taurean Embassy.title: Taurean Embassy
Taurean Embassy.extract: The Taurean Embassy is a residence built in the style of Iblydos, and it's where one of Absalom's most curious inhabitants, the albino minotaur Nuar Spiritskin, lives. He was banished by his tribe from the Kortos Mounts, and being just over seven feet tall, he had no trouble integrating into human society. When he intervened to save a man being beaten by sailors, his life changed drastically, for this man was none other than Lord Gyr on a confidential mission. Gyr bestowed upon him the title of Prince of Minotaurs, but he knew it was merely a flight of fancy from the Primarch. Now, he works to keep the streets crime-free, with no aspirations to leadership, content simply to have a place to call home.

Al'Hakam Estate.title: Al'Hakam Estate
Al'Hakam Estate.extract: The Al'Hakam Estate is an impressive manor house on the edge of The Coins, near the Ascendant Court. Its grandeur is understandable, as it is not only the home of the influential merchant Aaqir al'Hakam but also of Emir Thalzar Gaatan, ambassador of Qadira. The couple lives there with their three adopted daughters, whom they adore.

Turning Leaf.title: Turning Leaf
Turning Leaf.extract: The Turning Leaf is a very popular restaurant in Eastgate that focuses on fish and vegetable dishes sourced from the Green Ridge. For two or three days each month, traveling chefs make appearances, preparing specialties from far-flung regions around the world. Tickets for these days are not only expensive because of the food but also because they are a significant social status symbol in the city, and there is fierce competition to get even one seat at the table. The owner is a ghoran named Qidesca who doesn't understand the intricacies of comedy, but for some reason, their stand-up routines have the clientele roaring with laughter each time they perform.

Blackblade's.title: Blackblade's
Blackblade's.extract: Blackblade's is a successful fighting academy where former pathfinder and mercenary Benkhal Blackblade instructs his students in combat for five pieces of silver twice a week. Novices spar in the academy's courtyards, serving as an advertisement to attract more students, while the most talented are personally trained by Benkhal inside the building. Most students do not live on the premises, with the exception of the instructors, who at the end of the week lock the doors and meet with former students for parties that last until dawn.

To Eat the World.title: To Eat the World
To Eat the World.extract: To Eat the World is a famous restaurant in Westgate thanks to its chef, Sammael Rantore, who transforms the process of cooking and serving food into a dramatic spectacle that people even pay to watch. Only the wealthiest and most noble can afford to book a dinner here, which can take weeks or months, but the price is commensurate with the work and entertainment provided. Adventurers are hired to obtain the specific ingredients desired by their clients, and mementos of the most outlandish culinary feats adorn the walls. On one of the tables are statues of the Rantore family eating a cake shaped like Golarion.

Tomb of the Living.title: Tomb of the Living
Tomb of the Living.extract:
The Tomb of the Living is located in Westgate and is built in the Osirian style. Thanks to the loophole that those who continue to move when entombed can be imprisoned without trial after being buried, the city maintains that this is a tomb, not a prison and its spaciousness allows them to incarcerate enemies of the city indefinitely, whenever they wish, without technically breaking the laws established by Aroden. The prison's existence is a secret from the masses and other nations, who would not react well to its presence. The city performs rituals in the upper crypts, which contain the corpses of nobles who lacked the means for burial elsewhere in the city and below lies a labyrinth of cells accessible only to members of the Sally Guard, those who reveal its secret are punished by being imprisoned in the tomb. The darkness, scarce food and water, and limited space drive those imprisoned here insane, causing their muscles to atrophy and constantly weakened.

Some of the prisoners here whose names have been forgotten include:

  • The Almost Goddess, around 4700 AR, a charismatic woman who arrived in Absalom to take the Test of the Starstone. She amassed a large following and was talented in everything she did; no one doubted she would become a goddess. On the day she was to take the test, she disappeared and was forgotten.
  • The Forever Wizard, a researcher obsessed with his own mortality, tried to overcome it, and surprisingly, contrary to what usually happens with these immortality schemes, he achieved eternal life. After experimenting on his own body, he achieved perfect regeneration, making it impossible for him to die from violence or old age. He was going to leave to go see his elderly parents in Taldor to share the secret of immortality with them and then with the rest of the world. He disappeared the day before he was to leave by ship.
  • The Quiet King, a nobleman of the city, witnessed all the atrocities Absalom committed for the "good of the nation", and his conscience wouldn't allow him to let this continue. He spoke to journalists, telling them he would bring them proof of these atrocities and that they should wait for him. Now he is in a cell under constant magical silence, and his tongue has been cut out.

Avenue of the Hopeful.title: Avenue of the Hopeful
Avenue of the Hopeful.extract: The Avenue of the Hopeful is a winding street in Absalom's Ascendant Court and a busy market catering to those fascinated by the Test of the Starstone: hopefuls, followers, worshippers, and the merchants, pickpockets, and charlatans who hope to make money from them. Some of the sincere hopefuls have lived on the street for years, while others are merely confidence men. Merchants offer religious symbols, holy water, and relics, as well as "faith papers", which indicate the popularity of a hopeful deity. The booth of the Masked God of Secrets, a presumed hopeful, is a front for an organization that collects and sells secrets.

Irorium.title: Irorium
Irorium.extract: The Irorium is an enormous open-air coliseum located in Absalom's Foreign Quarter that showcases non-lethal gladiatorial combat, mock sea-battles, religious festivals, and many other types of events and is the largest arena in the Inner Sea region.

Guild of Wonders.title: Guild of Wonders
Guild of Wonders.extract: The headquarters of the Guild of Wonders is ostensibly an unmarked, poorly received tourism attraction composed of creative taxidermy that purportedly depicts cryptids and other beings, and is located in a shoddy building in the Westgate neighborhood of Absalom. However, its decrepit appearance masks its true purpose as a guildhouse for assassins, spies, and saboteurs.

Stoneshield House.title: Stoneshield House
Stoneshield House.extract: Stoneshield House serves as the headquarters for the Graycloaks, the district watch of the Ascendant Court district of Absalom. Stoneshield House is named in honour of Dunarr Stoneshield, a former captain of the Graycloaks who is treated almost like a secular saint by serving members of the Graycloaks. The Graycloaks themselves are all atheists, allowing them to police the countless religions that call the Ascendant Court home with a sense of fairness, uninfluenced by any faith in the gods. They are currently led by Runewulf the Unbeliever, who lives within Stoneshield House. The building of Stoneshield House is understated in form, particularly when compared with the endless ostentatious temples and shrines within the district. Still, the Stoneshield House has its own imposing, ominous air to it. The Graycloaks are called on to deal with strange and powerful threats, and who knows what they have locked away within their headquarters.

Tower of Twin Truths.title: Tower of Twin Truths
Tower of Twin Truths.extract: The Tower of Twin Truths is the largest and most important temple of Nethys in Absalom.

Windarium.title: Windarium
Windarium.extract: The Windarium is a clockworks shop in Westgate that is going through difficult times. The shop is located in a shopping district frequented by those looking for parts or repairs. Founded by Symo in 4705 AR, the shop began to gain popularity when he met Mirtion, who had already successfully run several shops. In 4710 AR, they married, and Symo was promoted in 4716 AR. The shop's popularity was booming, and everything was going great. After the Black Echelon Uprising of 4717 AR, Symo was promoted again, and with no time for the shop, Mirtion has had to run it alone. He has no idea about the engineering required to maintain clockworks and, demotivated, has put his passion into the badgers who fill the shop and generate complaints about the bribes they take from customers and employees alike.

Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch.title: Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch
Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch.extract: The Absalom Chirurgeon Dispatch is where the doctors, physicians, and healers of the Westgate district meet. It operates similarly to the practices of smaller towns where there is no hospital, and the doctor travels to the patient's home when called. The Sally Guard maintains magical communications with the Dispatch, allowing them to act quickly in emergencies. Although their primary focus is the district's health, they can be employed throughout Absalom and swear to assist in situations such as a siege. Classes are also offered on how to provide basic medical services, and they are always hiring new doctors, paying those with magical healing powers quite well.

Watercleft.title: Watercleft
Watercleft.extract: The Watercleft is a cliff on the border between the Petal District and Eastgate, where a spring is moved by aqueducts, creating a beautiful view. There is much social activity in the area as people come to collect water from the spring. The distribution of water through private tunnels is seen as a disgrace by the residents of Green Ridge, which was once a separate district from Eastgate with its own council. After being absorbed, the people of Green Ridge lamented the loss of the values ​​that made the Watercleft so appealing.

Hothouse.title: Hothouse
Hothouse.extract: The Hothouse is the administrative headquarters of the Petal District, where the Conclave of Flowers meets. It also serves as a greenhouse, housing plants imported from around the world, symbolizing the transparency of the council's actions. In practice, however, the interior is uncomfortable and unpleasant, also serving as a metaphor for the interests behind the councilors' actions, which are directed by the local aristocracy.

Sally Port.title: Sally Port
Sally Port.extract: The Sally Port is the Sally Guard's base of operations and the gateway to the district, connecting it to Westerhold and Shoreline outside the city. The gate is specifically designed to facilitate cavalry charges, and tax collectors and bureaucrats record the trade passing through it, as the settlements of Otari, Galizhur, and Diobel use this gate to enter the city. A ditch surrounds the wall, and four drawbridges allow for adaptation to the city's defensive needs. Lady Seleenae regularly visits her office on the walls, where her financial acumen makes extensive use of the records of the economy flowing through the gate. Exports from the nearest settlements include fish from Shoreline and dwarven crafts from Westerhold, often incorporating the district's cultural influences.

Seventh Church of Iomedae.title: Seventh Church of Iomedae
Seventh Church of Iomedae.extract: The Seventh Church of Iomedae is an important temple of goddess in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom, and the site of the seventh of Iomedae's 11 miraculous acts, the calling of the Undying Light. This miracle is commemorated by the temple itself and the Iomedaenne, a 10-foot statue of the goddess carved from red limestone and banded with sapphires. Although the goddess is popular in the city, and the Seventh Church trains most of her clerics here for three or four years, most of the priests at the church are young or elderly, since her most vital followers take up service elsewhere. Absalom is seen as a place of recruitment by the goddess's church, rather than a bulwark of faith in peril. The high priest of the temple is Tavorae Falsebane, who replaced the aged Genedair the Faithful who passed on to his final reward. Across the main street from the church is the Tempering Hall, which trains paladins of Iomedae, as well those of the churches of Abadar, Irori, and Shelyn.

Cayden's Hall.title: Cayden's Hall
Cayden's Hall.extract:
Cayden's Hall in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom is a large, boisterous meadhall and temple of Cayden Cailean, built of wood as was the original meadhall from which Cayden set out to take the Test of the Starstone. Due to the drunken revelry, religious bonfires, and consequent accidents, this is the fourteenth temple built on the current site near a large fountain, which is itself at least the fourth site to house Cayden's temple.

Efforts are made by the local priests and the house guards to quell any serious fights and extinguish uncontrolled fires, despite the fact that they are often as inebriated as the patrons who frequent the hall. Aside from simple drinks and food, the hall also boasts a mid-sized speaking hall, intended for both religious proclamations and general entertainment.

Ruled by High Priestess Saphira, the Lady of Intemperance, it is not merely a place to get drunk, but also a gathering-hall for adventurers to meet and plot expeditions, rebellions, and exploits of all kinds.

Pleasure Salon of Calistria.title: Pleasure Salon of Calistria
Pleasure Salon of Calistria.extract: Elves and aiuvarins are common among the temple prostitutes of the Pleasure Salon of Calistria in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom. Elves are otherwise relatively uncommon in Absalom, mostly consisting of adventurers or outcasts; more common are their half-elven descendants. Although Calistria's priests play a significant role in the operations of the temple, the Pleasure Salon is managed by the ranking sacred prostitute, Dyrianna of House Avenstar.

Court of Black Paper.title: Court of Black Paper
Court of Black Paper.extract: The Court of Black Paper in Absalom is, in nearly equal parts, a powerful cartel-like law firm, a cutthroat school of legal studies, and a cult of Norgorber. Its ranking members are known as priest-advocates and are led by Senior Priest-Advocate Samel Maleagant, who has a reputation as the city's most prominent lawyer.

Azari Palace.title: Azari Palace
Azari Palace.extract:
The Azari Palace is an ossified remnant of the period when worship of Aroden was a prominent feature of life in Absalom, before his death and the dawn of the Age of Lost Omens. Aroden's church was once an integral part of civic life in the City at the Centre of the World, but, with his passing in 4606 AR, his followers fell to infighting and despair. Many worshippers converted to other faiths, but 5,000 years of history, relics, and influence do not disperse quite as easily. The Azari Palace is the physical embodiment of this. Once called Saint Solian Temple, it once served as the second-largest temple of Aroden in the Ascendant Court; the largest ex-cathedral in the district now serves as the Chelaxian embassy. Nevertheless, it is still one of the district's most beautiful buildings.

Azari Palace is named after its owners, House Azari, one of Absalom's numerous noble houses that traces its roots back to one of Aroden's earliest disciples and was intrinsically linked to Aroden's church. Dozens of House Azari's scions were elected to lead the church of Aroden, and three members of the house, including St. Solian, ascended to become saints of Aroden's religion. House Azari's support of the crumbling church of Aroden cost them everything, bankrupting the family and forcing them to sell their palatial estate in the Petal District. Led by house patriarch Lord Toiden, they moved into St. Solian Temple instead.

As more and more churches and temples of Aroden have closed, the Azari Palace has become a vast repository for relics and artifacts of the now-dead religion. With no new worshippers coming to the faith and House Azari fallen in all but name, the Azari Palace has a feeling of forlorn abandonment, with near-endless chambers of dusty relics and forlorn treasures left forgotten in a temple to a god that Golarion has moved on from.

Fierce Stripe.title: Fierce Stripe
Fierce Stripe.extract: The Fierce Stripe is a shop where all of Absalom's badger needs are met. Lady Chesne of House Yuriel runs the shop, acquiring unique breeds of badgers for adoption by the residents of Westgate, where the animal has supplanted dogs and cats as a pet. The Tri-Stripe Society shops there and meets weekly to drink tea and discuss the town's badger competitions.

Metringer Sanitarium.title: Metringer Sanitarium
Metringer Sanitarium.extract: Metringer Sanitarium has been in operation since 4000 AR, serving as a place of healing and medicine for those suffering from mental illness, as well as a site for ethically questionable experiments on these patients. Its director, Flevvid Grummlin, has shown the facility to the Sally Guard on several occasions, but they have found nothing out of the ordinary. In secret dungeons, patients deemed unfit for service are taken to end their stay. Salindra Concilio escaped the building and has been warning others and seeking help to reveal the situation inside, though many consider her delusional. Only one person is offering a reward for this information.

Embassy of New Thassilon.title: Embassy of New Thassilon
Embassy of New Thassilon.extract: The Embassy of New Thassilon, located in the Foreign Quarter, represents the interests of Eurythnia and Edasseril in Absalom. During its first year of operation, both navion agents cooperated with their stone giant allies to renovate a dilapidated building into a magnificent embassy. The embassy is divided into three parts, the first is a central hall open to the public, with the Sihedron symbol engraved on the floor. It is here that diplomats assure everyone that their masters do not cause problems in the region. The west hall is where Edasseril's agents paint and renovate the structure, aiming to make it more magnificent, Ambassador Firandivar is found there, claiming to be one of Belimarius's grandsons. In the west hall, Eurythnian agents have created a shrine for Nocticula, and a simulacrum of Sorshen acts as ambassador, calling herself Ayandai to avoid confusion, she offers the hall to artists persecuted by the city's inhabitants.

Gerig's Workshop.title: Gerig's Workshop
Gerig's Workshop.extract: Gerig's Workshop is where the popular drink of Gerig the Inspirer is produced, and since its production is a trade secret, there are always many Guild of Spears guards patrolling the area.

Lions' Square.title: Lions' Square
Lions' Square.extract: The Lions' Square is located in the Foreign Quarter and is one of the largest enclaves in the district where Taldor expatriates can live in peace. After every war, famine, and political conflict, the district receives new migrants, and with the prohibition of faith in Sarenrae by Stavian I, many of the inhabitants are devout followers of the goddess. After the War for the Crown, many nobles, exiled or dissatisfied with the new direction of the country, have come to enjoy the freedom that Absalom offers.

Vault of Abadar.title: Vault of Abadar
Vault of Abadar.extract:
For other meanings of "Bank of Abadar", please see Bank of Abadar (disambiguation).

The Vault of Abadar, also known as the Bank of Abadar, in Absalom's Ivy District is the most holy site to the faithful of Abadar.

While the Vault may appear to be unstable, it is sturdy and solid. Jostlin Ferqyr, councilwoman for the Ivy District, runs the vault like a merchant bank and provides venture capital to young merchants and artist groups.

Bor Dralfo, a paladin affiliated with Jostlin and the Vault, leads the Brotherhood of Abadar and enforces Ivy District rules to keep streets safe and clear, which has lead to tension with Nomarch Alain Always.

Menhemes Manor.title: Menhemes Manor
Menhemes Manor.extract: Menhemes Manor has served as the residence for members of the Menhemes family for generations in Otari. It is currently the residence of Oseph Menhemes, the village mayor, along with his wife, Emnala, and five children. Part of the manor serves as a public museum chronicling the history of Otari.

Bank of Absalom.title: Bank of Absalom
Bank of Absalom.extract: The Bank of Absalom has a far-reaching power, influencing trade and finance beyond the City at the Centre of the World across the Inner Sea region. From the Bank of Absalom, its bankers can influence the flow of trade and commerce by adjusting interest rates, approving (or not) massive loans, and issuing debt collection orders. The Bank of Absalom is nominally a secular institution, rather than a temple devoted to Abadar, the god of trade, but, in reality, the Bank of Absalom is run by Vroclaw of Brevoy, the most powerful Archbanker of Abadar in Absalom. The Bank of Absalom takes its religious orders from the Vault of Abadar in the Ivy District, but Vroclaw controls all loans and debt collection. Vroclaw's critics accuse him of facilitating the exploitation of indigenous peoples to extract profit, a claim Vroclaw does not deign to refute.

Street of Little Gods.title: Street of Little Gods
Street of Little Gods.extract: The Street of Little Gods is located in the Tarheel Promenade district, where deities without large congregations have small altars dedicated to them. From minor northern gods like Demon Lords, to the faith of Razmir or the Prophecies of Kalistrade, all have a place to pray and pay homage to their faith, seeking new followers.

Castle Blakros.title: Castle Blakros
Castle Blakros.extract: Castle Blakros is an imposing black stone structure in the Petal District. The Blakros family lives surrounded by 20-foot-high walls and beautiful gardens near a natural lake. Inside, Lady Hamaria manages the artifacts that don't fit in the Blakros Museum, frequently hiring adventurers to handle problems her own agents can't solve, focusing instead on political and commercial issues.

Hillview Station.title: Hillview Station
Hillview Station.extract: Hillview Station is the Lotus Guard's base of operations and the Petal District courthouse, hosting the city's highest-profile trials. Inside, the galleries offer spacious viewing areas and elevated balconies for observing the proceedings, and the holding cells resemble luxury suites compared to other jails in the city.

House of Seven Faces.title: House of Seven Faces
House of Seven Faces.extract: The House of Seven Faces is a Westgate temple dedicated to Desna where her followers can rest. The clergy dislike ostentatious cathedrals, so there is little decoration except for the seven faces that appeared on the facade the first night the temple was built, and whose origin remains a mystery. There are many theories: some link the seven faces to Thassilon, others suggest they represent Sivanah and that Desna and she are the same person—a theory met with laughter or annoyance by the clergy. But the most sensible theory points to a connection with the Sevenfold Cynosure, where she dwells, the realm being a tribute to the seven planets Rovagug destroyed first. The faces, then, represent seven cultures from these planets that may have arisen and they also serve as a memorial.

Archerus Manor.title: Archerus Manor
Archerus Manor.extract: Archerus Manor is the residence of one of the Inner Sea's most famous artists, the painter Endrik Archerus, who lives in a massive stone tower visible throughout the district.

Statue of Primarch Harvi.title: Statue of Primarch Harvi
Statue of Primarch Harvi.extract: The Statue of Primarch Harvi, located in the Petal District, is one of the city's most famous public artworks, but not for a positive reason as it's considered the most ugly statue in all of Absalom. It depicts this Primarch from the Age of Enthronement on horseback during the Red Siege of 4499 AR, but his face makes him look more like he has a cold or intestinal problems than anything that inspires courage. The Conclave of Flowers seems to agree that it should be removed, but the problem is what to put in its place. Some want a statue of Lord Gyr and his fellow adventurers, including Lord Avid, of course. Others, associated with the Citizens' League, prefer a statue honoring the soldiers who fell in the city's many sieges. And the new nobles want to improve their reputation with the Vault of Abadar by erecting a statue of Abadar.

Sirola Manor.title: Sirola Manor
Sirola Manor.extract: Sirola Manor is a spacious building in Westgate, but not as ostentatious as some others in the city, being the residence of retired Pathfinder Venla and Urmas Sirola.

Sech Nevali.title: Sech Nevali
Sech Nevali.extract: Sech Nevali is a relic from the Thassilonian realm of Haruka✝︎. Also called the Hanging Monastery, it is a vast stone temple complex built upon a very thick metal platform suspended over a mile-deep chasm by three immense chains attached to three nearby mountain peaks. A fourth chain failed centuries ago, making the monastery sway disconcertingly in strong winds.

Martel.title: Martel
Martel.extract:
The city of Martel is in the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, along the Ustradi River west of Alkenstar City and east of the Gunworks. Its most notable features are the two enormous buildings that make up most of the city, which are connected by a number of stone and metal bridges. The Grand Duchy of Alkenstar maintains an extensive treasury in Martel, composed of vaults below the city that are guarded by both clockwork and explosive traps.

The Grand Duchy's gunmarshals make their headquarters in Martel. Additionally, the town is the center of banking for the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar, where the financial practices are as advanced as Alkenstar's level of technology. While this makes Alkenstar effective in their financial dealings, it has left the city-state with a slightly-less-than-trusted reputation throughout the Inner Sea region.

Cloudreaver Keep.title: Cloudreaver Keep
Cloudreaver Keep.extract:
An immense fortress called Cloudreaver Keep guards the eastern border of the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar. It is one of the three points connected by the massive Bridge of the Gods, the other two being Alkenstar City and Dongun Hold on the far side of the canyon. While the keep was intended to serve as a defense against outside threats, since its construction it has been plagued almost constantly with hauntings from ghosts within and attacks from nearby bands of yetis.

The keep stands on the western bank of the Ustradi River, and all trade from Nex passes through it.

Coastal Cairns.title: Coastal Cairns
Coastal Cairns.extract: The Coastal Cairns are located near the banks of Lake Encarthan in the nation of Razmiran and are believed to have been built by a race that predates human settlement in the region.

Hawk's Nest.title: Hawk's Nest
Hawk's Nest.extract: Hawks' Nest is the fortress-headquarters of the Daggermark Assassins' Guild in the River Kingdoms.

Samsara Oasis.title: Samsara Oasis
Samsara Oasis.extract: Samsara Oasis is a small town established by calikangs horrified at their role in the destruction of the region. They use Samsara Oasis as a place to meet and research restoration efforts to try to make up for this.

Mercy.title: Mercy
Mercy.extract: Mercy is a small town about 20 miles south of Alkenstar and a mile south of the Ustradi River. It is made up of a wooden palisade that surrounds the roughly one hundred buildings.

Garden of Lead.title: Garden of Lead
Garden of Lead.extract: The Garden of Lead, also known as the Pleasure Palace of the Dead, is a horrifically haunted place even by the standards of Virlych. It hosts endless "parties" among pools of molten metal where tortured ghosts of costumed revelers and undead servants provide grotesque entertainment for their dread mistress, the Skeleton Countess Dissayn.

Casnoriva.title: Casnoriva
Casnoriva.extract:
Casnoriva is a ruined star-shaped manor in the heart of the Hungry Mountains in the modern Ustalavic county of Virlych. It was founded as Ustalav's arcane college by Casnori, a Varisian wizard, in what was then called Virholt. Casnori aided Soividia Ustav at the time of the founding of the nation of Ustalav in the 24th Century AR.

Teaching at the college continued until it became one of the first targets of Tar-Baphon on his return to Golarion as a lich in the 33rd century AR. The lich threw hordes of demons from the Outer Rifts to attack the school, led by his marilith servant Kaltestrua. The attack was successful, and as of 4713 AR Kaltestrua still controlled Casnoriva while awaiting signs from Gallowspire of her master's return. Nevertheless, she still suffers ongoing resistance to her occupation from the ghosts of the Ustalavic mages she defeated centuries before. The mages are led by Mistress Qai in an endless arcane battle within the ruins.

Skull Hill.title: Skull Hill
Skull Hill.extract:
Skull Hill is a Chelaxian fort located in the southern reaches of the Hold of Belkzen in a region known as the Skittermounds.

The fortification marks the furthest extent of the Everwar campaign and the conquest of Cheliax. The fort was used for mercenary recruitment and weapons trading. Skull Hill consists of a motte and bailey keep, surrounded by a wooden wall with a surrounding moat.

Until recently, it was believed to have been destroyed not long after the events of the Everwar but, in fact, continued to be used as part of a spy network to monitor orc activities: an attempt by Cheliax to ensure the protection of its southern territories.

The fortress was invaded and sacked in 4709 AR by a tribe of orcs known as the Skittering Ravagers who had been terrorising the region for years.

Ghasterhall.title: Ghasterhall
Ghasterhall.extract:
Ghasterhall is an infamous library located in Virlych. It is also known as the Palace of Travesties. Although a place of retained knowledge, Ghasterhall displays horrific methods of storing information. Ghasts possessing oracular power stalk the blood-splattered halls and dead victims are nailed to the walls, used for necromantic experimentation.

Ghasterhall has been described as an inverted tower-pit, serving as both prison and library. The awakened demilich Geir acts as head researcher for Tar-Baphon, establishing himself as curator of the massive library, laboratory, and prison. Although a horde of undying scholars still possess tremendous accumulated knowledge, only Geir continues with his endless experimentation on esoteric matters.

Ambition for dark knowledge waned with the imprisonment of the Whispering Tyrant, but after his escape in 4719 AR, Tar-Baphon sought Geir's aid in developing a method of either transforming the orcs of Belkzen into an undead army or destroying them.

For a time, Lucimar the Lich-Wolf sequestered himself in the prison-library. By 4711 AR, Ghasterhall served as headquarters for the Whispering Way.

Pit of Zedoran.title: Pit of Zedoran
Pit of Zedoran.extract:
The Pit of Zedoran is a vast sinkhole within the depths of the Northern Fangwood. Streams of water carrying leaves and organic debris cascade down its sides, and layers of moss and lichen cover hanging vines and ancient roots. The thick forest canopy allows little sunlight to reach the moss-covered earth.

The pit is named for the green dragon Zedoran who as of 4722 AR still laired here. At the edge of the central pit is a wooden cage lift, manually operated by Zedoran's minions. There are two cages, one at the top and the other at the bottom of the pit, each large enough to hold eight humans.

Heliu's Folly.title: Heliu's Folly
Heliu's Folly.extract: Heliu's Folly was one of the westernmost fortresses of the defensive line known as the Sunwall in the Hold of Belkzen.

Dortrot Ranch.title: Dortrot Ranch
Dortrot Ranch.extract: Dortrot Ranch is a large horse ranch located on the plains of the eastern Gravelands just south of the Path River. The family of the owner, Chap "Old Man" Dortrot, has been raising both draft and warhorses there for 11 generations.

Fort Morrine.title: Fort Morrine
Fort Morrine.extract: Fort Morrine is a Iomedaean fortification in disputed territory of the Hold of Belkzen.

Redlake Fort.title: Redlake Fort
Redlake Fort.extract: Redlake Fort was part of the Harchist's Blockade, defending the borders of Lastwall from its position in the Mindspin Mountains. The fort is built on a moat of the River Esk, whose algae give the water a reddish color, hence its name. Like other forts of the blockade, it fell to an orc siege in 4482 AR. In their desperation and hunger, its defenders were fed the flesh of their comrades by the fort's castellan to resist the attack, though it was in vain and these defeated guardians haunt the fort. In the following centuries, different forces have seized the fort, from orcs to hill giants and even ogres, with no one remaining for long due to the unsettling haunts that plague its inhabitants.

Table of Ralock.title: Table of Ralock
Table of Ralock.extract: The Table of Ralock is a flat rock formation located on the plains south of Urgir, near the end of Flood Road. Under a policy first created by Grask Uldeth, at least one representative from each orc hold is to be stationed there with a retinue of bodyguards. These orcs offer their tribal tokens to those who wish to be under their protection in the nation, and the prices vary depending on the group and tribe.

Sicnavier's Lair.title: Sicnavier's Lair
Sicnavier's Lair.extract:
Sicnavier's Lair is a dark chasm located in the Hungry Mountains in eastern Virlych, a grim mountain range in southern Ustalav.

A place haunted by the spirits of evil dragons, Sicnavier's Lair is always ruled by a sole umbral dragon who had succeeded the previous draconic master. The spoils of this rivalry is an enormous accumulation of wealth, lore, and slaves, more than enough to convince the successor to ignore ghostly whispers, even as the growing number of restless ghosts attempt to drive them insane.

Whichever dragon controls the lair names themselves 'Sicnavier', a title that has passed to no fewer than six dragons over the centuries. Attempts by outsiders to slay the dragon usually fail, but on those rare occasions when an attempt succeeds, a new, younger dragon takes on the mantle for him or herself.

The deepest pits of Sicnavier's Lair connects to the Darklands, although the dragon master and the creatures below have limited interaction with one another.

As of 4711 AR the ruler was Sicnavier VI, a very old umbral dragon who is said to have been driven to near madness from the tormenting whispers of its slain kin. Other inhabitants, such as Dark folk, nightwings, and tenebrous worms, also inhabit the lair and are most frequently slaves or pets of their draconic overlord.

Cauldheart.title: Cauldheart
Cauldheart.extract:
Cauldheart is a pit-like valley of grey stone in the Amaans peaks of the Hungry Mountains, between Mount Aremetrus and Gristleknob in the nation of Ustalav. It stretches over three miles in the shape of a human liver, and is so steep that no path leads to its depths; it is not considered scalable, and only ivy escapes from the pit.

This dark location generates rumours, including the use of Cauldheart as a gigantic witches' cauldron used by covens to perform all manner of evil: summoning foul weather and evil gods, crafting slave abominations, and corrupting the land.

Marching Springs.title: Marching Springs
Marching Springs.extract: The Marching Springs are a 30 square mile region of Nirmathas between the Fangwood and the Mindspin Mountains. Geysers erupt in a procession through the region.

Castle Penatha.title: Castle Penatha
Castle Penatha.extract: Castle Penatha was originally built in the World's Edge Mountains in Qadira, but disappeared down to its foundation around 4691 AR. Its fate remained unknown until it suddenly reappeared completely intact—with the addition of a tower with a golden cupola—near the summit of Mount Rectzaid, in the mountains of the Ustalavic county of Ulcazar. It is now said to be the home of Isla Bellsan, the purple-cloaked, self-proclaimed greatest thief in Avistan.

Fort Ozem.title: Fort Ozem
Fort Ozem.extract: Fort Ozem is a Chernasardo Rangers fortress in Southern Fangwood. Fort Ozem was once used as a staging post in the war against the Whispering Tyrant. It is said that the fort was briefly home to Iomedae herself. The Rangers use it as a hideout. The worn outer walls are overgrown with vegetation. Its appearance has been deliberately neglected to indicate that there is nothing inside worth investigating. If enemies ever do draw near, the fort can be evacuated at a moment's notice.

Sombrefell Hall.title: Sombrefell Hall
Sombrefell Hall.extract: The massive manor known as Sombrefell Hall is located about 25 miles northeast of Rozenport in Ustalav. It is situated at the end of a fairly well demarcated road abutting the Lantern Lake. The manor has belonged to the Oscilar family for generations. It is built in the gothic Ustalavic fashion and its grand entrance consists of mahogany double doors.

Elkhaven Lodge.title: Elkhaven Lodge
Elkhaven Lodge.extract: Elkhaven Lodge was a hunting lodge dedicated to Erastil in the Fangwood, though it fell into ruin when the Darkblight spread through the forest in 4062 AR. Rangers and freedom fighters have used the lodge as a hideout sporadically, but no one has rebuilt the place due to the conflict with Molthune.

Fort Faelon.title: Fort Faelon
Fort Faelon.extract: Fort Faelon is an abandoned Nirmathi fort. Located along the coast north from Tamran, this fort overlooks the waters of Lake Encarthan from the marshy area where it was built.

Flowering Jasmine Monastery.title: Flowering Jasmine Monastery
Flowering Jasmine Monastery.extract: The Flowering Jasmine Monastery is a secluded monastic temple on the island nation of Jalmeray. Known for its interfaith devotion and tranquil setting, the monastery historically served as a place of worship for worshipers of many Vudrani gods and empyreal lords before suffering a catastrophic desecration at the hands of Miranksha.

Pure Temple of the Maharajah.title: Pure Temple of the Maharajah
Pure Temple of the Maharajah.extract: The Pure Temple of the Maharajah is built over the spring that is the source of the River Sald in the small eastern hills of Jalmeray. The structure is white and gold and is maintained by bound genies and a huge number of slaves and their duty-bound overseers. The temple is dedicated to Khiben-Sald, the ancient ruler who founded Jalmeray. Every four years, thousands of people make their way up the Sald from the city of Niswan to pay respects to his memory. Even though the maharajah is not buried here (and some claim that he is still alive), the people treat the Pure Temple as if it was his final resting place.

Tiger's Eye Monastery.title: Tiger's Eye Monastery
Tiger's Eye Monastery.extract: The Tiger's Eye Monastery is a remote building located in the northern forests of Jalmeray, far from local trade routes. It is said to be decorated with depictions of tiger-headed humanoids and currently is occupied by a group of bandits.

Bastion of Light.title: Bastion of Light
Bastion of Light.extract: The Bastion of Light is a temple of Sarenrae located in the southwestern Gravelands, near Roslar's Coffer. As of 4722 AR it was fortified by the Burning Sun tribe orc Uirch and his band of Sarenite orcs. They work on both restoring the temple and defending it from any undead that come near.

Mount Kla.title: Mount Kla
Mount Kla.extract: Mount Kla is the 12th-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 14,411 feet. It is located in the central parts of the range east of Emperor's Peak and west of the Verduran Forest and is visible from the sunlit East End of Highhelm's King's Crown layer.

Weeping Stairwell.title: Weeping Stairwell
Weeping Stairwell.extract:
The Weeping Stairwell is a hidden series of caverns on the eastern coast that provide access to the isolated upper reaches of the secret island of Grand Sarret.

The Weeping Stairwell can be accessed from the waters of the Obari Ocean via a cave that lies concealed below the waterline along the eastern reaches of Grand Sarret. The Weeping Stairwell can also be accessed from the island's top via a narrow fissure hidden within the dense foliage of Grand Sarret. The Weeping Caverns gained their name as a small stream cascades down the through the caverns making the climb up from the ocean even more treacherous. To make matters worse for any trying to traverse the Weeping Stairwell, the masters of the Conservatory, the Jalmeri training school for spies that sits atop Grand Sarret, are well aware of the Stairwell's existence. As such they have placed a pair of faydhaan to guard the Weeping Stairwell to prevent intruders from accessing the island.

Nature's Glass.title: Nature's Glass
Nature's Glass.extract: Nature's Glass is a small, natural pool of water deep within the Fangwood and to the west of Crossfen. Its surface reflects like a mirror, and a legend has grown up around it that the water reflects the true nature of those who gaze into it. This phenomenon has attracted the attention of some fey, among them Leeta, a swan maiden determined to protect the waters by drowning those who try to poison them.

Tourondel Marches.title: Tourondel Marches
Tourondel Marches.extract: The Tourondel Marches surround the Tourondel River and mark the northern border of Nirmathas. The earliest settlements in the area date back to the Shining Crusade era, and several ruins can be found in the region, which is populated by orcs and undead who sometimes harass travelers. Those who reside in the area have developed skills as merchants and tend to have a more formal demeanor compared to their southern compatriots, likely based on their historical proximity to the lawful nation of Lastwall.

The Three Furies.title: The Three Furies
The Three Furies.extract: The Three Furies is the name given to the three highest peaks of a mountain range in the center of the Isle of Terror. A storm of void energy surrounds the mountains, making it impossible to see the peaks from the base. However, if one passes through the clouds, the summits can be seen rising to great heights. From the top, one can observe the Wizard-King's Pit at the epicenter of the range, filled with the most horrific creatures on Golarion.

Minderhal's Valley.title: Minderhal's Valley
Minderhal's Valley.extract: Minderhal's Valley is located within the Mindspin Mountains, surrounded by high mountains in an area where a river flows. This valley contains a cathedral dedicated to Minderhal, the giant god. Various tribes of giants have inhabited the valley, considering it sacred ground and defending it against intruders.

Whitebridge Station.title: Whitebridge Station
Whitebridge Station.extract:
This page contains spoilers for the following products: River into Darkness.
You can disable this banner in your personal preferences.

Whitebridge Station was an outpost of the Aspis Consortium on the Vanji River in the Mwangi Expanse. It was located roughly 200 miles upriver from the town of Bloodcove, and was a 200-foot-long fortified wooden bridge across the river with numerous wooden structures built upon it. The Consortium allowed others to use the bridge to cross the river in return for a small toll payment.

The Consortium was breeding the magical fungus known as arcophage here.

Ghataking.title: Ghataking
Ghataking.extract: Located in the central Napsune Mountains of Rahadoum, the mountain Ghataking is most famous for the foul inhabitants who dwell upon it. Ghataking is the lair of a zahhak, a powerful type of azi dragon, named Khanjar-Khanayr but more commonly known as "Stone Maw". He lairs on this peak awaiting a divine prophecy delivered by foul Ahriman himself and is served by a cult of Usij, evil div-worshippers all but wiped out by the church of Sarenrae.

Citadel Gheradesca.title: Citadel Gheradesca
Citadel Gheradesca.extract:
Citadel Gheradesca is the headquarters of the feared Hellknight Order of the Chain. Located near the Chelaxian city of Corentyn on a small spit of land connected to the mainland only at low tide, the citadel is thought to be Avistan's most secure prison. The Order of the Chain gladly holds any prisoners rightfully sentenced to jail by a government.

It can be accessed only during low tide, as there are jagged rocks and supposedly sea monsters that feed on the prison's dead. Potential invaders must climb wet, sharp stone stairs that wind up the outside of the island and overlook cliffs of the same jagged rocks that are under the waters. This is the only path up the island and is riddled with locations perfect for ambushes or hidden guard posts.

The prison part of the citadel is said to delve not only into the earth but also under the waves of the ocean surrounding the island. The lowest levels have special cells for more powerful prisoners that have been developed over many decades, and might house powerful creatures that must be caged as well as imprisoned. Some have unusual layouts, such as aquatic terrariums or enchanted game boards. Each is designed to house a specific type of prisoner.

Sele.title: Sele
Sele.extract: The red granite city of Sele in the Mwangi Expanse was once the site of an ancient Mauxi culture. The city's foundations were built on limestone, and eventually it sank beneath the waters of the Mbernbe River. This may have been a catalyst for the Mauxi migration into Thuvia. The only structure which remains above water—a domed palace—is home to a family of wyverns.

Fangspire.title: Fangspire
Fangspire.extract:
Fangspire is one of the shelters of the Abendego Piercing created by the Knights of Salvation after the Eye of Abendego devastated the Sodden Lands.

The magically constructed tower of stone and metal has withstood the storms for centuries and remains in good condition. However, the storms have undermined its foundations, and as a result the tower leans over the sea at a dangerous angle.

Fangspire is the traditional domain of the largest of the region's boggard tribes. The constant warfare between the various boggard tribes ensures no tribe can claim the honour for very long.

Brimstone Falls.title: Brimstone Falls
Brimstone Falls.extract: The Brimstone Falls is a 60-foot-tall waterfall on the Kurka River in the Mwangi Expanse that flows into a wide lagoon at the edge of the Kurka Swamp. The many small channels that form the cataract throw up enough spray that a permanent rainbow hangs above it. The slope of the waterfall's cliff is not steep, and numerous switchback trails climb up its face.

Deep Sky.title: Deep Sky
Deep Sky.extract: Deep Sky is a ruined fortress in the Napsune Mountains of Rahadoum that had once been settled by a group of Jistkan elementalists escaping the elemental storms at the Citadel of the Weary Sky with their elemental servants and golems. Under the elementalists' direction, it was constructed by four genies bound to obey their orders. However, the genies conspired to find a way to curse their masters, and succeeded in creating a curse that led to their masters' downfall.

Guidestar of Desna.title: Guidestar of Desna
Guidestar of Desna.extract: The Guidestar of Desna is a haunted lighthouse and former temple to Desna located on Phahalen Island's southern cliffs. Despite being unmanned, its light still serves as a beacon for ships traveling around the Eye of Abendego.

Shepherd's Rock.title: Shepherd's Rock
Shepherd's Rock.extract:
The cliff-top citadel of Shepherd's Rock is situated in the Napsune Mountains in Rahadoum and has a commanding view of the nearby desert. As of 4711 AR, it was the centre of operations for the Pure Legion and residence of the organisation's leader Karsakim, the Sword of Man.

The citadel's Vault of Lies houses the Legion's vast store of confiscated religious writings. It also trains new recruits into the Pure Legion.

Lost Yemal.title: Lost Yemal
Lost Yemal.extract: Lost Yemal is a ruin of the ancient Jistka Imperium located in the nation of Rahadoum. It is situated in the south-east of the country, in the Napsune Mountains near the mouth of the Uta River.

Tomb of Emperors.title: Tomb of Emperors
Tomb of Emperors.extract:
The Tomb of Emperors is a ruin of the ancient Jistka Imperium located in what is today the nation of Rahadoum. It is situated in the eastern desert, beyond the Uta River, and is a destination for the Pathfinders of Swordmeet Lodge in Manaket.

The Tomb of Emperors was a sprawling, colonnaded structure built at the Oasis of Xerapsion and it is known to have housed treasures made by famous Jistkan artisans. The Tomb was originally dedicated to the fifth ruler of Jistka Imperium, Inperantike Eraliskos, after her death at a decisive battle at Xeraspion—a battle that expanded the Imperium to the region east of the Uta. The nomadic tribes of the area became so impressed by the monument that a lot of nomads joined the Imperium army, forming an entire legion. Ancient Osirian invaders and their div allies looted the surface of the structure and most of its offering chambers during the era of the Night Plague but the Tekritani refused to further pillage the Tomb, leaving its lower levels and catacombs untouched.

Cave of Tiandra.title: Cave of Tiandra
Cave of Tiandra.extract:
The Cave of Tiandra was the cave complex where Tiandra hid the Azlanti relics she pillaged in the later years of the Age of Darkness. Generations later, Arustun discovered and miraculously interpreted these relics, which enabled the rise of the first human civilization after Earthfall: the Jistka Imperium.

Most Jistkan scholars agree that the legendary Cave of Tiandra is located northeast of what is now Manaket on the Inner Sea coast of Rahadoum. The cave appears to be nothing more than a hillside grotto with a single barren chamber and its low ceiling eroded. However, a narrow pitch in the back of the entrance cavern reveals that beneath the initial level lies a vast network of tunnels, dens, and hollows, with soaring drops and huge domes.

The presence of cullitox has led the scholars to believe that the Cave of Tiandra once had a link to the Plane of Earth, and perhaps to other planes. This might explain how Arustun gained the miraculous insight from the relics found there in -4160 AR.

Dim Gate.title: Dim Gate
Dim Gate.extract:
The Dim Gate is an interplanetary gate deep beneath a peak in the Napsune Mountains of the eastern Sodden Lands. It is under construction by the lich diviner Meyi Panaho under the patronage of a mysterious being calling himself Tzriek, a bone sage from the dead planet Eox. Tzriek convinced Meyi to become immortal through lichdom in order to have the time to unlock the secrets of the universe.

A narrow trail winds up the mountain's peak to the Dim Gate complex's observatory, which overlooks a narrow, secluded valley. The mountains protect the observatory from the perpetually rotating arms of the Eye of Abendego that deluge the lowlands of the Sodden Lands, and the observatory's elevation provides a clear view of the sky on most nights. Skeletal champions patrol the grounds around the observatory, and a nightwing called Gloomtide soars above the site keeping watch for intruders.

Menador Keep.title: Menador Keep
Menador Keep.extract:
Menador Keep is a two-story dwarven-engineered gatehouse at the narrowest point of the Menador Gap, which connects the Chelaxian Archduchy of Menador to Ravounel through the Menador Mountains. The keep forms a short, artificial tunnel that bottlenecks traffic, which House Thrune staffs with loyal soldiers who exert heavy taxes and strict regulations on travel.

During the Glorious Reclamation insurgency of 4716 AR, Menador Keep was commanded by Lucian Thrune.

Mount Spire.title: Mount Spire
Mount Spire.extract: At almost 16,500 feet, Mount Spire is the highest peak in the Menador Mountains. The Veil of the Spire falls more than 300 feet into a large pool that is the origin of the Yolubilis River. Bellezeph Villa caters to an elite clientele of tourists who come to admire the falls, try out their mountaineering skills, or engage in amateur spelunking. Behind the falls are extensive networks of caves, home to cave fishers, chardas, goblins, and kobolds.

Citadel of the Weary Sky.title: Citadel of the Weary Sky
Citadel of the Weary Sky.extract: The Citadel of the Weary Sky, formerly known as the Jaizun Citadel, is a mysterious and enigmatic historical site located in the realm of Rahadoum. It holds a significant place in the history of the Jistka Imperium, a once-powerful empire known for its mastery of elemental magic and extraplanar conjurations.

Stygia.title: Stygia
Stygia.extract: Every lie spoken throughout the planes condenses as a drop of poison to flood Stygia (pronounced STIJ-ee-uh), the fifth layer of Hell. It is ruled by the archdevil known as the Source of Lies, Geryon. Amid the tangled swamps and fetid jungles rise moldy ruins, mired temples to false deities, and whole blasphemous cities. The waters of the Styx mix with the layer's venomous bogs, creating vast noxious moors before flowing into vast black seas. Dilapidated avenues paved with cracked stones—remnants of empires that never were—cut through these dense bogs, though they regularly succumb to unexpected floods of stagnant water. Travelers who brave the paths or manage to fight their way through the swamps for long enough inevitably discover examples of the layer's countless ruins, overgrown temples and cathedrals, disparate crumbling monuments, and fortresses upturned as if flung by gigantic and careless hands. Most of these decrepit structures—drawn in their entirety from innumerable mortal worlds—still bear artifacts and artistry from forgotten epochs, typically idols and icons of deities and divine forces unknown to even the longest-lived inhabitants of the multiverse.

Caina.title: Caina
Caina.extract: The archdevil Mephistopheles rules his layer of Caina (pronounced CANE-uh), an unending expanse of hanging cages, narrow stone platforms, and iron bridges. Caina is also known as the Bones of Hell. The realm is regarded as a land of eternal torture and unceasing darkness.

Avernus.title: Avernus
Avernus.extract: Avernus (pronounced ah-VUR-nuhs) is the top-most layer of Hell, and ruled by the archdevil Barbatos.

Menador Gap.title: Menador Gap
Menador Gap.extract: The Menador Gap is the sole passage through the Menador Mountains and Ravounel Forest, connecting the northwestern corner of the Archduchy of Menador to southeastern Ravounel in Cheliax. Part of the gap is a treacherous stretch of narrow mountainside roads constructed long ago by dwarven engineers, with Menador Keep located at the roads' narrowest point.

Revousa Ditches.title: Revousa Ditches
Revousa Ditches.extract: The Katharevousa River meets the Yolubilis River in the Revousa Ditches, deep rents in the ground that are prone to flooding. The site is so well traveled by riverboats that temporary shops set up on anchored, floating platforms at all times except during the rainy season. The Ditches are believed to be home to a rusalka.

Deepmar Penal Colony.title: Deepmar Penal Colony
Deepmar Penal Colony.extract:
The keyword exceeded the maximum length of 150 characters.

Under Chelaxian rule, Deepmar hosted a small penal colony known as Deepmar Penal Colony, where the prisoners mined crystals useful as arcane reagents. The prison camps were abandoned before Ravounel's secession in the Kintargan Rebellion, and the new government has let them remain so.

Grundlescorn Lair.title: Grundlescorn Lair
Grundlescorn Lair.extract: The Rovagug-worshipping hag sisters Grava, Mopple, and Polnivaar Grundlescorn dwell in a marshy section of Ravounel Forest known as Grundlescorn Lair, in a ramshackle hut partially built into the boughs of an ancient oak tree and supported by several crooked but sturdy stilts. The hut overlooks a low hillock in the marsh atop which the hags keep their cauldron and fire pit.

Urchin's Maw.title: Urchin's Maw
Urchin's Maw.extract: Located in the Arcadian Ocean west of Acisazi and northwest of the Dismal Nitch,✝︎ the entrance to the underwater cave known as Urchin's Maw gapes from the side of the continental shelf, overlooking a precipitous drop into the inky depths below. It earns its name from the riot of sizable deep sea urchins that dwell on the rock face surrounding the cave entrance.

Ostergard Manor.title: Ostergard Manor
Ostergard Manor.extract: Ostergard Manor is located in the northwest of Cheliax, near the Menador Gap. Although easily accessible by road, no one dares visit the manor due to rumors that it is haunted. House Kastner lived here, acquiring diabolical lore even before the rise of House Thrune. The Kastners abandoned the manor and moved to Egorian when they no longer needed to conceal their research and devil-binding. The estate passed to the Blakros family in 4712 AR after Damian Kastner married Michellia Blakros.

Tower of Slant Shadows.title: Tower of Slant Shadows
Tower of Slant Shadows.extract: Located in the southwestern plains of Nidal, the Tower of Slant Shadows is a rugged iron edifice that juts diagonally from the umber grassland. It is believed that it sinks several times its own height into the earth beneath it. A mystery to most, the tower is in fact one of the almost-forgotten Star Towers, constructed untold millennia ago by Zon-Kuthon (then Dou-Bral) as part of the process necessary to confine Rovagug within Golarion.

Terapasillion.title: Terapasillion
Terapasillion.extract: Terapasillion (which means "Ziggurat of Night" in the Shadowtongue) is an ancient step pyramid eternally cloaked in shadows that predates Taldan settlement in the region, and is located on the southern North Plains of Ravounel near Nidal. It is rumored to have been built by shadow giants during the Age of Darkness, but was more recently inhabited by an umbral dragon. Despite the fact that the dragon has not been seen in decades, locals still avoid the place with a passion.

Summershade Quarry.title: Summershade Quarry
Summershade Quarry.extract: Summershade Granite Quarry is about seventy miles up the Katharevousa River from the Revousa Ditches. Once a significant source of granite for buildings in Whiterock and Kintargo, it was abandoned as uneconomical by House Jhaltero shortly before Ravounel's secession.

Aevolar's Crossing.title: Aevolar's Crossing
Aevolar's Crossing.extract: Aevolar's Crossing looks like a safe crossing of the Usk River, but this is wrong. A Chelaxian army once tried and failed to cross here, and their bones and rusted armor remain as a reminder.

Blacksulfur Pond.title: Blacksulfur Pond
Blacksulfur Pond.extract: Blacksulfer Pond is a still, black pond southwest of Nisroch. No rivers or stream feed into it, no fish or other aquatic life inhabit it, and no plants grow near it.

Castle of the Captive Sun.title: Castle of the Captive Sun
Castle of the Captive Sun.extract: The Castle of the Captive Sun in Nidal is home to the vampiric nobleman Volsazni Dezarr. The castle is primarily known for housing a very large collection of holy artifacts.

Dauphenal Vineyard.title: Dauphenal Vineyard
Dauphenal Vineyard.extract: The Dauphenal Vineyard has existed since Nidal was under Chelaxian occupation. The vineyard produces a highly sought-after wine called Dauphenal alvarino.

Edammera's Folly.title: Edammera's Folly
Edammera's Folly.extract: Edammera's Folly is a tall spire locked behind large rune-scribed steel doors. It is one of the Towers of the Fiendlorn constructed to serve the experiments toward immortality by Mesandroth Fiendlorn, but was sealed after a planar rift to the Netherworld opened in its base in -398 AR and flooded the tower with creatures of shadow.

Winter Garden.title: Winter Garden
Winter Garden.extract: Owned by a former member of the Umbral Court, the Winter Garden is a cottage in the western Uskwood whose land around it is haunted by disorienting mists and unending snowfall.

Nightbinder's Wharf.title: Nightbinder's Wharf
Nightbinder's Wharf.extract: Nightbinder's Wharf is a fortress located where the Usk River flows into Nisroch Bay just to the south of Nisroch. Shadowcallers are dispatched here for missions abroad, where their mastery of shadows makes them invaluable to dignitaries from various countries. Most of those who hire the shadowcallers are Chelaxian, though private companies also gather here, even though such activities are prohibited in their home countries. Discretion is paramount, and thus Nightbinder's Wharf is shrouded in shadow magic; those unfortunate enough to stumble upon it often vanish.

Novoboro.title: Novoboro
Novoboro.extract: The town of Novoboro, hidden behind a large fortified stone wall, is a trading post originally built by the elves in what is now Cordelon. It boasts a dozen timber buildings and a two-story stone tower, with archer lookouts on top watching the river on which the town is situated. The path to the town is marked by painted trees and tattered banners bearing the image of a crow. Crows are an important omen to the locals, and killing one is punishable by death.

Nystra.title: Nystra
Nystra.extract: Nystra is a ghost-town in the far-eastern reaches of the River Kingdoms. Once a thriving community centered around the cultivation and production of the fine silk produced by the insectoid silk gargoyles, or silkgoyles, its inhabitants were totally wiped out in a revenge plot by the Taldan druid Tharl Grimull in 4664 AR.

Citadel Enferac.title: Citadel Enferac
Citadel Enferac.extract:
Citadel Enferac is the stronghold of the Hellknight Order of the Gate. Located in the Menador Mountains, Citadel Enferac is the primary place where Hellknight signifers (spellcasters) are trained.

Constructed by inhuman hands, only a small part of the fortress rises upon the slopes of Mount Nyisaid. The larger part of the citadel lies within the mountain, spread through great caverns and halls. The smoke of numerous forges and laboratories rises from the fortress like eerie pennants; and spiked towers and precarious walkways mar the higher slopes. At the top of the mountain, the iron statue of a huge Hellknight guards a gate for flying visitors. The citadel includes garrisons, training halls, armories, libraries, workshops, and fortifications. Tales also tell of a prison for terrible creatures within the citadel which resembles a pit into a black eternity.

Deep within Citadel Enferac is a portal that leads to the Razor Stair, a library in Stygia, fifth level of Hell. The Order of the Gate's signifers frequently go there to consult with Mahshoka and Sister Drove, two uniilas who curate the Razor Stair. Despite decades of control, the Order of the Gate has not explored far beyond the portal due to the difficulty of navigating the Razor Stair; nevertheless, its members' sojourns have brought back many fantastic secrets and powerful weapons. Spies infiltrating Citadel Enferac have reported that this portal is no longer the only one within Mount Nyisaid, and nor is it the most terrifying.

House of Lies.title: House of Lies
House of Lies.extract: The House of Lies is the famous meeting place of the Guild of Liars, an organization of storytellers, con men, actors, and other conversational acrobats. Founded in 4354 AR and located on a river island in the part of Nidal's Uskwood north of the Usk River, the Guild meets here every five years to exchange stories, quips, tall tales, and other feats of verbal legerdemain. The person judged to be the most convincing liar is awarded the coveted title of "truth maker", and is given access to the House of Lies' extensive cabinet of curiosities.

First Step.title: First Step
First Step.extract: First Step is a massive fortress located at the heart of the Exalted Wood in the nation of Razmiran. Here, new recruits to the Church of Razmir are trained to become missionaries for placement both at home and abroad.

Forgotten Track.title: Forgotten Track
Forgotten Track.extract: The Forgotten Track is a prison mine located at the bottom of a large canyon in a region of Razmiran known as the Tracks. Those who have lost faith in or displease the Church of Razmir are sent here; it is called the Forgotten Track because that is the eventual fate of most who are sent there.

Mount Rinia.title: Mount Rinia
Mount Rinia.extract:
Mount Rinia is a 15,000-foot-tall peak in the western Menador Mountains, located in the Archduchy of Menador in Cheliax. It is the Maiestas River's source,✝︎ and home to orcs, humans, and yrthaks.

The mountain is named for the subject of a Taldan ballad, a noblewoman who flung herself from a mountain to escape an arranged marriage, but sprouted wings and flew away to unknown freedom.

Bleachbone Cave.title: Bleachbone Cave
Bleachbone Cave.extract:
Bleachbone Cave is a concealed cavern located in the Vergan Forest in Razmiran. This cave remains shrouded in secrecy, its sole entrance artfully hidden by an enormous, bone-shaped white rock. Few outside of its clandestine inhabitants are even aware of its existence, rendering it an ideal refuge for those seeking to elude the ever-watchful eye of the Razmiri clergy.

In recent times, a small group from Whispertruth, a faction dedicated to opposing the rule of the Living God Razmir, has sought sanctuary within Bleachbone Cave. Korellen Duinsol, an aiuvarin ranger, leads this band of rebels. Concerned about Razmir's apparent knowledge of their activities and the countermeasures he has been taking, the group has chosen to keep their newfound sanctuary a closely guarded secret.

Unbeknownst to the rebels and their captive, a malevolent presence known only as the Ancient One resides in the deepest recesses of the cave, patiently waiting. It comes from the Dominion of the Black and patiently waits for the right living mortal to possess in order to escape its trap in this cave.

Moonless Mirror.title: Moonless Mirror
Moonless Mirror.extract: The Moonless Mirror is a relic of the Everwar with Cheliax, a sealed portal to the Netherworld that sits in the cliffs overlooking Nisroch Bay and seethes with magical energy.

Wingless Rock.title: Wingless Rock
Wingless Rock.extract:
Wingless Rock is a small, sparsely vegetated island off the west coast of Nidal. Local fishermen avoid it because there is nothing to catch, and there are also strong superstitions about bad luck. The Aspis Consortium used the island for smuggling artifacts from Nidal, but the operation was shut down by the Pathfinder Society.

What few people know is that beneath the island lie laboratories dedicated to Conference Z research.

Deadeye's Haunt.title: Deadeye's Haunt
Deadeye's Haunt.extract: Deadeye's Haunt is a narrow valley on the border between Molthune and Nirmathas, at the southern edge of Fangwood. It is the site of the bloodiest battle in the war between the two nations.

Queen Bes.title: Queen Bes
Queen Bes.extract: Queen Bes is the only settlement of any size on the Shackles island of Besmara's Throne. Because the entire isle is a holy site to the worshipers of the pirate goddess Besmara, the town contains a large proportion of pilgrims.

Lilywhite.title: Lilywhite
Lilywhite.extract: Lilywhite is a small pirate town on the western coast of Motaku Isle in the Shackles that is home to a large number of worshipers of Cayden Cailean. It is the smallest of Motaku's settlements and was founded in 4682 AR by two pirates, Delemona Burie and Shaggard Halleck, devotees of Cayden Cailean. Alongside the foundation of the town, the pair funded the construction of a grand stone temple to the Drunken Hero. Halleck was killed in an alcohol-fueled duel in 4692 AR. Halleck's son, Little Shaggard, and Burie are the town's pirate lords.

Rapier Bay.title: Rapier Bay
Rapier Bay.extract:
Rapier Bay is a rowdy small town on Motaku Isle in the Shackles. It is led by the pirate lord Maxevale Janis, captain of the galleon Motaku Maiden. Maxevale always supports Lady Tessa in the Pirate Council, earning the nickname "the Puppy", though no man dares to say it to his face.

Rapier Bay is not a busy harbor like Quent, but is used by pirates who do not want to draw much attention to themselves while sailing near the island. Rapier Bay is famous for one of its smithies, the Steel-Clad Giant, that makes some of the finest blades west of the Arch of Aroden.

Heggapnod.title: Heggapnod
Heggapnod.extract: Heggapnod is a small village on the Cannibal Isles in the Shackles. Heggapnod has been at war with the neighboring village of Kukgukmol for years. The two villages rapidly lose men, and they will surely obliterate one another if nobody steps in to stop them. The Blood Queen enjoys this bloodshed and watches the weekly massacres through its kuru surrogates in each village.

Spiral of Bones.title: Spiral of Bones
Spiral of Bones.extract:
For another meaning of "Spiral of Bones", please see Spiral of Bones (Fangwood).

Pathfinder Volume Seven: Spiral of Bones was released on January 30, 2019. The graphic novel follows the Pathfinder iconic heroes to the city of Kaer Maga, where they find themselves involved in an ancient plot to cheat death deep below the city. The series is written by Pathfinder developer Crystal Frasier. This book compiles the five-part "Spiral of Bones" story arc—including game content by Jason Keeley—as well as a never-before-released poster map, but does not include the five-part Starfinder comic "Helping Hands" that appeared within the individual "Spiral of Bones" issues.

Fort Drejas.title: Fort Drejas
Fort Drejas.extract: Fort Drejas is one of the many forts abandoned by Nirmathas during the war with Molthune. The fort is located in the southern area, right where the Marideth River flows into Lake Encarthan. Easy access to the river is one of the fortification's strategic advantages.

Fort Machema.title: Fort Machema
Fort Machema.extract: Fort Machema is an abandoned fort a few days' journey from Tamran along the shore of Lake Encarthan. The fort is in a well-protected area overlooking the lake, but there are no soldiers to maintain it.

Fort Ursoss.title: Fort Ursoss
Fort Ursoss.extract: Fort Ursoss is an abandoned fort on the south bank of the Marideth River in the interior of the Fangwood. The swampy terrain makes travel on foot difficult and serves as a defensive feature. The fort dates back to the Shining Crusade era and was restored by Cheliax after its independence. Nirmathi soldiers burned the fort to prevent it from falling into Molthune's hands, though the stone basement still stands.

Banukmaud.title: Banukmaud
Banukmaud.extract: The village of Banukmaud is the second-largest Ghol-Gan ruin in the Cannibal Isles. It is inhabited by the cannibalistic humanoids known as the kuru.

Vilelock.title: Vilelock
Vilelock.extract:
Vilelock is a shady, lawless settlement on the Shark Island in the Shackles. As expected, gory brawls, lurking cutpurses, and casual murders are frequent in town.

A thriving market in Vilelock specializes in the acquisition and sale of exotic poisons and alchemical substances outlawed in the more civilized nations of the Inner Sea region.

The town changes overlords often but its current overlord is Nisia Gbele, the Mwangi captain of the frigate Bloody Cudgel.

Raketooth.title: Raketooth
Raketooth.extract: Raketooth is the second-largest port on Shark Island in the Shackles. It is a dreary settlement specializing in pilfered luxury wares that are usually sold on Motaku Isle or to the few non-slaver merchants who come to Shark Island. Raketooth rarely has difficulties with sedacthys who plague the island. The pirate lords of Raketooth, Mauril Breakwater and "Baron" Venigo Palpathe, maintain the relative peace of the town by providing tribute to the nearby sedacthy tribes.

Moak Harbor.title: Moak Harbor
Moak Harbor.extract: Moak Harbor was a port city on Shark Island in the Shackles that was home to more than 8,000 people at its peak more than 200 years ago, but today is only rotting ruins.

Blackblood Cay.title: Blackblood Cay
