Blackblood Cay.extract: Blackblood Cay is a small port town on Shark Island in the Shackles. It is located on a small hidden inlet of the island and was founded by Free Captain Simeon the Vain in 4703 AR. The port now hosts ten more pirate captains, while Simeon remains lord of Blackblood Cay. The town features berths along the wharf for only six vessels at a time.

Zhenbarghua.title: Zhenbarghua
Zhenbarghua.extract: Zhenbarghua is the westernmost kuru settlement in the Cannibal Isles and maybe the least dedicated to the Blood Queen. Kuru from Zhenbarghua sometimes manage to escape their depraved culture and islands in search of a new life.

Halabad.title: Halabad
Halabad.extract: Halabad is a town on the Rampore Isles in the Shackles, inhabited mainly by slaves who are born and raised there. The town is full of ramshackle housing complexes and equally decrepit breeding centers, where slaves are forced to intermingle and create more slaves. Brutal trainers in the town's training camps teach children at an early age to perform the tasks for which they are made for, breaking their backs and spirits.

Goatshead.title: Goatshead
Goatshead.extract: Goatshead is the main settlement on the Firegrass Isle in the Shackles. It was founded In 4642 AR by a trio of pirate captains who settled on the island. The Pirate Council laid the blame for all and any pirate misbehavior against Sargavan vessels on the Firegrass island buccaneers. Made the scapegoats of every Sargavan complaint, these three captains named their settlement Goatshead and adopted a flag with a white goat's head on a black field.

Kora.title: Kora
Kora.extract: The settlement of Kora, the seat of the Rampore Isles' administration in the Shackles, is surrounded by rice and sugar plantations. The crops are sold to the less self-sufficient islands in the Shackles, and the trained slaves who work these fields also serve as a commodity to be traded to slavers. Brutal overseers run the operations and mercilessly exploit the slave labor.

Siren's Lash.title: Siren's Lash
Siren's Lash.extract:
Siren's Lash is a temple in Port Peril devoted to the goddess Calistria in her aspect as a deity of lust. As of 4712 AR, the courtesans of Siren's Lash were often invited to attend parties at the Hurricane King's palace of Lucrehold, but they also served as secret information brokers.

The temple's aiuvarin high priestess Mistress Livdana Giedrence is a known aficionado of all things Kyonin, and anyone able to procure Livdana a gift from the elven kingdom is practically guaranteed to earn an audience with her.

Siren's Lash was once devoted to the goddess of love Shelyn and contains many architectural features of the goddess, including topiary gardens and exotic bird gardens.

Slipcove.title: Slipcove
Slipcove.extract:
Slipcove is a small port on Bag Island in the Shackles.

The lord of Bag Island, Jolis Raffles, attacks the slave ships of Cheliax and offers freed slaves the chance to live on Bag Island. As a result, Slipcove has the largest halfling population in the Shackles.

Colvaas Gibbet.title: Colvaas Gibbet
Colvaas Gibbet.extract: The small river port of Colvaas Gibbet is located on the Mainland area of the Shackles, approximately 25 miles northeast of Port Peril.

Pex.title: Pex
Pex.extract: The pirate town of Pex is located on the Devil's Arches island of the Shackles, at the south end of the perilous Straits of Balthus. It is a rough, ramshackle settlement, ruled by the capricious Hardluck Massey. He is known for his impulsive punishments against the unfortunates who have annoyed him. Pex boasts a large three-story inn called Damned If It Didn't Rain run by Hegmar of Frembrudd that gathers buccaneers of Ulfen descent as well as Varisian merchants who own warehouses in the town.

Rumbutter.title: Rumbutter
Rumbutter.extract: Rumbutter is the southernmost settlement on Bag Island in Shackles. It is a sprawling, hilly, farming community known for its apiaries and honey. Rumbutter honey finds its way throughout the Shackles and Sargava. Rumbutter's three taverns on its main road, Bedda's Berth, the Gilded Lily, and Shopin's Roundhouse, serve unique blends of iced butterscotch beers in the rainy season and honey wine in the drier months.

Mezdrubal.title: Mezdrubal
Mezdrubal.extract:
Mezdrubal is a small town at the southwestern region of Devil's Arches island in the Shackles. It is ruled by a petty buccaneer called Longbeard who resents his tribute that Hell Harbor demands at the beginning of each month.

Longbeard could not even think to refuse to pay his tribute, and he usually takes out his frustration at the poor victims of his cruel and often arbitrary judgement. Mezdrubal's ruler is also known for his love of fine clothing and jewelry, and many pirate vessels head to his town to sell such wares to Longbeard.

Heslandaena.title: Heslandaena
Heslandaena.extract:
Heslandaena is a subterranean settlement below the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles. The cenotes that dot the island offer aquatic entry to miles of sea caves, which eventually emerge into unflooded, humid caverns. These moldy dark caves and passages lead to Heslandaena. The isolated town is inhabited by drow cut off from the Darklands many years ago by a curse.

The dark elves were part of the Harkule clan. They offended a powerful matriarch and were banished for their insolence. The isolated drow town is ruled by matriarch Pelanquelle, wicked and resentful for her exile. A number of pirate captains know about the drow settlement and are trusted by its ruler: Adis Vraisdottir, captain of the fast sloop North Wind, which sails out of Mezdrubal; White Hollis Dobilo, captain of the brig Stormrunner, which sails from Drenchport; and Constanza Purgote, captain of the sloop Constanza's Whim which sails from Port Peril.

The three pirates, each unaware of the others, all have lucrative trading deals with matriarch Pelanquelle bringing foodstuffs and supplies in exchange for raw gemstones and rare subterranean substances.

Yelligo Wharf.title: Yelligo Wharf
Yelligo Wharf.extract:
Yelligo Wharf is a small pirate settlement on the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles.

The settlement was established in 4709 AR and hides itself in Leatherwing Bay. The ramshackle settlement consists of a few squat buildings made of driftwood and dirt clinging to the shore. It is home to four pirate crews that prey on the merchant ships travelling close to the mainland, east of Shenchu Bay. Kamshika Heavyhand captains the armored sloop Baleful Glare, Mean Steebyn captains the barkentine Rough Customer, Noila the Knife captains the brig Harm's Way, and Pendahl of Rahadoum captains the sloop-of-war Baalzebul.

These four captains capture Inner Sea merchant vessels on their way home from Port Peril, stealing their cargoes and selling their crews to the cultists of Dahak for sacrifice. Chadric of Laekastel, who lives in the only stone building of the town, is the apparent head of the business.

Egregzia.title: Egregzia
Egregzia.extract: The ruins of the Ghol-Gan city of Egregzia are located on the western side of the Terwa Lake in the Mwangi Expanse. The ruins are mostly buried beneath the alluvial grime that has spread from the lake in the thousands of years since Ghol-Gan's fall. Freshwater monsters lurk in the sunken structures of the dead cyclopes' waterside city, such as merrows, scrags, and ulat-kinis.

Blood Fountain.title: Blood Fountain
Blood Fountain.extract: The Blood Fountain is a Ghol-Gan ruin on the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles. A magical cistern stands at the center of the ancient ruined settlement. The cistern appears to be fully functional, save that red, bilious liquid constantly pours from it, seeping into the ground around it and ensuring that no flora grows there.

Haigui Wan.title: Haigui Wan
Haigui Wan.extract: Haigui Wan is a small town in Shenchu Bay island in the Shackles. It is built on the northeastern peninsula of the island to serve as the base of Shenchu's defensive fleet which protects the island from Dahak's Fang. Most of the fleet's ten ships are kobuksons ("turtle ships").

Arena.title: Arena
Arena.extract: Arena is a small town on the northeastern coast of Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. It gets its name from the enormous gladiatorial arena that occupies the center of the settlement. This arena is home to some of the most competitive and violent blood sports in the region.

Tyvas-Devas.title: Tyvas-Devas
Tyvas-Devas.extract:
Tyvas-Devas is an inland fortress town on the Devil's Arches island in the Shackles. It is made of stone by slave labor on the banks of the Holshod River. Arronax Endymion, the ruler of Devil's Arches, forbids outsiders from entering the town without his permission. The castle at the town's center is built as the admiral's redoubt should his Chelaxian enemies arrive.

Tyvas-Devas is overseen by Endymion's fiercely loyal lieutenant, Iome Paemadar. The black-clad summoner and her fearsome eidolon are known to walk the streets at night, making certain that everything is ready for her beloved admiral. The town is surrounded by rich farmland tended by slaves, which provides food crops for all of Devil's Arches. It is known that the admiral's great wealth lies in the cellars below the Tyvas-Devas' fortress.

City of Bleeding Stones.title: City of Bleeding Stones
City of Bleeding Stones.extract: The City of Bleeding Stones is the largest set of Ghol-Gan ruins on the island of Devil's Arches in the Shackles. It is nestled in a cove beside the island's vast, hilly rainforests. Many major expeditions have been sent to the City of Bleeding Stone, but each has ended in disaster, even in the expedition's group complete disappearance. The few survivors claim that some primordial evil plagues the ruins. The Pathfinder Society has expressed interest in exploring the city.

Downpour.title: Downpour
Downpour.extract: Downpour is a small fishing town on Tempest Cay island in the Shackles. It was founded to take advantage of the plentiful fish that once made their way up and down the Bluehook River during spawning season. Since the cruel catoblepas Gruzugr made its home in the river, the waters have been poisoned and the fish are gone. The residents survive by harvesting the numerous nearby clam colonies.

Maidenspool.title: Maidenspool
Maidenspool.extract: The town of Maidenspool once vied with Drenchport for dominance of the island of Tempest Cay in the Shackles. Free Captain Bradesmar Wache of Mediogalti poured enormous resources into the town, building structures of stone and fine lumber. At the peak of Maidenspool's growth, Bradesmar openly boasted that the Master of the Gales's city would soon be just so much driftwood. Quickly after Bradesmar's proclamation, Maidenspool was attacked by swarms of athamarus. Most of the town was destroyed by fire and hundreds of its residents were killed, including Bradesmar's darling wife Ella Wache.

Chalk Harbor.title: Chalk Harbor
Chalk Harbor.extract:
The grim mining town of Chalk Harbor is the only functioning settlement on Whyrlis Rock island in the Shackles. The town is the base of operations of the slaver Alahandra Boisich, rumored to have once been a powerful baroness of Cheliax. The ruthless Alahandra captains the barkentine Cloudbuster's Dream.

There is no wildlife around Chalk Harbor save for bands of earth elementals and the few clusters of cullitox that lair underground, and no one can eat the sparse blighted crops that grow from the rocky soil. Water is drawn from the Elsewhere River, but all food is supplied by other islands and paid for by Alahandra.

Vezhnu.title: Vezhnu
Vezhnu.extract:
Vezhnu is a small town on the shore of Rampore Isles in the Shackles. Large herds of livestock graze in the plains surrounding the town, and several slaughterhouses are arranged on the shore. The excess blood from the slaughterhouses drains into the ocean, attracting ravenous sharks.

The Docks of Chastening are used for the punishment, which entails lashings on the docks and—for more serious crimes—being repeatedly dipped beneath the shark-infested waves until the beasts consume the victims.

Myscurial.title: Myscurial
Myscurial.extract: Myscurial is a small town on Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. It is built on Sweet Tooth Lake in the island's interior and hosts naval gladiatorial games between captives or poor debtors armed with simple spears in rowboats. The games become more interesting because of the unique beasts brought into the aquatic arena by mayor Salty Chuzek, which include fiendish lukwatas, giant toads, snapping turtles, and an unbeatable, ravenous catoblepas.

Norboro Ashes.title: Norboro Ashes
Norboro Ashes.extract: Norboro was the second town built on the cursed island of Whyrlis Rock, in the Shackles. It was founded only 6 months after its sister town of Heskott Bay. At about the same time that the residents of Heskott Bay vanished into thin air, the town of Norboro turned to ash. The location is now known as the Norboro Ashes.

Heskott Bay.title: Heskott Bay
Heskott Bay.extract: Heskott Bay was a town founded on the cursed island of Whyrlis Rock in the Shackles. It was located on one of the three decent anchorages left on the island. It was built as an outpost for the fleet of the pirate lord Baillie Heskott in the early years of the Shackles. It seems that three years after its foundation, all the residents of the town vanished into thin air. Those few who visited the abandoned town said that they heard the sounds of a busy city interrupted by screams of terror.

Shouk.title: Shouk
Shouk.extract: The Shouk is a tribe of degenerate ogrekin that once were human holy men. Their lair is in the Barrier Wall mountains, roughly south-east of the Thuvian city of Duwwor. Once the Shouk were holy men and oracles of some renown. Their wisdom was sought by many other nomadic tribes. However, at some point in the distant past, this clan of holy men became tainted by the blood of ogres and ogrekin began to be born among the ranks of the Shouk. With each generation the taint spread, and more and more brutish, vile ogrekin were born. Now the tribe is composed exclusively of ogrekin, and has lost all prophetic ability. Purely out of tradition, the tribes of the area still occasionally seek out the Shouk in times of great need. These visits rarely yield any result other than the loss of life, as the Shouk are also now cannibals.

Gembasket.title: Gembasket
Gembasket.extract: The Gembasket spans an area in the Barrier Wall mountains approximately a mile in diameter, across razored peaks, unexpected drops, rocky ground, and impassable rises. Natural resources are abundant within the Gembasket, including nodes of gemstones, precious metals, and veins of minerals that run its rocky walls. A narrow and dangerous path navigable only by experienced travelers winds up through the mountains to the Gembasket.

Citadel of the Alchemist.title: Citadel of the Alchemist
Citadel of the Alchemist.extract:
The fortress called the Citadel of the Alchemist is carved out of the base of a mountain in the Barrier Wall known as the Bent Razor in Thuvia. Above ground it appears as a modest stone structure. However, below ground there are vast tunnels housing alchemical traps and guardians. Hidden somewhere in this network are some of the best alchemical laboratories in the Inner Sea region. Artokus Kirran, creator of the sun orchid elixir, lives in the Citadel of the Alchemist, which was built for him many years ago.

Every year a blind, mute servant comes forth from the citadel with six vials of the elixir, to be auctioned off in one of Thuvia's five city-states.

The Stonewarden druids of the Barrier Wall mountains have a formal agreement with Thuvia to assist travelers that are seeking to reach the Citadel of the Alchemist.

Painted Flutes.title: Painted Flutes
Painted Flutes.extract:
The Painted Flutes is a vast canyon nestled within the Barrier Wall mountains of western Katapesh. The valley is named for the unique geological phenomena found within, including dozens of hoodoos—tall, wind carved pillars of rock that look like totem poles—that are made of a variety of layers of different, brightly-coloured rocks as a spectacular landscape. The wind howls through the valley and when it reaches the right speed in creates haunting music like some sort of giant wind instrument.

Due to the forces of erosion constantly changing its shape, as well as its strong winds, the direction and duration the song created by the hoodoos is never the same twice. The valley attracts many bards and other musicians to hear its wondrous song. The valley is also inhabited by clans of janns and wandering air elementals who roam the deep canyons.

Uldani Necropolis.title: Uldani Necropolis
Uldani Necropolis.extract: The Uldani Necropolis is a site in the desert interior of Thuvia. It lies not far from the city of Pashow.

House of Oblivion.title: House of Oblivion
House of Oblivion.extract: In Thuvia's interior desert lies the House of Oblivion. The towering structure was built over 7,000 years ago and was designed to lure Ahriman to Golarion. While the House of Oblivion is primarily built above ground, its various wings do not fully reside in the Universe.

Tomb of the Bound Sphinx.title: Tomb of the Bound Sphinx
Tomb of the Bound Sphinx.extract:
The Tomb of the Bound Sphinx is a pyramid shaped tomb constructed in the Barrier Wall mountains. It was constructed in -1116 AR by Pharaoh Kenaton to be his final resting place, but weas unknowingly built over an underground chamber into which it collapsed a few decades after construction. This destroyed some of the treasure it contained and shifted some of its passages, making exploration difficult.

As well as the final resting place for Kenaton, the tomb also contains a magical prison which contains Hephtethnet the Reaver, a hieracosphinx who terrorized Osirion during Kenaton's life.

Mephit Spring.title: Mephit Spring
Mephit Spring.extract: Mephit Spring is a valley in the Footprints of Rovagug mountains of western Osirion that is the locus of several powerful elemental forces. Located between the Kho-Rarne Pass and Tar Kuata, the springs once were the home of many scamps who communed peacefully with one another. As of 4714 AR the mood has darkened, as a demon had taken over the area.

Olivine Tomb of Djederet II.title: Olivine Tomb of Djederet II
Olivine Tomb of Djederet II.extract: Little is known about the Olivine Tomb of Djederet II, located in Osirion during its First Age in what is now Thuvia. Its namesake pharaoh Djederet II was the nephew of Hakotep I and succeeded him as Osirion's ruler in -1611 AR, and is known for reverting many of the Sky Pharaoh's works.

Asuulek's Mouth.title: Asuulek's Mouth
Asuulek's Mouth.extract:
Asuulek's Mouth (sometimes spelt Asulek's Mouth) is the most active of Osirion's two volcanoes and the lair of the ancient red wyrm Asuulek, for which it is named. At its summit is a large magma lake, and the volcano frequently spews out toxic gases, ash, and lava bombs onto the nearby area. Small communities work the fertile soil around the base of Asuulek's Mouth, however, they do so with a watchful eye, keeping watch for both eruptions and dragons.

Some locations on Golarion are infused with elemental power and Asuulek's Mouth is one such location. Occasionally, a person living near Asuulek's Mouth will be touched by the fiery elemental energies of the volcano and gain the extraordinary ability to rapidly heal when covered in the ashes of destructive fire.

Lost Fortress of Mekshir.title: Lost Fortress of Mekshir
Lost Fortress of Mekshir.extract: The Lost Fortress of Mekshir guarded the trade routes from Osirion to its province of Thuvia until it was buried in a sandstorm during the khamsin season of -841 AR. The fortress is rumored to contain treasures including an armory of magical weapons and several months of payroll, but is said to be guarded by the undead.

Sphinx Head.title: Sphinx Head
Sphinx Head.extract: The Sphinx Head or Black Sphinx is the largest sphinx statue in Osirion, located in the desert between Shiman-Sekh and the Junira River. It is constructed from black granite, and has sunk up to its chin in the desert sands. Its features are believed to be those of the Sky Pharaoh, Menedes I, staring into the center of Osirion.

Klarwa Fountain.title: Klarwa Fountain
Klarwa Fountain.extract:
The Klarwa Fountain is located in the desert of western Osirion, in the region known as the Footprints of Rovagug. The site is a bowl-shaped depression filled with rain water atop a steep cinder cone from a long-extinct volcano, which rises hundreds of feet above the surrounding area. The water never quite evaporates despite the heat, and gases occasionally vent from deep within the cone. The violent plumes of water are sometimes visible from miles away, and one such column drew the attention of the anchorite Klarwa as he was dying of thirst in the badlands during the Second Age, giving the landmark its name.

As of 4714 AR the hill is used by the few travelers making their way through western Osirion, and its water makes the ascent up the steep hill well worth the effort. Some hermits live within sight of the Klarwa Fountain and hike up its sides when driven by desperate thirst, as anchorite holy Klarwa was.

Cathedral of Claws.title: Cathedral of Claws
Cathedral of Claws.extract: The Cathedral of Claws is an ancient site in Thuvia that predates the Age of Legend. It is located at the foot of the mountain Elzehan in the Barrier Wall range east of the Thuvian city of Pashow,

Ytterjorna.title: Ytterjorna
Ytterjorna.extract: Ytterjorna is a trading town on the eastern shores of Glacier Lake in the Irriseni province of Thronehold.

Pjallarane's Stone.title: Pjallarane's Stone
Pjallarane's Stone.extract:
Pjallarane's Stone is a rock formation in Irrisen's province of Thronehold. According to legend, Baba Yaga turned her rebellious daughter Pjallarane to stone here—and the formation does bear a remarkable resemblance to a woman clinging to a tree and begging for mercy. However, most scholars believe Pjallarane departed Golarion with her mother, just like all the other former Queens of Irrisen.

The stone does seem to have some strange arcane power, however, although no one has ever been able to access it. Some believe the stone might hold the key to banishing Irrisen's eternal winter, although there is scant evidence for this.

The local snow goblins view the stone as a holy site, and occasionally bury offerings to Baba Yaga nearby.

Desert's Mouth.title: Desert's Mouth
Desert's Mouth.extract:
Desert's Mouth is a black pit at the bottom of a conical hole in the shifting sand dunes of the Osirian Desert near the Thuvian border. It resembles the lair of a giant ant lion and its circular hole is a strangely regular entrance into a huge cavern which is probably connected to the Darklands. Phosphorescent gases drift out of the pit at night and those who stay too near the pit report hallucinations about dark caves dripping with water. Descending the sandy slopes is difficult but ascending is almost impossible. The Desert's Mouth hasn't been explored in recent memory but ancient texts describe forests of glowing fungus and strange crustaceans skittering in the dark.

The Yerbira are said to use the Mouth to execute those who breach their few taboos. They lower the condemned into the pit with a rope and throw the rope after him. If the condemned can climb out the pit he is considered forgiven. No one has ever climbed out the pit so far.

Killik-Tura Oasis.title: Killik-Tura Oasis
Killik-Tura Oasis.extract: The Killik-Tura Oasis, sometimes spelt Kilik-Tura Oasis, is located in the western reaches of the Footprints of Rovagug region of Osirion, east of the Junira River. The water at the small oasis is cool and plentiful. Killik-Tura Oasis is a popular stop for caravans despite the rumors that large worm-like creatures occasionally emerge from the oasis. The oasis is formed as a perfect circle, effectively bottomless, and its subterranean, water-filled tunnels branch out for miles. A fantastical tale says that the tunnels open into dry caverns filled with crawling horrors and feral humanoids. The truth is that Crawlbrother, a distinguished agent of the nearby Darklands ghoulish city of Nemret-Noktoria, lurks beneath the waters.
